Notices
Copyright
© 1996-2026 Rocket Software, Inc. or its affiliates. All Rights Reserved.
Trademarks
Rocket is a registered trademark of Rocket Software, Inc. For a list of Rocket registered trademarks go to:

the trademarks, service marks, or product names of their respective owners.
Examples
This information might contain examples of data and reports. The examples include the names of individuals,
companies, brands, and products. All of these names are fictitious and any similarity to the names and addresses
used by an actual business enterprise is entirely coincidental.
License agreement
This software and the associated documentation are proprietary and confidential to Rocket Software, Inc. or its
affiliates, are furnished under license, and may be used and copied only in accordance with the terms of such license.

of encryption technologies, and current use, import, and export regulations should be followed when exporting this
product.

## Page 3


Corporate information
Rocket Software, Inc. develops enterprise infrastructure products in four key areas: storage, networks, and
compliance; database servers and tools; business information and analytics; and application development,
integration, and modernization.
Website: www.rocketsoftware.com
Rocket Global Headquarters
77 4th Avenue, Suite 100
Waltham, MA 02451-1468
USA
To contact Rocket Software by telephone for any reason, including obtaining pre-sales information and technical
support, use one of the following telephone numbers.

• Australia: 1-800-823-405
• Belgium: 0800-266-65
• Canada: 1-855-577-4323

Contacting Technical Support
The Rocket Community is the primary method of obtaining support. If you have current support and maintenance
agreements with Rocket Software, you can access the Rocket Community and report a problem, download an update,
or read answers to FAQs. To log in to the Rocket Community or to request a Rocket Community account, go to
www.rocketsoftware.com/support. In addition to using the Rocket Community to obtain support, you can use one of
the telephone numbers that are listed above or send an email to support@rocketsoftware.com.

## Page 13


Introduction to Mobius
Rocket® Mobius is a powerful enterprise-level software solution designed to optimize information storage and
management across enterprises. Mobius Server acts as the central repository for storing and managing content,
while Mobius View/Rocket® Content Explorer serves as the user interface to access both Mobius Server and other
repositories.
Note: Rocket Content Explorer is an alternative option for Mobius View, and you can use either one.
Mobius View is the heritage client while Rocket Content Explorer offers an improved user experience featuring
equivalent functionality and smoother navigation. During deployment, you can choose the interface that best suits
your needs.
Close to 80% of information in an enterprise is unstructured, making it difficult to search for and retrieve specific
information. Structured data helps enterprises derive business value from it. Mobius technology consolidates
unstructured content — reports, data transactions, and images generated by applications throughout the enterprise
— into an integrated repository; and delivers it to employees, customers, internal auditors, suppliers, and business
partners. Mobius integrates, tags, and stores content, enabling users to search for and access a specific piece of
content through Mobius View.
Mobius makes it easy to manage different content formats. It supports formats from content generated by
enterprise-level applications like SAP and Baan, as well as desktop applications such as Excel, Word, and Open Text
Analytics. It also handles common formats like XML, HTML, PDF, PostScript, PCL, IBM AFP, Xerox DJDE/Metacode, and
image formats like JPG and PNG.
Users can extract specific pages from these diverse content types and seamlessly deliver them as an integrated set of
pages through three primary channels: email, web, and print.
Mobius makes content processing easier with its client-server architecture. This architecture includes a main
repository with a database, DAFs (Document Archive Files), and configuration files. There are also network clients and
services, a search engine for quick searches, and server tools to directly manage both databases and archives. This
architecture helps streamline content management and boosts productivity.
To provide a holistic content management solution, Mobius works with additional software components such as
repositories, load balancers, API gateways, and application adapters. Mobius takes in large volumes of enterprise
content, stores it, and delivers it back on demand.
Here are some of the key features of Mobius:
Easy access to documents
Search for and easily access content using Mobius View/Rocket Content Explorer. Share content with customers.
Define rules to extract structured information from unstructured data and generate reports to make them
easily searchable.
Privacy and legal stipulations
Mobius is a custodian of content, retaining content for exactly the time it needs to be preserved for legal
reasons, and deleting content based on legal and privacy requirements. Mobius also enables redaction to mask
sensitive information.
Data analysis and in-context audit logs
With the capabilities of Content Insights, view the audit event log of a document in the context of a document
viewer. Analyze audit data better by viewing audit log along with the document in the document viewer.
Analyzing data from audit events helps businesses derive value from a large volume of data by spotting trends
to make better business decisions.
Related information
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 13


## Page 14


Mobius components
Changed and discontinued functionality
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 14


## Page 15


Mobius components
Mobius consists of components that when used together help manage a complete content life cycle, from creating or
adding content, to retaining and deleting content based on an organization’s regulatory requirements.
The following components comprise the Mobius solution.
Mobius Server
Mobius Server acts as a repository that provides storage and management of content. When used together with
Mobius View/Rocket Content Explorer, they provide content management features.
Mobius View/Rocket Content Explorer and Mobius Administrator
Mobius View provides two web-based interfaces called Mobius View/Rocket Content Explorer (clients) and
Mobius Administrator. Both interfaces allow access to Mobius Server and other repositories.
Mobius View is the heritage client while Rocket Content Explorer offers an improved user experience featuring
equivalent functionality and smoother navigation.
Note: Currently, Rocket Content Explorer and Mobius View are offered as ‘tandem options’; Rocket
Content Explorer will ultimately replace Mobius View. For more information, see the Rocket
Content Explorer user guide.
Mobius View/Rocket Content Explorer is used by end users to search for and view content that they are
authorized to access.
Mobius Administrator is used by administrators to configure content sources and repositories.
Content source
Content sources manage the stored collection of archives and the database containing information about the
archives. Mobius Server and other repositories work as content sources. After creating a Mobius content
source, create a Mobius repository within the content source.
Repository
Repositories represent source servers that contain documents and any settings required to talk to the source
servers, or restrict or customize the view of documents. Mobius Server, CMIS servers, or any other custom
servers are repositories.
Mobius Server and Mobius View databases
Mobius Server and Mobius View/Rocket Content Explorer each require a separate database. Both use separate
schemas and can run from the same or different database servers. All components require configuration, which
needs to be persistent. Configuration information is stored in each respective database.
External storage systems
Besides locally defined storage, supported external storage systems can be used to store content. For example,
Amazon S3 or EMC Centera. A list of supported external storage systems are in the system requirements.
Interfaces and adapters
Different interfaces allow Mobius to interact with content storage systems. For example, Mobius View
SharePoint interface includes adapters to access content in SharePoint applications.
Mobius REST API
REST API that enables Mobius to connect to other applications.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 15


## Page 16


Getting started with Mobius Administrator
Mobius Administrator is a browser-based interface that administrators can use to configure content sources,
repositories, and various Mobius View features.
Mobius Administrator provides a separate interface for a Mobius View administrator and a Mobius Server
administrator. Different URLs are used to log on to the administrator interface separately.
If an administrator manages Mobius View and Mobius Server together, then the Mobius Server administrator
interface is accessible as an additional menu item called Mobius Server in the left menu of Mobius Administrator.
As an administrator, use the left menu of Mobius Administrator to configure the features that users will access in
Mobius View.

The URL to access Mobius Administrator only for Mobius Server is:

For both URLs:

installed.

A content source needs connection information to a specific Mobius Server instance, also referred to as a document
server that is on a Windows or Linux system. A document server binds a database to a Mobius Server instance.
Add and configure a content source on Mobius Administrator to connect to a specific document server. Each content
source has repositories that store a collection of archives. Once a content source is added, add and configure each
repository for:
• Archiving content to create ingestions.
• Providing content to viewing clients, like Mobius View.
• Distributing portions of content to users.
• Granting content access to users.
Administrators use the Mobius Server > Mobius Content Sources panel to add and manage Mobius Servers as
content sources, followed by repositories specific for a Mobius content source, called Mobius repositories.
Alternatively, use the Content repositories panel to add content sources other than Mobius Servers, and to manage
all content sources and repositories.
Related information
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 18


## Page 19


Content repositories
Defining document server connections
A document server binds a database to a Mobius Server instance. To access a document server from Mobius
Administrator, Mobius Administrator requires a connection to the document server. You define this connection using
the Document Server Connection Configuration utility (cfgdsc) that is provided with the Mobius Server on Windows
and Linux installation.
Before you begin: Mobius Server is running.
Mobius Administrator connects to a document server using protocol and port number information that is provided
with each document server. Document server connections are shared among all client applications on a computer,
but not between computers. To access multiple document servers from a computer, define a connection for each
document server you want to access.
After defining a document server connection using the cfgdsc utility, use the same information to configure a
content source and repository creation on Mobius Administrator.
1. Navigate to the Mobius Server installation directory.
2. To add a document server connection, type the following command at the command prompt:
cfgdsc add connection name -c SOA -s http -h host machine -p port -e service endpoint –d do
cument server
where:
connection name
A user-defined name to identify the connection you are defining. For easy reference, use the name of the
document server that you are connecting to.
host machine
The name or IP address of the host system the document server is installed on.
port
The non-SSL HTTP port number used by Apache Tomcat.
service endpoint
The service endpoint that allows a client to connect to the document server. The default is /vdrws/
services.
document server
The name of the document server to access.
For example, add a connection called 'Payroll', that connects to a document server named 'CustomerDocs' on
port 1540 on a host named 'Myserver':
cfgdsc add Payroll -c SOA -s http –h Myserver –p 1540 –d CustomerDocs -e /vdrws/services
Modify the port, for example, in the 'Payroll' connection from port 1540 to 1560 :
cfgdsc modify Payroll -c SOA -s http –p 1560 -e /vdrws/services
Modify the host, for example, that is used in the 'Payroll' connection to 'Server2', the port to 1560, and the
document server to 'InternalDocs' :
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 19


## Page 20


cfgdsc modify Payroll -c SOA -s http –h Server2 –p 1560 –d InternalDocs -e /vdrws/services
3. To delete a document server connection, type the following command:
cfgdsc delete Payroll
4. To display a document server connection, type the following command:
cfgdsc print Payroll
5. To list existing document server connections on the current computer, type the following command:
cfgdsc list
Related information
Document server connection configuration utility (cfgdsc) keyword reference
Document server connection configuration utility (cfgdsc) keyword reference
Use the Document Server Connection Configuration (cfgdsc) command line utility program to configure document
server connections on Windows and Linux systems.
To view the syntax and full list of command line keywords, enter the following command:
cfgdsc ?
The keywords for this utility are:
add
Creates a new document server connection.
delete
Deletes a specified document server connection.
modify
Modifies a document server connection.
The modify [option] keyword has several options:
• -h host machine
Name of the machine that hosts the document server. This name must match the name you entered
when you defined the document server.
• -p port
Primary port number for the Mobius server.
• -d document server
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 20


## Page 21


Name of the document server to access.
• -s protocol
Protocol type, specify either http or https.
• -e service endpoint
The service endpoint that allows a client to connect to the document server. The default is /vdrws/
services.
print
Displays settings of a document server connection.
list
Lists existing document server connections.
Adding Mobius content sources
To use a Mobius content source with a Mobius repository, add and configure this content source type.
Before you begin: A defined document server connection is available.
Use the document server connection information (host name, port number) for the content source configuration.
1. Select Mobius Server.
2. On the Mobius Content Sources panel, click Add.
3. Complete the following fields.
HTTP schema
Select HTTP or HTTPS as the SOAP protocol.
For HTTPS, provide a Mobius certificate to the Mobius View JVM as explained in the Mobius View
documentation.
Host name
The fully qualified domain name of the computer where Mobius Server is installed.
Port number
The port number for Mobius Server. Default values are:
◦ 8443 for an SSL connection
◦ 8080 for a non-SSL connection
Note: These port numbers should correspond to the SSL and non-SSL port numbers
specified in the Mobius Server Controller Configuration window.
REST endpoint
The REST service endpoint value is set by default. For Mobius 8 or later, this is set to /vdrws/admin .
Authentication
Select one of the authentication options for the authentication process on the content source level.
◦ NONE
◦ ALIAS
Access the content source without providing authentication credentials. The alias user name and
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 21


## Page 22


password are used to authenticate users. Each content source can have a different alias.
◦ PROMPT
Entered credentials of logged on users are passed to the content source.
RMI port
The port number of the RMI service when using Mobius SAP interface. Default is 1099. Do not change the
default settings unless there is a port conflict.
Max open connections
Maximum number of available open connections in the connection pool of the content source is 8. Do not
change this setting unless instructed by Rocket Software Technical Support.
4. Click Apply.
Related information
Defining document server connections
Configuring encryption for content sources
Create an encryption key to encrypt data between a viewing client and Mobius Server. For example, Mobius View and
Mobius Server.
Before you begin: A security key file is already provided.
1. From Mobius Server > Mobius Content Sources, hover over a content source.
2. Click the ellipsis icon and then select Encryption.
3. Enter the directory path that contains a key file with an encryption key.
A key file is used to encrypt and decrypt information between a viewing client and Mobius Server.
4. Click Create a new key and complete the following fields.
Key Name
Unique key name.
Key Value
A phrase to create the key. It can be between 16 to 32 characters in length.
5. Click Apply.
Customizing legacy settings
A configuration file provides the possibility to customize Mobius behavior. For example, mobius.reg is a configuration
file provided in the Mobius Server installation directory at ProgramData\ASG\VDRNET\version.
Before you begin: Rocket Technical Support has provided instructions to configure a legacy setting.
Note: If you edit legacy settings without instructions from the Rocket Technical Support, Mobius
operations might get affected.
From Mobius Administrator, you can customize a legacy setting on the Legacy Settings panel. New settings or
changes to existing settings are applied immediately to the configuration file. Restarting Mobius Server is not
required.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 22


## Page 23


1. From Mobius Server > Mobius Content Sources, hover over a content source.
2. Click the ellipses icon and then select Legacy Settings.
Note: Legacy Settings is not available for MVS content sources on the mainframe.
3. On the Legacy Settings panel, configure the fields as instructed by Rocket Technical Support.
Copy paste the provided information from Rocket Technical Support into the fields.
a. To update a setting, copy paste an existing subsection and key name while the cursor is in the Value
field to automatically populate the field with an existing value. Then, copy paste the provided value
information.
b. To add a setting, copy paste the information into the fields.
c. To return a value in any of the fields to its previous state, click Revert.
4. Click Apply.
Configuring tracing for Mobius Server
Tracing helps you record Mobius Server activity for this server instance to help diagnose specific issues. Set the
severity level for recorded information for primary and Java tracing.
Tracing uses settings from two different files:
• mobius.reg for primary tracing
• mobiuslog.properties for Java tracing
The output of the tracing configuration are written respectively to rsdwin.msg and vrdws.log files.
The changes in the severity level for both types of tracing are applied only when you restart the Mobius Server.
1. From Mobius Server > Mobius Content Sources, hover over a content source.
2. Click the Settings icon of a content source.
3. Expand the tracing sections.
4. Configure the following fields in each of the tracing sections.
Default warning level
(default) Errors and above. You can change to another severity level as needed.
(primary tracing only) Current warning level
The selected level is the same as the default.
Trace path and file name
For primary tracing, the directory path must include the log file name - rsdwin.msg.
For Java tracing, the directory path must include the log file name - vrdws.log. Any updates to Java
tracing settings automatically update the mobiuslog.properties file.
Diagnostic trace flag value
Default is 0.
(Java tracing only) Performance trace flag value
Default is 0.
Note: To log diagnostics and performance, a specific trace flag value defines the logging level. For
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 23


## Page 24


example, 32 is the default value for both primary and Java tracing for complete diagnostics and
performance information. To change trace flag values, contact Rocket Software Technical
Support.
5. To configure rollover tracing, select one or more options.
On startup
(default) Start logging on start up.
On reaching maximum size
Creates a new trace file once the trace file reaches 10 MB (default) which is the maximum trace file.
Periodically
Configure a logging policy based on time and date, followed by the number of days before creating a new
log file. The date time format to use is YYYYMMDDHHMMSS . The default number of days to rollover is 7.
6. To specify the maximum number of backup files to keep, enter a numeric value.
Default is 10.
Adding Mobius repositories
A Mobius content source can host one or more Mobius repositories. Add and then configure a repository on the
content source's panel.
Each Mobius repository needs a Mobius Server database. The different types of information stored in a repository's
database are represented as separate icons on a repository panel:
• Users and user groups
• Indexes and index groups
• Content classes
• Encryption keys
• Archive policies
• Archive Creator and print spool policy selection rules
1. From Mobius Server > Mobius Content Sources, click a content source.
2. On the content source panel, click Add.
3. On the New Mobius Repository panel, configure the following fields:
Repository ID
An identifier for the repository.
DSN
Select an ODBC DSN.
JDBC Driver Name
Select a valid JDBC driver name based on the DBMS of the selected ODBC DSN.
JDBC URL Name
Enter the JDBC URL name for connecting to the database.
DSN Username, DSN Password
Enter the database user credentials.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 24


## Page 25


4. Click Apply.
Related information
Viewing repository sessions
Configuring authentication for content sources
Mobius content source authentication consists of configuring an authentication option that applies to the content
source. Content source authentication controls how users connect to a content source.
1. From Mobius Server > Mobius Content Sources, select a content source.
2. Click the Edit icon to configure an authentication option.
a. Select an authentication option for the content source level.
▪ NONE
▪ ALIAS
Access the content source without providing authentication credentials. The alias user name and
password are used to authenticate users. Each content source can have a different alias.
▪ PROMPT
Entered credentials of logged on users are passed to the content source. The username and
password for the user are the same for all of the repositories assigned with this authentication
method.
b. Click Apply.
Configuring custom authentication
Use custom authentication when implementing external authentication for Mobius or a custom authentication DLL
file.
Before you begin: Depending on the authentication method:
• A custom security DLL is available.
• Environment variables are added or the Helm chart already contains the appropriate custom header variables
containing user information, for example, custom authentication using SSO with Apache and LDAP.
Whichever method is used, the authentication type is set to 'Custom' at the repository level once the Mobius Server
instance is added as a content source. The availability of additional fields for authentication depend on the
authentication method.
1. Log in to Mobius Administrator.
2. Select Mobius Server > Mobius Content sources to view all content sources.
3. Click a content source to view its repositories.
4. Click the Edit icon to open a repository definition.
5. From the repository's Details pane, expand the Authentication section, then select Custom as the
authentication method.
6. Enter the path and file name of the library that contains the security information.
7. Select Retrieve group membership from this Custom Authentication Exit to obtain a list of the content that
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 25


## Page 26


users have access.
8. Click Apply.
Related information
Creating a custom authentication DLL
Setting up Mobius custom authentication
Configuring Mobius Server database authentication
Mobius server database authentication is valid in both Windows and Linux environments. For a testing environment
without an identity provider, Mobius Server can act as an identity provider by comparing users and their credentials
against those stored in the Mobius server database by default.
The system administrator provides credentials to the application users and can control whether the user can change
the password, based on which the Change Password option appears on the Mobius View application dashboard.
1. Log in to Mobius Administrator.
2. Select Mobius Server > Mobius Content sources to view all content sources.
3. Click a content source to view its repositories.
4. Click the Edit icon to open a repository definition.
5. From the repository's Details pane, expand the Authentication section, then select Mobius Database as the
authentication method.
6. Select Disable authentication for users with no password to prevent users who do not specify a password
from authenticating to the document server.
If a user does not provide a password or if a user is created without a password in the Mobius server database,
then any attempt to authenticate to the document server is rejected.
7. Click Apply.
Configuring Single Sign-On for document servers
When multiple repositories are configured to use the same SSO key, users can log in once and have access to all of
the repositories.
Each repository is linked to a content source, with the document server providing the content. The defined server-
side SSO key authenticates users. Configure the same key in the Mobius host definition under the Content
Repositories panel (Mobius Administrator) to enable Mobius View to authenticate with the server.
1. From Mobius Server > Mobius Content sources, click a content source to display its repositories.
2. Click the Settings icon.
3. Expand Single Sign-On in the Details pane, then select an option.
Separate sign-on to each document server
Select this option to require users to log in each time they want to access the repository.
Allow single-sign on to multiple document servers
Select this option to allow users to log in once to access multiple repositories.
4. When the option multiple repositories is selected, click Add to add a new SSO key, then select an option:
a. Select to automatically create a new key.
For a new key, enter the directory path.
b. Select Upload an existing single sign-on key to use an existing SSO key.
To use an existing key, click Browse.
5. Click Apply.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 26


## Page 27


Configuring session inactivity
Specify the maximum time a session can remain inactive by configuring the inactivity threshold for sessions.
1. From Mobius Server > Mobius Content Sources, select a content source to display its repositories.
2. Hover over a repository, and click the Settings icon.
3. Expand Sessions in the Details pane.
4. Configure these fields.
Maximum time for inactive sessions (seconds)
Enter the length of time a session can be inactive before this repository terminates the session.
Maximum time to check inactive sessions (seconds)
Enter the number of seconds to specify the frequency that this repository checks for session inactivity.
5. Click Apply.
Configuring the database connection
Configure a connection to a database that will be used by the repository. You must specify parameters, such as JDBC
driver, JDBC URL, and user credentials, before fine-tuning the database access performance.
1. From Mobius Server > Mobius Content Sources, select a content source to display its repositories.
2. Hover over the repository you want to configure.
3. Click the Settings icon.
4. Expand the Database section.
5. Configure the following fields:
ODBC data source
The data source identifies the database that the repository uses. Click Test ODBC to test the current
ODBC connection.
JDBC driver
Name of the JDBC driver that is used to access the database management system.
◦ SQL Server - com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerDriver
◦ Oracle - oracle.jdbc.driver.OracleDriver
◦ PostgreSQL - org.postgresql.Driver
JDBC URL
The URL that the driver uses to access the database.
◦ SQL Server URL
jdbc:sqlserver://<DBhostname>:<port>;DatabaseName=<databaseName>[;SelectMethod=cursor]
Where:
▪ DBhostname is the name or IP address of the machine on which the database server is
running.
▪ port is the port number that this server uses. 1433 is the default port for the SQL Server.
▪ databaseName is the name of the database to access.
For example:jdbc:sqlserver://MOBNY:1433;DatabaseName=VDRNET;SelectMethod=cursor
◦ Oracle
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 27


## Page 28


jdbc:oracle:thin@<server>:<port>:<OSN>
Where:
▪ server is the name or IP address of the machine on which the database server is running.
▪ port is the port number that this server uses. 1521 is the default port for the Oracle database.
▪ OSN is the Oracle service name associated with the database to access.
◦ PostgreSQL
jdbc:postgresql://<server>:<port>/<db>
Where:
▪ server is the name or IP address of the machine on which the database server is running.
▪ port is the port number that this server uses. 5432 is the default port for PostgreSQL
database.
▪ dbis the name of the PostgreSQL database to access.
User ID, Password
The User ID and password that the server needs to access the database.
(optional) You can use Integrated Windows authentication on the SQL Server database instead of
requiring a log-in ID and password from the user. To use Integrated Windows authentication:
a. Clear the Assign a user ID and password for the database checkbox.
b. Add the string ;integratedSecurity=true at the end of the JDBC URL to indicate the Mobius-
supplied JDBC driver that Windows Authentication is used.
c. Modify the ODBC DSN configuration for the SQL Server ODBC data source to indicate that the
Integrated Windows authentication is being used, then select With Integrated Windows
authentication.
Once the required authentication is set, verify the user ID used for authenticating to SQL Server matches
the account ID entered on the Log On tab for the properties of each Mobius service.
6. Expand Database Connection Options to fine-tune the database-access performance.
Related information
Optimizing database connections
Configuring memory and database fetch limits
Optimizing database connections
Reduce processing overhead by configuring a database connection pool so that multiple user sessions can share
active database connections.
Before you begin: A database connections is already set.
Database connections are opened as required, and will slowly reach the maximum number of allowed connections.
After the connections are established, sessions share them as needed, and idle connections are reassigned. Not
closing the sessions saves processing overhead by preventing many redundant open and close connection
operations.
1. From Mobius Server > Mobius Content Sources , select a content source to view its repositories.
a. Hover over a repository and click Settings.
b. Expand the Database > Database Connection Options section.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 28


## Page 29


In most cases, the default values are sufficient. Modify the following fields only if instructed by Rocket
Software Technical Support.
Maximum number of open connections
Maximum number of open connections available in the connection pool. Values are 1 to 9999.
Default value is 500.
The value cannot be less than the minimum number of open connections set in the next field. It
cannot exceed the maximum number of allowed connections available from the database server.
Setting the value to 9999 allows the pool to grow infinitely. Use this with caution.
The number of connections equal to the default value of connections are not opened immediately.
Connections are opened only as needed.
Minimum number of open connections
Note: Modifying this value is only recommended for Linux systems.
Minimum number of open connections available in the connection pool. The values are 0 (default)
to the maximum value set in the previous field.
Increasing this value to a larger number can improve server performance, but do not set it higher
than necessary. Because the server always opens these database connections, opening more
connections than needed might result in unnecessary processing and reduce overall efficiency.
Maximum idle time
Maximum idle time for a connection, in seconds. Connections that are idle beyond the value of this
parameter are closed unless the minimum number of open database connections is not yet
reached. Default value is 300.
Change this setting only if the time between the database transactions exceed the default value.
Patrol frequency
Specifies how often (in seconds) to check for idle connections. Default value is 30.
Unless instructed by Rocket Technical Support, do not change the default value.
c. Click Apply.
2. Monitor the system to check if the new values are improving the processing efficiency and continue to modify
the values until the system performance is satisfactory.
Related information
Configuring the database connection
Configuring memory and database fetch limits
Configure the maximum amount of memory used by Mobius server to retrieve multiple pages from the repository
and the maximum number of returned rows.
1. From Mobius Server > Mobius Content Sources, select a content source to display its repositories.
2. Hover over the repository you want to configure.
3. Click the Settings icon.
4. Expand the Limits section.
a. Set the maximum amount of memory used by the Mobius server to retrieve multiple pages from the
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 29


## Page 30


repository.
The default is 1024 KB.
Set the value depending on the number of pages in each content section. Increasing memory increases
the performance by reducing the number of server calls.
b. Select to specify the maximum number of rows retrieved for each search result.
Helps limit the number of returned rows that are displayed to a user while entering a query. There is no
default limit. All rows matching the search criteria are returned, but it can affect the response time.
Apply a limit to improve the response time, but the returned results list might be incomplete.
5. Click Apply.
Adding encryption keys for archiving
Add and then set the encryption key as the active key when archiving content. You can view the encryption key
properties and any of the archives encrypted with this specific key.
You must specify the encryption key properties, such as the key name, key-value, and (optional) select the option to
make the encryption key the active key on the Add an Encryption Key panel.
1. From Mobius Server > Mobius Content Sources, click a content source to view its repositories.
2. Click a repository to view its items.
3. On a repository panel, click Encryption keys.
4. To add a new encryption key, click Add.
a. Configure these fields.
Key Name
Unique key name.
Key Value
A phrase to create the key. It can be between 16 to 32 characters in length.
Set this encryption key as the active key during archive creation and retrieval processes
Select the checkbox to use this encryption key as the active key. An upright key icon is appears for
the active key.
You can always change to another encryption key as the active key. The new active key is applied
immediately to subsequent archiving.
b. Click Apply.
5. To view encryption key properties, click the Settings icon.
Information about the encryption key are listed, for example, the key creation date, and when the key was last
used.
6. To enable encryption for archives of a content class, click Content Classes on the repository panel.
a. Click Add to add a content class.
b. Specify general content class information in the General section.
c. Expand the Archiving section.
d. Select the Encrypt all archives for this content checkbox.
Archives for this content will be encrypted using the active encryption key.
e. Click Apply.
7. To view archives encrypted with a specific encryption key, return to the repository's main panel and click
Encryption keys.
a. Click an encryption key.
A table with a list of ingestions encrypted by this key is displayed.
b. From the list of displayed ingestions, click an ingestion to view its properties.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 30


## Page 31


You can sort the list of ingestions using the column headers, and also delete one or more ingestions, or
all ingestions.
To delete one or more ingestions, hover over an ingestion, select the checkbox, then click Delete.
Configuring archive processing
Configure storage of content pages that do not match to any policy, logging, creating and exporting index options
when processing created archives.
1. From Mobius Server > Mobius Content Sources, select a content source to display its repositories.
2. Hover over the repository you want to configure.
3. Click the Settings icon.
4. Expand Archive Creation on the Details page.
5. To store pages of a content when archiving cannot identify the content class, expand Unmatched Pages
options, and enter a directory path.
By default, these pages are stored in a file with a SPL extension in the server’s system TEMP directory.
6. To configure logging information related to archiving processing, expand Logging Options.
Configure the following fields.
Directory
The archiving log file, which records all processing actions, is stored by default in Mobius Server’s system
TEMP directory.
Extension of the archiving log file
LOG is the default extension of the archiving log file.
Keep the archive creation processing log file after processing is collected
By default, this option is not selected.
7. To use one or more commands to call a program after the archive is created, expand Index Options.
Select the Issue the following command after the archive is created checkbox, and then enter the absolute
path and file name of the command in the Command field. The program must reside in a location accessible to
the Mobius server.
Table: List of applicable macros used as commandsTable with macros that are used in the command
and the values that are replaced when the program runs
Macro Value that replaces the macro
@ReportID Content class
@VersionID The ingestion
@Path Path to the exported index files
@Dir Path to the location of the exported index files
@Format File format of the exported indexes.
@XmlFilename To export to XML, enter the name and path of the generated XML.
8. Archiving performance might be affected when many archive creations are started within a short time frame.
Select Optimize for archiving bursts to handle the archiving processing for high frequency of incoming
requests.
Related information
Exporting indexes

## Page 32


Exporting indexes
If archive indexes are stored in the Mobius server database, specify the options to insert the indexes in the Index
Options section of a repository.
Before you begin:
• It is recommended to take a backup of the exported file.
• If the indexes are exported and are not committed to the database, all indexing information will be lost.
• If Simple Search is enabled, select not to insert indexes to disable the automatic indexing.
1. From Mobius Server > Mobius Content Sources, select a content source to view its repositories.
2. Hover over the repository you want to configure.
3. Click the Settings icon.
4. Expand Archive Creation > Index Options.
The indexes file is exported to either .xml or .csv file format. This text file is used as input to another program,
such as a workflow program or a bulk load utility.
a. Select the Export index names checkbox to export indexes.
b. Specify the directory in which the indexes are created.
c. Specify the export file type, either .xml or .csv.
d. Click Apply.
5. In the exported file, check for the following:
◦ If column headings are added to the .csv file.
◦ If indexes are inserted into the database during archiving.
If no indexes are inserted into the database during archiving, you can use a bulk load utility to insert the
indexes.
Table shows how the exported file names are constructed and created after export is complete
File type File name File created
XML Report_ID_ReportVersion.xml One XML file
CSV file with data from the
Document table Report_ID_ReportVersion_DOCUMENT.csv One CSV file
CSV file with data from the
Topic table Report_ID_ReportVersion_TOPIC_Topic_ID.csv One CSV file for
each index
This sample CSV index file example shows data that is exported into a CSV index file. The column headings are
added to this file.
"RPT_VERSION_ID","RPT_AND_PAGE","PAGE_OF_DOC","LOCATION","TOPIC_VALUE"
769007116,10868444960000000001,0,-1,"0000009"
769007116,10868444960000000001,0,-1,"0000019"
769007116,10868444960000000001,0,-1,"0000029"
769007116,10868444960000000001,0,-1,"0000039"
769007116,10868444960000000001,0,-1,"0000049"
769007116,10868444960000000001,0,-1,"0000059"
769007116,10868444960000000001,0,-1,"0000069"

## Page 33


Managing the content input file after archiving
Use the Post-Archiving section of a repository to specify whether to keep, move, or delete the content input file.
1. From Mobius Server > Mobius Content sources, and select a content source to display its repositories.
2. Hover over the repository you want to configure.
3. Click the Settings icon.
4. Expand Post-Archiving .
5. Specify whether to keep the content input file in the current location, move it to another directory, or delete it.
For each condition, the following action is performed:
◦ Archive is created successfully.
◦ Archive is created, but the archiving process could not find a required index item.
◦ Archive is not created.
Note: The Move to new directory option for any post-archiving action does not apply when the
content input file is on Amazon S3.
To control deletion from the acreate or MobiusRemoteCLI acreate commands, select the Control content
input file deletion from the client instead of the server checkbox.
Note: Use this option only for backwards compatibility with earlier Mobius versions.
6. Click Apply.
Related information
acreate command - Creating archives
MobiusRemoteCLI acreate
Configuring search
Use the Full Text and Simple Search section of a repository to configure simple search, set automatic indexing, and
specify the collection information for the repository.
Note: When you change the already set collection information for a repository, access to the existing
indexed information is lost.
1. From Mobius Server > Mobius Content sources, select a content source to display its repositories.
2. Hover over the repository you want to configure.
3. Click the Settings icon.
4. Expand the Full Text and Simple Search section.
Solr and ElasticSearch are two options; select one to configure.
Note: Container deployments do not support ElasticSearch.
a. Specify the required collection information (protocol, port, host server name, collection name) if this is
the first time Simple Search is enabled for this repository.
b. (Solr only) Click Set to the current Mobius instance to set the collection information for the Mobius
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 33


## Page 34


Server instance associated with the repository.
c. To set the type of search results you expect, select one or both options in Search control to allow:
▪ Simple searches of document metadata
▪ Full text searches of document content
d. For Indexing control, select the Automatically create indexes and content during archiving for full
text and simple search checkbox to enable automatic indexing.
Note: Enabling automatic indexing affects only new archives. Existing data is indexed with
command line commands.
5. Click Apply.
Configuring a repository for Records Management
Specify automatic deletion of documents, the time interval for records management processing, and number of
active threads to process.
1. From Mobius Server > Mobius Content Sources, select a content source to display its repositories.
2. Hover over the repository you want to configure.
3. Click the Settings icon.
4. Expand the Records Manager section.
5. Configure the following fields:
Automatically manage event-based retention for compliant storage systems and automatically delete
documents for eligible content
Select to automatically deletes expired content and extends the retention for an archive that is controlled
by event-based retention stored on compliant storage systems. If the content has no retention, it is not
considered by the Records Manager for automatic deletion.
Time interval
Specify the number of hours between Records Manager processes. The default time interval (hours) is 5.
Threads
Specify the number of threads between Records Manager processes. The default number of active
threads is 4.
6. View the Records Manager status and start and end time of the previous run.
Statuses include:
◦ ACTIVE - Currently deleting expired content.
◦ IDLE - Records Manager is on but is not currently running a job.
◦ EXCEPTION - Records Manager failed with an exception and automatic processing has been disabled only
for this run.
To re-enable automatic processing, use Mobius Administrator or restart the server.
7. Click Apply.
Related information
Records Management
Configuring archive retention for Records Management
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 34


## Page 35


(optional) Setting an external encryption key for a repository
By default, an internal encryption key is provided to encrypt or decrypt other encryption keys which are stored in the
Mobius server database, and used for encrypting archives. To use an external encryption key instead, configure the
Keys Encryption section of a repository.
Before you begin: Decide between using an internal encryption key or an external encryption key. Each time you
create a new external encryption key, you must configure the file name and location of the key.
When you restart Mobius server, all encryption keys stored in the Mobius server database are re-encrypted with the
new encryption key.
1. From Mobius Server > Mobius Content Sources, select a content source to display its repositories.
2. Hover over the repository you want to configure.
3. Click the Settings icon.
4. Expand Keys Encryption on the Details pane, and then select the encryption key to use.
Use the internal encryption key
The internal encryption key will encrypt all keys and archives stored in the database.
Use the external encryption key
Enter the file name and location of an external encryption key that is used to encrypt the customer's key
to encrypt content. The encryption key must be an AES 256-bit key encoded as an HEX string.
Make a copy of the file that contains the encryption key and store the file in a secure location. If this file is
corrupted or accidentally deleted, all the keys encrypted using this file are unusable. As a result, all the
archives encrypted using these keys become unreadable. There is no way to recover the keys or the
content archives.
◦ If you use the vdrdbxml command or dbimport command to import encryption keys or archives to
another machine, copy the file that contains the encryption key to that machine.
5. Click Apply.
6. Restart Mobius server.
Viewing repository sessions
View any running repository session on a Mobius Server. To check the running sessions, click the refresh icon to poll
Mobius Server.
The session information table lists Mobius View requests to a repository on a Mobius Server. Use this information,
when a session between Mobius View and Mobius Server fails, for example.
1. From Mobius Server > Mobius Content Sources, click a content source to view its repositories.
2. Hover over a repository and click the ellipses icon.
3. Select View Sessions.
Clicking each session displays session information on the details panel.
To terminate an active session, hover over the session and click Delete that appears on the Session ID column.
4. Click the Quicker Refresh icon to poll the server for any new sessions every 2 seconds, otherwise click the
Slower Refresh icon.
A slower refresh will poll the server every 10 seconds. A quicker refresh action does not persist when switching
to a different panel. Returning to the View Sessions panel will display the Quick Refresh icon.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 35


## Page 36


Note: Polling the server every 2 seconds might impact Mobius Server performance.
Related information
Adding Mobius repositories
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 36


## Page 37


Define Mobius content repositories
Manage Mobius repositories from the Content Repositories page.
The following information is required to define a Mobius repository:
• Name: A name that uniquely identifies the Mobius repository.
• Repository type: Specify the type of repository that will be accessed. The supported types are as follows:
◦ Mobius
◦ CMIS
◦ SharePoint
◦ Custom
• Connection settings: Information required to connect to the content source and repository subsets that
specify the scope of the content that can be accessed.
• Mobius repository options: The following options apply to all Mobius repositories:
◦ Universal Indexes: When enabled, the repository configuration specifies which indexes are available in
search requests and results. The index names from the repository can also be mapped to a more user-
friendly Universal Index name. Universal Indexes are also defined to support federated search across
multiple Mobius repositories by mapping index names from different repositories to a common
Universal Index name.
◦ Redaction: Allows redaction policies to be applied to content retrieved to hide sensitive information.
◦ Authentication: Specifies the authentication type used to access the repository: Alias, Prompting, Single
Sign-On.
◦ Repository type-specific options: Additional configuration settings are available and vary depending on
the type of repository being defined.
Related information
Index mapping guidelines
Redaction
Revision control
You can create new revisions of a document by using Revision Control. You can manage the revisions of a new
document with multiple actions, such as Check-In, Check-Out, Cancel Check-Out, and View History.
To use revision control, Mobius View users must have the following Mobius server permissions that you can assign on
the Authorization panel.
• Global archive creation
• Add or modify document index name values
• Define policies (for creating revisions in policy-parsed documents)
• Annotation View and Create annotations (if Retain annotations from previous versions is selected during
check In)
For repositories enabled for revision control, lists such as search results include only the most recent revision of the
document. To view previous revisions, Mobius View users select View History.
To find documents they have checked out, users can go to the My Checked Out Documents entry in the Favorites
panel.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 37


## Page 38


Users may only update the status of checked-out documents (using the Check In or Cancel Check Out action) for
initially checked-out documents. Users granted the Document Revision Manager role may also update the status of
checked-out documents for documents checked out by other users.
For Records Management and Revision control, placing a hold on a document puts the latest revision of the
document on hold. While on hold, a document cannot be checked out.
Related information
Checking out and checking in documents
Canceling Check Out
Viewing History
Revision Control
Revision Control
Revision Control provides the ability for users to create and review revisions of a document by selecting a document
and specifying a Revision Control related action.
Revision Control is available in the following panels:
• Browse (Classic Tile, Content Tree, and Grid modes)
• Search
• Favorites
• Collections
• Enterprise Folders
Revision Control supports document related use cases, for example:
Use Case: Document Portal
A Mobius View administrator creates a Mobius View repository, “HR,” containing company-wide documents
published by the HR department. Users view the most recent revision of the document.
Members of the HR department can create new revisions of the documents.
• The Mobius View admin enables a revision control option on the HR repository.
• Users can browse or search for documents in the HR repository . For example, Vacation Policy.docx,
Telecommuting Policy.docx.
• The user responsible for maintaining HR documents publishes a new revision of the vacation policy doc
for 2020. They do the following:
◦ Check Out Vacation Policy.docx. A copy of the document is downloaded.
◦ Edit Vacation Policy.docx.
◦ Check In Vacation Policy.docx. The value of the Year metadata field is changed from 2019 to
2020.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 38


## Page 39


• Users can now see the updated vacation policy document.
• New documents can be added to the HR repository using the Capture feature or by calling Mobius View
Archive Write API.
Use Case: Document Collaboration
In preparing a presentation, a group of team members is responsible for creating a PowerPoint (.ppt)
presentation.
• The team lead uploads a PPT file using the Capture feature, which contains an outline of the topics to be
covered.
• A team member checks the file out, edits it to include additional details, and checks in the updated file,
creating Revision 2.
• The team lead reviews the presentation and makes final changes by checking the document out, editing,
and checking in the updated file, creating Revision 3.
• Team members can also select the View History action to see previous revisions made to the document.
Checking out and checking in documents
To make changes to a document, check out the document. Then, check in the document to upload the changes made
to the checked-out document. A newly revised document is created when a document is checked in. The current
revision is always the latest revised document.
Note: Checking out a document is not possible when index-based page access is enabled for the
content source this content class belongs to, and this content class is configured with a page-access
index.
1. Hover over the document name.
2. To check out the document, click the ellipses icon, and select Revision > Check Out.
A message indicating the document is checked out is displayed under the document name, and a check mark
icon is displayed.
3. To check in the document after changes are made, click the ellipsis icon, and select Revision > Check In.
a. Upload the document by dragging and dropping the document file, or click Browse.
b. Add a comment for the Check In action describing the changes made.
By default, annotations from previous revisions are included in the uploaded document.
Editing metadata is only possible when permission is enabled in Mobius Administrator.
c. Click Check In.
A message indicating the document is checked in is briefly displayed, and the updated document
displays an updated revision number. The check mark icon disappears when the document is checked in.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 39


## Page 40


Canceling Check Out
Use the Cancel Check Out option to remove the lock on the checked-out document and enable other users to check
out that document.
1. Hover over the document name.
2. Click the ellipses icon, and then click Cancel Check Out from the Revision menu.
Alternatively, the user can also click the ellipses at the top of the document viewer, and then click Cancel
Check Out.
3. Click Yes to confirm the cancel the check out in the dialog.
After successfully canceling the check out, the check in icon will not appear next to the document name,
making the document available for other users to check out.
Viewing History
Use the View History option to view a list of all revisions of a document.
Document related actions such as View, Print, Download, Email, etc., can be performed for specific revisions of the
document.
1. Navigate to any of the document revisions.
2. Hover over the document name and click View History from the Revision menu.
Alternatively, you can click View History at the top of the document viewer.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 40


## Page 41


The previous revisions made to that document appear.
Revision control operations can also be performed on each of the revisions.
Integrating Mobius repositories with A&AS
You can archive data by integrating Mobius with Audit & Analytics Services (A&AS).
Before you begin:
You must have installed and configured A&AS.
1. Configure the required repository in the Content Repositories screen.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 41


## Page 42


2. Navigate to the respective repository in the Mobius Content Sources screen.
3. Hover over A&AS Integration, and then click Settings.
4. Select the Enable A&AS Integration checkbox and specify the interface as Legacy Interface  or REST
Interface.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 42


## Page 43


5. Perform any one of the following methods to configure the server:
a. If you choose a Legacy Interface for integration, provide an A&AS installation directory path installed in
the same machine and then click Apply to save the configuration in the server.
b. If you select the REST Interface for integration, configure the A&AS server details under the A&AS
Integration tab and then click Apply to save the configuration in Mobius server. The A&AS application
content field has the default value as SystemApplicationContext, which is the default context of A&AS
used for multiple document servers to work with various A&AS databases.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 43


## Page 44


6. Restart the Mobius server.
For more information about Mobius integration, see the A&AS documentation.
Configuring repository security
You can secure the Mobius repository by specifying details in the various sections of the Repository Security screen.
Before you begin: To enable index update process,
• Environment must be a part of Content Automation deployment.
• Process Access, Basic Policy Management, and Policy Management licenses must be enabled.
• Default Index Update Process application package (MobiusApps) must be uploaded in the Content Automation
Portal environment.
You can click the
  icon on the left pane to open the Repository Security screen to view the following sections:
• Federation
• Sign Out Button
• SAP Repository
• Encryption Key
• Policy Enforcement
You can then either click Apply to apply the changes or click Reset to reset the changes.
1. Select Repository Security in Mobius Administrator.
If you are not using application server security (that is, if you specified type=NONE in the application.yaml file
during installation), you must select a primary repository in the Primary repository section.
Notes:
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 44


## Page 45


◦ Primary repository can be selected only when Enable Federation is selected and single
sign-on is not configured.
◦ EOS 360 and Folders Access repository groups are supported only when federation is
enabled.
2. Select Enable Federation to allow users to work with the content in multiple repositories at the same time. By
default, the Enable Federation check box is disabled.
3. Select Show Sign Out to display the Sign in and Sign out options.
4. Optional: Select When users sign out, automatically forward them to a specific URL if you create a page to
display when Mobius View users sign out and then enter the URL for the page in the Sign Out URL field.
For example, enter https://mobiusviewhost:8443/mobius/signout.html. Create the following page, and put
it in the webapps/mobius/WEB-INF/classes/static folder.
<html>
     <head>
          <script language="javascript">
          fetch('https://mobiusviewhost:8443/mobius/rest/resources', {
               method: "GET",
               headers: {"Authorization":""}
          }).then(response => response.json());
          </script>
    </head>
    <body onload="window.location='https://mobiusviewhost:8443/mobius/view/assets/html/logg
edoff.html'">
    </body>
</html>
Notes:
◦ Replace all the instances of mobiusviewhostwith the hostname or IP address that is
applicable to the deployment. This should be the same in all places. If these values differ,
the process might not be secure.
◦ Use the body onloadelement to further redirect the sign out to a non-secure page. Using
this element prevents the display of a login prompt after the user logs out.
◦ If your site uses single sign-on, you might want to clear the buttons.
5. Select a SAP repository to enable SAP features.
6. Click Generate New Encryption Key if you need to generate a new encryption key because your security key
was compromised.
Notes: .
◦ It is necessary to have a folder in the Mobius server to store the encryption key via a REST
call.
◦ Be aware that if you previously used email distribution to deliver DirectConnect URLs to
recipients, generating a new encryption key invalidates those URLs.
7. Enable or disable the policy enforcement rules using the Enable Policy Enforcement checkbox.
8. Select Enable Geo Location to define policies based on the user's geographical location.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 45


## Page 46


9. Enable the Enable Index Update Process checkbox to add metadata correction permissions.
A policy is created with the required rules and actions to invoke the default Index update process. To view the
policy rules, go to the Policy Management screen in Mobius Administrator.
Related information
Activating the default index update process
Using Mobius Administrative REST API
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 46

Configuring Mobius repositories
Configure the Mobius repository to specify parameters for communicating with Mobius server or for managing and
customizing the access of documents
Before you begin: Configure the Mobius Server to configure a Mobius Server repository.

colored box indicates the starting point, while the dashed outline box represents an optional task.

1. Select Content Repositories in Mobius Administrator, and then click Add.
2. Add the following repository details in the Repository section:
a. In the Repository Name field, enter a unique name for the repository.
Note: The field allows a maximum of 50 characters, including spaces. A minimum of three
characters is required to avoid login issues with the prompting authentication type.
b. Select the Repository Type as Mobius .
c. Select the adapter from the Adapter list.
3. Clear the Disable universal indexes checkbox to federate the repository.
4. Optional: Select the Enable Redaction checkbox to enable the redaction feature.
5. Select the Mobius host from the Mobius Host list.
6. Select the document server in the Document Server list.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 47


## Page 48


Note: If the selected document server is already configured for another repository, the new
repository configuration is not permitted. If more than one document server is selected and one
is already configured for another repository, that document server is removed from the
repository configuration.
7. Upload the session key.
8. Select the Allow mixed case login checkbox to configure the document server login with mixed case.
Note: Clearing the Allow mixed case login checkbox converts the user ID and password to
uppercase before sending them to the server.
9. Select the Use TLS protocol option to configure the document server and use TLS for secure communication.
Notes:
◦ The Use TLS protocol option is available only for Mobius zOS and Mobius zOS via REST
Mobius versions.
◦ TLS must be enabled on the Mobius zOS host.
◦ The certificate generated as part of enabling TLS must be imported into the default trust
store of the Java JRE that is used to run Mobius View.
10. Enter the administrator user name in the Admin user name field and the password in the Admin user
password field.
Note:
◦ For the Mobius zOS repository, select the Allow password changes checkbox to enable
password change and to add the error message in the Password Expiration Message
from the Server field. This error message is displayed in the repository for password
expiry. The user is prompted to change the password after logging into Mobius View.
11. Select the authentication type in the Authentication section.
◦ Select Single Sign-On authentication to use the same user identity to access the application server (or
other third-party security provider) and access the repository.
Single Sign-On
When users log on to Mobius View and submit a search, Mobius View passes these credentials to
the source. The user credentials are same for all the repositories assigned with this authentication
method.
OR
◦ Select Prompting authentication and select Apply logout timeout.
Prompting
When users log on to Mobius View and submit a search, Mobius View passes these credentials to
the source. The username and password for the user are the same for all of the repositories
assigned with this authentication method.
Apply logout timeout
Mobius View waits for the specified time before it displays the log-on dialog. Requires the users to
enter the user credentials. Enter a timeout in minutes.
OR
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 48


## Page 49


◦ Select Alias authentication, and enter the User name and Password.
Alias
Users can access the source without providing personal authentication credentials. Mobius View
uses the alias user name and password during a search and to authenticate users. Each repository
can have a different alias.
◦ To restrict access to the repository or the content in the repository, click Advanced. The Advanced
Settings window appears.
Select one of the following options in the Repository access filter section.
▪ Users - Select this option and enter a user name to provide access rights. The field allows multiple
user lists.
Or
▪ Groups - Select this option and enter a group name to provide access rights. The field allows
multiple user lists.
▪ Click Apply.
Add the following content filter options in the Content filters section:
▪ Content Class ID - Enter the ID of the content class to limit access. Applying this filter returns only
results that exactly match the Content Class ID.
▪ Ingestion ID - Enter time in the format YYYY-MM-DD to retrieve ingestion. Applying this filter
retrieves content from the set time to the current date.
▪ Section ID - Select the filter option and enter the section of the content class to limit access. The
operators include LIKE and EQUAL TO.
▪ Click Apply.
12. Update the repository time out and Mobius repository setting in the Detailed section.
a. In the Repository timeout field, enter a number that represents seconds.
b. Select the following checkboxes as required.
▪ Enable Archive Write - Write archives to the repository. If this option is disabled, Revision Control
is also disabled.
▪ Enable Index Update - Updates indexes associated with the repository. This option is only
applicable to sources that support index updates. If this option is disabled, Revision Control is also
disabled.
▪ Enable Revision Control - Enables the repository for revisions.
▪ Enable CMIS Document Updates - Enables access to CMIS document updates through the
Mobius View folder. This option can only be enabled when Archive Write and Index updates are
enabled. Once this option is enabled, Archive Write and Index updates, and CMIS document are
enabled.
▪ Enable Annotations - Allows the addition of annotations to the contents in the repository.
▪ Enable Folder Server - Enables access to the document server through the Mobius View folder
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 49


## Page 50


server. This option requires universal indexes. When the Disable universal indexes option is
disabled, the Enable Folder Server option is available to select.
▪ Enable Records Management - Enables applications to access this records management feature.
This option requires universal indexes.
▪ Allow browsing in Mobius View - Displays the repository when Mobius View users use the
Browse feature.
▪ Enable Ask a Question - Enables the Ask a Question feature for the repository. The Rocket Smart
Chat terms are displayed when you select this checkbox, click Accept to enable this feature.
13. In the Search Service section, update the required search index and metadata options for performing
searches.
Update the following fields:
a. Default search index for full-text search - Enter a value from the following list.
▪ __AllMetaData (for simple search, searches only indexes)
▪ __FullContent (searches document body)
▪ __ContentWithAllMetaData (searches indexes and document body)
b. Default search index for document name - Enter the index ID value that will be appended to the
qualified section name in the document name when Mobius View users browse or search for
documents.
c. Select a type of consolidation of metadata that appears in the search results.
▪ No consolidation - This option stops the document server from consolidating metadata in the
search results.
▪ Page range consolidation - This option consolidates neighboring found pages into more
significant segments.
14. In the Navigation Service section, select the required navigation option. The selected navigation option is an
expansion mode for the repositories displayed in the Mobius View Browse screen.
a. Content Classes only - This option displays only content classes.
b. Indexes only - This option displays only indexes.
c. Both Content Classes and Indexes - This option displays both content classes and indexes.
Note: When upgrading from an earlier version of Mobius View, the Navigation Service in the
existing repositories should be set to Both Content Classes and Indexes.
15. In the Default Settings for Content Retrieval using the REST API section, update the content rendering
details.
a. Enable direct access for retention change - Select this option if the repository is created for a Mobius-
z/OS repository and needs to configure Records Management functionality.
b. Perform additional consolidation of metadata returned from the get documents method -
Optional. Select this option to return metadata as found.
c. Default rendering for text documents - Select the format such as, Text, HTML, or PDF.
d. Default rendering format for image documents - Select the format for rendering an image. Select
Original Format when the viewing client is Mobius View or viewing client performs rendering.
e. Retrieve multiple pages as one page - Select this option to return an entire segmented document in a
single file. Clearing this option returns the document as an array of pages.
f. Default document width - Enter the width in pixels to render an image or LPFD source document. The
recommended width is 400 pixels.
g. Default rendering format for LPFD documents - Select the format such as HTML, SVG, VML, PDF, PNG,
TIF, JPG, or BMP.
16. In the Redaction Migration Service section, select the option to save the redaction policies to Mobius server.
17. Click Apply. The repository is now available in the repositories list.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 50

After configuring a Mobius repository, map the universal indexes to the repository indexes.

Adding a new Mobius host
Mobius Administrator provides the option to add a new Mobius host while adding a Mobius repository in the
Content Repository panel.
Before you begin: (optional) To enable Mobius View to connect to Mobius Server using the same SSO key, define
the SSO key for the document server as part of the content source configuration.
1. Select Content Repositories in Mobius Administrator, and then click Add.
2. Add the following repository details in the Repository section:
a. In the Repository Name field, enter a unique name for the repository.
Note: The field allows a maximum of 50 characters, including spaces. A minimum of three
characters is required to avoid login issues with the prompting authentication type.
b. Select Mobius as the Repository Type.
3. Click New in the Mobius Connection section of the Content Repository panel to add a Mobius host.
In the New Mobius Host window, configure the Mobius host:
a. Enter a host name in the Mobius host field.
b. Select Mobius 8 or later from the Mobius Version list.
The fields displayed in the New Mobius Host window depend on the Mobius version you select.
Mobius Version Available fields
Mobius zOS
▪ Service name
▪ Transaction name
▪ Document Server Name
▪ Session key (upload the file)
▪ Maximum open connections
Mobius zOS via REST
▪ REST protocol
▪ RMI Port
▪ REST endpoint
▪ Port number
▪ Maximum open connections
Note: You must select Mobius zOS
via REST as the Mobius Version to
use Revision Control with the
Mobius-z/OS server.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 51

Mobius Version Available fields
You can then configure the REST
endpoint parameters for Mobius
zOS via REST host and select the
Document Server. The TCI/IP
protocol parameters for each

Deploying Mobius-z/OS REST
Application topic in Mobius-z/OS
documentation.

Mobius 8 or later

The description of fields displayed in the New Mobius Host window is as follows:
▪ SOAP Protocol/REST Protocol: Select HTTP or HTTPS.
▪ RMI port: Enter the port number of the RMI service.
The default value is 1099.
▪ Service endpoint: Enter the default value /vdrws/services.
▪ Service name: Enter the port number or symbolic representation of the service associated with
the repository.
▪ REST endpoint: Enter the default value /vdrws/admin.
▪ Port number (for HTTP): Enter the SOAP Mobius non-SSL port number. The default value is 8080.
▪ Port number (for HTTPS): Enter the SOAP Mobius SSL port number. The default value is 8443.
▪ Transaction name: Enter the transaction ID used to access the repository. The default value is
IN22.
▪ Document Server Name: Enter a descriptive name for the document server.
This is used to identify the host configuration for Mobius View. The name can be a maximum of 30
characters.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 52


## Page 53


▪ Upload from file: Select and upload the security key token (STK) file that is required to enable
Single Sign-On (SSO).
▪ Maximum open connections: Enter the maximum number of open connections.
c. Click TEST CONNECTION.
A successful connection displays a “Connected successfully” message.
Note: The response time is delayed if the document server is unresponsive. The default
timeout value is 30000 milliseconds. Update the soa.config.timeout property name in the
applicationDefaults.properties file or localconfig.properties file to change the
timeout value. You can set the value to 0 to remove the timeout.
d. Click Apply.
The Mobius host is added to the Mobius Host list.
Backing up indexes
Enable either automatic or manual backup for indexing for data retrieval in case of database failure. You can also re-
index reports, if back up is not required.
Note: If the indexing data is lost, it is recommended to delete the content of all FTS tables in the
database before re-indexing.
Backup options are:
Manual periodic backup
Backs up both the FTS database tables and indexing data. You must stop the Mobius FTS service on the indexing
server and enable the backup manually.
Automatic periodic backup
Performs backup and indexing simultaneously without affecting the production server and replicates the main
copy of the index at regular intervals in the backup indexing server.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 53
