SSO using IIS and AD authentication
Use IIS for SSO, which allows Windows authentication on its websites.
To use IIS for SSO, you must set a URL rewrite rule on an IIS website that is enabled for Windows authentication. The
rewrite rule directs traffic to Mobius once authenticated. Mobius uses custom authentication and reads the incoming
request headers to obtain the user and group information.
Use the following sections to set up SSO using IIS.
Enabling IIS (Windows Server)
Select a server to enable IIS and Windows authentication for security.
1. In Windows, navigate to the Control Panel and select Programs and Features.
2. Click Turn Windows features on or off.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 54


## Page 55


3. In the Server Manager window select Roles, and then click Add Roles.
4. On the Installation Type screen, select either Role-based or feature-based installation and click Next.
5. On the Server Selection screen, select the Select a server from the server pool option, highlight the current
server in the list, and click Next.
Note: By default, the local server is selected.
6. Enable Web Server (IIS).
7. Select Windows Authentication under Security.
8. Select all .NET and ASP options under Application Development.
9. Click Next to complete the process.
Enabling IIS reverse proxy
By default, the reverse proxy functionality is disabled. You must enable it, and install Application Request Routing and
URL rewrite modules to use IIS as reverse proxy.
1. Install Application Request Routing module. For information on installation, see https://www.iis.net/downloads/
microsoft/application-request-routing.
This automatically installs URL Rewrite module.
2. Open IIS Manager and select a server node in tree view.
3. Click Application Request Routing.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 55


## Page 56


4. Select the Enable Proxy checkbox.
Retain the default values for all the other settings on this page.
Enabling Windows authentication for a site
Set Windows authentication as the security for a site.
1. Select the site for which Windows authentication is to be enabled, in IIS Manager.
2. Click Authentication.
3. Set Windows authentication for the site.
Note: Ensure that all other authentication methods are disabled for that site.
Registering custom HTTP module
Add a custom module to IIS, which sets the custom request headers after authentication as IIS does not send logged-
in user and group information automatically in request headers.
1. Right-click on the site from the left pane and select Explore to open the Windows folder with all the files for the
site.
2. Add a new folder named App_Code.
3. Provide the code for custom module in the above directory.
A sample code for custom module, that sets the user and group information in HTTP request headers after
successful authentication, is given below.
SampleCustomHeaderModule.cs
SampleCustomHeaderModule.cs
using System;
using System.Collections;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;
using System.Security.Principal;
using System.Web;

//Set custom request headers using this module
public class SampleCustomHeaderModule : IHttpModule
{
    public void Dispose()
    {

    }

    public void Init(HttpApplication app)
    {
        //Add event handler post authorization and authentication
        app.PostAuthorizeRequest += new EventHandler(AddCustomHeaders);
    }

    /*Event handler that will run post authorization. Set Mobius related custom headers
     * X-PROXY-USER - Current logged in user
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 56


## Page 57


* X-PROXY-GROUP - Authenticaged user's groups
     *
     * Please note these header names can be anything.
     * Mobius doesn't require them to be of specific name.
     * However, same header names need to be configured in Mobius
     * ASG_BOOTSTRAP_VENDOR_TYPE=CUSTOM
     * ASG_BOOTSTRAP_VENDOR_ATTRIBUTES_USERNAME=X-PROXY-USER
     * ASG_BOOTSTRAP_VENDOR_ATTRIBUTES_GROUPNAMES=X-PROXY-GROUP
     * ASG_BOOTSTRAP_VENDOR_ATTRIBUTES_GROUPSEPARATOR=,
     */
    public void AddCustomHeaders(object sender, EventArgs e)
    {
        HttpApplication app = (HttpApplication)sender;
        app.Context.Request.Headers.Set("X-PROXY-USER",     app.Context.Request.ServerVaria
bles.Get("LOGON_USER"));
        app.Context.Request.Headers.Set("X-PROXY-GROUP", GetGroups(app.Context.Request));

    }

    //Get groups of logged in user
    public string GetGroups(HttpRequest request)
    {
        string groups = string.Empty;

        foreach (IdentityReference group in request.LogonUserIdentity.Groups)
        {
            groups = groups + (group.Translate(typeof (System.Security.Principal.NTAccoun
t)).ToString()) + ",";
        }

        return groups;
    }
}

Once this custom module is available, all requests directed to Mobius from this site includes user and groups in
headers.
4. Add the custom module to the web.config file to register it under the site.
Sample web.config
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<configuration>
    <system.webServer>
        <modules>
            <add name="SampleCustomHeaderModule" type="SampleCustomHeaderModule" />
        </modules>
    </system.webServer>
</configuration>

© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 57

Adding URL Rewrite rule
Add a URL Rewrite rule for the site to direct all URLs that start with Mobius to your Mobius installation.
1. Open a site from the left pane and then open URL Rewrite for the site.
2. Click Add Rule(s).
3. Set the inbound rule by providing the Pattern and Rewrite URL as shown below.
Configuring Mobius authentication
Set up Mobius authentication by configuring the environment variables.
1. Set the below environment variables in compose.yaml file, while starting the Mobius View container.
ASG_BOOTSTRAP_VENDOR_TYPE=CUSTOM
ASG_BOOTSTRAP_VENDOR_ATTRIBUTES_USERNAME=<custom user header used in the IIS module, ex: X-
PROXY-USER>
ASG_BOOTSTRAP_VENDOR_ATTRIBUTES_GROUPNAMES=<custom group header used in IIS module, ex: X-P
ROXY-GROUP>
.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 58


## Page 59


2. Customize bootstrap users and/or administrator groups.
3. Verify the setup by connecting to the website using any of the below URLs.
<IIS website URL>/mobius/admin
or
<IIS website URL>/mobius/view
If you are not already authenticated, a log-in page appears. Once authenticated, you can access Mobius View as
per the permissions.

Configuring Windows authentication
Use Windows authentication for a Windows environment. The Windows server submits user IDs and passwords to
the Mobius authentication for validation.
Before you begin:
• Install and register the service on a Windows machine.
• Ensure that the machine is in the same domain as the user workstation, as the user credentials are validated
against the environment in which the server is running.
If the machine running the Mobius Server does not meet these requirements, install the authentication service on
another machine that does. In this case, you must copy the following files from the Mobius installation directory to
the machine where the authentication service is being installed.
• ATHNTSRV.EXE
• *.DLL
1. Install and run the Mobius authentication.
a. Run the following command on the Windows server to install the authentication service and register it
with the Service Control Manager.
ATHNTSRV -install
b. Click Control Panel > Services to start the authentication service.
c. Select Mobius Authentication Service from the list of installed services, then click Start.
2. Configure the document server for Windows authentication on Mobius Administrator.
a. Log in to Mobius Administrator.
b. Select Mobius Server > Mobius Content sources .
c. Click a content source to view its repositories.
d. Click the Edit icon to open a repository definition.
e. From the repository's Details pane, expand the Authentication section and then select Windows
authentication.
f. Specify the name of the Windows server.
g. Select the Retrieve group memberships from this Windows Server option to make the document
server authenticate members of Windows user groups.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 59


## Page 60


If a user is a member of a Windows user group, and that group is defined as a recipient group in Mobius
Administrator, the user can access any content or index to which the recipient group has access rights.
h. Click Apply.
SSO using Apache and LDAP
Secure a Mobius instance for SSO using Apache as a reverse proxy and LDAP as an identity provider (IdP).
This configuration allows you to access the endpoint of the proxy, which authenticates the user against LDAP. LDAP
contains user and group information which are matched against the groups defined in Mobius. The groups defined in
Mobius allows to define roles which have common authorization, while keeping the user provisioning in the existing
identity provider.
Use the following sections to configure an LDAP group, set up Mobius for custom header authentications, and
configure Apache reverse proxy.
Setting up users in LDAP
Configure users in LDAP by assigning them as members of a group.
1. Assign a user to be a member of the mobiusadmin group.
2. Use the below command with OpenLDAP.
- ldapsearch
The result of this command is shown below.
root@dldapsearver:/# ldapsearch -x -D cn=admin,dc=example,dc=org -w admin -b uid=john,ou=pe
ople,dc=example,dc=org dn mem
berof
# extended LDIF
#
# LDAPv3
# base <uid=john,ou=people,dc=example,dc=org> with scope subtree
# filter: (objectclass=*)
# requesting: dn memberof
#

# john, people, example.org
dn: uid=john,ou=people,dc=example,dc=org
memberOf: cn=mygroup,ou=groups,dc=example,dc=org
memberOf: cn=mobiusadmin,ou=groups,dc=example,dc=org

# search result
search: 2
result: 0 Success

# numResponses: 2
# numEntries: 1
In this example, John is a user defined in LDAP and a member of the groups, mygroup and mobiusadmin. When
John logs in, only mobiusadmin, the default administrator group in the Mobius deployment, provides access
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 60


## Page 61


rights to John.
Configuring Mobius custom authentication
Set up external authentication for Mobius using custom authentication. When a Mobius instance is configured to
accept custom headers, additional network security is required for Mobius deployment as it does not accept any
traffic coming from the authentication proxy.
1. Use the below command to verify if the Mobius instance is running.
#docker ps
Note: The Mobius instance is deployed using Docker-compose and is running already.
2. Before starting the Mobius instance, ensure that it is configured with the values given in the following docker-
compose.yml file.
The docker-compose.yml file shows the configuration of the Mobius instance that allows it to accept custom
headers specifying users and groups. In this docker-compose.yml file, the custom headers used are:
PROXYUSER and PROXYGROUP.
#docker-compose.yml
#Create Mobius View container
mobiusView:
image: "$mobiusview_image"
container_name: mobiusview
#ports:
#- "2111:8080"
#- "2333:8443"
environment:
MOBIUS_LICENSE: "$mobiusview_license"
...
#New section, for custom headers:
ASG_BOOTSTRAP_VENDOR_TYPE: "CUSTOM"
ASG_BOOTSTRAP_VENDOR_ATTRIBUTES_USERNAME:"PROXYUSER"
ASG_BOOTSTRAP_VENDOR_ATTRIBUTES_GROUPNAMES:"PROXYGROUP"
ASG_BOOTSTRAP_VENDOR_ATTRIBUTES_GROUPSEPARATOR: ","
3. Configure the Mobius instance to use custom authentication.
Apache forwards the authenticated user and group information to Mobius using custom headers in the HTTP
request.
Configuring Apache reverse proxy
Set up Apache as a reverse proxy and configure LDAP.
1. Use a docker-compose file to load a local httpd.conf file to run Apache, s shown in the below sample.
version: '2'
services:
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 61


## Page 62


apache:
container_name: apache
image: docker.io/bitnami/apache:2.4
ports:
- 7080:8080
- 7443:8443
#volumes:
# - ./httpd.conf:/opt/bitnami/apache/conf/httpd.conf
2. Start Apache using the below command.
docker-compose up -d
3. Download a copy of the default httpd.conf file for editing using the below command.
docker cp apache:/opt/bitnami/apache/conf/httpd.conf .
4. Remove the comments for volume in the docker-compose file for Apache to allow it to accept the latest
changes made.
volumes:
   - ./httpd.conf:/opt/bitnami/apache/conf/httpd.conf
5. Add the below changes to the default httpd.conf file.
#cat httpd.conf

#1. Enable modules
LoadModule authnz_ldap_module modules/mod_authnz_ldap.so
LoadModule headers_module modules/mod_headers.so
LoadModule proxy_module modules/mod_proxy.so
...

<LocationMatch />

#2. reverse proxy configuration
ProxyPass http://<mobius http endpoint>/
ProxyPassReverse http://<mobius http endpoint>/
Order allow,deny
Allow from all
AuthName "Private"
AuthType Basic

#3. LDAP configuration
AuthBasicProvider ldap
AuthLDAPBindDN " cn=admin,dc=example,dc=org "
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 62


## Page 63


AuthLDAPBindPassword "admin"
AuthLDAPURL "ldap://ldap_server:389/DC=example,DC=org?uid,memberOf?sub?(objectClass=*)"
AuthLDAPBindAuthoritative On
LDAPReferrals Off

#4. set information returned from LDAP
RequestHeader set PROXYUSER "%{AUTHENTICATE_UID}e"
RequestHeader set PROXYGROUP "%{AUTHENTICATE_MEMBEROF}e"

#clean up group listing PROXYGROUP is
#cn=mygroup,ou=groups,dc=example,dc=org; cn=mobiusadmin,ou=groups,dc=example,dc=org
RequestHeader edit* PROXYGROUP ",.*?;" ""
RequestHeader edit* PROXYGROUP ",.*" ""
RequestHeader edit* PROXYGROUP "cn=" ""
RequestHeader edit* PROXYGROUP " " ","
#PROXYGROUP is now mygroup,mobiusadmin
Require valid-user
</LocationMatch>

The details of the sections of the httpd.conf file include:
◦ #1 - Enables mod_authnz_ldap, mod_headers, mod_proxy modules if they are not already enabled.
◦ #2 - The proxy configuration allows the incoming requests to treat the Apache server as if it is the
upstream Mobius instance. The Mobius instance is running on the URL specified in the proxy declaration.
◦ #3 - The LDAP configuration specifies the configuration for the LDAP server, and what attributes are to
be returned from it. The LDAP attributes uid and memberOf, of an authenticating user, are placed in
Apache environment variables to be passed upstream.
This varies according to the LDAP or Active Directory implementation. The memberOf attribute is available
on Microsoft Active Directory and openldap.
◦ #4 - Sets the headers to the information given in the Apache environment variables by LDAP
authentication. The headers for groups come back with LDAP organizational unit and domain
information in this example so a few lines are added to remove the unnecessary information.
6. Restart the Apache instance to obtain the httpd.conf file changes and log in as the configured user, by
pointing the web browser at the Apache proxy, which is running at http://localhost:7080/mobius/admin.
Configuring LDAP authentication
Repositories send user IDs and passwords to the LDAP server for user validation.
Before you begin:
• A signed certificate for the LDAP server is already available from a certificate authority (CA) for a SSL (Secure
Sockets Layer) connection.
Note: For Active Directory and document servers on Windows, the certificate path must point to
the signed certificate.
• The certificate is already imported into the cacerts file for LDAP on any platform and Active Directory for
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 63


## Page 64


repositories on Linux.
1. Log in to Mobius Administrator.
2. Select Mobius Server > Mobius Content sources.
3. Click a content source to view its repositories.
4. Click the Edit icon to open a repository definition.
5. From the repository's Details pane, expand the Authentication section, then select the authentication
method.
For a Windows or Linux system, select Active Directory (Microsoft) or LDAP.
6. Configure the following fields:
Network address or logical name of the LDAP security host
Specify the network address (or logical name) and port number of the LDAP security server host. The
default port number is 389.
To connect to the LDAP security server over SSL, use port 636.
Base DN (Distinguished Name)
Specify the base DN name in the LDAP tree. The authentication search begins at the base DN.
Login DN, Password
Enter the credentials of an authorized user to search the LDAP security server.
7. Select Connect to the LDAP security server over SSL.
All plain text user IDs and passwords that are sent to the LDAP server will be secured.
8. Select Retrieve group membership from the LDAP security host.
When selected, defining individual users in the Mobius Server database is not required.
9. Click Apply.
Related information
Securing Mobius View to Mobius Server communications with SSL
Extending LDAP authentication
Extend LDAP authentication to include other authentication methods by editing the
ldapExtendedAttributes.properties file. A sample file is found in the samples directory under the Mobius Server
installation directory. To update this file, move it to the same location as mobius.reg.
To use this file, add the following entries in the mobius.reg file:
[HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Mobius\VDRNet 3.2\VDRSRV\Document Servers\document
server\Authentication]
"LDAP Extra File"="fully-qualified-ldapExtendedAttributes.properties-filename"
For Windows, the document server path name must use double back slashes (//) instead of a single back slash (/).
To prevent losing changes on a reinstall, make sure the file is in the same location as the mobius.reg file.
For LDAP authentication, Windows uses two libraries - authLDAPJava.dll and auth_ldap_ad.dll. Linux uses a single
library called libAuthLDAPJava.so.
authLDAPJava.dll corresponds to the LDAP - Active Directory option in Mobius Administrator's Authentication
section, using Windows APIs for LDAP. authLDAPJava is the library for both platforms that uses Java APIs for LDAP.
Selecting the LDAP - SunOne IPlanet authentication option will use the authLDAPJava library, which is configured by
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 64


## Page 65


the ldapExtendedAttributes.properties file.
Refer to the comments within the ldapExtendedAttributes.properties file for information about the attributes
that provide LDAP authentication functionality.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 65

Configure Mobius View
The configuration and management of Mobius View is performed using the Mobius Administrator application. An
administrator can configure and customize the Mobius View application for the users based on their requirements
and preferences.
The following diagram shows the position of this task within the workflow of common administrative tasks. The
colored box indicates the starting point, while the dashed outline box represents an optional task.

Configuring Mobius View includes creating and configuring repositories, licensing Mobius View, and defining content
viewing options.
Administrators use the Content Repositories panel to add and manage content repositories, Authorization Code
panel to add licenses, and Content Viewing panel to define the content viewing options in Mobius View.
Licensing Mobius View
The Viewer license is required for users to access and manage content in Mobius View.
Content repositories
A content repository is a source server that requires specific settings or customization in order to access it or to
limit the view of documents.
Content viewing
The Content Viewing panel of the Mobius Administrator enables the administrator to define how the content
appears in Mobius View.
Configuring email
Use the email feature in Mobius View to send mails and attachments to multiple recipients.
Configuring processes
Mobius View works with Content Automation to create and manage Processes. Processes are created and
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 66


## Page 67


designed in Content Automation.
Configuring the navigation and display of documents
You can change the settings of Content Viewing (Greenbar Options) in Mobius View Administrator.
Configuring the background of documents
Use the Content Viewing panel to change settings related to text documents stored in Mobius repositories.
Configuring Mobius View for Amazon S3 storage
You can configure Mobius View for Amazon S3 storage.
Customizing the Mobius View application
You can use the Application Server panel in Mobius Administrator to customize the Mobius View application
and set inactivity timeout for Mobius Administrator. In most cases, the default settings are appropriate.
Licensing Mobius View
The Viewer license is required for users to access and manage content in Mobius View.
1. Select Authorization Code in Mobius Administrator.
2. Enter the Viewer license code in the Enter Authorization Code field.
3. Click Save. The Authorization Code pane displays the added license.
4. Refresh the Mobius Administrator browser window to activate the license.
Various other features of Mobius Administrator and Mobius View require licensing.
Related information
Licensed options
Licensed options
The Authorization Code panel lists the features of Mobius Administrator that you have purchased.
Table: Available license options
License option Description
Viewer (Mobius View) Browser-based application allowing authorized users to access and manage content in
Mobius View repositories.
REST API Allows third-party applications to access and manage content using a REST API. This option
is enabled for all authorization codes.
CMIS Client Adapter Allows to access Mobius View application as a CMIS compliant server.
Open Systems
Repository Access
Access to archived content in Mobius repositories. Administrators can limit the number of
repositories that can be accessed.
z/OS Repository
Access
Access to archived content in Mobius z/OS repositories. Administrators can limit the
number of repositories that can be accessed.
CMIS Repository
Access
Access to archived content in CMIS repositories. Administrators can limit the number of
repositories that can be accessed.
EOS 360 Repository
Access Access to content in EOS 360 repository groups.
Folders Repository
Access Access to content in Folders Access repository groups.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 67


## Page 68


License option Description
Data Miner Access to extract data from documents, and filter and export the extracted data.
Data Miner for Files Allow data extraction from user files.
Records
Management Manage document life cycle for legal compliance.
Annotations Add annotations to content.
Redaction Redact sensitive information in content accessed by the Mobius View or the REST API.
PDF/PS Retrieve PDF content.
AFP Retrieve AFP content.
DJDE/Metacode Retrieve DJDE/Metacode content.
HPTS Image Retrieve HPTS image content.
PCL Retrieve PCL content.
Image Retrieve general image content.
ABSNET Repository
Access Access to content in ABSNET repositories.
SharePoint
Repository Access Access to content in SharePoint repositories.
Custom Adapter
Repository Access Access to content in a repository that is accessed by using a custom adapter.
Content Automation Access to assemble embedded processes to match the business operations. The process
can be monitored and modified to meet business needs.
Capture Access Access to use the capture feature in Mobius View to archive content.
Postscript Access to retrieve Adobe PostScript content.
Revision Control Access to use the revision control feature in Mobius View to create revisions of a document.
Distribution Services Access to distribute content in email and print.
Microsoft 365
Connectivity Access to Microsoft 365 connectivity.
OCR Services Access to OCR processing.
Policy Management Access to the Policy Management panel with all the Policy Management roles and
permissions.
Basic Policy

Content Insights Access to enable the Content Insights features.
Customized Search
Forms Access to enable the Search Form features.

## Page 69


Content repositories
A content repository is a source server that requires specific settings or customization in order to access it or to limit
the view of documents.
Mobius supports configuration of various source servers. The source server can be a Mobius Server, a CMIS server,
SharePoint, or any other customized server. Options like indexes, revision control, and, records management are
pertained to Mobius Server configuration.
Related information
Configuring Mobius repositories
Configuring EOS 360 repositories
The repository group in Mobius View is configured to access the environments in EOS 360 as the repositories are
automatically created for the list of environments in EOS 360.
The EOS 360 server is configured to the repository group with the server address and the port number. The
environments in EOS 360 are not displayed in the Content Repositories page of Mobius Administrator, but the
repositories are created for each environment. Settings such as, redaction or authentication that are applied to a
repository group are applicable to all the environments in the repository group.
1. Select Content Repositories in Mobius Administrator, and then click Add.
2. Add the following repository details in the Repository section:
a. In the Repository Name field, enter a unique name for the repository.
Note: The field allows a maximum of 50 characters, including spaces. A minimum of three
characters is required to avoid login issues with the prompting authentication type.
b. Select the Repository Type as EOS 360.
c. Select Adapter to establish communication with the EOS 360 server.
d. Optional: Select the Enable Redaction checkbox to enable the redaction feature.
3. Perform the following sub-steps to update the EOS 360 details in the EOS 360 Connection section:
a. Select an existing EOS server from the EOS 360 Server list.
Alternatively, you can click New and add an EOS server.
The New EOS 360 Server pane is displayed.
b. Enter the EOS server address in the Server address field.
c. Enter the server port details in the Server port field.
d. Enter the service endpoint URL to access the service in the Web service endpoint field.
e. Enter the EOS server credentials in the User name and Password fields.
f. Click TEST CONNECTION and verify that a successful notification “Connected successfully” is
displayed.
The new server address is added to the EOS server list.
4. Select one of the authentication types from the Authentication section.
Authentication
types Descriptions Actions
Prompting Prompting mode enables users to enter Perform the following steps to choose the

EOS 360 supports Browse, Locate, Redaction, Pagination, and Custom Button features.
Configuring Folders Access repositories
The repository group in Mobius View is configured to access the environments in the Folders Access as the
repositories are automatically created for the list of environments found in Folders Access.
The Folders Access server is configured to the repository group with the server address and the port number. The
environments in the Folders Access are not displayed in the Content Repositories page of Mobius Administrator,
but the repositories are created for each environment. Settings such as, redaction or authentication that are applied
to a repository group are applicable to all the environments in the repository group.
1. Select Content Repositories in Mobius Administrator, and then click Add.
2. Add the following repository details in the Repository section:
a. In the Repository Name field, enter a unique name for the repository.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 70


## Page 71


Note: The field allows a maximum of 50 characters, including spaces. A minimum of three
characters is required to avoid login issues with the prompting authentication type.
b. Select the Repository Type as Folders Access.
c. Select the Adapter to establish communication with the Folders Access server.
d. Optional: Select the Enable Redaction checkbox to enable the redaction feature.
3. Perform the following sub-steps to update Folders details in the Folders Access Connection section:
a. Select an existing Folders server from the Folders Access Server list.
Alternatively, you can click New and add a Folders server.
The New Folders Access Server pane is displayed.
b. Enter the Folders server address in the Server address field.
c. Enter the server port details in the Server port field.
d. Enter the service endpoint URL to access the service in the Web service endpoint field.
e. Enter the Folders Access server credentials in the User name and Password fields.
f. Click TEST CONNECTION and verify that a successful notification “Connected successfully” is
displayed.
g. Click Apply.
4. Select one of the authentication types from the Authentication section.

Folders Access supports features such as Browse, Locate, Redaction, Pagination, and Custom Button.
The group repository is now available in the Content Repositories list.
Configuring CMIS repositories
Configure the repositories in Mobius View to access content from CMIS (Content Management Interoperability
Services) with parameters like CMIS host, binding, and authentication type.
1. Select Content Repositories in Mobius Administrator, and then click Add.
2. Add the following repository details in the Repository section:
a. In the Repository Name field, enter a unique name for the repository.
Note: The field allows a maximum of 50 characters, including spaces. A minimum of three
characters is required to avoid login issues with the prompting authentication type.
b. Select the Repository Type as CMIS.
c. Select Adapter as Local to establish communication with the CMIS server.
d. Clear the Disable universal indexes checkbox to federate the repository.
In Mobius View, the Indexes link appears below the repository name.
e. Optional: Select the Enable Redaction checkbox to enable the redaction feature.
3. Perform the following sub-steps to update the CMIS details in the CMIS connection section:
a. Select an existing CMIS host from the CMIS Host list.
b. Alternatively, you can click New and add a CMIS host.
i. Enter a host name in the CMIS host field.
ii. Enter the service endpoint URL to access the service in the Web service endpoint field.
iii. Select AtomPub or WebServices from the Binding type list.
iv. Enter the credentials to access the CMIS host in the User name and Password fields.
v. Enter a numerical value to define a limit to the open connection in the Maximum open
connections field.
vi. Click TEST CONNECTION and verify that a successful notification “Connected successfully” is
displayed.
vii. Click Apply.
The new CMIS host is added to the CMIS Host list.
4. Select one of the authentication types from the Authentication section.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 72

5. Click Advanced to restrict access to the repository or content in the repository.
You can choose one of the following options in the Repository access filter section:
◦ Select Users to add a user, enter a username to provide access rights, and then click Apply. The field
allows multiple user lists.
◦ Select Groups to add user groups, enter a Group name to provide access rights, and then click Apply.
The field allows multiple group lists.
6. Perform the following sub-steps to update the repository time out in the Detailed section and set to enable or
disable folder server:
a. Specify the number of seconds for repository timeout.
b. Select the Enable Folder Server checkbox to enable applications to access the CMIS host through the
Mobius View folder server.
Note: This checkbox is available only when you select the Disable universal indexes
checkbox.
7. Select the Case Insensitive checkbox in the Search Service section to ignore the case sensitivity while
performing searches.

## Page 74


8. Click Apply.
The repository is available in the Content Repositories list. After configuring CMIS repository for Mobius View, map
the universal indexes to repository indexes.
Related information
Universal indexes
Configuring SharePoint repositories
The repositories in Mobius View are configured to access content from SharePoint using adapters. The adapters
provide information about indexes, search properties, and display capabilities of the content source to Mobius View.
Before you begin: The SharePoint adapter is already installed.
Specify the SharePoint adapter details to configure the repository. For users to connect to different versions of
SharePoint from the Mobius View Search Adapter, go to the Subset Configuration section. The Mobius View Search
adapter supports SharePoint on-premise and SharePoint online environments.
1. Select Content Repositories in Mobius Administrator, and then click Add.
2. Add the following repository details in the Repository section:
a. In the Repository Name field, enter a unique name for the repository.
Note: The field allows a maximum of 50 characters, including spaces. A minimum of three
characters is required to avoid login issues with the prompting authentication type.
b. Select the Repository Type as SharePoint.
c. Select Adapter as SharePoint Enterprise Search Adapter to establish communication with the
SharePoint server.
You can also create a new adapter by performing the following steps:
i. Click New in the New SharePoint Adapter section.
ii. Select either HTTP or HTTPS as the protocol to access the SharePoint adapter in the SharePoint
adapter scheme drop-down list.
iii. Enter a name for the host where the adapter is deployed in the SharePoint adapter host field.
Note: You must not include the protocol (HTTPS) or "/" characters.
iv. Enter the port number where the adapter is deployed in SharePoint adapter port field.
v. Enter the service endpoint URL to call the adapter REST APIs in the Service endpoint field.
vi. Enter credentials used to call the adapter REST APIs during configuration of the Mobius View
repository in the User name and Password fields.
vii. Click Test Connection and verify that the message “Connected successfully” is displayed.
viii. Click Apply.
d. Clear the Disable universal indexes check box to federate the repository.
In Mobius View, the Indexes link displays below the repository name.
e. Optional: Select the Enable Redaction checkbox to enable the redaction feature.
Related information
Installing SharePoint adapters
Prerequisites for SharePoint adapters
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 74


## Page 75


Configuring repositories for custom adapters
The repositories in Mobius View are configured to access content from a custom source using a custom repository
adapter. A custom adapter defines the repository subset schema. Mobius View displays subset lists to select and a
prompt for credentials as specified by the custom adapter.
Before you begin: Create a custom adapter.
Specify the custom adapter and subset details to configure the repository.
1. Select Content Repositories in Mobius Administrator, and then click Add.
2. Add the following repository details in the Repository section:
a. In the Repository Name field, enter a unique name for the repository.
Note: The field allows a maximum of 50 characters, including spaces. A minimum of three
characters is required to avoid login issues with the prompting authentication type.
b. Select the Repository Type as Custom.
c. Select Adapter as Custom Adapter.
You can also create a new adapter by performing the following steps:
i. Click New in the New Custome Adapter section.
ii. Select either HTTP or HTTPS as the protocol to access the custom adapter in the Custom adapter
scheme drop-down list.
iii. Enter a name for the host where the adapter is deployed in the Custom adapter host field.
Note: Do not include the protocol (HTTPS) or "/" characters.
iv. Enter the port number where the adapter is deployed in Custom adapter port field.
v. Enter the service endpoint URL to call the adapter REST APIs in the Service endpoint field.
By default, adapters are installed in the root folder and “/” should be specified for this field.
vi. Enter credentials used to call the adapter REST APIs during configuration of the Mobius View
repository in the User name and Password fields.
vii. Click Test Connection and verify that the message “Connected successfully” is displayed.
viii. Click Apply.
d. Perform the following steps in the Subset Configuration section:
i. Select the required value to specify the repositories subset.
ii. The repository subset schema is defined by the custom adapter. Mobius Administrator displays
subset lists to select and a prompt for credentials as required by the custom adapter. Select the
required value for Database Server (Level 1).
iii. Enter the credentials in the User and Password fields.
Note: Authentication is optional in the adapter definition. Hence, login credentials
are not mandatory in all cases.
iv. Click the Load level two button when the value for Level 1 is selected. If second-level subsets are
available for the selected adapter, fields for the second-level information are displayed.
v. Repeat the above step for Level 3. When you enter data for Level 3, the subset configuration is
complete.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 75


## Page 76


vi. Clear the Disable universal indexes checkbox to federate the repository. In Mobius View, the
Indexes link displays below the repository name.
e. Optional: Select the Enable Redaction checkbox to enable the redaction feature.
3. Select one of the authentication types from the Authentication section.

Choose one of the following options in the Repository access filter section:
◦ Select Users to add a user, enter a username to provide access rights, and then click Apply. The field
allows multiple user lists.
◦ Select Groups to add user groups, enter a Group name to provide access rights, and then click Apply.
The field allows multiple group lists.
Configure the following options in the Content filters tab:
a. Enter the ID of the content class to limit the access in the Content Class ID field.
Applying this filter returns only results that match the Content Class ID.
b. Enter the time period in the format YYYY-MM-DD to retrieve ingestion in the Ingestion ID filed.
Applying this filter retrieves content beginning from a set time period to the current date.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 76


## Page 77


c. Select the filter option from the Section ID drop-down list and enter the section of the content class to
limit access. The operators include LIKE and EQUAL TO.
d. Click Apply.
5. Update the repository time-out period in the Detailed section by entering the numeric value in the Repository
timeout field.
6. Click Apply.
Related information
Custom repository adapters
Content viewing
The Content Viewing panel of the Mobius Administrator enables the administrator to define how the content
appears in Mobius View.
Sections of the Content Viewing panel:
Browsing
The Browsing section provides the options to define the browse navigation strategy that controls how items are
displayed in folders and how users can quickly access the items of interest in those folders.
Searching
Lets you specify options for the searches conducted by the users.
Download
The settings in the Download section control how the users download the documents.
Print
The settings in the Print section controls how the users print the documents.
Find
The Find section provides the option to specify a value in the Limit a single find to the following number of
pages field to restrict the number of pages to perform find. Higher limit requires more processing time. The
default value is 100.
Extraction
The settings under the Extraction section controls the use of predefined extraction policies.
Content Duplication
The Content Duplication section provides the option to enable content duplication by entering a valid Amazon
S3 storage URL.
Redaction
The Preserve toggle state of View redacted/unredacted button checkbox in the Redaction section allows
Mobius View users to retain their selection of the redaction toggle button when the user moves to the next
document or the application is reloaded.
Greenbar Options
The fields under the Greenbar Options section provides the option to specify two background colors to render
text document. This is applicable for text documents stored in Mobius repositories. The default is the classic
greenbar pattern.
Document Viewer Options
The Document Viewer Options section helps to define the scope of navigation and the display of multipage
documents in the Mobius View document viewer screen.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 77


## Page 78


Annotations
Allows you to manage the look and feel of the document annotations used by the Mobius View users.
Smart Chat Configuration
The Enable Indexed Smart Chart checkbox allows you to select the indexed repository for Smart Chat. The
Rocket Smart Chat terms are displayed when you select this checkbox, click Accept to enable this option. The
selected index repository cannot be deleted in the Content Repositories panel without selecting another
repository to be the indexed repository. An error popup is displayed when attempting to delete an indexed
repository.
Service Configuration
Specify the URL for Smart Chat. This service configuration takes the highest precedence than application.yaml
or .env file.
Records Management
Allows you to control the hold functionality to read, add, or remove holds.
Related information
Mobius View browse options
Mobius View search options
Configuring Mobius View download options
Mobius View print options
Mobius View extraction settings
Mobius View browse options
The Browsing section provides options to control the display and access of items in the Mobius View Browse screen.
• Single Request, with a flexible filter option to display all returned items on a single page and allow the user
to enter text in the filter field to narrow down the results. Recommended for situations with a small number of
items, for example, under 1000.
• Multiple Requests, with prefix locate, only requesting more items as required option to display the
number of items that the user specifies in the Number of items to fetch per request text box(default value is
200) at a time, along with next and previous arrows to move between list pages. Recommended for situations
with many items, for example, over 1000. Select the Force locate value to uppercase checkbox to convert the
value entered in the Jump to field to uppercase. This option is selected by default. Deselect this option to
consider the value entered in the Jump to field as case-sensitive. In addition, this checkbox controls the
navigation path for DirectConnect. If the repository contains documents in lowercase, selecting this checkbox
affects the results.
• Automatically open item if it is the only item in a folder option to open folders that contain a single item
automatically. When this option is set, the content of the folder is displayed immediately.
• Quick Open option to open the first available document in a folder. This setting navigates through the first
available sub-folders within the selected folder and opens the first available document.
• The Enter key will open the first item in the list check box to open the first list item using the Enter key.
• Clear prefix locate/filter values on the sign-off check box to clear the locate/filter value that was entered
when the user signs out.
• The Default View drop-down displays the default view mode of the Mobius View Browse screen, Classic Tile
or Content Tree + Grid mode. Classic Tile is selected by default.
• The Allow user to change their browsing context view mode check box allows the user to switch between
the Classic Tile and Content Tree + Grid view modes. If this check box is not selected, the user can view only
the mode selected in the Default View drop-down.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 78


## Page 79


Mobius View search options
The Searching section in the Content Viewing panel specifies options to define the Mobius View search behavior.
• The maximum number of results that can be returned in a search can be defined under Configure Search
Behavior.
The default value is 200. This value can be changed if there are performance issues, depending on how many
repositories are being searched and how many users are searching.
In addition, specify the default sort order as Oldest First or Newest First for search results. This is applicable
only to Mobius repository types.
The Always show the Content Classes column in the search results check box helps to display the Content
Class column in the search output. For non-Mobius repositories, the Content Class column is left blank.
• The details to configure Text Filter Behavior:
◦ Number of index search results used in a single text filter operation.
◦ Number of items/results to fetch per request.
◦ Maximum number of pages to be searched during a single text filter operation. 0 indicates no limit.
• The Show full text search control check box under the Quick Full Text Search section allows Mobius View
client to display a control for performing full text searches against repositories that support it.
• Administrators can disable certain search operators to increase search efficiency and avoid over-usage of
server resources. The search operators that are specified in the Disable Search Operator section are disabled
in the Mobius View search expressions. This applies to general search, advanced search, and search templates.
To disable search operators:
◦ Select the search operators from the Active Search Operators list and click the single arrow to move
the selected operators to the Disabled Search Operators list. Click the double arrow to move the entire
list.
If all the search operators are disabled, the search operation cannot be performed.
Note: When a user attempts to use a saved search that contains disabled search operator,
an error message is displayed and the search cannot be executed. The user must modify
the saved search by selecting an active/available search operator from the list and save the
search again for execution.
Mobius View download options
The settings in the Download section control how users download documents.
• Specify a value in the Limit a single download to the following number of pages field under Page Limit to
restrict the number of pages to perform download. Higher limit requires more processing time. Therefore, you
must specify 0 to indicate no limit.
• Set the following options under Document Viewer Download Options:
◦ Select Enable download of large documents to a temporary file checkbox to store the downloaded
data in a mix of temporary files and RAM.
If this option is not selected, all downloaded data is stored in RAM.
The default location of the temporary files and the amount of RAM that is reserved for each download
can be changed by changing the following fields:
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 79


## Page 80


▪ The maximum amount of memory to use per download (in KB)- Any download data beyond
the amount specified by this value is stored in temporary files. This field helps to balance the
amount of download data between RAM and temporary files and adjust this balance as necessary.
The default value of 64KB of RAM is appropriate for most situations. Entering 0 in this field selects
the default value of 64KB.
▪ The temporary directory where large downloads are placed.
• Select the Don't ZIP Single File checkbox under the ZIP Download Options section to download the single
document as a single file.
This is applicable only if the Don't ZIP Single Files checkbox is not selected in the Mobius View Download
panel.
• Set the controls under LPFD Download Format Options to define:
◦ The default download format.
◦ Whether users can specify the download format.
◦ The formats available for users to specify. Note the format set as default cannot be deselected in this list.
• Set the controls under Image Download Format Options to define:
◦ The default download format. Whether users can specify the download format.
◦ The formats available for users to specify. Note that the format set as default cannot be deselected in
this list.
• Set the controls under Text Download Format Options to define:
◦ The default download format.
◦ Whether users can specify the download format.
◦ The formats available for users to specify. Note the format set as default cannot be deselected in this list.
• Set the controls under Export Format Options to define:
◦ The default export format, such as CSV, TEXT, or XLSX.
◦ Whether users can specify the export format.
◦ The formats available for users to specify. Note the format set as default cannot be deselected in this list.
◦ If the Enable exclude column header by default checkbox is selected, the Exclude Column Headers
checkbox is enabled by default in the Options section of the extraction results Export panel of Mobius
View.
Mobius View print options
The settings on the Print section in the Content Viewing page control how users print documents in Mobius View.
• To restrict the number of pages that a user can print per document, specify a value in the Limit a print
request to the following number of pages per document field. More processing time is required for a
higher number of pages. Specify 0 to indicate no limit.
• The Disable print for content ingestion check box disables the Print option for all content ingestions. The
check box is not selected by default.
Mobius View extraction settings
The settings under the Extraction section of the Content Viewing section controls the use of predefined extraction
policies.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 80


## Page 81


• Specify the location for storing policies in the Use policies from this directory field for legacy policies.
Notes:
• Multiple legacy policies are supported per content class. Legacy policies have .PLC extension.
• To display only policies with names that match the content class ID, do one of the following:
◦ Add all the legacy polices in the policy directory specified in the Mobius Administrator
settings. In the Browse screen, when an ingestion or section is selected for extraction, only
the policy names that match the content class ID are displayed.
◦ In the policy directory, create a sub-folder with the same name as the content class ID. Add
multiple policies inside it. In the Browse screen, when the user selects an ingestion or
section to extract, all the policies in the sub-folder are displayed. If policies are placed in the
main policy directory, only the policy that matches the content class ID (only one file named
<report>.PLC will be available) is displayed.
• Set the maximum number of pages to extract in The maximum number of pages to extract from field. The
default value is 99. A value of 0 indicates no limit.
• Set the maximum number of extracted results in The maximum number of extracted results field. The
default value is 499. A value of 0 indicates no limit.
• Select the Enable publisher compatibility checkbox to have the output compatible with Modeler/Publisher
models output in the extracted reports.
Note: The actual number of results returned could exceed this value. Mobius View collects all the
results from a page and sends them back, even if it reaches the specified limit in the middle of a page.
Configuring email
Use the email feature in Mobius View to send mails and attachments to multiple recipients.
1. Navigate to Email Settings in Mobius View Administrator.
2. Click the
  icon to open the Email Settings screen.
3. Click the
  icon to specify the email Server settings.
4. Perform the following configurations in the Service Configuration section.
a. Select a security protocol from the Protocol drop-down list.
b. Enter the address for the SMTP server in the SMTP Server Address text box.
c. Enter a port number in the Port field.
The default value is 25.
d. Enable Authentication Required if the user needs to be logged in to use the feature. Then provide the
credentials to log in.
◦
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 81


## Page 82


5. Perform the following configurations in the Widget Configuration section.
a. Enable From Read Only, if you do not want the user to edit the From email address.
b. Enter an email address in the Use a default from address field to use it as the default From email
address for all emails.
c. Enable Reply-To Read Only, to make the reply-to email address non-editable.
d. Enter an email address in the Use a default reply-to address field to use it as the default reply-to email
address for all emails.
e. Provide Link Text to use it as the default text for all links in all emails.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 82


## Page 83


6. Perform the following configurations in the General Configuration section.
a. Enable Auto Generate Email and enter an Email Domain address.
This automatically appends the domain address to the sender's ID.
b. Enable Inherit SMTP config from Global to use the SMTP settings from the client machine.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 83


## Page 84


7. Click Submit.
8. Perform the following configurations in the Client Settings section.
a. Select the Attach as a link in the email checkbox to allow users to add documents to the mail as Direct
Connect links for Mobius repositories and Content Stream links for other repositories.
b. Select the Attach as an attachment in the email checkbox to allow users to add documents to the mail
as attachments. This requires a published encryption key available on the Application Server.
c. Select the format for files to be downloaded in the File Format option.
d. Select the Zip if multiple files checkbox to download a zipped package of the documents.
e. Select the Allow users to customize email checkbox in the End User Options section to allow the user
to edit the body of the mail.
If this option is unchecked, the contents of the email body are configured on the server.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 84


## Page 85


9. Click Apply to save the settings.
10. Perform the following configuration if Mobius View is deployed with a load balancer, web server, proxy, or
redirection.
a. Navigate to the application.yaml under {user_home}/asg/mobius/config.
b. Update the value for the key email:link:host to the actual URL where Mobius View is deployed,
through http(s)://<mv_host_ip>:<mv_port>.
Where,
▪ <mv_host_ip> is the host name or the IP address of the system on which the Mobius View
instance is deployed.
▪ <mv_port> is the port number on which the Mobius View instance is listening.
For example: http://localhost:8080 or https://localhost:8443.
c. Save the application.yaml file and restart the Mobius View service.
asg:
  email:
    link:
      host: http://localhost:8080
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 85


## Page 86


Note: If this key is not available, it must be added to the file.
Configuring processes
Mobius View works with Content Automation to create and manage Processes. Processes are created and designed
in Content Automation.
1. Update the application.yaml file to allow Mobius View to communicate with the PRS service in Content
Automation to retrieve the Processes:
workflow:
  processContextRoot: http://host system:port number/process/rest
  processUrlPath: /repository/process-definitions
  processInstancesUrlPath: /runtime/process-instances
  processNotificationsUrlPath: /process/rest/query/tasks
  inboxUrlPath: /contentautomation/processinboxngx/index.html
  indexUpdateWorkflowProcessName: MobiusAppsProject-IndexUpdateProcess
  formsUrlPath: /rest/application/uiforms
  designModeFormsUrlPath: /rest/projects/uiforms
  formsContextRoot: http://host system:port number/contentautomation
  designModeFormsContextRoot: http://host system:port number/studio
2. Save the changes.
Configuring the navigation and display of documents
You can change the settings of Content Viewing (Greenbar Options) in Mobius View Administrator.
You can use the Document Viewer Options section to define the scope of navigation and the display of documents
in the Mobius View document viewer screen. You can select an option from the Scope Of Navigation section to
navigate between the pages of the document, the documents of the content ingestion, and the documents in the
content list. You can specify the Scope Of Navigation based on contexts such as Browse, Search, and other contexts
like Favorites.
You can then use the next page and the previous page arrows at the bottom of the Mobius View document viewer to
navigate between the pages of the document.
You cal also use the next document and the previous document arrows at the top of the Mobius View document
viewer to navigate between the documents.
You can use the find option for search navigation.
• Select the Content Document option to navigate between the pages of the document.
• Select the Content Ingestion option to navigate between the documents of the content ingestion using the
next document and previous document arrows at the top of the Mobius View document viewer. This option
enables the user to move to the first page of the previous document in the ingestion if the previous document
arrow is clicked and to the first page of the next document in the ingestion if the next document arrow is
clicked.
Using the next page arrow at the bottom of the Mobius View document viewer, the user can move to the next
page and to the first page of the next document and using the previous page arrow, the user can move to the
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 86


## Page 87


previous page and to the last page of the previous document. The previous document arrow and the next
document arrow are disabled for the first and the last document in the content ingestion, respectively. The
user can edit the page number in the bottom navigation to move between pages.
• Select the Content List option to navigate between the documents of the content list using the navigation
arrows at the top and the bottom of the Mobius View document viewer.
Select the Show multipage documents as a single scrollable page in Mobius View checkbox to enable single page
scrolling.
Configuring the background of documents
Use the Content Viewing panel to change settings related to text documents stored in Mobius repositories.
Select the Render Text Documents with a Background check box to specify two background colors. Clearing the
check box hides the specified background colors. The default is the classic greenbar pattern.
Configuring Mobius View for Amazon S3 storage
You can configure Mobius View for Amazon S3 storage.
You can enter a valid URL in the Enable Content Duplication field to specify a wildcard, and then add an asterisk to
the end of the URL.
Customizing the Mobius View application
You can use the Application Server panel in Mobius Administrator to customize the Mobius View application and set
inactivity timeout for Mobius Administrator. In most cases, the default settings are appropriate.
1. Set the values in the Client Inactivity Timeout section to control the automatic timeouts in effect for your
installation’s Mobius View users:
◦ Number of seconds of user inactivity until an automatic logoff specifies how long an active session
is maintained without input before the user is logged off and the connection is terminated.
The default is 1200 (20 minutes). A value of 0 (zero) disables the timeout.
◦ Number of seconds prior to logoff when a warning will appear specifies how long before the admin
is logged off for the warning message to display.
The default is 300 (5 minutes). A value of 0 (zero) disables the warning display.
Depending on the number of active users and the amount of network traffic, you might need to fine-tune the
timeout setting to conserve resources and improve efficiency.
2. Set the values in the Admin Inactivity Timeout section to control the automatic timeouts in Mobius
Administrator:
◦ Number of seconds of admin inactivity before automatic logoff specifies how long an active session
is maintained without input before the admin is logged off and the connection is terminated.
The default is 1200 (20 minutes). A value of 0 (zero) disables the timeout.
◦ Number of seconds prior to logoff when a warning will appear specifies how long before the admin
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 87


## Page 88


is logged off for the warning message to display.
The default is 300 (5 minutes). A value of 0 (zero) disables the warning display.
3. Select Display Home Screen in the Home Screen section to display the Mobius View home screen.
4. Enable or disable the client feature options in the Features section.
5. Use the Navigation Menu Customization section to customize the menu items in the side navigation bar of
the Mobius View application.
◦ Drag and drop the menu items in the Main Navigation Menu Items section to rearrange the order of
the items displayed in the side navigation bar.
◦ Drag and drop the menu items in the Popup Navigation Menu Items section to rearrange the order of
the items displayed within the ellipses of the side navigation bar.
◦ You can shuffle menu items between the Main Navigation Menu Items and Pop up Navigation Menu
Items sections.
◦ Use Add a custom navigation menu section to add a new custom menu item. Only custom menu
items can be removed.
6. Specify the languages available for user in the Languages section.
7. Enable or disable the keyboard shortcuts in the Keyboard Shortcuts section.
8. Enable or disable annotation autosave in the Auto Save section.
9. Use the Customize View section to customize the header and the side navigation bar of the Mobius View
application.
◦ Enter the path of the logo in the Logo URL Path field to customize the logo in the header.
◦ Enter the text that you want to appear in the header in the Header Text Value field.
◦ Choose a background color for the header from the Header Background Color field.
◦ Choose a header text color from the Header Text Color field.
◦ Choose a background color for the navigator bar on the left from the Left Navigation Bar Background
Color field.
◦ Choose a color for the icons on the navigator bar from the Left Navigation Bar Icon Color field.
◦ Choose a color that will denote the active icon background on the navigator bar from the Left
Navigation Bar Active Background Color field.
◦ Choose a border color for the currently active icon on the navigator bar from the Left Navigation Bar
Active Border Color field.
Note: Instead of selecting a color, you can also enter the RGB and HSL value or the HEX code
directly.
10. Specify the settings of the Self Correcting Facility in the Self Correcting Facility section. This feature detects
processing failures during the content-rendering stage and automatically corrects errors by locating alternate
content handlers.
Note: You can use the default settings.
Field Description
Number
of
processes
The number of processes. The default is 5.
Maximum
heap size Maximum heap size for each process. The default is 128 MB.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 88


## Page 89


Field Description
per
process
Monitor
for end of
main
process
Mobius View launches processes to run document content operations such as format
conversions, extractions, and redactions. When the main JVM shuts down in a normal manner,
these child processes automatically terminate. If the main JVM is terminated suddenly, this
option enables processes to check for the parent's existence and, terminate themselves if the
parent is no longer running.
11. Restrict file uploads in the Restrict All File Uploads section to disable the import of personal folders and to
prevent the uploading of session key.
12. Select the Display Browser Not Supported Message check box to display a message when the browser is not
supported.
13. Using the Error Customization section, you can customize the error messages received from repositories such
as Mobius z/OS, when they are displayed to the users. Enable the Customize Error Message check box to
customize the error message.
Do the following to specify an action for an error message:
a. Specify the starting value of the Mobius z/OS error message in the If error message starts with. field.
b. Select Show content repository message or Show custom message option from the Then take an
action field. The Show content repository message option displays the original message from the
repository while the Show custom message option displays the message entered in the Display
Message field.
c. Add a custom error message in the Display Message field.
For example, the original message from Mobius-z/OS is S076E-REQUEST REJECTED BY RACF If you enter S076E
in the If error message starts with field and select Show content repository message option in the Then
take an action field, S076E-REQUEST REJECTED BY RACF will be displayed to the end users. To display a
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 89


## Page 90


customized message to the user, you can select the Show custom message option to enter a custom message
, such as Content is not available . For more information on Mobius-z/OS error messages with Snnn
structure, see Error Messages from R001W to S245E topic in Mobius-z/OS documentation.
14. Using the Repository Login Dialog section, you can customize the repository login dialog box.
◦ Specify the image path in the Login dialog icon field that will be displayed on the login dialog box.
The path of the Login dialog icon must be a fully-qualified URL or it must be relative to the view/en-US (all or
specific languages) and directories (admin-ngxand vdradmin) within the Mobius View deployment.
◦ Enter a title for the login dialog box in the Login dialog title field.
◦ Enter the message for the login dialog box in the Login dialog message field. In this field, you can
include the repository name using the placeholder {{repository}}. This is optional, but it will be replaced
at the prompting time with the name of the repository.
◦ Enter the label for username in the Username label field.
◦ Enter the label for password in the Password label field.
◦ Enter the label for login button in the Login button label field.
◦ Select Allow the user to save login username in the browser to allow users to save their user names
in the browser. To prompt users for a user name when they log in, clear this setting. Passwords are not
saved.
Use the Custom Links section to add custom links to the login dialog box.
◦ Enter the text in the Custom link title field that will be displayed on the hyperlink.
◦ Enter the hyperlink in the Custom link URL field.
The Restore Mobius Theme option is enabled only if at least one of the above listed values is updated.
15. Use the Legacy Mobius Settings Customization field to carry over customizations from older products to
Mobius View. Instructions are included above the field. Some additional guidance:
• Customizations from a previous product (as provided by Rocket) are not automatically carried over to Mobius
View.
• Review the settings that you added to the ddrint.xml file or the luminist.xml file in your previous installation.
Those settings can be added in the Legacy Mobius Settings Customization field, but the format is YAML, not
XML, making it easier to insert individual customizations.
• Each entry in the ddrint.xml or luminist.xml file has a node name, a key name, and a key value.
• When adding values to the Legacy Mobius Settings Customization field, the node name is the top-level entry
(no indentation), followed by a colon (:) and new line. Indent the new line two spaces and insert the key name,
another colon and space, and then the key value.
TDS and external data can be stored in cache by configuring the legacy settings, as shown in the example
below.
tds:
 tdsresourcepath:/home/mobius/asg/mobius/data/cache
ddrint_locations:
 externaldatalocation:/home/mobius/asg/mobius/data/cache
• Multiple entries under the same node must be indented exactly two spaces.
• Modify the existing caching solution to remove the per-user functionality and replace it with per-recipient
approach to reduce the number of caches populated, GetTopicList call per Mobius View node, and the load on
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 90


## Page 91


z/OS. If there are no variances, we can specify the cache to be based on recipient ID and ignore the user ID.
This improves the performance. This setting is disabled by default.
Configure the following in legacy Mobius settings:
java_vdrapi:
 topiccachelifetime: 60
 enabletopiccaching: true
 sharedrecipienttopiccache: true
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 91

Configure users, roles, and permissions
Use the Authorization page to manage roles, role permissions, and map the roles to users and groups. You can also
create and manage the access of Application Administrator in the on-premise deployments.
The following diagram shows the position of this task within the workflow of common administrative tasks. The
colored box indicates the starting point, while the dashed outline box represents an optional task.

data (me
When you create a group, every group member inherits the permissions defined at group level, automatically. You
can also add a group (child) to another group (parent) to create nested permission levels. In this case, the child group
inherits the permissions assigned to the parent group. In case of nested levels, every user within the child group
inherits all permissions at all levels if the user and group are defined within Mobius.
However, if the user is defined in an external system, the user will have access to only the permissions defined for the
group that the user is added to and will not inherit any permissions created at nested levels. For users defined in an
external system, permissions are granted to:
• Specific sections of content classes, identified by the section ID
• Document identified by index ID
In the following image, a user defined in the Mobius Server database as a member of the Marketing group has access
to Content Class AC001, Content Section123, as well as Content Class BAS001, Content Section 777 because the
Marketing group belongs to the Development group. If a recipient is a member of a group, and that group is a
member of another group, the recipient inherits all the access rights of both groups.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 92


## Page 93


The user CALBAREZ and the user’s group membership in Marketing are defined in an external system. Marketing has
access rights to Content Class AC001, Content Section 123. Therefore, CALBAREZ has access to Content Class AC001,
Content Section 123 only. If CALBAREZ was defined in the Mobius Server database and belonged to the Marketing
group, he would have access to both pieces of content.
Configuring users
Create a new user and assign the required permissions to the user using Mobius Administrator.
1. Log in to Mobius Administrator with valid credentials and click Mobius Server from the left-pane menu.
2. Click a required content source and repository.
3. Click Users > Add to add a new user.
4. Expand the General tab and do the following:
a. Enter a unique ID for the user in the User ID field. This field accepts alphanumeric and special
characters.
Note: To use forward slash (/) and backward slash (/) in the Group ID field, configure the
application server to allow encoded slashes in the URL.
b. Enter a name for the user in the Name field.
This name is displayed in Mobius Administrator and Mobius View.
c. Optional: Enter the title of the user in the Title field.
d. Optional: Enter the user address in the Address section.
e. Click Password, enter the password for the user profile, and click Ok.
The password is case-sensitive and maximum character limit is 8. Any character entered after the 8th
character is ignored, and the password is saved with only the first 8 characters.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 93


## Page 94


Note: For Linux, if the password contains Unicode characters like ‘à’ or ‘ì’ , configure Mobius
to use the en_US.utf8 locale.
5. Expand the Permissions tab and select the required administration, annotation, overlay, and auditing
permissions.
a. Select the required administrative permissions for the user.

Configuring user groups
Simplify access control for users by creating user groups, enabling required permissions, and adding members to the
group.
1. Log in to Mobius Administrator with valid credentials and click Mobius Server from the left-hand menu.
2. Click a content source, followed by a repository.
3. Click User Groups > Add to add a new user.
4. Expand the General tab and configure the following fields:
a. Enter a unique ID for the user in the Group ID field. The maximum character limit is 10. This field accepts
alphanumeric and special characters.

authentication, the group ID you entered must match the identifier passed from Mobius
View.
b. Enter a name for the user group in the Name field.
This name is displayed in Mobius Administrator and Mobius View.
c. Optional: Enter the header details in the Details field.
5. Expand the Permissions tab and select the required administration, annotation, overlay, and auditing
permissions.
a. Select the required administrative permissions for the user.

Adding members to user groups
Adding members to user groups
Add users to a new or existing groups or add user group to another to create nested permission levels to manage
user permissions at ease.
After adding members to user groups, configure user access to content classes, followed by configure user/user
group access to indexes and index groups.
1. Log in to Mobius Administrator with valid credentials and click Mobius Server from the left-hand menu.
2. Click a content source, followed by a repository.
3. Click User Groups and click a user group.
4. Expand the Group Members tab, select the required member(s) from the Non-Members list, and add them to
the group using the navigation buttons.
5. Expand the Group Memberships tab, select the required member group(s) from the Non-Member Of list, and
add them to the group using the navigation buttons to create nested permission levels.
6. Click Apply.
Related information
Configuring user groups

Creating user roles
Create user roles to manage different levels of user access by assigning specific permissions and access rights to each
user role. You can either select a role and assign it to a user/group or select a user/group and then assign a role.
The user or group names are populated from the active directory if LDAP support is enabled.
1. Log in to Mobius Administrator and click Authorization from the left-hand menu.
2. On the Roles panel, use a predefined role or create a new user role.
If federation is disabled, you must enter the repository name along with a "/" (repository/username) before
the user or group name in the Users and groups field. Policy assets are displayed in the following format.
Policyname PolicyAsset (Policy or Policy group) Namespaces
a. To use a predefined role, select one.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 97


## Page 98


Mobius supports nine predefined roles. Enter the username or group name to assign to the role in the
Users and groups field.
b. Otherwise, click Create Role.
c. On the New Role panel, enter the role name and description in the Name and Description fields.
d. Select Read Only to restrict the user from deleting or modifying a user role.
e. Click Add Permissions to select the required permissions, policies, and access control policy groups and
click Apply. Refer to Mobius user permissions for more information.
f. Enter the user name or group name to assign to the role in the Users and groups field.
Values are case-insensitive. You cannot create more than one user/group with the same name. You can
create a user and a group with the same name, or the other way around.
g. Click Create to create the user role with the required permissions.
3. On the Users/Groups panel, select a user/group and assign to a role.
a. Select All Users or All Groups from the Show drop-down.
b. Select a user or group from the list.
c. From the right pane, click Add Role and select the roles from the list.
You can also modify the roles assigned to a user/group.
The information icon displays the scope of the role (permissions and list of users/ groups assigned to
that role).
d. Optional: Click Edit role in the information pop-up and modify the permissions or users/groups.
This option does not apply to read-only user roles.
4. To delete a user/group, a user needs both Assign and Unassign Role permissions.
Only Users/groups created in the Authorization panel can be deleted. Those added via LDAP or other user
directories cannot. Unused users and groups or those without any assigned roles, display a delete icon when
hovered over.
Related information
Mobius assets and permissions
