Mobius assets and permissions
Mobius admin user can assign permissions to a user role, which determines the level of access the user role has
within the application.
Mobius General Permissions
The permissions listed under this asset provide access to manage the Authorization screen in the Mobius
Administrator application.
• Create Role - Allows the user to create a new user role.
• Delete Role - Allows the user to delete a user role. The roles, which are marked as Read Only cannot be
deleted.
• Edit Role - Allows the user to edit a user role.
• Grant Permission - Allows the user to grant permission to a role.
• Assign Role - Allows the user to assign roles to another user.
• Unassign Role - Allows the user to unassign a role for another user.
• Deny Permission - Allows the user to deny user permissions to access the application.
• Create Asset - Allows user to create new assets.
• Delete Asset - Allows the user to delete an asset.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 98


## Page 99


Access Control PolicyGroup
The permissions listed under this asset gives access to a specific policy group or all policies under that group. For
example, when a user is granted View All Policies in Group permission, they can view all policies in that group. When
a new policy is added to this group, the user automatically gets access to it.
• View Policy Group - Allows the user to view the policy group.
• View All Policies in Group - Allows the user to view all the policies in the group.
• Edit Rules for All Policies in Group - Allows the user to edit rules for all policies in this group.
• Edit Hit policy for all policies in group - Allows the user to edit hit policies for all policies in the group.
• Edit Operations for all policies in group - Allows the user to edit operations for all policies in the group.
Policy permissions
The permissions listed under this each policy asset provides access to specific operations in that policy.
• View Policy - Allows the user to view the policy.
• Edit Policy Rules: Allows the user to edit rules for the policy.
• Edit Hit policy - Allows the user to edit hit policy for the policy.
• Edit Operations - Allows the user to edit operations for the policy.
The above permissions are available for the following policies:
• Annotations Policy
• Archiving Policy
• Capture Policy
• Collections Policy
• Content Duplications Policy
• Content Streams Policy
• Direct Connect Policy
• Discovery Policy
• Document Checkout Policy
• Extraction Policy
• Favorites Policy
• Find Pages Policy
• Host Viewer Policy
• Import Policy
• Index Update Process Policy
• Indexing Policy
• Navigation Policy
• Presentations Image Policy
• Presentations Policy
• Presentations Scope Policy
• Record Types Policy
• Records Management Policy
• Repositories Policy
• Search Policy
• Settings Policy
• UI Governance Policy

Identity Access Management Service

Mobius Administrator Permissions
The permissions listed under this asset provide access to the Mobius Administrator application. The access rights that
are available to the Mobius Administrator differ for each document server.
• View Repositories - Allows the user to view the repositories.
• Miscellaneous Administrator Permissions – Allows the user to perform miscellaneous tasks.
• Manage Repository Indexes - Allows the user to view and modify the repository indexes.
• Manage Enterprise Folders - Allows the user to view and modify the enterprise folders.
• Manage Access - Allows the user to manage the access . (access control permission but need to check if its valid
)
• Manage Content Viewing - Allows the user to view and modify content viewing.
• Manage Browsing Options - Allows the user to view and modify the browsing options such as languages, sign
out button, browsing and searching.
• Manage Download Options - Allows the user to view and modify the available download options.
• Manage Records Management - Allows the user to view and modify the event templates created in the Records
Management screen.
• Manage Custom Actions - Allows the user to view and modify the custom actions.
• Manage Custom Buttons - Allows the user to view and modify the custom buttons .
• Manage Capture - Allows the user to view and modify options in the capture screen.
• Manage Redaction Policies - Allows the user to view and modify the redaction policies.
• Manage Authorization - Allows the admin user to provide permissions to view and modify authorization screen
to another user.
• Login Access - Allows the user to access the Mobius View Administrator and Policy Administrator screens.
• Manage Search And Indexing Control - Allows the user to view and modify the search and index control
options.
• Manage Encryption Keys - Allows the user to view and modify the encryption keys.
• Manage Archiving Policies - Allows the user to view and modify the archiving policies in the Document Server.
• Manage Users - Allows the user to view and modify the users in the Document Server.
• Manage User Groups - Allows the user to view and modify the user groups in the Document Server.
• Manage Content Classes - Allows the user to view and modify the Content Classes in the Document Server.
• Manage Indexes - Allows the user to view and modify the indexes.
• Manage Index Groups - Allows the user to view and modify the index groups.
• Manage Archive Creator Policy Selection Rules - Allows the user to view and modify the policy selection rules
created during archive creation.
• Manage Message Filtering - Allows the user to view and modify message filtering settings in Kafka.
• Manage Database Auditing - Allows the user to view and modify the database auditing settings in Kafka.
Mobius Permissions
The permissions listed under this asset provides access to the Mobius application.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 100


## Page 101


• Trust DirectConnect User- Allows the user to access Mobius DirectConnect with Mobius View's single sign-on
feature.
• Trust SAP User - Allows user to access SAP applications with Mobius View's single sign-on feature.
• Use Records Management - Allows the user to perform all operations related to HOLDS in Mobius View.
• Manage Document Revision - Allows the user to see all checked-out items (documents) available under the
Favorites page in Mobius View.
• Correct Metadata- Allows the user to edit metadata values for any document.
• Request Metadata correction - Allows the user to request metadata value corrections for any document
through index update process.
• Administer Records Management - Allows the user to perform all operations related to records management
such as create event templates, and so on.
• Edit extraction policy - Allows the user to create, import, and/or delete extraction policies.
• Publish Search - Allows the user to publish or unpublish saved searches.
• Publish Extraction Policy - Allows the user to publish or unpublish saved extraction policies and create, import,
and/or delete extraction policies.
• Manage Presentation - Allows the user to perform all operations related to Presentations in Mobius View.
• View Audit Events - Allows the user to access audit information in Mobius View.
• Allow Smart Chat - Allows the user to access the Smart Chat feature in Mobius View at the repository and
document levels.
When the federation option is enabled, the primary repository user must be set with the permissions to access the
features for all the repositories for vendor type NONE. For vendor type TOMCAT, users in the Mobius View
Administrator group are granted all the administrator privileges. Users not added to the Mobius View Administrator
group must be set with the permissions to access the features for all the repositories.
When the federation option is disabled, the repository user ( REPONAME/USER ) for vendor type NONE and users in the
tomcat-users.xml (role as a group) or LDAP user/group ( REPONAME/USER ) must be set with the permissions to
access the features for the repository.
Policy Service Permissions
The permissions listed under this asset gives access to the Policy management screen (controls or manages the policy
screens).
For example, when the user is granted with View All Policy Groups permission, they can access all policy groups in the
system. When a new policy group is created, the user will automatically get access to that group.
• Manage Policy Groups - Allows the user to create or modify edit all policies in the application.
• View All Policy Groups - Allows the user to view all policy groups in the application.
• View All Policies - Allows the user to view all policies in the application.
• Edit Rules for All Policies - Allows the user to edit rules for all policies in the application.
• Import /Export Policies - Allows the user to import or export all policies in the application.
• Edit Operations for All Policies - Allows the user to edit operations for all policies in the application, create new
actions, and replace inputs.
• Edit Hit Policy for All Policies - Allows the user to edit hit policy for all policies in the application.
• Manage Geo References - Allows the user to create, edit, or delete geo references.
• Manage Policies - Allows the user to manage all policies.
• Manage Actions - Allows the user to manage actions.
UI Governance PolicyGroup

Creating user roles
Configuring Mobius application administrator role
Application Administrator is a user who has permission-based access to Mobius View Administrator.
1. Open the application.yaml file for non-container deployment , update the following settings, and restart the
application.
asg:
  security:
      app-server:
         enabled: true
         type: Apache
         url-patterns: ["/rest/*", "/adminrest/*"]
         is-strip-domain-enabled: false
2. Log in to Mobius Administrator and click Authorization from the menu.
3. Create a new role for the application administrator, assign the required permissions, and map it to a user/user
group as described in topic "Creating user roles".
The following permissions are required for the application administrator role:
◦ Log in access permission to access the Mobius View Administrator and Policy Administrator screens.
◦ Manage Redaction and Manage Authorization access along with General permissions.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 104


## Page 105


Configure content classes and indexes
A content class is a set of common characteristics across multiple pieces of content that differ only by the time
stamp, location, and so on. Archives created from these content classes are called ingestions.
The following diagram shows the position of this task within the workflow of common administrative tasks. The
colored box indicates the starting point, while the dashed outline box represents an optional task.
Click a bo
you to tha
Mobius S
called Vie

View (required)
Deploy Mobius
Server
Configure Mobius
View

features

Server

View trace
Configure Mobius
Server repositories
Configure auditing Configure content
classes, indexes
Configure roles,
users, permissions
Configur
Server
Enable
archiving
Manage
storage
Analyz
data (me
Indexing content makes it faster and easier to locate and retrieve a particular piece of archive as indexes point to the
location of the desired archived content.
Indexes that are used for indexing content are stored in Mobius databases, can be:
• Independent indexes or
• Member indexes - indexes that are part of an index group.
Index groups are collections of related member indexes in Mobius databases. They reduce the number of database
joins required to process a search and improve search performance, efficiency, and database maintenance. For
example, you can combine indexes related to a check, such as account number, check number, bank number, and
memo into a single index group, which becomes a table in the database.
Databases with grouped indexes are also the base for enterprise folders that enable enhanced content organization,
for example, records management.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 105


## Page 106


Features of Index groups
Table: Features of index groups
Feature Description
Improved
search
performance
• Use of Boolean operator OR for searching
• Faster querying
• A search on multiple independent indexes does not return content if any of the indexes in the
search criteria does not have a value. However, if these indexes are member indexes of an
index group, empty data fields have a null value in the Mobius database and the content
displays in the search results when it meets all other search criteria.
Improved
database
maintenance
• Reduced disk storage
• Simplified editing – Edit an occurrence of the index entry in the database and the change
appears in all occurrences.
• Better security – You can limit user access to only part of the data.
Changing indexes within an index group after the content is added requires Rocket Technical
Support assistance.
Controlled
access to
data
through
indexes
Controlled access to a user or user group by specifying an index and one or more specific values.
Accessible values can also be specified as wildcards.
Indexing methods
Table: Supported methods for indexing content
Method Description
Content
indexing
(required) Content indexing limits retrieval access and distributes portions of content. To use this
method , you must define subdivisions of each content class. This lets you to control the portions of
the content class that are allowed for the user to view from a viewing client (for example, Mobius View)
or distributed to users.
Enterprise
indexing
(optional) Enterprise indexing quickly locates data when you are viewing information online. To use this
method, you must define the location of information in a content class. When you specify index items ,
Mobius View lists all the content sections and pages containing that information to locate the required
information at ease.
Creating content class
Create a content class or clone an existing content class using Mobius Administrator, build a template to generate the
archive filename, and configure the archiving method, retention rules, FTS indexing, and section code location
processing method .
1. Log in to Mobius Administrator with valid credentials and click Mobius Server from the left-hand menu.
2. Click a content source, followed by a repository.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 106


## Page 107


3. Click Content Classes > Add to add a new content class.
4. Expand the General tab and configure the following fields:
a. Enter an ID for the content class in the Content Class ID field.
b. Enter a name for the content class in the Name field. This name is displayed in Mobius Administrator
and Mobius View.
c. Enter the details in the Details field, if required.
5. Expand the Archiving tab and configure the following fields:
a. Enter the name of an archiving policy in the Policy name field.
b. Select a value from the Index ID drop-down, which will be used to identify indexes in the content class.
c. Enable the following checkboxes, as required.
▪ Compress all archives for this content - Stores all archives in compressed (zip) files, which
eventually increases the time required to create each archive.
▪ Encrypt all archives for this content - Stores all archives in encrypted files, which eventually
increases the time required to create each archive.
Note: To enable this option, you must have set up an encryption key for this
repository as a prerequisite.
▪ Perform OCR processing for this content - Enables OCR processing for content classes with
images and generates index values for content classes that are archived using list files.
6. Expand the Filename tab and define a template for the Document Archive File (DAF) name in the Template
field.
◦ The template is a combination of text and variables, which the repository uses to name the archive
during creation.
◦ The values inserted into the variables are truncated to 8 characters, by default. To increase or decrease
the variable length, suffix the variable with .n. For example, enter %SERVERNAME.20% to have 20
characters for server name. The following table lists the available variables for template creation.
Table: Template variables
Variable Description
%ARCHIVEDATE%
Date of archive creation, in YYYYMMDD format. Use this variable to limit the number
of files in a directory.
For example, to keep a month’s archives in a single directory, enter
%ARCHIVEDATE.6%. This generates a six-character directory in the format YYYYMM.
%ARCHIVETIME% Archive creation time the in HHMMSS format.
%RECIPIENTID% User ID that is signed into Mobius Administrator .
%UNIQUE% System-assigned characters for unique file names.
%REPORTID%
Content class to archive. Setting this variable in the path name will keep each
content class in its own directory. To avoid sub-directories, combine variables to
make multiple high-level directories.
%SERVERNAME%
Name of the repository where the file is archived. Use this variable when you want
to:
▪ Create distinct document archive file names with a common Content Class ID,
and
▪ Use multiple repositories pointing to the same database.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 107


## Page 108


Variable Description
%USERPROFILE%
Location to place the archive, which is the location of the,
▪ Local application data on Windows, or
▪ User's home directory on Linux.
Note: This variable is applicable only to the local file systems.
You can use the below file path delimiters based on the target file system.
◦ Windows - backslash [\]. For example, C:\mobius\
archives\%REPORTID%\%ARCHIVEDATE%\%ARCHIVETIME%%UNIQUE.2%.daf.
◦ Linux - forward slash [/]. For example, /opt/mobius/
archives/%REPORTID%/%ARCHIVEDATE%/%ARCHIVETIME%%UNIQUE.2%.daf.
◦ System agnostic - %PATHDELIM% . For example,
%USERPROFILE%%PATHDELIM%mobius%PATHDELIM%archives%PATHDELIM%%REPORTID%%PATHDELIM%%ARCHIVEDATE%%ARCHIVETIME%%UNIQUE.2%%.daf
You can also select predefined templates for network attached storage systems.
◦ Centera - <CENTERA>::\. You need not define anything after the backslash.
◦ Snaplock - <SNAPLOCK>:://snaplock.mydomain.com/
my_archives/%ARCHIVEDATE%/%ARCHIVETIME%%UNIQUE.2%.daf
Note: Different OS and storage systems have different formats for document archive file names.
7. Expand the Retention tab and select the required retention method from the drop-down.
8. Open the FTS Indexing tab and select the Disable Metadata Indexing and Disable Content Indexing
checkboxes to disable the automatic text indexing of the index values (metadata) and archived content,
respectively. If you do not enable these options, the system continues to index FTS records.
Note: Enabling or disabling the indexing options after the content is archived with existing
versions existing for this content may result in discrepancies between the Simple Search and
Enterprise Search results.
9. Open the Section Code Location tab, select the Allow archive processing by section code location
checkbox to enable this feature for the content class of text-based content, and define the following:
◦ Type of character data to specify the type of text in the content class.
◦ Row and column where the section code starts and the number of characters in the section code. You
can define a maximum of 30 locations. The archives created by section code location process are
compatible with earlier versions of Mobius.
10. Click Apply.
Related information
Adding encryption keys for archiving
Template examples for document archive file names
Configuring retention type for content class
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 108


## Page 109


Configuring retention type for content class
There are different types of retention the user can select while creating a content class in Mobius Administrator. Each
retention type is provided with a specific configuration.
1. Log in to Mobius Administrator with valid credentials and click Mobius Server from the left-hand menu.
2. Click a content source, followed by a repository.
3. Click Content Classes > Add to add a new content class.
4. Configure the content class.
5. To configure retention, expand the Retention tab and select the retention method.
◦ No retention - (default) Deletes the archive whenever required.
◦ Fixed retention period - Deletes the archive after the period defined in the Days of retention field. You
can also do the following:
a. Click Advanced Options to define retention based on the ingestion ID, or date and time of
archiving.
b. Define the deletion type (automatic or manual ) for the expired documents.
c. Define the number of recent content ingestions to be retained.
d. Choose to round the date of expiry to the last day of the year.
Note:
▪ If this option is selected for content archived on EMC Centera or a Net App
SnapLock-compliant storage system, this value is passed to the external
storage system as it might block the archive deletion before the specified
retention date has passed.
▪ Also, you can configure a SnapLock-compliant Net App storage system using
the Net App Data ONTAP software to specify values for minimum, maximum,
and default retention period. In this case, the value defined in the Retention
tab is valid only if it is compliant to the SnapLock retention definition.
◦ Event-based retention - Deletes the archive when triggered by an external event. Also, you can define
whether the expired documents must be deleted manually or automatically.
If the retention is changed from a fixed retention period type to an event-based retention, the existing
ingestions are migrated. Mobius estimates the time required to migrate the ingestions before the
process gets started.
◦ Forever retention - Retains the archive permanently, which cannot be deleted. Applicable to only
Centera storage systems.
◦ Event-based retention with an initial fixed retention period - Deletes the archive when triggered by
an external event after the period defined in the Days of retention field. You can also do the following:
a. Click Advanced Options to define retention based on the ingestion ID, or date and time of
archiving.
b. Define the deletion type (automatic or manual ) for the expired documents.
c. Choose to round the date of expiry to the last day of the year.
◦ Retain recent content ingestions - Retains only the number of ingestions defined in the Number of
recent content ingestions field.
Note: If you do not select this retention rule or specify a value of zero, the retention rule will be
disabled.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 109


## Page 110


6. Click Apply.
Related information
Creating content class

Modifying and deleting ingestion
View and delete existing files that stores the archived data (ingestions) and their properties as required in the
Content Ingestion panel. When a content is archived, a new ingestion is created with date and time stamp, which is
displayed on the Content Ingestion panel of the corresponding content class.
1. Log in to Mobius Administrator with valid credentials and click Mobius Server from the left-hand menu.
2. Click a content source, followed by a repository.
3. Click Content Classes and open the required content class.
4. Click Content Class Settings > Content Ingestions to view the existing ingestions.
5. Click on an ingestion to view the general information about the ingestion, such as Mobius version that was
used, whether the file is available for distribution, next date, and time when the Records Manager processes
the archive, expiry date of the ingestion, and the status and creation date of the Full Text Index.
6. Optional: Edit the default filename and filepath created from the template.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 112


## Page 113


7. Select the Allow Distribution checkbox to distribute the archive.
8. Click Apply.
9. To delete one or all ingestions, select the required ingestions and click the Delete icon in the left pane. A status
panel displays the statuses of deleted ingestions.
Creating indexes
Create indexes, mention how the index versions should be displayed on screen, select the type and length of the
index data, and define whether the index category should be process or document metadata.
1. Log in to Mobius Administrator with valid credentials and click Mobius Server from the left-hand menu.
2. Click a content source, followed by a repository.
3. Click Indexes > Add to add a new index.
4. Expand the General tab and configure the following fields:
a. Enter a unique identifier for the index in the Index field, which must be same as the one defined in the
policy.
Note: Do not use ' (apostrophe), " (quote), ? (question mark), { (left brace), } (right brace) in
the identifier as the Index ID is a part of the name of its database index table.
b. Enter the index name to be displayed on the viewing client in the Index Name field.
c. Optional: Enter the details in the Details field.
d. Select how you want the index versions to be displayed in the viewing client. You can either choose to
display all the versions or select Monthly, Quarterly, or Yearly as required.
Note: It is recommended to select a shorter period to accelerate the display of the indexes.
e. Select the Allow all users to access this index checkbox to enable all users to access the content class
from the index.
You can also select users to access the index on the Index Access Rights dialog box.
5. Expand the Format tab and configure the following fields:
a. Select a data format for the index from the Type of data contained in this index drop-down.
This data type for an index impacts how Mobius stores indexes and how viewing clients display values
during a search. Setting the index type and format allows you to search an index using relational
operators such as the greater than (>) sign and sort results correctly.
b. Select the maximum character length supported from the Maximum length drop-down depending on
the data type for the index.
6. Expand the Details tab and select the index category from the Index Category drop-down.
◦ Document metadata – Select this option if the indexes are generated from the content, which do not
change after archiving. For example, names, contact information, and ID numbers.
◦ Process metadata - Select this option if the indexes have values added or modified after archiving. For
example, reference numbers, status, and dates.
7. Click Apply.
Creating index groups
Combine the indexes that are frequently used or retrieved during search to a group, which improves the search
performance by using multiple index IDs in the search conditions.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 113


## Page 114


1. Log in to Mobius Administrator with valid credentials and click Mobius Server from the left-hand menu.
2. Click a content source, followed by a repository.
3. Click Index Groups > Add to add a new index.
4. Expand the General tab and configure the following fields.
a. Enter a unique identifier for the index group in the Index Group ID field, which must be same as the one
defined in the policy.
Note: Do not use ' (apostrophe), " (quote), ? (question mark), { (left brace), } (right brace) in
the identifier as the Index ID is a part of the name of its database index table.
b. Enter the index group name to be displayed on the viewing client in the Index Group Name field.
c. Select the location of the content class all index group instances refer to from the Index Group Scope
drop-down. 'Page' is the default.
5. Expand the Indexes tab and click +Add to add a new member index.
You can also clone, modify, or delete an existing member index in the group or move the index position within
the group.
6. Expand the Details tab and define a table name for the index group in the Table name field.
7. Click Apply.
Content indexing examples
Examples of section indexing and enterprise indexing are described below.
Content indexing example
By default, Mobius Administrator is provided with a content class sample “AC001”.
The AC001 accounting content class is a default sample containing a typical section index.
• The boxed area shows that this section contains information from the Manufacturing department in region EA.
• The Department Name is MANU and the Region Name is EA. By concatenating this information, the section
name becomes MANUEA.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 114


## Page 115


In Mobius View, you would find this section listed as MANUEA.
When content is distributed, the manager of each department receives only the relevant content sections. For
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 115


## Page 116


example, the manager of the manufacturing department receives only the MANUEA and MANUWE content sections.
Enterprise indexing example
The following use cases would benefit from enterprise indexing for online viewing:
Indexed content retrieval
The E.Z. Electric Company produces a customer bill whenever the customer’s meter is read. This process
generates different versions of the content class, and each ingestion contains only a subset of the customers.
E.Z. Electric customer service representatives need to access billing information for a specific customer but do
not know which ingestion of the content class contains the bill they need.
It would be inconvenient to examine every ingestion for the required customer’s bill. Each customer ID is an
index value associated with an ingestion of the bill generated when the customer’s meter is read. When a
customer service personnel or other users need information on a specific customer, they can search the index
for the specific customer ID and see a list of only those sections that contain the data they need.
For example, configure an index called CustomerID, which is extracted from the bills using an archiving policy.
The following three ingestions contain bills from various customers.
Ingestion Customer ID
January 15, 2024 000-105
January 15, 2024 000-115
January 15, 2024 000-203
January 15, 2024 000-216
January 15, 2024 000-345
January 15, 2024 000-399
January 15, 2024 000-410
January 15, 2024 000-702
December 31, 2023 000-415
December 31, 2023 000-579
December 31, 2023 000-616
December 31, 2023 000-771
December 31, 2023 000-856
December 31, 2023 000-908
December 31, 2023 000-993
December 31, 2023 000-115
December 31, 2023 000-203
December 31, 2023 000-216
December 31, 2023 000-345
December 31, 2023 000-399
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 116


## Page 117


Ingestion Customer ID
December 31, 2023 000-410
December 31, 2023 000-702
December 31, 2023 000-105
Bills for customer 000-345 are found only in two of the three ingestions, that are generated on December 15,
2024 and January 15, 2024. A customer representative searching for CustomerID Equal To 000-345 will see
only the ingestions containing these bills as shown below.
Ingestion Customer ID
January 15, 2024 000-345
December 15, 2024 000-345
High volume content classes
MobiusTel is a phone company with a very high transaction volume, averaging 200 gigabytes of data per month.
To ease the strain of this high volume, the company breaks processing into batches. Instead of one ingestion
being created for a month, a new ingestion is created at the end of each day.
A typical content class for MobiusTel summarizes the activity of each area code. Users of the MobiusTel content
class want information from area code 914. However, they will not know which ingestion contains 914.
MobiusTel sets up enterprise indexes and specifies the area code as an index. Each area code in each content
class becomes an index value so that the users can see a list of area codes and select the one they want.
For example, configure an index called AreaCode, which is assigned to the transaction summary files during
archiving using list archiving. The following table displays the results for a total of 122,275 ingestions by Area
Code for 202 :
Ingestion Area Code
January 1, 2023 201
January 1, 2023 202
January 1, 2023 203
January 1, 2023 204
January 1, 2023 . . . For the rest of 335 US Area Codes
. . .
January 2, 2023 201
January 2, 2023 202
January 2, 2023 203
January 2, 2023 204
January 2, 2023 . . . For the rest of 335 US Area Codes
. . .
December 31, 2023 201
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 117


## Page 118


Ingestion Area Code
December 31, 2023 202
December 31, 2023 203
December 31, 2023 204
December 31, 2023 . . . For the rest of 335 US Area Codes
To view transaction summaries for Area Code 202, search for AreaCode Equal To 202 and a list of 365
summaries appears by date. It avoids scrolling through 122,275 ingestions.
Different clients for each ingestion
ABC BankService produces a Demand Deposit Account (DDA) content class for each of its client banks. Each
ingestion of the content class deals with a different client bank. The user is unaware which bank is associated
with which ingestion. which bank is associated with which ingestion.
When ABC BankService content class users need information for MoreMoney Bank, they will not know which
ingestion of the content class has the information for MoreMoney Bank. With enterprise indexes, they do not
need to know the ingestion as Client ID is defined as an index and each client ID such as MoreMoney Bank is an
index value. The users can select the client they want to view.
Managing user access to indexes and content
Control user access to at the level of content, indexes, and index-based pages using Mobius Administrator. Content
access rights control a user’s access to content and sections of the content. Index access rights provide access to
specific indexes or information within sections of the content.
To view a specific index in a content, the user must have access to both the index and appropriate content.
Content access rights
Content access rights give users access to a content class and list of class IDs. You can use wildcards in the list of class
IDs to specify a range of accessible class IDs. To access any page in an ingested class of content, the user must have
permission to access that ingested class. This access permission overrides any other type of permission access.
Index access rights
Index access rights give users access to content that is based on specified index values, which are associated to the
ingested content. An index value provides user access to content based on:
• An entire section, also referred to as a document scope.
• One or more pages in a section, also referred to as a page scope.
• One or more locations on a page, also referred to as a location or line scope.
Index access can be given to users with or without global index permissions and for member indexes. Indexes within
the index group are not globally accessible.
Within Mobius View, index access rights apply to the following functions:
• View access for automatic folders and content
• Searches
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 118


## Page 119


• Browsing
• Locate function
• Hold processing
Index values impact browsing and searching. For appropriate search results, indexes must contain valid values.
Index-based page access rights
Page-level access based on index values allows the user to access specific pages of archived content. You can apply
the index-based page access rights as an additional filter to the existing content access control methods (content or
index).
For example, if an index value has document scope, a user can access each individual page in a document. When an
index value has a page scope or location scope, the user is only given access to one page from an entire section.
If a user searches or browses content secured by index-based access rights, that is when a content class is associated
with a specific index, the index values will filter the pages and displays only the pages that are accessible to the user.
This result may also contain only a subset of the pages in the section. The pages returned may not be continuous in
the ingestion although their order in the ingestion is preserved.
Index access and index-based pages access rights work together with content access, which controls the specific
content that users can view. Mobius Server prioritizes content access before index access.
Related information
Configuring user groups
Configuring user access to content classes
Analyze either the configuration of individual users or groups to determine the content classes and sections they can
access or the content class to view a list of users and groups who can access it and configure user access accordingly.
1. Log in to Mobius Administrator with valid credentials and click Mobius Server from the left-hand menu.
2. Click a content source, followed by a repository.
Each repository contains configuration items like Encryption Keys, Users, Indexes, Content Classes, policies,
and rules.
3. Click User Groups and open the required user group.
4. Click Content Class Access Rights > Add and configure the following fields.
a. Enter the content class ID that is already defined for this repository in the Content Class ID field.
b. Specify sets of sections using the wildcard characters (*, ?) in the Sections field.
c. Optional: Enter the details about the access rights in the Details field.
d. Select the Allow online viewing and Allow immediate archiving check boxes to enable online viewing
and immediate archiving of the matching sections, respectively.
5. Click Apply.
6. To view a list of users and groups who can access a content class:
a. Go to the content sources page, click Content Classes, and open the required content class.
A list of users who have access to this content class is displayed in the content class access rights
configuration panel.
b. Click a user or group to view the access permission details.
Based on the content class ID and section corresponding to the content class, a user or group will have
access to that section of the content.
Related information
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 119


## Page 120


Configuring user groups
Configuring user/user group access to indexes and index groups
Use the Index Access Rights option of a selected user or user groups to add the indexes that a user or user group
can access from Mobius View.
• For users, access rights to indexes within and between index groups follow OR logic. A user can view any page
that matches any of the index access right values of any index the user has permission to view.
• For user groups, the same index access applies to all members of the group. If a user belongs to multiple
groups, Mobius applies OR logic to determine the user's access.
You must configure the user or group to access each index to enable a user or group to access a content class from
multiple indexes.
1. Log in to Mobius Administrator with valid credentials and click Mobius Server from the left-hand menu.
2. Click a content source, followed by repository.
Each repository contains configuration items like Encryption Keys, Users, Indexes, Content Classes, policies,
and rules.
3. Click Users and User Groups to open the required user and user group, respectively.
4. Click Index Access Rights > Add and do the configure the following fields:
a. Enter the user ID that must access the indexes in the User ID field.
b. Enter the ID of the index group who must access the indexes in the Group ID field. Applies to only user
groups.
c. Select an index from the Index drop-down that the user or group can access from Mobius View.
d. Define an index value in the Index Value field for the user to view and archive content that contains an
index with that specific index value.
You can also use wildcard characters (*, ? ) in the index value for the user to view and archive content
that has that index irrespective of the index value. A single asterisk (*) grants access to all values.
e. Optional: Configure the access rights in the Details field.
5. Click Apply.
Configuring index-based page access for content sources
Enable index-based page access for a content source through the Settings panel of the required content source.
Once enabled, you can define index-based page access for content classes for all the repositories under this content
source.
1. Log in to Mobius Administrator with valid credentials and click Mobius Server from the left-hand menu.
2. Select a content source and click the Settings icon.
3. Expand the Index-based Page Access Control tab and select the Enable index-based page access checkbox
to enable index-based page access.
4. Click Apply.
Index access examples
Example: Applying index access to one index group
The following example explains applying index access rights to a single index group.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 120


## Page 121


The index group CHECKS, consists of the indexes ACCOUNT, SSN, CHECKNO, and AMOUNT. Assuming that the
following check data exists in the database:
CHECKS
ACCOUNT REPORT SECTION SSN CHECKNO AMOUNT
ACCT100 A 123-45-6206 1000 10.00
ACCT100 A 123-45-6352 1001 50.00
ACCT300 B 123-45-6409 234 55.00
ACCT501 A 123-45-6409 948175.00
ACCT502 A 123-45-3487 740 11.00
ACCT503 A 123-45-1111 754 90.00
ACCT700 B 123-45-6708 106 15.00
ACCT999 B 123-45-6993 9864 1001.00
Use Case 1
If a user’s index access rights for ACCOUNT are defined as ACCT5* and ACCOUNT = ACCT999, when the user searches
for checks greater than $15.00, the search results are limited to the following:
CHECKS
ACCOUNT SSN CHECKNO AMOUNT
ACCT501 123-45-6409 9481 75.00
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 121


## Page 122


CHECKS
ACCOUNT SSN CHECKNO AMOUNT
ACCT503 123-45-1111 754 90.00
ACCT999 123-45-6993 9864 1001.00
Note: Indexes meeting any of the user’s index access rights appear in the search results when they
meet the search criteria specified by the user. In this example, account ACCT502 would appear in the
search results if check #740 were over $15.00.
Use Case 2
To demonstrate how index access rights works in conjunction with content access rights, if the same user whose
index access rights for ACCOUNT are defined as ACCT5* and ACCOUNT = ACCT999 only has content access rights to
section B of the Checks content, when the user searches for checks greater than $15.00, the search results are
limited to the following:
CHECKS
ACCOUNT CONTENT SECTION SSN CHECKNO AMOUNT
ACCT999 B 123-45-6993 9864 1001.00
Use Case 3
If this same user has content access rights to the Statements content but not to the Checks content, the user does
not see any information from the Checks content but sees available information for ACCT501, ACCT503, and ACCT999
in the Statements content.
Example: Applying index access to multiple index groups
The following examples explain applying index access to multiple index groups in situations where indexing content
using two index groups is required.
An archived content contains two index groups, SSN and ACCT. The SSN topic group contains a list of customer social
security numbers along with customer names. The ACCT index group holds balance and date information by
customer account number, as follows:
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 122


## Page 123


SSN ACCT
SSN LastName FirstName Acct Balance Date
101594567 Smith Jane 100-10 $22,776 1/31/07
102587654 Smith Joe 100-20 $35,000 1/31/07
082345874 Smith Mary 100-30 $5,000 1/31/07
082594567 Smith Bob 100-40 $500 1/31/07
201563445 Jones Grace 200-10 $10,000 1/31/07
201563445 Jones Grace 200-20 $20,000 1/31/07
201591234 Jones Jim 200-30 $25,211 1/31/07
Use Case 1
If the Indexes in the ACCT index group are “globally accessible,” when a user queries information through the SSN
index group, and retrieves information from the ACCT index group, indexes from the SSN index group are included.
For example, when a user searches for Smith, where there are index access rights on SSN and LastName and global
access rights for all indexes in the ACCT index group; the search results are limited to the following:
SSN ACCT
SSN LastName FirstName Acct Balance Date
101594567 Smith Jane 100-10 $22,776 1/31/07
102587654 Smith Joe 100-20 $35,000 1/31/07
082345874 Smith Mary 100-30 $5,000 1/31/07
082594567 Smith Bob 100-40 $500 1/31/07
However, user queries through the ACCT index group return statement information for any account, since all of the
ACCT indexes have global access.
For example, when a user searches for any ACCT, Balance, or Date from the ACCT index group; the search results in
the following:
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 123


## Page 124


ACCT
Acct Balance Date
100-10 $22,776 1/31/07
100-20 $35,000 1/31/07
100-30 $5,000 1/31/07
100-40 $500 1/31/07
200-10 $10,000 1/31/07
200-20 $20,000 1/31/07
200-30 $25,211 1/31/07
While archiving, the user can only archive content for the Smiths. Global index access (at the index level) is not
verified for archiving and since the user has no other index access, the user cannot archive information to the ACCT
index group or archive content with any ACCT group indexes.
Note: Establish index access rights carefully to make sure that a restriction is carried throughout the
application and applied consistently to all related content. Even if the user can view information, it does
not mean that they have archiving access to that content.
Use Case 2
In this use case, the user does not have access to any indexes under the ACCT index group and these indexes are not
“globally accessible.”
When the user queries information through the SSN index group, and tries to retrieve information from the ACCT
index group, the user only sees information from the SSN index group.
For example, when a user searches for Smith, where the user wants to retrieve the SSN, LastName, FirstName, Acct,
and Balance and there is index access on SSN and LastName and no access for any index in the ACCT index group;
search results are limited to the following:
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 124


## Page 125


SSN
SSN LastName FirstName
101594567 Smith Jane
102587654 Smith Joe
082345874 Smith Mary
082594567 Smith Bob
To retrieve the desired data, the user should have access to the Smiths by ACCT, such as ACCT=100*. Then the user
would be able to see the required information from both index groups.
SSN ACCT
SSN LastName FirstName Acct Balance Date
101594567 Smith Jane 100-10 $22,776 1/31/07
102587654 Smith Joe 100-20 $35,000 1/31/07
082345874 Smith Mary 100-30 $5,000 1/31/07
082594567 Smith Bob 100-40 $500 1/31/07
While archiving, if the user does not have index access to the ACCT index group; the user cannot archive content with
indexes from that index group. However, with access to ACCT=100*, the user can archive content in that index group.
Example: Combining index access and content access
In some situations, you will want to combine index access with content access rights.
Content access rights control a user’s access to content and sections of content. Index access rights provide a finer
granularity because they grant access to specific indexes or information within sections of content. To view a specific
index in a content, a user must have both the index access and the appropriate content access.
To access content through an index:
• The Global index access permission was enabled for a user or user group. By default, this is not enabled.
• An index was created that allows all user access through the Allow all users to access this index setting.
Align content access and index access to prevent unauthorized viewing of information.
For example, two users have access to a section in a content that contains account information. One user can see
pages for all accounts, while the second user can only see the pages for one account. When index access rights are
applied to a search, the search returns the page range for the second user that only includes the pages for one
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 125


## Page 126


account.
In the example below, Grace Jones has index access to ACCT=200* and Jim Jones has access to ACCT=200-30. They
both have content access to Section B. On retrieving account information, Jim can see one entry for 200-30, and
Grace can see all entries starting with 200-.
ID index group Section
ACCT index group
No access)
ID LastName FirstName Acct Balance Date

201591234 Jones Jim B 200-30 $25,211 1/31/07
Content access reports
To summarize the content access from a repository that is on a Mobius Server, you can generate a content access
report.
The access report is written to a CSV file on the computer that generates the report.
You can choose whether the report organizes the information by content class ID or recipient ID cross-referenced by
the content class ID or recipient ID.
A content access report that is cross-referenced by content class ID will display information in a table that is
organized by Content class ID.
Table: Content access report cross-referenced by content class ID example
Content class ID Recipient ID Section Source View Print E-mail E-mail Notification Archive
11673 AAMOSBEY * GROUP(ESIRPTS) Y N N N N
A content access report that is cross-referenced by Recipient ID will display information in a table that is organized by
Recipient ID.
Table: Content access report cross-referenced by Recipient ID example
Recipient ID Content class ID Section Source View Print Email E-mail Notification Archive Details
101535 ALLXXFS78B 101535 Direct Y N N N N
Generating content access reports on Windows
You can generate access reports for the content classes on a Windows system.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 126


## Page 127


Before you begin:
• Download the Sqlplus command line utility from the Oracle website. This utility is typically part of the Oracle
client installation or Oracle database installation.
• Download the Sqlcmd command line utility from the Microsoft website.
A script that accesses the database content and calls the database command line utility, generates the report.
1. Open the command prompt on the computer where Mobius Server is running.
2. Navigate to the Mobius Server installation directory.
3. Based on the database, run the following command.
Microsoft SQL Server
generateVDRReportMSSQL.bat <username> <password> <server_name> <database>
<report type> <(optional path_and)_ filename>.csv
To view the allowed options, run the following command:
generateVDRReportMSSQL.bat
Oracle
generateVDRReport.bat <username> <password> <Oracle service_name>
<report type> <(optional path_and)_ filename>.csv
To view the allowed options, run the following command:
generateVDRReport.bat
For both databases:
◦ report_type is cross-referenced by Content Class ID content.
◦ If path is not specified, the file is created in the current directory. You must modify the other options to
correspond to your database settings
Generating content access reports on Linux
You can generate access reports for the content classes on a Linux system.
Before you begin:
• Download the Sqlplus command line utility for Linux from the Oracle website. This utility is typically part of the
Oracle client installation or Oracle database installation.
• Download the Sqlcmd command line utility for Linux from the Microsoft website.
A script that accesses the database content and calls the database command line utility, generates the report.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 127


## Page 128


1. Navigate to the Mobius Server installation directory on the computer where Mobius Server is running.
2. Run the following command:
generateVDRReport.sh <username> <password> <Oracle service_name> <report type> <(optional pa
th_and)_ filename>.csv
where:
◦ set report_type to 1 for cross-reference by the user, and 2, for cross-reference by content.
◦ if path name is not specified, the file is created in the current directory. You can modify other options
corresponding to the database settings.
3. Run the following command to view the allowed options:
generateVDRReport.sh
Universal indexes
Create universal indexes to simplify the creation of complex search criteria and queries. A universal index acts as a
surrogate for multiple indexes that represent the same type of content in multiple repositories.
Universal indexes in the index map correspond to the indexes in the sources. In your applications, universal indexes
appear as search criteria fields in searches.
For example, a CMIS repository has an index named AcctNo, and a Mobius repository has an index named
AccountNum and both indexes contain customer account numbers. To search both repositories simultaneously, you
must create a universal index named AccountNumber and map the AcctNo index and AccountNum index to the
universal index AccountNumber. You can then use the AccountNumber index in a search and Mobius View
automatically builds the appropriate search query for each source.
You can create multiple universal indexes as required and map more than one repository index to each universal
index. However, you cannot map a repository index to more than one universal index.
Note: Mapping independent topics in the same repository to the same universal index is not supported.
The results obtained when querying this universal index are unpredictable.
You can also assign searchable and returnable properties to the repository indexes that are listed automatically on
the Content Repositories page after you add a repository and create a universal index.
To enable the search API to use the universal index instead of repository index, set
fallbackToRepositoryIndexWhenUniversalAbsent in the application.yaml file to false. If this parameter is set to
true and there is no matching universal index, then the search is performed using the repository index.
Configuring universal indexes
Create universal indexes and use them to access a set of associated indexes and for federated searches.
Before you begin: You must have enabled each repository for universal indexes.
1. Log in to Mobius Administrator and click Mobius Server from the menu.
2. Click a content source to view its repositories.
3. Click a repository.
4. Click Indexes and select the required index from the list of indexes returned from the repository, with the data
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 128


## Page 129


type of each index specified.
5. Do either of the following steps:
a. Select the Map indexes automatically checkbox to assign a universal index name to each repository
index automatically.
b. Enter a universal index name in the Universal Index field. When you begin to enter a universal index
name, Mobius suggests existing universal indexes that match the data type and character set of the
repository index.
6. Do either of the following to manage repository index searchability.
a. Select the Toggle All Searchable checkbox to make all indexes searchable (restore the default) .
b. Select the corresponding Searchable checkbox to make an individual index searchable.
7. Do either of the following to manage repository index returnability.
a. Select the Toggle All Returnable checkbox to make all indexes returnable (restore the default) .
b. Select the corresponding Returnable checkbox to make an individual index returnable.
8. Enter the index description in the Description field, if required. An initial value is retrieved from the repository,
if available.
9. Define the format in which the index values are stored within the source.
This is an optional feature but is important when the values for an index are stored in different formats in
different sources. You can use an X and # for each alphabet character and numeric value, respectively.
10. Click Apply.
Related information
Formatting search values
Formatting search values
Mobius View can reformat the search values automatically in the defined format when multiple repositories have the
same type of information stored in different formats.
For example, two repositories contain ID numbers. In repository A, the ID numbers have hyphens to separate the
number into segments, for example, 123-45-6789. In repository B, the ID numbers do not have hyphens, for example,
123456789. If a user enters 123456789 as a search value, the search does not find a match in repository A because
that repository uses hyphens in the numbers.
After specifying the index format, if a user enters a search value in the wrong format, the value is automatically
reformatted for the search query. If you specify the index format XXX-XX-XXXX  for repository A and a user enters
the search value 123456789, the search value is reformatted to 123-45-6789 for the search query.
If the user enters a search value that cannot match the format, the value is not reformatted. For example, if the
search is "equals to": "123456789"  and index format is ###-###-### , the value is reformatted as "123-456-789".
If the search is "is like": "123456"  and the index format is ###-###-### , the value is not reformatted and
remains 123456.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 129

An archiving policy contains a set of rules that identify the content to archive. Create an archiving policy and define
rules using the Mobius Administrator.
You can define rules graphically by identifying the critical elements on each page of the content. You can use the
sample policy as a base policy, which you can customize and save under a different policy name.

Creating archiving policies
Create archiving policies and, define rules and field groups.
1. Log in to Mobius Server Administrator.
For example:
(https://your_host:your_port/mobius/vdradmin/ ).
2. Select a content source from Mobius Content Sources, and perform the following tasks.
a. Select a document server to view and create policies.
b. Click Archiving Policies.
The Archiving Policies pane is displayed with a SAMPLE_POLICY, which is available by default.
3. Click Create Policy.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 130


## Page 131


4. Select a document to upload, and click Next.
5. Click Page Settings and select a data type for the uploaded document. Depending on the data type that you
select, different fields are displayed.
6. Click Add Rule to add rules, and then drag the cursor to select the page area or field to add to the rule.
Note: You can select either a part of the page or the entire page. On selection, the left pane
opens with the properties for the respective field.
a. Define the properties for the selected area.
b. Click Add Field to add additional fields to the rule, and then select the field.
Click the Fields drop down or Add Field to rename and define the extraction criteria.
c. Select the rule level that defines your rule hierarchy.
Header, Details, and Footers are available for both Rule level and Field level. Rule level change affects the
whole set of fields related to the rule. Field level change affects only the individual field.
Select a Header option to define the top level of the column and select a Footer option to append footer
fields with fields from detailed transactions.
d. Use the following options to increase efficiency of the field selection process:
Suggest Rules
Automatically select fields for data extraction.
Clear All Rules
Restart the process of field selection.
Show Labels
Display field names and rule names the right pane. These labels are useful to associate the fields
and rules in the left pane with the data in the sample document.
Preview
Generate a preview to check the extracted data.
You can update the field selection and rule definitions based on the preview of extracted data. You
can also customize the preview. For more information, see Preview of the extracted data.
e. In the Make Selection area, select the data for archiving.
▪ Use entire page - Select to makes the entire page the selection area.
▪ Use partial page - Select the boundaries of the selection area.
▪ Relative to anchor points - By defining the top and bottom anchor positions in every page, the user
can ensure the selection area is in a position that is relative to the anchor points.
The top anchor defines the upper left corner of the rule area. The bottom anchor defines the
bottom right corner of the rule area. Specifying the anchor occurrence to All for either the top or
bottom anchor applies to both anchors. The user can define the rule anchor by selecting some
text on the page or defining a field:
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 131


## Page 132


▪ Specify text- User can either type in the text to be used as anchor, or drag the yellow anchor
box to another location in the document to locate the text in the right panel.
▪ Select field- The Select field option for an anchor is enabled only when more than one rule is
created. The selected field must be outside the rule containing the anchor. To use the Select
field option :
▪ Add more than one rule.
▪ Select the Relative to anchor points and define the top or bottom anchor from any
one of the rules created.
▪ Click the Select field radio button and select a field from the drop down.
▪ Fixed position- This is the default option. Selecting this ensures that the selection area
remains constant across the pages of the content class.
▪ Always expand selection area to the full width of the page- Extends the selection area to fit
the width of the page.
▪ Always expand selection area to the bottom of the page- Extends the selection area to the
bottom of the page.
f. Click Next to view the field group pane.
7. Select the rule area in the document and click the ellipses to set the text alignment to left, right, center, or
justified.
◦ Collapse Horizontal- Aligns the text to the column width.
◦ Expand Horizontal- Allows you to increase the width of the column.
◦ Collapse Vertical- Allows you to apply a fixed row height.
◦ Expand Vertical- Allows you to increase the height of the row.
◦ Discard Current Changes- Resets all the column widths and row heights to default before closing the text
alignment dialog.
8. Click Add Field Group to define fields as a group and select the required index from the indexes list defined in
the document server.
9. Select the fields and the order in which they are combined to create the index value.
After you select the fields, the right pane displays the index value on each of the page of the content.
10. Click Settings, and then define the required properties for the policy.
11. Click Apply.
Related information
Defining field groups

type and attributes for the document. The chosen data type determines the fields and attributes.
