Configuring Field and Advanced Field settings
A field describes criteria for extraction of particular text data from a document. During archiving, data from multiple
fields is collected together in an extraction result row. You can then use Advanced Settings to add more settings to
the field.
Field settings typically consists of basic field information, such as field name, and field type (data, string, or date).
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 136


## Page 137


1. Click Advanced Settings from the Fields section.
The Advanced Settings consists of several tabs for configuration.
2. Click the General (Advanced Settings) tab to view field information.
◦ Field Name - Enter the field name.
◦ Data Type - Select the data type as either string, date, number or currency.
◦ Field Level - Select the field level.
3. Click the Extract From (Advanced Settings) tab to specify the position and data to extract in the document.
Multi-line fields - Sometimes it is necessary to extract multiple lines of information from the original content as
a single output field, which is then extracted to a single cell in Microsoft Excel. For example, some descriptive
information or address may expand to multiple lines in the original content, but when you extract to Microsoft
Excel you can combine the description and the output to a single cell.
a. Expand the Adjust Position section to configure the position to get data from a document.
▪ Select the Use bounded area check box to use data from the selected area.
▪ Enter the column number in the Start at character column from which you want to extract data.
▪ Select the Use specific row check box and specify the row number from which you want to extract
data.
▪ Enter the minimum and the maximum length of the data to extract. The fields are blank by default.
The minimum value that can be entered in these fields is 1.
▪ Select the Span for Rows check box and enter the number of rows required in the output.
▪ Select a value from the String Terminator drop-down to append at the end of the string in the
selected area. Space is selected by default. If Null is selected, the string ends at the end of the
selected area. Clear the Use bounded area check box to enable this option.
b. Click Match String for string Data Type and Match Number for number Data Type.
▪ Select Allow blank field to allow blank fields to display in the extracted data.
▪ To select a specific range, select the range by entering the minimum number in the Min field and
the maximum number in the Max field.
4. Click the Format To (Advanced Settings) tab to modify the data by changing the spacing, case sensitivity and
length.
a. Enter the text in the Sample field.
b. Select Replace extracted data with a specified value to replace the extracted data with your desired
data, then enter a replacement value.
c. Select Remove spaces to remove extra spaces in the start (Leading) or end (Trailing) areas of the
extracted data.
d. Select Change the case to: to change the character case to either upper or lower.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 137


## Page 138


e. You can choose to adjust the length of the extracted value to a minimum length by selecting Pad the
extracted value to a minimum length. Choose the padding character as either Space or Zero.
f. Select Truncate the extracted value to a maximum length to display a lengthy string and enter a
value.
g. Select an option from the Decimal places section. Then, enter the decimal precision range which
decides the number of values after the decimal point for the selected field values.
h. Select an option from the 1000 Separator drop-down to apply separator (,) for the extracted data.
▪ Use original formatting - Retain the original format.
▪ Include separator - Apply separator comma (,) for numbers greater than 999.
▪ Do not include separator - Exclude the separator.
i. Select the Hide Field check box to hide the field. This option does not hide the field from extraction
calculation.
5. Click the Dependency (Advanced Settings) tab.
a. Choose either after or before to select text on the previous or next row.
b. Select your desired field from the selection.
c. Chose the desired condition on same row, on any row, on next row, or on different row
You can edit the field as required. For example, you can change a field with date format from MM/DD/YY to
DD/MM/YY or the date separator from “/” to “-“. Similarly, you can change the format of a number.
You can also rename the field, edit the data type of the field, and also edit the format of the data in the fields.
Note: Editing format of the data is applicable only for Number and Date data types.
6. Click Add Field in the rule editor to add a new field.
7. Select the rule area.
8. Click Delete to delete the fields that are part of a rule.
Defining field groups
Creating an archiving policy definition includes defining the field group occurrence. When a document is uploaded,
define the field group to specify whether the index value must appear on every page, section, and content class.
Creating the field groups requires defining the index values for an uploaded document as part of archiving policy
creation. The archiving status depends on the below conditions.
• When Group must occur in every is selected, and Stop archiving if this group is missing is not selected -
Archiving proceeds even if the group is missing in the scope. However, a message is generated, and the index
value is sent to auditing.
• When Group must occur in every and Stop archiving if this group is missing are selected - Archiving fails if
the group is missing in the scope.
1. Click Add to define fields as a group.
2. Configure the index from the list of indexes defined in the repository on the Mobius Server, as follows.
Group must occur in every
During archiving, check whether this index group exists in the content class. Define the index group by
selecting this option, and then select Page, Section, or Report (also referred to as content class) from the
drop-down list depending on where you want the index group to appear in the section. Stop archiving if
this group is missing is optional.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 138


## Page 139


Use location index
Select this checkbox to store index values on the same row of the index group table.
To extract columns that contain the AC001 content class and store values from the same rows in the
content class on the same row in the index group table, you must select the Use Location Index for each
index. This ensures that the values are organized correctly in the table.
You can preview the indexes for which location index is enabled in the Settings panel.
Apply section scope for the index
By default, the index scope is a page, which means that the index search results will point to individual
pages. However, you can treat the entire section of the document (that is, all pages of the section) as one
logical unit.
For example, you have the bank account number on the first page of the bank statement, and you have
the total for the bank account statement on the last page. If you want to search for accounts with a total
balance greater than $1M and point to the first page of such bank statement, then, you must store the
account number and total on the same row in the Index Group table for the entire section. One way to
achieve this is to extract data in the DataMiner policy so that the account number and total appear in a
single row in Preview and select Apply section scope for the index.
Allows the fields to retain the value from the previous page
Select this checkbox to force the creation of index entries when the construction of the content class
would bypass the index entries. This situation occurs when a value of a field in this group is the same for
successive pages, but the value for the field appears only on the first page in the succession, and not on
all subsequent pages.
Note: Content class pages must be sequential to create the index entries.
A&AS field groups
Designate field groups for A&AS usage in the archiving policy.
If a field group is designated to A&AS, the field values from that group are passed to A&AS for analysis during
archiving.
To designate field groups to A&AS, select AAS in the Add Field Group pane.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 139


## Page 140


Defining metadata fields
Define metadata fields to extract additional information about the document other than the data within the
document, such as file name, file drive, page number, and so on.
1. Click Settings on the Define Field Groups tab of the new archiving policy definition.
2. Expand the Metadata section and select one of the following:
a. Select the Create fields based on internal metadata checkbox and specify the sample file path to
populate and display the values of the internal metadata in the archiving policies and click Apply.
Note: The Internal Metadata Fields section is created automatically in the Create Rules
tab (Step 2 of the archive policy creation).
Return to the Create Rules tab, and expand the Internal Metadata Fields section.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 140


## Page 141


i. To add new fields, click Add field.
ii. Specify the field name in the Rename to field.
iii. Select the data to be extracted from the Data is extracted from metadata drop-down list.
The SAMPLE_FILE_PATH will be replaced with the actual file path for the input file during archiving.
The following data is included:
▪ Page number
▪ Date
▪ Time
▪ File directory
▪ File drive
▪ File path
▪ File server
▪ File name with part number - In the file name, dot (.) or underscore (_) are the separator
characters. FILE_NAME_PARTx is created based on the separator characters. If
FILE_NAME_PART1 is selected, the first part of the file name is extracted, if
FILE_NAME_PART3 is selected for a file name with one separator, no data is extracted.
▪ File type with part number - In the file extension (type), underscore (_) is the separator
character. FILE_TYPE_PARTx is created based on the separator character. If FILE_TYPE_PART1
is selected, the first part of the file extension is extracted, if FILE_TYPE_PART3 is selected for
a file extension with one separator, no data is extracted.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 141


## Page 142


Note: You can also define index entry fields from the internal document-level
and page-level metadata associated with reports, such as AFP indexes (TLEs).
You can either use these fields as they appear in the Internal Metadata
window or create other fields/field groups as required. For example, you can
use NBST_ACCT to create a field called ACCOUNT_NUMBER.
b. Select the Create fields based on external metadata checkbox, select a .xml file and click Apply.
Once the external metadata file is loaded, the rule editor is split into two sections, Sample Document
viewer and External Metadata document viewer.
Fields created from the sample document viewer are displayed separately and the fields created from
external metadata document viewer are displayed inside the External Metadata Fields tab.
Note: When you reload the policy that has external metadata configured, you will be
redirected to the Settings panel where the external metadata file must be uploaded. If the
policy does not contain external metadata, then the rule editor works as usual.
3. Click Next to view the extracted metadata.
Defining a pattern to extract data
Use a line or a value in a field to create a template for data extraction. Mobius extracts all values from the field that
matches the pattern that is defined in the template.
1. Create or edit a policy.
2. Select fields for archiving data.
3. In the Rules Editor, expand the group of fields that belong to the same rule level.
In the right pane, a blue border appears to highlight the group of fields.
4. At the upper right corner of the highlighted area, click the ellipses icon and then select Define line template.
5. Optional: To learn more about the line template editor, click Give me a walk through.
6. To define the line template, click Skip.
In the Define Line Template area, the first row contains the data selected by default to define the template.
Use the empty row to create a pattern for extraction.
You can move the Define Line Template area in the right pane.
7. To select a value for defining the template, click the value or row in the highlighted area.
8. In the Define Line Template area, in the empty row, click the location where you want to create a pattern for
extraction.
9. Select one of the following options to extract a value of specific data type:
◦ a - alphabetic
◦ d - digit
◦ b - blank
◦ strike-through b - non blank
◦ # - digit or blank
◦ l - literal
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 142


## Page 143


For example, to extract only 3 digit values from a field, create a pattern bddd  in the empty row.
10. In the Define Line Template area, click Apply.
In the right pane, in the sample document, all the selected values are highlighted with the pink border.
11. Save the policy to save selection of fields and line templates.
Exporting and importing archiving policies
Export multiple policies at a time and import the exported policies to other document servers. However, you can
import only one policy at a time.
Exporting Archiving Policies
To export archiving policies you can either,
• Hover over a policy you want to export and click
 .
Or
• Select the archiving policies that you want to export and click the Export icon.
Note: You can specify the download location and enable the Ask where to save each file before
downloading option in the browser settings.
Import archiving policies
To import archiving policies, use the Import icon to browse and select the required policy file. Then, provide a name
for the policy and click Ok.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 143


## Page 144


Note: The supported file formats are JSON and PLC.
Additional settings for data extraction, processing, and display
Use the additional settings in an archiving policy to extract data that Mobius cannot extract based on existing rules
and fields selection. The settings are useful for handing exceptions in a document or additional processing of
extracted data.
To access the additional settings, click Settings in the Define Field Groups tab of the Archiving Policies page.
The following table contains a list of additional settings that you can configure for selected fields:
Table: Additional settings for data extraction
Option Description
General
Decimal Separator Select a symbol for the decimal separator in the
extracted data.
When a two digit year is entered, interpret it as a
year between:
Specify the range of years for extracting year values in
two digits. Mobius adds two more digits in the year
value so that the year falls in the given range.
For example, a selected field contains a date value, 05/
10/99. Mobius interprets it as October, 05, 1999.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 144


## Page 145


Option Description
The format of the date value in the extracted data
depends on the advanced settings in the rules editor.
Default Date Order
Specify the default order of day, month, and year in a
date value.
Mobius uses it interpret date values for data extraction.
Enable calculated fields
Create fields that are functions of data extracted from
the selected fields. For example, convert a date in the
Julian format to year-date-month format.
Section Scope
Combine all the pages in the section as one page for
the policy
Extract values from a field when they are spread over
multiple pages and the field name is not available in
each page.
For example, you have bank account number on first
page of bank statement and you have total for the bank
statement on the last page. To extract account number
and total as single row, you can process the entire bank
statement as a single logical page and extract the values
from these fields as located on single page.
Enhanced field level joining
Instead of using a rule type and hierarchy to append
values, specify the location of the field that contains the
values to append.
Append values extracted from two fields at the same
level of the rule hierarchy.
For example, append all values from different header
fields in a single row.
Extract empty value when data does not match the
field definition
Select this option to handle exceptions in the data
extraction for all rules in the policy.
If any values in the selected fields does not match the
criteria specified in the basic and advanced settings in
the rule editor, Mobius extracts empty value.
Location Scope
Location has pageset scope Defines a field as a pageset index even if the field
appears only on one page of the pageset.
Related information
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 145


## Page 146


Predefined Mobius user roles and permissions
Using calculated fields in policies
Archiving and extraction policies support calculated fields. The calculated field option allows you to add more fields
based on the calculations from other fields. You can use functions and arithmetic operators to define the formula.
Before you begin: Depending on the type of policy, select the Enable calculated fields check box in the
following locations in Mobius View:
• On the Settings tab under the Extract > My Extraction Policies panel. If no rules are created, this tab is
disabled for extraction policies.
• In the Archiving Policy panel under the Define Field Groups tab, click the Settings icon, then go to the
General section.
The Calculated field option is enabled only when at least one rule has been created.
1. Click the Rules tab for extraction policy or click the Create Rules tab for archiving policy.
2. Expand the Calculated Fields drop-down list and select a calculated field.
3. Click Add Field to add a new calculated field.
4. Enter the name of the calculated field in the Rename to text box.
5. Select a data type from the Data is extracted if the field is of type drop-down list.
6. Click Enter formula to select and preview Fields, Functions, and Operators from their respective lists.
You can also use advanced filters to add additional operators, functions used for strings and dates
manipulations, and formulas. The selected formula is displayed in the Formula text box.
Notes:
◦ Mobius repositories 12.2 version supports Capture for calculated fields in the archiving
policy.
◦ Calculated fields can have multiple values based on the list and look up formulas. The
multiple values are displayed in multiple rows. The field values are duplicated for the fields
extracted from the report. If there are multiple calculated fields, the calculated field with
the maximum field values is used.
7. Click Save Policy As.
Related information
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 146


## Page 147


Advanced filters
Operators used in expressions
Functions sorted by functionality
Generating multiple rows using lists in archiving and extraction policies
Configuring text extraction
The text extraction mode allows you to determine how text is extracted during archiving.
The text extraction modes are displayed for AFP, PCL, PDF, PS, TXT, and XRX report types. You can select the text
extraction mode from the Settings pane of the Archiving Policy page.
Note: If the rules already exist in the document, selecting the text extraction mode clears all the existing
rules.
• Text only mode omits font and character mapping information for LPFD documents and does not convert text
pages to graphical representation for text documents.
Note: The rule area with fixed position in page is not supported in Text only mode. While
creating the rules with Text only mode, a default rule is created with the entire page and the
fields can be added using the add field option.
• Text with layout is the default mode and it considers font and character mapping information for LPFD
documents.
The Preserve blank text rows checkbox is selected by default for the Text only mode. This preserves the
blank lines from the document during text extraction, and it can be enabled for both modes. In some cases, it
appears as though there are one or multiple empty rows for PDF, AFP, and LPFD type reports when the space
between rows is larger, but they are not blank rows.
Roles and permissions for policies
The supported roles and permissions for different policies are listed below, indicated by x.

Policy
Publish/
Unpublish

and

## Page 148


Generating multiple rows using lists in archiving and extraction policies
Calculated field functions create lists, which in turn creates multiple rows in the archiving or extraction policies in
combination with other fields.
When a calculated field returns a list, it creates multiple rows for the same set of input data in the extracted results.
Functions that supports lists are explained below.
Supporting functions
Functions allow you to build complex advanced filters using expressions, for data extraction. The below table
describes the functions that are supported.
Note:
• Boolean values are shown as "1" for true and "0" for false.
• Lists are shown as (x, x, x).
Function Description
abs(N)
Returns the absolute value of N.
Example:
Parameter Call Result
-15 abs(-15) 15
asc(S)
Returns the ASCII code of the first character of string S.
Example:
Parameter Call Result
"check" asc("check") 99
ceiling(N1,N2)
Returns smallest multiple of N2 greater or equal to N1.
Syntax: ceiling(N1[,N2])
N1: value to round
N2: optional. Default value: 1
Examples:
Parameters Call Result
262.16 ceiling(262.16) 263
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 148


## Page 149


Function Description
Parameters Call Result
262.16 and 5 ceiling(262.16,5) 265
chartoseconds(S)
Scans the string S as a date/time value and returns the number of seconds
since the start of the day.
Example:
Parameter Call Result
"2:10:45AM" CharToSeconds("2:10:45AM") 7845
chr(N)
Returns a character equivalent to the ASCII code N.
Syntax: chr(N)
Parameter: N, for ASCII code number.
Example:
Parameter Call Result
36 chr(36) $
concat(list1, list2 ...)
Return a list that contains elements of all provided lists in order.
Syntax: concat(list1, list2 ...)
Parameter: One or more lists.
Example:
Parameter Call Result
list(“a”, ”b”)
list(“1”, ”2”, ”3”)
concat(list("a", "b"),
list("1", "2", "3"))
a
b
1
2
3
CRC(S)
Returns the 32-bit CRC of the string S.
Syntax: CRC(S)
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 149


## Page 150


Function Description
Parameter: S, for string
Example:
Parameter Call Result
"Report" CRC("Report") C38372B2
ctod(string[,date format[,extraction
format]])
Scans the string as a date/time value and returns the date. date format is an
optional parameter that indicates the date format, "m/d/y", "d/m/y" or "y/m/
d". extraction format is an optional parameter that indicates the date/time
extraction pattern. "D" extracts a date, "DT" extracts a date followed by a
time, "T" extracts a time and "TD" extracts a time followed by a date. More
than one extraction pattern may be combined to form a list of extraction
patterns. For example: "D,DT" means extract a date OR a date followed by a
time.
Example:
Parameter Call Result
"03/08/2015" and "m/d/
y"
ctod("03/08/2015","m/
d/y") 20150308
date([date])
Returns date as a date, where date can be a date/time string or a numeric

Parameter: Date: optional date/time.

Field D contains "4/1/
2015 09:05:17" date([D]) 20150401

## Page 159


Function Description
Syntax: lookup(key)
Parameter: key
Example: Upload an external XML file that contains <Mobius_Lookup> and
multiple <entry> with key attribute and multiple values. When you perform

found. If the key is not found, it will return an empty list.
lookup(key, default)
Returns a list of 1 or more entries from a lookup table if the key is found in
the table.
Returns a list with a single entry equal to the default value if the key is not
found in the table.
Note: This function is applicable only for archiving policies.
Syntax: lookup(key, default)
Parameter: key and default
Example: Upload an external XML file that contains <Mobius_Lookup> and
multiple <entry> with key attribute and multiple values. When you perform
lookup(key, default), it returns a list of values defined in the XML file if the key
is found. If the key is not found, it will return a list with a single value equal to
the default value.

## Page 173


Archive content
Mobius can process content created by any application in different environments. Any type of content can be
archived and stored on any platform. Mobius archives are immediately available for viewing, and print and email
distribution.
The following diagram shows the position of this task within the workflow of common administrative tasks. The
colored box indicates the starting point, while the dashed outline box represents an optional task.

classes, indexes

users, permissions

archiving

data (me
Types of archiving
Mobius provides three options to archive your content.
Policy archiving
If Mobius can read the content, you can create a policy that defines the rules for identifying the content class
and index entries. In policy archiving, the document gets paginated.
When policy-based processing is used, you must indicate the policy that identifies this information. Policy
archiving is useful for AFP, PDF, text, and XML files.
Non-policy paginated archiving
You can archive in paginated mode without policy processing of content along with the ability to specify page-
scope index values.
List file archiving
If Mobius cannot read the content, a policy cannot be applied. In such cases, content is archived through list file
processing; that is, the input file lists multiple pieces of the content to be archived. The list file includes the path
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 173


## Page 174


to each file, the content class ID for all pieces of content referenced in the file, and the index entries for each.
Related information
Creating archiving policies
Non-policy paginated archiving
List file archiving
Policy archiving
Use policies to extract data, and capture and archive metadata.
A policy is a set of field-specific criteria that locates and extracts data, and metadata from content. The extracted
information is used to create archive index entries and to determine whether to archive content in a new or existing
archive. Policies are used to create archive index for the following reasons:
• Define content class ID, ingestion ID, content section ID, and indexes for the archived content.
• Verify the accuracy of extraction criteria – Preview the values that will become archive index entries.
• Use one policy to create different types of index entries for multiple pieces of content, even when the pieces of
content do not have similar formats.
• Extract and create index entries for data in laser-printed formatted documents (LPFDs).
• Extract and create index entries from metadata and archive the metadata.
• Replace the content of a field with a value that you select and convert data and standardizing index entries.
Defining index entries
Index entries can be created for the entire content or a set of content, by defining areas on a content page that exist
on one or more pages or in one or more content. Every time archive creation identifies one of these areas, it creates
an index entry that allows you to locate the information.
For example, the content may comprise a series of sales content where, department names appear in the same
position of each content page. Archive creation identifies the department names as index entries. When the archive is
created, content pages that contain specific department names can be accessed.
Index entries can also contain extracted fields of data and combinations of fields called groups. The fields can contain
content class IDs, content section IDs, indexes, department names, and so on. Regions help locate field positions.
Page and region contents can be customized and data can be extracted by creating and implementing an exit
routine.
Creating index entries includes the following:
• Opening sample content – Content of a specific type that has data in the same format as other content of that
type can be used as a sample. This sample content helps to locate the index items.
• Defining index entry fields – Defining index entry fields depends on the type of sample content you have
opened. Optionally, you can define index entry fields from the metadata of an external content. This topic
includes directions for defining index entry fields from: text content, LPFDs, scanned images, XML pages, and
external metadata.
• Determining if you can use an index entry field to define a pageset – A pageset is a contiguous set of pages
within a content section whose content is related. A pageset has a common index entry (or Index) that logically
links the pages together. Breaking document archive files into pagesets can improve performance by reducing
retrieval time. If you determine that the content contains pagesets, you can use Mobius Administrator to define
which Index to use as the index.
• Combining fields to create the index entries.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 174


## Page 175


Creating archiving policies
Create archiving policies and, define rules and field groups.
1. Log in to Mobius Server Administrator.
For example:
(https://your_host:your_port/mobius/vdradmin/ ).
2. Select a content source from Mobius Content Sources, and perform the following tasks.
a. Select a document server to view and create policies.
b. Click Archiving Policies.
The Archiving Policies pane is displayed with a SAMPLE_POLICY, which is available by default.
3. Click Create Policy.
4. Select a document to upload, and click Next.
5. Click Page Settings and select a data type for the uploaded document. Depending on the data type that you
select, different fields are displayed.
6. Click Add Rule to add rules, and then drag the cursor to select the page area or field to add to the rule.
Note: You can select either a part of the page or the entire page. On selection, the left pane
opens with the properties for the respective field.
a. Define the properties for the selected area.
b. Click Add Field to add additional fields to the rule, and then select the field.
Click the Fields drop down or Add Field to rename and define the extraction criteria.
c. Select the rule level that defines your rule hierarchy.
Header, Details, and Footers are available for both Rule level and Field level. Rule level change affects the
whole set of fields related to the rule. Field level change affects only the individual field.
Select a Header option to define the top level of the column and select a Footer option to append footer
fields with fields from detailed transactions.
d. Use the following options to increase efficiency of the field selection process:
Suggest Rules
Automatically select fields for data extraction.
Clear All Rules
Restart the process of field selection.
Show Labels
Display field names and rule names the right pane. These labels are useful to associate the fields
and rules in the left pane with the data in the sample document.
Preview
Generate a preview to check the extracted data.
You can update the field selection and rule definitions based on the preview of extracted data. You
can also customize the preview. For more information, see Preview of the extracted data.
e. In the Make Selection area, select the data for archiving.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 175


## Page 176


▪ Use entire page - Select to makes the entire page the selection area.
▪ Use partial page - Select the boundaries of the selection area.
▪ Relative to anchor points - By defining the top and bottom anchor positions in every page, the user
can ensure the selection area is in a position that is relative to the anchor points.
The top anchor defines the upper left corner of the rule area. The bottom anchor defines the
bottom right corner of the rule area. Specifying the anchor occurrence to All for either the top or
bottom anchor applies to both anchors. The user can define the rule anchor by selecting some
text on the page or defining a field:
▪ Specify text- User can either type in the text to be used as anchor, or drag the yellow anchor
box to another location in the document to locate the text in the right panel.
▪ Select field- The Select field option for an anchor is enabled only when more than one rule is
created. The selected field must be outside the rule containing the anchor. To use the Select
field option :
▪ Add more than one rule.
▪ Select the Relative to anchor points and define the top or bottom anchor from any
one of the rules created.
▪ Click the Select field radio button and select a field from the drop down.
▪ Fixed position- This is the default option. Selecting this ensures that the selection area
remains constant across the pages of the content class.
▪ Always expand selection area to the full width of the page- Extends the selection area to fit
the width of the page.
▪ Always expand selection area to the bottom of the page- Extends the selection area to the
bottom of the page.
f. Click Next to view the field group pane.
7. Select the rule area in the document and click the ellipses to set the text alignment to left, right, center, or
justified.
◦ Collapse Horizontal- Aligns the text to the column width.
◦ Expand Horizontal- Allows you to increase the width of the column.
◦ Collapse Vertical- Allows you to apply a fixed row height.
◦ Expand Vertical- Allows you to increase the height of the row.
◦ Discard Current Changes- Resets all the column widths and row heights to default before closing the text
alignment dialog.
8. Click Add Field Group to define fields as a group and select the required index from the indexes list defined in
the document server.
9. Select the fields and the order in which they are combined to create the index value.
After you select the fields, the right pane displays the index value on each of the page of the content.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 176


## Page 177


10. Click Settings, and then define the required properties for the policy.
11. Click Apply.
Related information
Defining field groups
Non-policy paginated archiving
Perform paginated archiving without policies using Meta-JSON.
The paginate property in Meta-JSON allows you to archive content without using policies. This enables faster retrieval,
optimal performance and content indexing using page ranges.
Related information
Meta-JSON archiving
List file archiving
For content such as images, if Mobius cannot read the content, rules from a policy are not applied. In this case, list
file processing is used to specify the content class ID and indexing information.
A list file references multiple pieces of content and allows to specify the content class ID used for all pieces of content
in the list, along with the indexing used for each content.
A list file includes the following:
• Any type of image file. Refers to any images that are scanned with third-party software, uses policy-based
processing and a configured configuration file.
• Associated data files - This includes data files associated with Windows applications such as Microsoft Excel
spreadsheets, Microsoft Word files, and HTML (used with web browsers).
• Transparent data - This includes data files that are non-viewable by the client. This is typically raw data that is
meaningful only to a specific application, for example, data created by the Mobius z/OS API.
• Unicode data files - This includes associated data files that can be in any language. Unicode files can only be
indexed for full-text search.
• Data files for Optical Character Recognition (OCR) - This includes digital images (PNG, JPEG, BMP, TIFF, and JPG)
and PDF files with scanned content.
Image file types
The image file types displayed by the viewing clients are given in the following table. If any other type of image is
archived, you must view it with an external viewer.
File
extension File type
BMP All uncompressed bitmap graphics.
CAL CALS Raster (Type 1, 2, 3, and 4). Typically CCITT type compressed bi-tonal images.
DIB Device Independent Bitmaps.
FPX Kodak FlashPix. A common image file format.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 177


## Page 178


File
extension File type
GIF Graphics Interchange Format.
ICA
IOCA images with these compression types:
• Uncompressed.
• ABIC and Concatenated-ABIC (grayscale).
• ABIC compressed HPTS check and deposit slip image pieces of content, single or multi-page, in
both monochrome and 8-bit grayscale.
• CCITT Group 3 1D, Group 3 2D, and Group 4.
• JPEG.
IMG All GEM bitmap graphics.
JBG JBIG. A lossless compressed file format for bi-tonal (black and white) grayscale and color images.
JPG All JPEG files that comply to the JFIF standard.
PCD Kodak PhotoCD. A common high-resolution format for images on CD-ROM.
PCX/DCX Monochrome, 16 color, 256 color, and 24-bit color PC Paintbrush bitmap graphics.
PNG Standard and interlaced Portable Network Graphics bitmap graphics.
PSD Photoshop 3.0. The format produced by the Adobe Photoshop graphics editor.
RAS Sun Raster format. Native to Sun Linux platforms.
TGA 8-bit, 16-bit, 24-bit, and 32-bit TARGA bitmap graphics, including both versions 1 and 2 of the file
format.
TIF
All of these compression types and colors, including multi-page TIFF files:
• Uncompressed
• Packbits
• Modified Huffman Encoding
• CCITT Group 3 1D, Group 3 2D, and Group 4
• Monochrome
• LZW
• 16 color
• 256 color
• 24-bit color
ZIP All compressed data files including all types of image files.
Keywords for list files
To configure list files, you must use keywords. The following rules apply for the usage of keywords in the list files:
• Keywords must be entered in uppercase only.
• Each keyword is followed by a value.
• Use a comma to separate multiple keywords on the same line.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 178


## Page 179


• Keyword values cannot contain embedded null characters.
• If a keyword value includes a comma, enclose the keyword and the value in double quotation marks. For
example, TOPIC-ID=NAME, "TOPIC-ITEM=Smith, Jane"
• If the value being quoted contains any double quotation marks, they must be doubled up. For example, the
string, This is an "easy" example must be written as: TOPIC-ID=DESCRIP, "TOPIC-ITEM=This is an
""easy"" example".
Note: To use the same list file to perform archiving on Mobius z/OS, you must align the keywords in the
file.
Keyword Value
REPORT-ID (required) Content class.
POLICIES (optional) Applies only to content classes that are configured for OCR processing. Specify one or more
policies to apply during OCR processing to extracted indexes.
VERSION
(optional) Overrides using the current date and time for the ingestion, specify a specific ingestion date
and time in YYYYMMDDhhmmss format .
VERSION must follow the REPORT-ID keyword on the same line.
FILE (required) Specifies the path and file name of one or more files to archive. The list can contain
different file types.
TYPE
(required) Specifies the file extension that identifies the type of image or Windows application that is
associated with the data file.
If the file contains raw data that Mobius View cannot display, use the .TPG (transparent data) file
extension.
If an application on the user’s computer is associated with a file type in the archive, Mobius View
automatically invokes the application when it retrieves the page.
SECTION
(required) Specify one or more images or data files in a content section. If you specify multiple images
or data files, they are displayed in the content in the order they appear in the list file.
The content section ID cannot exceed 30 characters.
ENCODING
(optional) For TYPE=txt files, the text encoding is in uppercase. If ENCODING is not specified, the
default encoding of the Mobius Server host is used. Values include:
• UTF-8
• UTF-16LE
• UTF-16BE
LOCATION-
TOPIC-IDS
(optional) Allows users to filter their search results to information that is displayed within a single
entity of the image file. This keyword must be on the line directly after the REPORT-ID keyword. Enter
a value for each index that is part of a single entity. Separate each value by a comma and a space.
Note: If you enter a value for one index, you must enter values for all indexes.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 179


## Page 180


Keyword Value
TOPIC-ID (optional) Specifies the name of an index that is associated with the image or data file. To specify
multiple index names, enter each TOPIC-ID on a new line.
TOPIC-
ITEM
(Required if TOPIC-ID is specified) Specifies the index associated with this page. Set the TOPIC-ITEM
keyword on the same line as the TOPIC-ID keyword.
To index a file with multiple indexes, use a separate line in the file for each TOPIC-ITEM keyword.
Note: For policy-based archiving, changing the index maximum allowed length to 255
characters might alter how existing policies extract index values. Apply a new maximum
allowed length to a new installation with new contents, or validate each policy before
using it.
TOPIC-
SCOPE
(optional) Set TOPIC-SCOPE to PAGESET. A pageset is a contiguous set of pages within a content
section whose content is related. A pageset has a common index entry (or index) that logically links
the pages together.
Breaking document archive files into pagesets reduces retrieval time. If the content contains pagesets,
define the index to use.
Notes:
• To use TOPIC-SCOPE, the LOCATION-TOPIC-IDS keyword is required.
• Set TOPIC-SCOPE directly after the LOCATION-TOPIC-IDS line.
Related information
Archiving from third-party applications
Archiving Unicode documents through list file processing
Optical Character Recognition (OCR) with list file archiving
Archiving a list file
Use keywords to specify the files to archive. When you archive a list file, indexes are created which are a combination
of index names and index values.
A sample list file is given below:
REPORT-ID=IMAGES, VERSION=20060915182536
LOCATION-TOPIC-IDS=DATE, AMOUNT
TOPIC-SCOPE=PAGESET
FILE=C:/CHECKS/2004.JPG, TYPE=JPG, SECTION=CHECK
FILE=C:/CHECKS/2005.JPG, TYPE=JPG, SECTION=CHECK
                TOPIC-ID=DATE,  TOPIC-ITEM=02/11/2005
                TOPIC-ID=AMOUNT, TOPIC-ITEM=100
                TOPIC-ID=DATE,  TOPIC-ITEM=02/12/2005
                TOPIC-ID=AMOUNT, TOPIC-ITEM=200
FILE=C:/CHECKS/2006.JPG, TYPE=JPG, SECTION=CHECK
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 180


## Page 181


TOPIC-ID=DATE,  TOPIC-ITEM=03/11/2006
                TOPIC-ID=AMOUNT, TOPIC-ITEM=100
                TOPIC-ID=DATE,  TOPIC-ITEM=03/12/2006
                TOPIC-ID=AMOUNT, TOPIC-ITEM=200
FILE=C:/CHECKS/2007.JPG, TYPE=JPG, SECTION=CHECK
                TOPIC-ID=DATE,  TOPIC-ITEM=04/11/2007
                TOPIC-ID=AMOUNT, TOPIC-ITEM=100
                TOPIC-ID=DATE,  TOPIC-ITEM=04/12/2007
                TOPIC-ID=AMOUNT, TOPIC-ITEM=200
1. Create a list file that specifies the file to archive.
2. Enter the following keywords.
a. Enter the keyword REPORT-ID= in the first line of the file, and then enter a value that names the content.
For example: REPORT-ID=IMAGES Mobius, generates the ingestion from the current date and time in
timestamp format.
b. Enter the keyword VERSION= to designate a specific ingestion directly after the REPORT-ID keyword. The
value for the VERSION keyword must be formatted as YYYYMMDDhhmmss.
Note: The REPORT-ID value is a content class ID defined in the Mobius Server database.
For example: REPORT-ID=IMAGES, VERSION=20010915182536
c. Enter the three keywords, FILE=, TYPE=, and SECTION= in the next line and their respective values
separated by commas.
For example:
FILE=C:/DDR/IMAGES/CARDSTMT.TIF, TYPE=TIF, SECTION=STATEMENT
Keyword formatting is important here.
3. Provide indexing information on the subsequent lines, if indexes must be provided for the file specified.
The indexing information consists of a line in the list with keywords and their values:
◦ TOPIC-ID: An index associated with an image or desktop application file. Multiple indexes can be
specified for each referenced file.
◦ TOPIC-ITEM: The specific index entry for that index.
The following example shows the index entry for the date: 04/11/2023. Here,
TOPIC-ID=DATE,  TOPIC-ITEM=04/11/2023
a. Enter one line for each index that you want to specify.
b. Provide two keywords and their values for each line. If the value of the TOPIC-ITEM keyword contains a
comma, enclose the keyword and value in double quotation marks.
The following example shows three indexes for one file:
FILE=C:/CHECKS/2006.JPG, TYPE=JPG,SECTION=FEB
        TOPIC-ID=DATE,    TOPIC-ITEM=04/11/2007
        TOPIC-ID=PAYEE,    TOPIC-ITEM=JBROWN
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 181


## Page 182


TOPIC-ID=AMOUNT,   “TOPIC-ITEM=-$2,567”
4. Optional: Set up location indexing for the indexes, which allows you to filter the search results for information
in a specific location of a file.
For example, if a file includes multiple checks, you can search for information that appears within one check.
Or, if a file includes multiple statements, you can search for information that appears within one statement.
a. Add a line immediately after the REPORT-ID line and enter the keyword LOCATION-TOPIC-IDS= followed
by the name of each index separated with a comma and space.
For example:
REPORT-ID=BILLS, LOCATION-TOPIC-IDS=DATE, TIME, PLACE
b. Ensure that a complete set of index lines is available for each entity that needs to be indexed.
A set includes an index line (TOPIC-ID and TOPIC-ITEM keyword and value) for each index listed in the
LOCATION-TOPIC-IDS keyword. If the LOCATION-TOPIC-IDS keyword contains three indexes such as
DATE, TIME, and PLACE, enter a set of three index lines for each entity that needs to be indexed within
the file, one for DATE, one for TIME, and one for PLACE, as shown in the following example:
REPORT-ID=BILLS,
LOCATION-TOPIC-IDS=DATE, TIME, PLACE
FILE=c:/BILLS/QTR1.JPG, TYPE=JPG, SECTION=ABC
      TOPIC-ID=DATE,  TOPIC-ITEM=JAN
      TOPIC-ID=TIME,  TOPIC-ITEM=1200
      TOPIC-ID=PLACE, TOPIC-ITEM=NY
      TOPIC-ID=DATE,  TOPIC-ITEM=FEB
      TOPIC-ID=TIME,  TOPIC-ITEM=1200
      TOPIC-ID=PLACE, TOPIC-ITEM=NY
      TOPIC-ID=DATE,  TOPIC-ITEM=MAR
      TOPIC-ID=TIME,  TOPIC-ITEM=1200
      TOPIC-ID=PLACE, TOPIC-ITEM=NY
c. Set the scope to pageset by adding TOPIC-SCOPE=PAGESET in the line, after the LOCATION-TOPIC-IDS=
keyword , separated by commas if the location indexing entries apply to the entire content section.
For example:
LOCATION-TOPIC-IDS=DATE, TIME, PLACE
TOPIC-SCOPE=PAGESET
5. Specify the file encoding for text files using the ENCODING= keyword as shown in the following example.
Example:
FILE=C:/ASG/REPORTS/CARDSTMT.TXT, TYPE=TXT, SECTION=STATEMENT, ENCODING=UTF-8
Note:
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 182


## Page 183


◦ The supported encoding values are UTF-8, UTF-16LE, and UTF-16BE. The encoding value
must be in uppercase.
◦ For a text file, you can specify the character encoding. If you do not specify the encoding,
the default encoding of the operating system is used.
6. Create a content class in Mobius Administrator.
The name of the content class must match the value that you specified for the REPORT-ID keyword in the list
file.
7. Use the acreate command to create the archive.
Related information
List file archiving
Location indexing example
acreate command - Creating archives
Archiving Unicode documents through list file processing
Use the acreate command line utility to archive Unicode documents using list file processing.
Before you begin: Configure the document server for full-text search before archiving Unicode data.
Archive creation list file processing and full-text search with indexing configured is used to specify the Content Class
ID and indexing information.
1. Create a list file that identifies each Unicode file to be archived, the file type, and indexing information.
2. Define a content class in Mobius Administrator, through the content classes interface of the selected document
server.
Note: The Content Class ID must match the Content Class ID in the list file.
3. Run the following acreate command from the command prompt.
acreate -s MyServer -F ship.lst receive*.lst -M i
You must specify the following values:
◦ Name of the document server.
◦ Full path of the file you want to archive. You can use the wildcard characters * and ? to specify multiple
pieces of content.
◦ Specify -M i, where i refers to list file processing for images, associated data, and transparent data.
4. Press Enter to start archive creation.
Related information
Configuring full-text search
List file example
acreate command - Creating archives
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 183


## Page 184


Optical Character Recognition (OCR) with list file archiving
Mobius supports Optical Character Recognition (OCR) during list file archiving. You can use this processing for
content that contain images.
Using regular archiving methods, Mobius cannot read scanned content and images of the content. When Mobius
cannot read the content, policies are not applied, and the text is not indexed. In this case, OCR archiving specifies
how Mobius reads the images.
Mobius uses embedded OCR software to identify typed, handwritten, and printed text in digital images and PDF files
that contain scanned content. The OCR text is the converted into usable text that can be added to digital archives as
metadata associated with the image it was scanned from using predefined policies. The OCR text can also be used for
full-text indexing if configured. OCR archiving allows the extracted text to be searched either using an index-based
search or a full-text search from Mobius View.
To configure archiving through OCR, you must configure the OCR feature in Mobius Administrator by selecting the
Perform OCR processing for this report option in the Archiving section of a content class definition.
The OCR feature can be used for index item extraction, such as indexes and full-text indexing. Both extraction
methods can be used, based on the business needs.
Related information
Creating content class
Meta-JSON archiving
Use a single meta file to archive multiple documents in one instance, through the ArchiveWrite REST API. This
supports archiving in paginated mode, without policy processing of document content, along with the ability to
specify page-scope index values.
Meta-JSON archiving allows you to:
• Support both non-policy and policy archiving using the same API.
• Generate paginated archives through non-policy archiving.
• Paginate with external indexes.
• Support multiple index combination modes, by combining the indexes listed in JSON with the information
generated in policy archiving.
These capabilities provide various archiving options, including EOS migration.
Archiving using JSON
Archive document(s) by specifying the details through JSON definition.
To perform meta-JSON archiving, you must provide the archiving details in JSON format, as shown in the below
schema.
openapi: "3.0.3"
info:
  title: "Meta archiving JSON"
  version: "1.0"
servers: []
paths: {}
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 184

## Page 187


pages:
                            type: string
The properties in the above schema are described in the below table.
Property Type Description
documents object
array List of input document descriptions and their archiving information.
documentInfo object Document file description and information for parsing its content.
fileInfo object Document file information.
location string Document location which is a URI pathname. Used by acreate to read the
document; ignored in REST API since the document content is passed inline.
fileType string
Document type, typically its file extension. It is usually the same as
parsingInfo.format but not always. For example, file type ABC associated with a
custom application may be a plain text document for parsing purposes. If
omitted, parsingInfo.format should be used for archiving.
originalFilename string The full path name of the document file. It is used for internal metadata fields in
policy archiving and as a document name for policy selection rules.
originalFileTimestamp ISO-8601
string
Timestamp of the document file. It is used for internal metadata fields in policy
archiving.
parsingInfo object Document format and content parsing information.
format enum Document format. It supports: AFP, PCL, PS, PDF, Text.
encoding string Document data encoding for text documents.
pageBreak object Page delineating information for text documents.
type enum Page delineation type which includes: Formfeed, AnsiCC, MachineCC,
FixedLength, startMarkers, endMarkers.
fixedLength integer Length of the rows or lines.
markers string
array Marker list for startMarkers and endMarkers.
lineBreak object Line delineating information for text documents.
type enum Line delineation type which includes: CRLF, LF, FixedLength, RDW.
formDefinition string Form name for AFP documents with external resources.
resourcesLocation string URI of external resource location for AFP document archiving, accessible to
Mobius Server.
archivingInfo object Information on how to archive the document.
documentClassId string Document/content class id. This is required when the content class is not
specified by an archiving policy.
ingestionInfo object Ingestion information.
timestamp ISO-8601
string Ingestion timestamp.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 187


## Page 188


Property Type Description
paginate boolean Intimation to paginate the document in the archive. Pagination allows additional
options such as assignment of page and line-level indexes.
policyInfo object Archiving policy information.
policySelectionMode enum
Method in which the policy must be selected, to indicate the type of archiving. It
is an enumeration field with values such as UseCandidatePolicies (to use the list
of candidate policies), UseAllPolicies (to use all the policies) and
UsePolicySelectionRules (to use preset policy selection rules instead of the
provided candidate policies).
candidatePolicies string
array
List of candidate policies. This is ignored with policy selection modes other than
UseCandidatePolicies.
indexes object
array List of index values.
entries object
array List of name/value pairs
name string Index name. Index names that begin with two underscores are reserved. Index
__SECTION is used to pass section information.
value string Index value.
scope object
array Index value scope.
pages string Page range. Supported formats: x (page x), x-y (pages x-y), x- (all pages from x to
the end)
The following example shows some properties defined in JSON format for archiving.
{
  "documents": [
  {
   "documentInfo": {
     "fileInfo": {
      "location": "c://migration/hr/000001/202101012303040.txt",
      "fileType": "txt",
      "originalFilename": "d://hr//January2021.txt",
      "originalFileTimestamp": "202101012303040"
     },
     "parsingInfo": {
       "format": "Text",
       "encoding": "EBCDIC",
       "pageBreak": {
         "type": "Formfeed"
       },
       "lineBreak": {
         "type": "LF"
       }
      }
     },
     "archivingInfo": {
       "documentClassId": "HRRPT",

"ingestionInfo": {
         "timestamp": "2023-07-13T09:04:11"
       },
       "paginate": true,
       "policyInfo": {
          "policySelectionMode": "UseCandidatePolicies",
          "candidatePolicies": [
              "SAMPLE_POLICY"
          ]

## Page 190


Archive clients
Perform various types of archiving using different clients such as REST API, Mobius View Capture and Mobius Remote
CLI and archive printers.
MobiusRemoteCLI acreate
The acreate command in Mobius Remote CLI is used to create content archives. The archive content details are
passed through parameters to perform policy, list file and meta-JSON archiving.
Mobius View Capture
Mobius View Capture provides immediate archiving by ingesting data and metadata into the Mobius system. It
takes any type of document and archives it in native format with index values provided by the user.
Policy and list file archiving use Capture to archive content.
ArchiveWrite REST API
ArchiveWrite API allows for meta-JSON, non-meta-JSON policy and list archiving. Meta-JSON archiving is
performed by correlating the information in the JSON file with that of the document data passed to the API.
ArchiveWrite API provides the ability to identify pages for index assigning without extracting indexes through
policy processing. This is achieved by passing a flag to the API informing it to paginate the documents without
using policies.
Archive printer
Capture content, send it to the print spool, and archive it on Mobius Server by selecting a printer that is set up
as an archive printer.
Related information
acreate command - Creating archives
Creating immediate archiving (Capture) targets
Print spool archiving
Print spool archiving
Archive content from systems such as z/OS, Windows, and Apple iOS by sending the content as a print job to an
archive printer on Windows.
On Windows, print spool archiving is performed through the Mobius spool interface for Windows. On Linux, a similar
operation is performed when the Mobius Server is on a Linux system.
Print spool archiving allows you to archive content in two ways:
• Archiving content through the Mobius Spool Archiving System, by sending it as a print job to an archive printer.
• Saving content to a file or sending it to a printer. You can then use the acreate command to archive the
content.
Archiving content through Mobius spool interface for Windows
This method is used when the Mobius Server is on a Windows system.
Mobius Spool Archive Server is essentially viewed as a Windows or LPD printer. Any print client software or LPR client
can send content to the Mobius Spool Archive Server by printing to a virtual printer. The virtual printer is defined on
Windows using the Windows printer setup procedure.
The Mobius Spool Archiving System consists of a port monitor and Mobius Spool Archive Server as shown in the
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 190


## Page 191


following image.
• The port monitor establishes a connection with Mobius Spool Archive Server and sends the job to be archived
to it.
• Mobius Spool Archive Server manages the archive printers. Each archive printer acts as a logical printer and
communicates with a document server. The archive printer can be added, deleted, and configured through
policies. These policies are applied to the content and sent to the archive printer.
• When you send a print job to the archive printer, the archive printer communicates with the corresponding
document server and archives the print job.
Configuring print spool rules
You must specify candidate policies through the print spool policy selection rules to archive content from a print
spool. A policy selection rule consists of a set of conditions and policies.
Configure the print spool rules as shown below.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 191


## Page 192


Here,
• Rule name - A unique name that identifies the rule.
• Printer name - Linux or Windows printer.
• User name - Name of the user who is logged into the machine.
• Document name - Linux job title or the Windows document name assigned to the print job. This is not the
filename.
• Machine name - Name of the machine that generates the print job request.
• Policy - A candidate policy from the list displayed.
Printing to archives
Print to archives from Windows and non-Windows workstations, using the Print option, and LPR and LPD commands
respectively.
For a Windows workstation:
If the archive printer is defined on the workstation, you can select it from the list of options available on the
application’s Print dialog box. The content is then sent to the archive printer from within any Windows
application.
For a non-Windows workstation:
You can create archives by printing from systems running z/OS and other methods, by specifying the LPR or LPD
commands. To archive a print job using LPR, an LPD (line printer daemon) must be running on a Windows
machine. All z/OS machines support LPR capabilities. The archive printer is defined on these systems as a
regular printer.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 192


## Page 193


For example, on Linux systems, printers are defined in the file /etc/printers.conf. The Linux printer queue
name must be the shared printer name. You must contact the system administrator for specific details of the
local operating system.
To print content to a Microsoft LPD from Linux for archiving, on the Linux command line, run the following
command:
> lpr –P FinancialArchPrinter -l myreport.ps
Note: The -l parameter sends the raw data to the printer and bypasses formatting by the printer
driver. If the printer driver can format the content, you can omit this parameter.
> lpr -P FinancialArchPrinter myreport.txt
Archiving from third-party applications
Mobius can capture, archive, and distribute content and images from SAP.
Enterprise Resource Planning Systems
Administrators use section indexing to determine the content and content sections that Mobius View users can
access. Administrators can export security parameters, such as user credentials, from the ERP program to
Mobius.
SAP
Mobius has an interface to SAP ArchiveLink. For information integrating SAP and Mobius, refer to the "Mobius
SAP Administrator" guide.
Migrating archives
Set up migration rules, migrate document archive files from one storage medium to another, and retain the
migration using Mobius.
Mobius allows you to:
• Define criteria to control the migration of Mobius archives between disk file and other storage systems.
• Maintain multiple copies of archives on different storage media.
• Control deletion of an archive copy after a defined expiration period.
• Control where an archive resides and for how long it must be retained, enabling more dynamic control over
archive storage.
• Migrate archives and content from EOS.
Related information
Migrating content from EOS to Mobius
Setting up migration
Define migration rules and automate migration using commands.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 193


## Page 194


1. Identify the migration criteria.
a. Analyze the content of the archives and determine which archives are to be migrated.
b. Determine when and how often, and the location to which these archives must be migrated.
c. Establish retention criteria for the post-migrated archives.
2. Define migration rules in the Rocket database.
3. Update the Rocket database tables, with criteria to establish migration rules, using the vdrdbxl command.
4. Automate migration by setting up the scheduling system with a script to run the amigrate command daily.
For each content class ID that is specified, amigrate verifies the existence of migration rules and creates a
content migration record for each new ingestion. This record indicates the next action date of the migration
rule.
For the specified content class ID, amigrate determines if it must process any migration actions. If an ingestion
has a next action date that has expired and is not on hold, amigrate performs the migration action. Upon
successfully completing the migration action,amigrate updates the content migration table to indicate the next
migration action and date. Before processing is complete,amigratecontinues to loop through the content
migration table until no actions require further processing.
Related information
vdrdbxml - Update migration rules
amigrate command - Migrating document archive files
Migration retention
Retain the configured migration through ingestion retention or ingestion instance retention.
Ingestion instance retention (as defined in the migration rules) is different from ingestion retention (as defined by the
content definition).
• Ingestion retention is the amount of time that an ingestion exists before adelete deletes it. If an ingestion is
due for deletion, adelete deletes it without considering the migration rules.
• Ingestion instance retention allows for an instance of an ingestion to have a retention value that is
independent of the ingestion retention.
When migrating to a storage system that supports retention, ingestion retention is used only if the ingestion instance
retention is not set in the migration rules. By setting the retention in the migration rules to a shorter period than the
ingestion retention, an ingestion instance can be removed or migrated to another storage system before the
ingestion is deleted. If the ingestion retention is not set, setting the retention in the migration rules forces retention
of the specified instance.
For both types of retention, the period is calculated based on the settings configured in the Advanced Retention
Options dialog box in Mobius Administrator for the content class ID being migrated.
• If the setting is Archive creation time, then the period is calculated from the time of the initial archive
creation.
• If the setting is Report version ID, then the period is calculated from the time specified in the ingestion ID.
Note: If the underlying storage system does not support retention, ingestion instance retention is
ignored.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 194

Set up Mobius features
Mobius features can be configured in the Mobius Administrator application.
