The left-hand pane in Mobius Administrator provides administrator controls for Mobius View.
Authorization code
Enter a Mobius View authorization code:
• Enable access to Mobius View features controlled by licensed options.
• Extend the expiration date of an existing authorization code.
Content Repositories
View the list of existing content repositories. Administrators can create new content repositories for different
repository types.
Enterprise Folders
Enterprise folders are virtual folders that Mobius View presents dynamically by querying servers and
repositories according to rules that are specified in the Enterprise Folders panel.
Application Server
Configure and customize the Mobius View application server features.
Repository Security
Configure options related to end-user access security.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 195


## Page 196


Content Viewing
Manage content viewing options such as browse, search, download, and extract.
Custom Actions
Create and map custom webhooks to predefined Mobius View server events.
Records Management
Create and maintain policies and event templates.
Custom Buttons
Create custom features accessible from Mobius View.
Capture
Archive content directly into Mobius. Add, delete, and edit targets to archive content.
Email
Configure options for Client and Server Settings to send mails using Mobius View.
Distribution Services
Distribute Content Classes to recipients via email using Output Services. Use Distribution Services to create
predefined packets from contents retrieved from Mobius and deliver to intended recipients.
Diagnostics
Configure logging options.
(Federated) Redaction
Set up document redaction for repositories connected to Mobius View.
Authorization
Manage roles, role permissions, and role users/groups mapping.
Mobius Server (Content Sources)
(Optional) Configure Mobius Content Sources and SAP archiving.
Auditing
Configure audit log file and audit events.
Policy Management
Configure and define decision models.
Microsoft Office Integration
Configure Microsoft Office integration with Mobius View.
Policy management
Use the policy management features to control who can access the REST services for Mobius View and parameters
that can be passed to the service. You can also allow users to access specific services depending upon their
geographical location.
A policy consists of a set of decision tables. The set of decision table is also called as decision model. Add rules in a
decision table to control the access of a Mobius View service or a display of an UI element in Mobius View.
The Policy Management section in Mobius Administrator contains the following tools:
• Policies — View and update decision models that are created for your organization. Import or export a decision
table in the decision model and add rules to a decision table.
• Policy Group Administration — Create and update decision models for an organization. Add or remove
decision tables in a decision model.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 196


## Page 197


• Actions — Define calls to specific webservices. These can be Mobius View services or any external webservices.
Use a defined action in a decision table and set up criteria to control access of the webservice.
• Geo References — Define geographical reference points that can be used in decision models. This is useful
when you want to control access of specific services depending upon user's geographical location.
• Bulk Import and Export — Import and export multiple decision tables at a time.
Related information
Policy groups
Policies
Actions
Geo References
Bulk import and export of decision tables
Prerequisites for policy management
Mobius system administrators, data officers, or records managers can use the policy management features when you
activate them in Mobius Administrator.
Based on the license type, you can activate different features of policy management.
Basic policy management license
In the Authorization Code section, when you enter the code to activate the basic policy management license, you
can enable the following options:
• In the Repository Security section, select the Enable Policy Enforcement checkbox.
• Assign the Manage Policies permission in the Policy Service Permissions category to users who want to use
the policy management features.
When you complete these configurations, user can use all the features available in the Policies section of the Policy
Management.
Advanced policy management license
In the Authorization Code section, when you enter the code to activate the advanced policy management license,
you can enable the following options:
• In the Repository Security section, select the Enable Policy Enforcement and Enable Geo
Locationcheckboxes.
• Assign the all permissions in the Policy Service Permissions category to users who want to use the policy
management features.
When you complete these configurations, user can use all the features available in the following sections of the
Policy Management:
• Policies
• Policy Group Administration
• Actions
• Geo References
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 197


## Page 198


• Bulk Import and Export
Related information
Configuring repository security
Entering the authorization code
Mobius assets and permissions
Policy groups
The policies for controlling the access of services or display of UI elements are divided into groups. Each policy group
consists of a set of decision tables.
Use the Policy Group Administration section of the Mobius Administrator to manage the set of decision tables in
different policy groups. The default policy groups in Mobius Administrator are as follows:
• Access Control. A set of decision tables that are used to control the access of Mobius services.
• UI Governance. A set of decision tables that are used to enable or disable UI elements in Mobius View.
Ensure that the Policy Group Administration features are activated in Mobius Administrator and your user role has
the required permissions to use the features. For more information, see Prerequisites for policy management.
Custom policy group
You can create a custom policy group. It is a set of decision tables that are required for your organization to govern
the accessibility of services and UI elements.
You can add the decision tables from the default policy groups to a custom policy group. You can also import the
decision tables in Mobius Administrator and add them to the custom policy group.
Related information
Prerequisites for policy management
Adding decision tables to a policy group
Manage the set of decision tables in a default or custom policy group. The decision tables in a policy group provide
REST API resources that are required to set rules for governing Mobius services or UI elements.
1. In Mobius Administrator menu, click Policy Management.
2. In the Policy Management section, click Policy Group Administration.
The left pane of the Policy Groups section displays the list of default and custom policy groups. By default, the
first policy group in the list is selected. The right pane displays information about the selected policy group.
3. Select the policy group for which you want to add or remove decision tables.
4. In the Decision Tables section, select a table from the Available list and add it to the Added list.
You can select multiple tables from the Available list and add all the selected tables to the Added list.
By default, the Available and Added lists display decision tables available for all namespaces. In the Showing
list, select a namespace to view the list of decision tables for the selected namespace.
5. Click Apply.
Related information
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 198


## Page 199


Policy groups
Prerequisites for policy management
Creating a policy group
Create a custom policy group. It is a set of decision tables that are required for your organization to govern the
accessibility of Mobius features or UI elements.
1. In Mobius Administrator menu, click Policy Management.
2. In the Policy Management section, click Policy Group Administration.
The left pane of the Policy Groups section displays the list of policy groups.
3. In the left pane, click Add.
4. Configure the following fields :
◦ Name — Enter a name for the custom policy group. You can not change the name of a default policy
group.
◦ Display Name — Enter a display name.
◦ Policy Group Icon — Select an icon from the list. This an optional configuration.
◦ Description— Enter a description. This an optional configuration.
The information that is entered in these fields appears in the Policies section. Mobius system administrators
can use this information to select the correct policy group and set rules for controlling the accessibility.
5. In the Decision Tables section, select a table from the Available list and add it to the Added list.
You can select multiple tables from the Available list and add all the selected tables to the Added list.
By default, the Available and Added lists display decision tables available for all namespaces. In the Showing
list, select a namespace to view the list of decision tables for the selected namespace.
6. Click Apply.
Related information
Policy groups
Prerequisites for policy management
Policies
The Policies section in the Policy Management consists of default and custom policy groups. Each policy group is
used to control the access of Mobius View services or UI elements in Mobius View.
The default policy groups in Mobius Administrator are as follows:
• Access Control. A set of decision tables that are used to control the access of Mobius services.
• UI Governance. A set of decision tables that are used to enable or disable UI elements in Mobius View.
You can create rules in the decision table to govern the accessibility Mobius services or UI elements in Mobius View.
Each decision table provides resources that are required to define rules.
For example, the Content Streams decision table contains all the actions associated with the content streams
service. You can set rules to govern the content retrieving process.
Use the Policies section to perform the following tasks:
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 199


## Page 200


• View the default list of decision tables in a policy group
• Import decision tables to a policy group
• Export decision tables from a policy group
• Update decision tables
• Test decision tables
Ensure that the Policies features are activated in Mobius Administrator and your user role has the required
permissions to use the features.
Related information
Prerequisites for policy management
Importing a decision model
Import a decision table in the default and custom policy groups. The decision table is an XML file adhering to the
DMN XML-Schema.
1. In Mobius Administrator menu, click Policy Management.
2. In the Policy Management section, click Policies.
The Policies section displays the tiles for default and custom policy groups.
3. Click the policy group where you want to import a decision table.
The left pane of the Policies section displays a list of existing decision tables in the selected policy group.
4. In the left pane, click Import.
5. In the Import a decision model dialog box, click Browse to select the DMN XML file.
6. Enter a comment in the Comment field.
When a decision table that you are importing already exists in the policy group, use comments to distinguish
between multiple versions of the decision table.
7. Click Import.
Related information
Policy groups
Inputs for rules in a decision table
Exporting a decision model
Export a decision table and reuse it in another Mobius environment to control the access of a specific service or UI
element.
1. In Mobius Administrator menu, click Policy Management.
2. In the Policy Management section, click Policies.
The Policies section displays the tiles for default and custom policy groups.
3. Click the policy group that contains the decision table that you want to export.
The left pane of the Policies section displays a list of decision tables in the selected policy group.
4. Select a decision table and click Export.
The Choose a version to select dialog box displays a list of versions of the decision table.
When you update a decision table and save it, a new version is created. The latest version appears at the top of
the list and selected for the export.
If you want to export a previous version, select it from the list.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 200


## Page 201


5. In the Choose a version to select dialog box, click Export.
6. Specify a name of the DMN XML file and location to save the file and click Save.
Related information
Policies
Updating a decision table
Adding operations to a decision table
Adding rules to a decision table
Rules in a decision table specify criteria to control access of a specific service or UI element.
1. In Mobius Administrator menu, click Policy Management.
2. In the Policy Management section, click Policies.
The Policies section displays the tiles for default and custom policy groups.
3. Click the policy group for which you want to update a decision table.
The left pane of the Policies section displays a list of existing decision tables in the selected policy group.
4. Select the decision table that you want to update and click Add Rule.
5. In the New Rule dialog box, enter the description of the rule.
6. In the Input section, specify criteria of the rule.
The fields in the Input section vary depending on the decision table in which you create the rule. A rule
consists of actions that are associated with a feature and criteria to allow or deny the actions for specific users
or user groups and specific information resources. The actions are REST APIs and attributes are parameters for
the APIs.
If you are setting up a rule for governance of UI elements, use the Input section to specify the context of the
governance.
For more information about the fields in the Input section, see Inputs for rules in a decision table.
7. In the Output section, set values for one of the following toggles:
Option Description
Allowed toggle
This toggle is available for rules in the decision tables
in the Access Control policy group.
Set one of the following values to allow or restrict the
actions that are specified in the Input section:
◦ Yes — User or user groups can perform the
actions if the criteria specified in the Input
section are satisfied.
◦ No — The actions are restricted for the user or
user groups if the criteria specified in the
Input section are satisfied.
For example:
Annotations decision table
Create a rule that restricts user A from creating,
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 201


## Page 202


Option Description
updating, and deleting annotations in
documents with Document Class or Report ID
Finance. If the specific user tries to perform any
of these operations in the documents in that
Document Class, an error message appears.
Buttons toggle
This toggle is available for rules in the decision tables
in the UI Governance policy group.
Set one of the following values to enable or disable
the buttons when the criteria in the Input section are
satisfied:
◦ Yes — User or user groups can view the
buttons if the criteria specified in the Input
section are satisfied.
◦ No — The buttons are disabled for the user or
user groups if the criteria specified in the
Input section are satisfied.
Enter the names of buttons and UI elements in the
List of Buttons.
For example:
UI Governance decision table
Create a rule to disable the Print button in the
Mobius View application. Once this rule is
deployed, the Print button is disabled in all the
contexts such as, Browse, Search, and Favorites
in Mobius View.
8. Optional: In the Operations section, for the Actions toggle, set one of the following values to allow or restrict
the additional actions that are specified in the Operations column of the decision table:
◦ Yes — User or user groups can perform the additional actions if the criteria specified in the Input
section are satisfied.
◦ No — The additional actions are restricted for the user or user groups if the criteria specified in the
Input section are satisfied.
9. Optional: In the Operations > Replace Inputs section, specify additional attributes for actions in the rule.
For more information about the fields in the Replace Inputs section, see Inputs for rules in a decision table.
Note: The Operations section appears in the New Rule dialog box, only when you add any
actions or attributes in the Operations column of the decision table.
10. In the New Rule dialog box, click Add Rule.
The rule appears in the decision table. If you want to edit the rule, click in a column that corresponds to the
rule, except for the column that indicates the rule number.
11. Add all the rules that are required for governance of a service or UI element and then click Save in the right
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 202


## Page 203


pane.
For more information about order of the rules in a decision table, check Updating a decision table.
You can test the effectiveness of a decision table using the Test button in the right pane.
Related information
Inputs for rules in a decision table

Geo References
Adding operations to a decision table
Use the Operations column in a decision table to specify additional actions and attributes for governance of a
feature.
1. In the Mobius Administrator menu, click Policy Management.
2. In the Policy Management section, click Policies.
The Policies section displays the tiles for default and custom policy groups.
3. Click the policy group for which you want to update a decision table.
The left pane of the Policies section displays a list of existing decision tables in the selected policy group.
4. Select the decision table that you want to update.
5. Specify the additional actions associated with a feature:
a. In the Operations column of the table, click +.
By default, this column is available only in the decision tables in the Access Control policy group.
b. In the menu, click Actions to view the list of predefined actions.
c. Select an action from the list of predefined actions.
You can predefine actions to call REST services using the Actions tool in the Policy Management
section. For more information about the tool, see Actions.
If you want to update a predefined action, in the list of predefined actions, click the pencil icon after the
action name.
d. Optional: Use one of the following options to define an action:
Invoke URL
Define an action to call a REST service. Click + to launch the New Action dialog box.
For more information about how to define an action, see Creating a new action.
Start Process
Define an action to start a process using an app in Content Automation. Click + to launch the New
Action dialog box.
When you select a predefined action from the list or create a new action, an Action column appears in
the Operations column of the decision table. It displays the name of the action.
e. Optional: Hover over an action name, expand the menu, and click one of the following options:
▪ Add action before
▪ Add action after
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 203


## Page 204


▪ Delete action
You can create additional actions before or after the selected action or delete the selected action. When
you create additional actions, you can select a predefined action or add a new action.
6. Specify an additional attribute in the rule.
a. In the Operations column of the table, click + > Replace Inputs.
b. In the Replace Inputs dialog box, in the Replace Inputs Selection list, select one or more attributes.
The list displays attributes that are required for the actions available in the decision table.
c. Click Apply.
Related information
Adding rules to a decision table
Inputs for rules in a decision table
Creating an action for an external service
Testing a decision table
Enter sample attributes and check if a user or user group can perform the actions specified in the decision table. You
can also test if certain buttons or UI elements are disabled for a user or user group.
1. In Mobius Administrator menu, click Policy Management.
2. In the Policy Management section, click Policies.
The Policies section displays the tiles for default and custom policy groups.
3. Click the policy group for which you want to test a decision table.
The left pane of the Policies section displays a list of existing decision tables in the selected policy group.
4. Select the decision table that you want to test.
5. In the right pane, click Test.
6. In the Test Rule dialog box, in the Input section, enter the actions and attributes.
The fields in the Input section vary depending on the decision table which you want to test. Typically, you can
specify inputs, such as:
◦ actions that are associated with a feature
◦ attributes that are required to perform the actions
◦ users or user groups that can perform the actions
◦ time period in which users can perform the actions
The actions are used to call REST APIs and attributes are parameters for the APIs.
If no value is specified for an attribute, it is not considered during the rule evaluation.
7. In the Test Rule dialog box, click Test to view the output of the rule in the Output section of the dialog box.
The Output section indicates if a user or user group can perform a specific action or not. It can also display
names of buttons or UI elements that will be available or disabled for a user or user group.
For example:
Annotations decision table
Test a rule that restricts user A from creating, updating, and deleting annotations in documents with
Document Class or Report ID Finance. In the Input section, enter any of the above actions and required
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 204


## Page 205


attributes, the Output section will indicate that user A cannot perform the action.
UI Governance decision table
Test a rule to disable the Print button in the Mobius View application for all documents with Document
Class or Report ID Finance. In the Input section, enter the print action and required attributes, the
Output section will indicate that the print button is disabled.
Related information
Inputs for rules in a decision table
Updating a decision table
Decide the order of rules in a decision table and hit policy for the decision table. You can add rules to the decision
table in a specific order or change the order of the rules later.
1. In Mobius Administrator menu, click Policy Management.
2. In the Policy Management section, click Policies.
The Policies section displays the tiles for default and custom policy groups.
3. Click the policy group for which you want to test a decision table.
The left pane of the Policies section displays a list of existing decision tables in the selected policy group.
4. Select the decision table that you want to update.
5. Add a rule in the decision table.
For more information about adding a rule, see Adding rules to a decision table.
6. Perform the following steps to change the hit policy of the table:
a. Click F in the first row of the decision table.
b. Select one of the following options from the Hit Policy list:
▪ FIRST — The decision table can contain multiple rules with the same input data. The output of the
first satisfied rule in table is used to govern the service or UI element.
▪ UNIQUE — Each rule in the table must contain unique combination of inputs.
▪ RULE ORDER — The decision table can contain multiple rules with the same input data. The
outputs of all satisfied rules in table are used to govern the service. If outputs are different, the
order of the rules in the decision table is considered when governing the service or UI element.
7. Select a rule by clicking the number in the first column of the table.
This number indicates the order of the rule in the decision table.
8. Use the following options to change the order of the selected rule:
◦ Move to Top—Moves the selected rule to the first row in the table.
◦ Move to Bottom—Moves the selected rule to the last row in the table.
9. Use the following options to add more rules in a specific order:
◦ Add Above— Adds a rule above the selected rule.
◦ Add Below—Adds a rule below the selected rule.
◦ Duplicate—Copies the currently selected rule and adds it as a new rule above the selected rule.
10. Click Delete to delete the selected rule.
11. Click Save to save the changes in the decision table.
Related information
Adding rules to a decision table
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 205


## Page 206


Inputs for rules in a decision table
Use the Inputs section in the New Rule dialog box to specify services that you want to govern and attributes that are
required for the services.
The fields in the Input section vary depending on the decision table in which you create the rule. Typically, you can
specify inputs, such as:
• actions that are associated with a feature
• attributes that are required to perform the actions
• users or user groups that can perform the actions
• time period in which users can perform the actions
The actions are used to call REST APIs and attributes are parameters for the APIs.
If no value is specified for an attribute, it is not considered during the rule evaluation. You can also specify a location
as a criteria for users and user groups that can perform the actions.
For more information about the fields in the Input section, see topics for each service.
Related information
Data types for Input fields
Data types for Input fields
Specify services that you want to govern and attributes that are required for the services in the Inputs section. Use
different data types when you specify the attributes.
The following table provides the supported data types that can be entered in the input fields:
Data
type Syntax Example
string
For single value: <"string"> "joe"
For multiple values:
<"string">,<"string">
"joe","john"
String functions:
starts with ("string") starts with ("joe")
ends with ("string") ends with ("john")
matches ("string") matches ("joe")
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 206


## Page 207


Data
type Syntax Example
contains ("string") contains ("john")
number
For single value: <number> 1
For multiple values:
<number>,<number>
1,2
Comparison Operators:
> <number> > 7
> = <number> > = 7
< <number> < 7
< = <number> < = 7
Date
For single value: date("yyyy-mm-dd")
Date must be selected from the date picker.
For example: date("2020-9-8")
For multiple values:
date("yyyy-MM-dd"),date("yyyy-MM-dd")
Date must be selected from the date picker. Multiple entries
should be separated by comma.
For example: date("2020-9-8"), date("2020-9-15")
Range function:
between date("<from_date>") and
date("<to_date>")
Use between and operators to specify a date range between
two specific dates. For example:
between date("2020-9-8") and date("2020-9-8")
in [
date("<start_date>")..date("<end_date>")
]
where,
[ - includes start date
Use the in operator and ellipses to specify a date range within
the specified start date and end date.
For example, to specify dates between 24th August 2020 and
31st August 2020, excluding 24th August 2020, enter the
following:
in (date("<2020-08-24>")..date("<2020-08-31>") ]
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 207


## Page 208


Data
type Syntax Example
( - excludes start date
] - includes end date
) - excludes end date
Comparison operators:
< date("<date>") Use less than (<) operator to specify a date before the
mentioned date. For example: < date("2020-8-9")
> date("<date>") Use greater than (>) operator to specify a date after the
mentioned date. For example: > date("2020-8-9")
<= date("<date>")
Use less than or equal to (<=) operator to specify a date before
or on the mentioned date. For example:
<= date("2020-8-9")
>= date("<date>")
Use greater than or equal to (>=) operator to specify a date
after or on the mentioned date. For example:
>= date("2020-8-9")
!= date("<date>") Use not equal to (!=) operator to specify a date not equal to the
mentioned date. For example: != date("2020-8-9")
Time
Single value:
time("HH:mm:ss")
Time must be selected from the time picker.
For example: time("12:15:20")
Multiple values
time("HH:mm:ss"),time("HH:mm:ss")
Time must be selected from the time picker. Multiple entries
should be separated by comma.
For example: time("12:15:20"), time("12:25:20")
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 208


## Page 209


Data
type Syntax Example
Range function:
between time("<from_time>") and
time("<to_time>")
Use between and and operators to specify a time range
between two specific times. For example:
between time("12:15:20") and time("12:25:20")
in [
time("<start_time>")..time("<end_time>")
]
[- includes start time
( - excludes start time
] - includes end time
) - excludes end time
Use the in operator and ellipses to specify a time range within
the specified start time and end time.
For example, to specify time between "12:15:20" and
"12:25:20", excluding "12:15:20", enter the following:
in (date(""12:15:20"")..date(""12:25:20"") ]
Comparison operators:
< time("<time>") Use less than (<) operator to specify a time before the
mentioned time. For example: < time("12:50:30")
> time("<time>") Use greater than (>) operator to specify a time after the
mentioned time. For example: > time("12:50:30")

Use less than or equal to (<=) operator to specify a time before

after or on the mentioned time. For example:

!= time("<time>") Use not equal to (!=) operator to specify a time not equal to the

Type Specifies the allowed types for the decision model.

© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 218


## Page 219


Input
Heading Attribute Description
• 1
• 3
• 4
• 5
• 6
• 7
• 8
For description about the types, see Direct Connect open, display, and
navigation parameters.
Redirect
Specifies how the encrypted Direct Connect URL should be redirected for
immediate display after the user sends the unencrypted source URL to Mobius
View Server. It sends the encrypted URL back to the browser, which
automatically submits it for display without user intervention.
• If set to True, Direct Connect redirects the encrypted URL to the browser.
• If set to False, or if this parameter does not appear, Direct Connect sends
the encrypted URL back to the user.
Report ID Specifies the Mobius report id.
Report Section
ID Specifies the Mobius report section id.
Server Name Specifies the name of the server and repository defined in Mobius
Administrator.
Topic ID Specifies the Mobius topic ID. Used only for a Topic Direct Connect request
(type=3 or type=5).
Topic Item Name Specifies the Mobius Topic Item name. Used only for a Topic Direct Connect
request (type=3 or type=5).
Topic Version Specifies the Mobius topic version ID in the format yyyymmddhhmmss. Used
only for a Topic Direct Connect request (type=3 or type =5).
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 219


## Page 220


Input
Heading Attribute Description
Report Version Specifies the Mobius report version ID in the format yyyymmddhhmmss.
Expiration Time
in Minutes
Specifies the elapsed time after which the Direct Connect URL is no longer valid.
The value is the duration time for the URL, in minutes. The entered value
should be greater than or equal to 0.
Note: Omitting the expirationTimeMinutes parameter means
that the URL never expires.
Exact Match
Specifies if the Direct Connect matches the values in a request. This is an
optional parameter. If it is not specified, the value is taken as 2.
Possible values are:
• 0 - If there is no exact match to the values entered for topic, topic
version, report, section, or version ID, Direct Connect retrieves the next
sequential value.
• If the value entered is sequentially higher than the value of all existing
items, then Direct Connect retrieves the last value.
• 1 - Retrieves only exact matches to the specified topic, topic version,
report, section, or version ID fields.
• 2 - Treats the values entered for topic, topic version, topic item, report,
section, or version ID fields as a prefix and retrieves the first exact match
for the prefix. This setting provides a shortcut to your parameter entries;
it allows you to omit some of the characters of a value.
Note: If your request includes the partialMatch=1 parameter,
Direct Connect retrieves all topic items that match the prefix.
No Navigation
Specifies whether the Navigation Pane is visible in the Mobius View display. This
parameter is not applicable for downloading operations. Possible values:
• 0 - Displays the navigation pane. This is the default.
• 1 - Hides the navigation pane.
Subtype Specifies whether both the topic and report branches are shown in the
Navigation Pane. If the value is given as 2, it displays both.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 220


## Page 221


Input
Heading Attribute Description
Recipient ID
Specifies the recipient ID for the specified serverID. Optional, if omitted for a
server that requires a recipient ID, or if an invalid value is specified, the login
dialog box is displayed.
Pages To
Download
Specifies the pages to be downloaded. Section boundaries respected at all
times is required.
Possible values:
• ALL - Downloads all the pages of the report. Not supported for direct
connects.
• ALLINSECTION - All the pages of the section are downloaded.
• RANGE,#,# - The specified range of pages is downloaded.
• PAGE,# - The specified page is downloaded
Direct Download
Invocation
Causes a download to occur immediately, without loading the Mobius View.
Requires that the URL contains a recipient ID and password. Best used with
embedded credentials, because if prompting is required, an error message
appears. Similar to the redirect parameter, when redirect=true, where it does
not generate an encoded URL.
EQDCSearchType Specifies the request as a search request as opposed to a download/
navigation/content retrieval. Required for search DC.
No Navigation
An optional interface parameter that determines whether users can navigate
the folder hierarchy after they run the search.
Possible values:
• 0 - Allow folder navigation
• 1 - Do not allow folder navigation (default value)
Search Editor An optional interface parameter that determines whether users can edit the
query in the search editor before running it.
CurrentTime
Time of day
Date Range
Specifies that a particular time or time range in a day can be selected.
Specifies that a particular date and month or a range can be selected.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 221

FindPages
For the FindPages decision table, every rule should include a value for the action attribute to identify the function for
which the rule applies.
This decision table impacts the Find Location API.
The below table provides the various attribute values.
Input
Heading Attribute Description
FindPages Action
Specifies the allowed actions for the decision model.
The allowed action is 'get'.
Scope
Specifies the scope for the search. The values that can be entered are section and
version.
• section - Specifies that the matches will be found only in the section specified in
the document id.
• version - Specifies that the matches will be found starting with the section
specified in the document id but will not be limited to the specified section. The
matches can be found in next or previous sections in scope of the report version,
depending on the direction (forward or backward).
Redacted
The flag determines whether the redacted areas of redacted documents are included
when searching for matching text. Only users with appropriate permissions can search
redacted areas of redacted documents.
• True - Unredacted areas of redacted documents are excluded.
• False - Unredacted areas of redacted documents are included.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 227

Navigation
For the Navigation decision table, every rule should include a value for the action attribute to identify the function
for which the rule applies.
The depth, tree, versionrange, topicitemkeyvalue, reportkeyvalue, and versionkeyvalue attributes apply
only to the getchildrenofrepository, getfoldercontents, and getrepositorycontents actions, and should be specified
only for rules that use one of those actions.
This decision table impacts the Navigation Service APIs.
The below table provides the various attribute values.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 232


## Page 233


Input
Heading Attribute Description
Navigation Action
Specifies the allowed actions for the decision model.
Any one of the following can be selected:
• getrepositorycontents
• getfoldercontents
• getfolderparents
• getdocumentparents
• getdocumentmetadata
• getdocumentretentions
• getdocumentproperties
• getrepositoryobjecttypes
• getobjecttypedetails
Depth
Specifies the depth of the result.
For example,
• The default value 1 returns children only for the specified folder.
• The value 2 will bring children for current folder and for one additional level of
sub-folders (if available).
• The value -1 removes restriction, returning complete subtree of children of the
specified folder.
Note: The repository implementation may have an internal restriction on
the size of the response. The default value is 1 returning children of the
specified folder only.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 233


## Page 234


Input
Heading Attribute Description
Tree
Boolean value which if set to True restricts the result items to folder objects only. This
allows a client, in combination with depth parameter, to retrieve a complete folder tree.
The default value is False returning both folder and document objects in Get Children
response.
Version
Range
A string value which denotes a version range constraint for the children of the folder.
Any one of the following values can be selected:
• All
• LastTwelveMonths
• LastThreeMonths
• LastSixMonths
• LastMonth
• LastWeek
• MostRecent
• Today
• YearToDate
Topic Item
Key Value
Specifies the Topic item values to be searched. Used with reportkeyvalue,
versionkeyvalue, and pagerbakeyvalue for retrieving topic item entries.
Report
Key Value
Specifies the Report ID where the member topic values will be searched. Used with
topicitemkeyvalue, versionkeyvalue, and pagerbakeyvalue for retrieving topic item
entries.
Version
Key Value
Specifies the Mobius Report Version ID. Version ID in the format yyyymmddhhmmss.
Used with topicitemkeyvalue, reportkeyvalue, and pagerbakeyvalue for retrieving topic
item entries.
Repository
ID
Repository
Name Specifies the repository name to which the repository id refers.
Document Content Specifies the content class id (report id for Mobius).

Templates
Specifies the event template name. This parameter can be repeated.
• For RM Events: If specified, filters events by template name. By default,
events for all templates are included.
• For RM Policies: Specifies the event template name, to restrict polices by
triggering event template. This parameter can be repeated. If not
specified, selected policies are not restricted by the triggering event
templates.
• For RM Retentions: If specified, the selected retentions are restricted to
the specified event templates. By default, the selected retentions are not
restricted for any event template.
RMEventTemplates Enabled
If specified, filters the enabled or disabled event templates.
If not specified, all Templates will be returned.

## Page 243


Input Heading Attribute Description
RMEvents Reasons Specifies that events can be filtered by retention reason. By default events for
all retention reasons are included.
Types
Specifies that events can be filtered by event processing type. By default the
events of all processing types are included.
The following values are allowed:
• RETENTION_CHANGE_BY_SEARCH
• RETENTION_CHANGE_BY_DOCUMENTS
• RETENTION_RESCIND_BY_REASON
From Date
Specifies that events can be filtered by events received after specified time
(encoded in ms of epoch time). By default do not restrict events by receiving
time.
Till Date
Specifies that events can be filtered by events received before specified time
(encoded in ms of epoch time). By default do not restrict events by receiving
time.
RMHold Repository

Report Specifies the report.
Report

RMPolicies Record
Types
Specifies the record type to restrict policies by record type. This parameter can
be repeated. If not specified, selected policies are not restricted by the record
types.
Policy
Type
Specifies the records that will be retrieved from database based on the type.

pagerbakeyvalue attributes for retrieving topic item entries.
• The reportkeyvalue is the Report ID where member topic values will be searched. Used with
topicitemkeyvalue, versionkeyvalue, and pagerbakeyvalue attributes for retrieving topic item entries.
