Actions
Use the Actions tool to call external services or start processes using apps in Content Automation. After you define
actions, you can set up rules to govern those actions.
When you define an action, you can specify the following details:
• URL for the REST service
• Method to call the webservice
• Parameters and headers in the request
• Payload data in the request
• Name of the app that you want to use to start a process
• Name of the process
• Parameters for the process
The list of predefined actions appears in the left pane of the Actions section. You can edit or delete actions from the
list.
When you use the action in a specific decision table, you can use the attributes in the table as parameters and
headers in the request or the process.
Ensure that the Actions tool is activated in Mobius Administrator and your user role has the required permissions to
use the features. For more information, see Prerequisites for policy management.
You must deploy the Content Automation tools in the Mobiusenvironment and configure apps. Then you can define
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 250


## Page 251


actions to start processes within the configured apps. For more information, see Content Automation 12.4 Library.
Related information
Prerequisites for policy management
Creating an action for an external service
Define an action to call an external web service. You can include the predefined action in the rules in the decision
table and control the access of the service.
1. In Mobius Administrator menu, click Policy Management.
2. In the Policy Management section, click Actions > Add.
The list of predefined actions appears in the left pane of the Actions section. You can edit or delete actions
from the list.
3. In the Name field, enter a name of the action.
4. Optional: Enter a description of the action.
5. Select the Invoke URL option.
6. Enter the URL for the web service in the URL field.
7. Select a method to call the web service.
8. In the Parameters column, in the Name box, enter the name of the parameter that you want to include in the
request.
9. In the Value column, select a value from the list.
The list contains the attributes available in the decision tables.
Click the Customize checkbox if you want to enter a value instead of selecting an attribute from the list.
10. Optional: Click + in the Action column to add more parameters.
11. In the Headers column, in the Name box, enter the name of the header that you want to include in the
request.
12. In the Value column, select a value from the list.
The list contains the attributes available in the decision tables.
Click the Customize checkbox if you want to enter a value instead of selecting an attribute from the list.
13. Optional: Click + in the Action column to add more headers.
14. If you select the POST or PUT method to call the service, in the Payload Template tab, define the payload
template.
You can use the sample payload in the Sample Data Payload tab when you define the payload template.
A payload is generated using the header and parameters values and the payload template. The generated
payload is used as a request body.
If you have mapped the attributes from the decision table with the headers and parameters, the attribute
values are used to generate the payload.
15. Click Apply.
Related information
Adding operations to a decision table
Adding rules to a decision table
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 251


## Page 252


Creating an action for a Content Automation process
Define an action to start a Content Automation process. You can include the predefined action in the rules in the
decision table and control the access of the process.
1. In Mobius Administrator menu, click Policy Management.
2. In the Policy Management section, click Actions > Add.
The list of predefined actions appears in the left pane of the Actions section. You can edit or delete actions
from the list.
3. In the Name field, enter a name of the action.
4. Optional: Enter a description of the action.
5. Select the Start Process option.
This option is available only when you deploy the Content Automation tools in the Mobius environment and
configure Content Automation apps.
6. In the App Name list, select an app.
The list displays all configured apps that have process models. The Mobius View users can execute these
processes.
7. In the Process Name list, select a process.
The Process Reference Mapping section displays a list of parameters that are required to execute the
process.
8. Use the Source Field column to map the process parameters with attributes in decision tables.
Include the predefined action in a rule in a decision table. The values of attributes in the rule determine
whether to allow or deny the execution of the process for a specific user or group of users.
9. Click Apply.
Related information
Adding operations to a decision table
Adding rules to a decision table
Configuring processes
Adding custom buttons for Content Automation processes
Geo References
Govern services and UI elements based on geographical location of a user.
Use the Geo References tool in Policy Management section of the Mobius Administrator. Use the latitude and
longitude of a geographical location to create a Geo reference.
You can control the access of services or UI elements for users that are located within a specific distance from the
Geo reference. When you create rules in a decision table, you can select the Geo reference in the Location section of
the New Rule dialog box and also specify the distance criterion to allow or restrict the access for users within the
specified distance from the Geo reference.
Ensure that the Geo References tool is activated in Mobius Administrator and your user role has the required
permissions to use the features. For more information, see Prerequisites for policy management.
Related information
Prerequisites for policy management
Creating a new Geo reference
Specify the latitude and longitude of a geographical location and save it as a Geo reference. Use these references in
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 252


## Page 253


rules to control access of services and UI elements based on geographical locations.
1. In Mobius Administrator menu, click Policy Management.
2. In the Policy Management section, click Geo References > Add.
The list of predefined Geo references appears in the left pane of the Geo References section. You can edit or
delete Geo references from the list.
3. In the Name field, enter a name of the Geo reference.
4. Optional: Enter a description of the Geo reference.
5. In the Latitude and Longitude fields, enter the latitude and longitude points.
Note:
The accepted ranges are as follows:
◦ Latitude range : -90 to 90
◦ Longitude range : -180 to 180
6. Click Apply.
Related information
Adding rules to a decision table
Bulk import and export of decision tables
Import and export multiple decision tables at a time. You can use the bulk import and export too when you move the
information resources from one environment to other.
Use the Bulk Import and Export tool in the Policy Management section of the Mobius Administrator. When you
import decision tables in bulk, you can identify the tables that already exist in your environment. Also, when you
export the decision tables, you can export only the rules or only structure, or both. The structure contains names of
services and attributes.
Ensure that the Bulk Import and Export tool is activated in Mobius Administrator and your user role has the
required permissions to use the features. For more information, see Prerequisites for policy management.
When vendor type is set as NONE and the Authentication Type in the Content Repositories section is set to
Alias, you must assign the ROOT role to the Alias user so that users can access the Bulk Import and Export tool with
alias usernames.
Related information
Prerequisites for policy management
Importing decision tables
Import multiple decision models in a zip file when you want to move decision models from one environment to other.
1. In Mobius Administrator menu, click Policy Management.
2. In the Policy Management section, click Bulk Import and Export.
3. Click Add File to select the zip file that contains the decision tables.
The name of the selected file appears in the Import field.
4. Click Preview to view the list of decision tables in the selected file.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 253


## Page 254


In the list, the Existing or New status indicates if a decision table already exists or its new in the environment.
5. Click Import.
Related information
Policies
Policy groups
Exporting decision tables
Export multiple decision models in a zip file. You can export rules or structure, or both. The structure contains names
of services and attributes.
1. In Mobius Administrator menu, click Policy Management.
2. In the Policy Management section, click Bulk Import and Export.
In the Export field, the Available list displays decision tables available for all namespaces. The Added list
displays the decision tables that you can export.
3. Optional: In the Showing list, select a namespace to view the list of decision tables for the selected
namespace.
4. Select a table from the Available list and add it to the Added list.
You can select multiple tables from the Available list and add all the selected tables to the Added list.
5. Select one of the following options to specify the contents of the export:
◦ Only Structure — Export only the structure of the table. It contains names of services and attributes
that are required to define rules of access control.
◦ Only Rules—Export only the rules in the table.
◦ Structure and Rules— Export both structure and rules.
6. Click Export.
Related information
Policies
Policy groups
Enterprise folders
Enterprise folders are system-generated virtual folders for organizing documents in a structure based on the
configurations defined by the admin user in Mobius Administrator to offer easy navigation and access.
Mobius View displays these folders dynamically by querying servers and repositories according to the user defined
rules. These folders help to deal with huge volume of content in industries like banking, educational institutions, and
so on.
The administrator can configure two types of enterprise folders during installation based on the requirement as
follows:
• Predefined folders – Presents data to fit specific requirements.
• Automatic folders – Organizes data in a preferred manner based on the presence of records for the user
specified index value.
Both types can contain sub-folders and current version of documents. When you open an enterprise folder, you see
only the files for which you have access privileges on the content server. Also, you cannot create, modify, copy, or
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 254


## Page 255


move the contents of these enterprise folders as they are just like shortcuts to the actual files saved in the content
repository. Only an admin user can add or manage the enterprise folders.
While you can create an automatic folder directly under the folder, you must define one or more predefined folders
to categorize your content.
Creating enterprise folders
Create pre-defined or automatic enterprise folders at the repository level using Mobius Administrator. You can create
enterprise folders for only one repository at a time. However, if there are common indexes mapped, you can create
enterprise folder for two or more repositories.
Before you begin:
• Ensure if the Enable Folder Server checkbox is selected and Disable universal indexes is unselected for the
required repository.
• Change reportid as RecordType  when you map the universal indexes for the repository.
• Ensure if the Enable Federation checkbox on the Repository Security screen is selected for the required
repository.
Note: There is no separate option to select the repository to create enterprise folders. The system
automatically picks the repository for which the above pre-requisite is done. To create enterprise
folders for another repository, you must select the Disable universal indexes checkbox for the current
repository, and then do the above pre-requisite to the required repository.
1. Login to Mobius Administrator with valid credentials and click the folder icon from the menu.
The Enterprise Folders screen displays the list of available folders. By default, a pre-defined folder will be
available, which represents the active repository.
2. Open the pre-defined folder, click the add icon and select the folder type from the Define Folder Content panel.
3. Do the following to create an automatic folder.
a. Select This folder will contain automatically generated folders from the Define Folder Content panel.
b. Select the folder name from the list displayed in the Folder Name dropdown. These names are fetched
in this field through indexing.
c. Select the content classes for the folder from the Content Classes to show in this folder: dropdown.
d. Select the required universal indexes from the Details to show for documents in this folder:
dropdown.
Note: Ensure if the value in Folder Name is mapped to the value selected in Details to
show for documents in this folder:.
e. Click Apply to save the changes.
f. Login to Mobius View with valid credentials, go to the selected repository and check if the created folders
are reflected.
You can define one or more pre-defined folders under an automatic folder for better content categorization.
4. Do the following to create a pre-defined folder.
a. Select This folder contains one or more predefined folders from the Define Folder Content panel.
b. Enter the folder name manually in the Folder Name field and click Apply.
Note: Do not select the content class and topics from the Content Classes to show in this
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 255


## Page 256


folder: and Details to show for documents in this folder: dropdowns.
c. Open the created pre-defined folder and click the add icon.
d. Do the steps given above (step#3) to create an automatic folder under this pre-defined folder.
You can create as many pre-defined folders and folder hierarchies as required for the repository.
Managing presentations
Use the Presentation Management feature to map multiple presentations to a single content class.
You can apply only one presentation to a particular version at a time. The Presentation Management screen lists all
the created presentations and allows you to import, export, clone, edit, and delete presentations. You can export and
delete multiple presentations at a time.
You can click Presentation to view the list of presentations and Content Class to view all the content classes. You
can use the Filter Presentations bar to filter or search for a presentation.
You can click on a presentation to view details such as the content class for which the presentation is applied and the
date range. Click on a Content Class row in the presentation view to view, edit, or add a date range. You can also add
or remove content classes.
Each presentation has the following options.
• Start - Select From Beginning or Specific Date to specify the version start date from which the presentation
should be applied. Selecting From Beginning includes all the versions created from the start.
• End - Select No End Date or Specific Date to specify the version end date till which the presentation should be
applied. Selecting No End Date includes all the latest versions.
• Edit icon - Click to view the document in Mobius View. The document with the specified end date or close to the
end date is displayed in Mobius View.
• + icon - Click to add a new date range.
• - icon - Click to remove a date range.
•
  icon - Click to switch to content class view and vice versa.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 256


## Page 257


Note: The "Requested scope overlaps with existing scope error" is displayed for the below scenarios:
• If the date overlaps for a presentation.
• If a presentation is already mapped to the version ranges specified in between the start and end
date.
Cloning legacy presentations
Clone legacy presentations to apply it to documents using Mobius Administrator.
1. Select Presentation Management in Mobius Administrator.
2. Select a legacy presentation and click Clone.
3. Enter a name for the cloned presentation and click OK.
After you clone a legacy presentation,
◦ The generated XML files can be moved to the Policies folder.
◦ The image field is updated with a default image containing the "Import actual image here" text.
Configuring Microsoft Office add-in for Mobius
Archive office documents directly using the Office add-in and display the content in the Mobius View document
viewer using Office Online Server. If this add-in is not configured, the documents get downloaded always and do not
get displayed in the document viewer.
Before you begin:
• Ensure Office 365 Connectivity option is enabled as part of the Mobius View license. Log on to Mobius
Administrator > Authorization Code and check if the license for Microsoft 365 Connectivity is available.
• Use trusted certificate for Mobius View SSL.
Note: Using an untrusted certificate for Mobius View might disrupt certain functions of the Office
add-in.
• Download and install Office online server and the required language packs to enable various language support.
Refer Deploy Office Online Server and Planning language packs for Office Online Server for more information.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 257


## Page 258


• Add the following security constraint configuration above all existing security constraints in an application
server's web.xml for non-OIDC vendor types (for example, Tomcat).
<security-constraint>
<web-resource-collection>
<web-resource-name>Mobius WOPI REST API</web-resource-name>
<url-pattern>/rest/wopi/files/*</url-pattern>
</web-resource-collection>
<user-data-constraint>
<transport-guarantee>CONFIDENTIAL</transport-guarantee>
</user-data-constraint>
</security-constraint>
Note: This security constraint configuration must be added at the top, above all existing security
constraints in the web.xml.
• Login to the Azure portal and fetch the tenant ID, clientID, and clientSecret for Mobius as these values are
required to configure the redaction settings.
1. Login to Mobius Administrator with valid credentials and click Microsoft Office Integration from the menu.
2. Click Download manifest.xml from the Add-in Download tab and make the required changes to the host,
port, and so on as required.
This xml file contains the details of the Office add-in, types of office docs supported, and how the buttons to
sync to Mobius and metadata properties should work for the add-in. Refer Deploy and publish Office Add-ins
for details about the add-in installation.
3. Expand the WOPI Client Settings tab and select the Enable viewing and editing of Office documents and
Enable business user check boxes to manage the settings for Microsoft web based document viewing and
editing, as required.
For the Enable viewing and editing of Office documents option, configure the following.
a. Enter the host URL (Office Online Server URL) with http or https in the Discovery URL field, where the
WOPI calls are made for Mobius View to display office docs and click Test Discovery to validate the
discovery URL.
For example, https://OFFICE-ONLINE-SERVER-HOST-NAME/hosting/discovery
b. Select the zone (SSL and non-SSL ports) from the list of auto-populated values in the Client Zone field.
4. Expand the Redaction Settings tab and configure the following fields for Azure.
a. Enter the tenant ID in the Tenant ID field.
b. Enter the ID used to associate the application with Azure Active Directory during run time in the Client
ID field.
c. Enter the client secret code in the Client secret field, which is used to authenticate the client.
d. Enter a valid username and password of the OneDrive of the user registered in the Azure AD in the
Username and Password fields, respectively.
e. Enter the OneDrive location details of the same user in the OneDrive location to store uploaded
documents field and click Test Connection.
The system displays a toast message to confirm if the connection to OneDrive is successful.
5. Click the apply icon to save the changes.
Creating immediate archiving (Capture) targets
The Capture feature is used to ingest data and metadata to the Mobius system. Immediate archiving archives
content directly into Mobius. Policy and list file archiving use this feature to archive any type of document in native
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 258


## Page 259


format with index values provided by the user performing the archive operation.
Before you begin: Enable Capture by selecting the Immediate Capture checkbox available in the Features
section of the Application Server screen.
Mobius View users can archive documents directly from their computer or device using Capture. When viewing the
page for Capture, the user is presented with a number of drop targets. They can drag and drop a document from
Windows Explorer into one of these drop targets and are then presented with a list of indexes that they need to
supply. Once submitted, the documents are sent to the REST service, where the document(s) are archived and
indexes are created. The following formats are supported:
• Financial text reports and PDF with invoice or expense. Here, the user would typically ingest using archiving
policies, where the policies extract content class ID and metadata.
• MS Office documents and video files. For example, an insurance company clerk submits a claim content class
with a video of the damage and specifies metadata information such as Claim Number.
• Ingest images using OCR. For example, a company mail room gets mail and scans them. Then during ingesting,
Mobius uses OCR to extract metadata from the scanned images.
Note: If Authentication is set to Prompt in Mobius View Administrator, credentials must be provided to
access the Mobius repository.
1. Click New in the Capture screen to create a new target.
2. Specify the following details in the New Capture Target screen.
a. Specify a Title.
b. Optional: Enter a Description for the target.
c. Select a Repository from the list of Mobius repositories that appear.
Depending on the authentication setting in the Mobius View Administrator application, the user may be
prompted to enter the credentials for the Mobius repository.
d. Select either Determine content class by policy or Designate one or more content classes in the
Select Content Class section associated with that repository.
i. Select Determine content class by policy to specify the policy names in the Select Policies field.
Multiple policies can be entered in the Select Policies field. Existing policies are available in the
Mobius Administrator. New policies can also be created.
ii. Select Designate one or more content classes to specify the content class names in the Enter
Content class name field.
When multiple content classes are selected, the user must choose one content class during
Capture.
iii. Select the Store captured files individually checkbox to archive each document in a different
ingestion while capturing.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 259


## Page 260


This checkbox appears when the Designate one or more content classes option is selected.
iv. Select the Enable access from Office Add-in checkbox to display the capture target while using
the Office add-in feature for the specified content class.
v. Select a content class ID.
vi. Check Enable metadata to enable metadata and to add indexes. Documents without policies can
be archived using Capture. This check box appears when the Designate one or more content
classes option is selected.
vii. Select the Trigger an action after archiving checkbox to enable the content automation during
Capture.
viii. In the Action Mapping section that is displayed, select an application and process under Start
Process and map the fields for the selected process from the drop-down list under Process
Reference Mapping.
ix. Check Perform OCR processing for the content class in Mobius Administrator to archive an image
file and enable full text search in the Content Viewing section to process a full text search.
e. Specify the system path in Legacy Path that is used along with the file name for legacy policies in
Mobius View.
It provides the complete path of the file while archiving in Mobius View.
f. Click Users can provide any value for Document Title to select the Input method.
g. Select the Input Method for the Document Title in the Edit Target Index dialog box.
i. If Users must choose from a list of values is selected, enter the list of valid values separated by
semi-colons (;) in Values.
Note: If this option is selected and only one value is entered as a valid value, it is
implied that this value must be used with this index. The value field appears as a
non-editable field in Mobius View.
ii. Click Apply.
iii. If Users can enter any value is selected, the Allow value to be blank checkbox is displayed, and
all types of values are accepted.
h. Click Add a index.
i. Select an Index to organize the documents in the Capture Target Index.
j. Select the Input Method.
i. User can enter any value and User must choose from a list of values input method options are
listed for all the data types. The other input Method options are listed based on the selected data
type. Select an option from the list.
1. Source File Name and Source File Extension input method options are listed for String
data types.
2. Source TimeStamp input method option is listed for date data types.
If Users can enter any value is selected, the Allow value to be blank optional check box is
displayed. When this check box is selected, the capture drop down target field in Mobius
View can be submitted without any value.
By default, the Allow value to be blank check box is unchecked.
ii. Click Apply to proceed. An X icon near each of the values allows an item to be removed from the
list.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 260


## Page 261


Note: If this option is selected and only one value is entered as a valid value, it is
implied that this value must be used with this index. The value field appears as a
non-editable field in Mobius View.
A summary containing the Index name and the Input method appears.
3. Click Apply to add the target.
4. Click Enable roles to display the capture target to the assigned roles.
Note: Disable Roles is the default option and it displays the capture target to all users.
5. Use the Assign Roles option to assign capture targets to specific roles in the Authorization screen. Once this
is selected, it prompts to Apply or Discard the unsaved changes before redirecting to the Authorization
screen.
The newly added target can be viewed in the Existing Capture Targets screen.
Note:
◦ Due to restrictions with Mobius for Section names, Document Title is limited to 30
characters.
◦ Due to limitations with Mobius for Index items, Indexes are restricted to 255 characters.
◦ It is recommended to use the Mobius server version 10.1.3 or later for the latest archive
features.
Document redaction
Redaction feature in Mobius View redacts documents from the repository connected to Mobius View.
For example, suppose Accounting and HR content reside in different document repositories. Social Security Numbers
(SSNs) need to be protected in documents in such repositories. The Redaction feature in Mobius View redacts
documents from the repository connected to Mobius View and enables the creation of a Redaction Policy for SSN.
This Redaction Policy hides SSNs when the end user views the document. You can associate this Redaction Policy with
documents from both Accounting and HR document repositories.
Note:
• Redaction is supported for both federated and non-federated repositories.
• Redaction is supported for Text, PDF, associated Text, associated PDF documents, LPFD
documents (only for Mobius repository), Microsoft Office documents (doc, docx, xls, xlsx, ppt, and
pptx), and images.
• Credentials are required to access content classes shared across various repositories for which
the Authentication type is set to prompt.
• To manage redaction policies, such as editing the policy, adding document class associations, and
getting the policy list, you need to have Manage Redaction Policies permission, which can be
configured in the Authorization screen of Mobius Administrator.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 261


## Page 262


An important feature of Mobius View system is the ability to apply a Redaction policies without a need to change or
re-archive documents to apply redaction. To apply redaction to a new class of documents, associate the Redaction
Policy with that Document Class, and every document that belongs to this Document Class is redacted.
“Document Class” is a special classification of documents in Mobius View. For example, in Mobius, the Content Class
ID is mapped to the Document Class, and the Content Class ID value becomes the value of the Document Class. So, if
the Redaction Policy is associated with the value of the Content Class ID in the Document Class, then all the
documents belonging to this Content Class ID will be redacted using the Redaction Policies.
Note: In Sharepoint, the Document Class corresponds to the Sharepoint Content Type. In CMIS, the
Document Class corresponds to the cmis:Document objects type and object types that inherit from
cmis:Document.
Mobius View allows you to apply multiple Redaction polices to the document. You can apply redaction to bank
accounts for documents in an Accounting system by creating an Accounting Numbers Redaction Policy. Mobius View
will apply it to the documents in the Accounting system in addition to applying SSN redaction to documents in the
Accounting system.
Mobius View supports applying Redaction policies for documents stored in the Mobius , Sharepoint, and repositories
that support CMIS access, such as Alfresco, OpenText, etc.
Once a Redaction Policy is applied to the Document Class, the end user, by default, will view the redacted document.
However, users can see the unredacted document if the user’s authorization role includes the Redaction Policy name
that protects the document.
Note: In Mobius, the indexes that may include sensitive information such as SSN should be excluded
from the search using the index access rights.
Redact sensitive data
Redaction is used to reduce exposure of sensitive information in Mobius View to ensure the privacy of sensitive data
for legal or security purposes.
Redaction removes sensitive data from query results before displaying them to clients, preventing unauthorized
access. Stored data remains unchanged, while displayed data is redacted. Data Redaction offers various
transformations for redacting data in specified areas or pages, preserving certain data, or generating replacement
data.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 262


## Page 263


Mobius provides redaction from Mobius Server or Mobius View. Mobius provides server-side redaction. The
redaction is performed on the Mobius server before the documents are returned to Mobius View. Mobius applies a
Redaction Rule policy during the retrieval process and redacts the data where sensitive details have been masked
according to the specified policy.
Note: If you have permission to view non-redacted data, Mobius returns the original report, allowing
the viewer to see the sensitive data.
Server-side redaction allows to redact text reports and full-text search results for display purposes and distribution of
text reports.
Mobius View provides client-side redaction. The redaction is performed on the client side. The data is returned non-
redacted along with the redaction policy. Mobius View then applies the redaction policy to the retrieved data and
presents a masked document to the user.
Client-side redaction allows redaction of AFP, PCL, PDF/PS, and Xerox reports for display purposes and print and e-
mail distribution.
With redaction, the user can:
• Assign permissions to view non-redacted data. By default, a newly created recipient is not permitted to see
non-redacted information.
• Enable or disable redaction on the report level from the Mobius server.
• Enable or disable redaction on the report version level from Mobius View.
Redaction authorization
For redaction, the Mobius View Administrator must have a Manage Redaction Policies permission and
RedactionPolicyManager role. Manage Redaction Policies permission provides tenant users access to the
Redaction Policies panel.
The RedactionPolicyManager role provides login access to the Mobius Administrator Redaction policies page from
Mobius View.
1. From the left navigation bar, click Authorization.
2. Select the administrator role, and then click Add Permissions in the Permissions field.
3. Select the MobiusViewAdminApplication permission drop-down list, and then select the Manage Redaction
Policies checkbox.
4. Select the View Unredacted Content checkbox to view the unredacted content in Mobius View.
5. Click Update.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 263


## Page 264


6. Click the
  icon on the left pane.
7. Select RedactionPolicyManager role in the Access Control section of the Role to Manage drop-down list.
8. Click Apply.
Once a redaction policy is applied to the document class end users can view the redacted document. However,
users can see the un-redacted document, if the authorization role in which they belong to, includes the
redaction policy name that protects the document.
Mobius View assets management and permissions are based on Authorization Roles. Administrator can assign
roles to users and user groups. To allow the user to view the redacted content in the document, include the
applied redaction policy name to the Authorization Roles for the user.
For example, to redact SSN numbers from an accounting document, create a redaction policy and associate it
with the document class for accounting documents. A regular user will only see documents with SSN redacted.
However, the manager of the Accounting department can view the SSN unredacted. To allow access to
unredacted data for a user, add the name of the redaction policy to the authorization role for the user.
Perform the following sub-steps to provide authorization:
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 264


## Page 265


a. Create a redaction policy.
b. Select Authorization in Mobius Administrator.
c. Click Create Role to add the new role to provide access to view the redacted document.
i. Enter the name of the role in the Name field.
ii. Enter the description in the Description field.
iii. Click Add Permissions to add the redaction policy that you want to provide permission for this
role.
iv. Enter the user names or group names in the Users and Groups field.
v. Click Create to create the role or click Revert to discard the changes.
▪ Users who are authorized to view unredacted documents, can toggle between the redacted and
unredacted views of the documents by clicking the
  icon.
Related information
Creating redaction policies
Enabling repositories for redaction
You must have redaction permissions to manage redaction policies.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 265


## Page 266


Before you begin: You must have redaction permissions to manage redaction policies.
1. From the left navigation bar in Mobius Administrator, click Repositories.
2. Click a repository to view its definition information. Then, select Enable Redaction to enable redaction.
3. You can select or clear the Disable universal indexes checkbox.
If universal indexes are enabled, you need to map the “__DocumentClass” index.
The Document Class is a special classification of documents in Mobius View.
You can view all the list of Document Classes in the Redaction screen when you click the
  icon on the left
pane. From there, you can associate Document Classes with Redaction Policies.
Redaction policy can be associated with many documents. For example, SSN can be applied to Accounting and
HR documents. So, in Mobius View Administrator you can assign SSN Redaction policy to Document Classes for
HR and Accounting documents.
In some cases, multiple Redaction Policies need to be applied to the document. Then, multiple redaction
policies should be applied to the Document Class to which the document belongs. Mobius View allows you to
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 266


## Page 267


preview documents by selecting the redaction policy.
Related information
Document redaction
Mobius assets and permissions
Migrating redaction policies
If a customer had used redaction in Mobius (VDRNET) in releases prior to version 11.1 and upgrades to Mobius View
11.1, then Mobius View allows you to migrate redaction policies from Mobius (VDRNET).
You can go to the Content Repository Configuration section and click Migrate Redaction Policy to download the
existing Redaction policy and associations to ReportID from Mobius(VDRNET) repository.
If a customer upgrades to Mobius View 10.0 and uses Mobius (VDRNET) distribution, and the customer needs to
apply Redaction Policy to Mobius (VDRNET) distribution then select the Save redaction policy on Mobius server
checkbox.
Mobius z/OS customers who use Mobius z/OS Redaction functionality for text reports for 3270 viewing or Mobius z/
OS distribution can continue using such redaction policies.
Review Mobius z/OS documentation on how to migrate Mobius z/OS redaction policies to Mobius View.
Assigning multiple redaction policies to content classes
You can assign multiple redaction policies to content class.
1. Select the required Redaction policy form the Redaction Policies screen.
2. Click Apply to apply the policy to the content class.
3. Click Preview to preview the documents with the applied redaction policies.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 267


## Page 268


An administrator can view all redaction policies created by clicking the All Policies entry in Content Classes list in the
Redaction screen.
The following image lists all the Redaction policies:
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 268


## Page 269


Redaction of Microsoft Office documents
Mobius View supports the redaction of Microsoft Office documents. Mobius View converts Microsoft Office
documents to PDF using the Microsoft Graph API during redaction.
Microsoft Graph API is used to access files on a Microsoft cloud document library, such as OneDrive. For more
information about working with files in Microsoft Graph, go to the Microsoft website. The PDFs are redacted and
returned to the client.
To use Microsoft Graph API, the system administrator must register the application in the Microsoft Azure Active
Directory. For more information about registering an application with the Microsoft identity platform, go to the
Microsoft website.
When using Microsoft Graph API, an Office document is uploaded to Microsoft OneDrive with an Office 365
subscription. The document is then downloaded back in PDF format deleting the Office document. Click the Start
icon on the left pane to open the Microsoft Office Integration screen.
Managing redaction policies
Management of Redaction Policies is associated with document classes and is performed using Mobius
Administrator.
1. Select Redaction in Mobius Administrator to view the list of document classes.
2. Select a document class to view the list of policies associated with that document class.
3. Hover over the policy name and click Edit to edit the policy.
4. Click the ellipses on the policy and select Remove Associations to remove the policy associated with the
document class.
5. Optional: Click Delete to delete the policy from the database.
You can select multiple policies within a document class to delete policies simultaneously.
6. To assign multiple redaction policies to content classes:
a. Select the required Redaction policy form the Redaction Policies screen.
b. Click Apply to apply the policy to the content class.
7. Click Preview to preview the list of policies.
You can select or clear the policies you want to apply in the preview window.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 269


## Page 270


8. To associate a policy to a document class:
a. Enter a policy name in the Enter policy name field.
You can also click the drop-down arrow in the Enter policy name field and select a required policy.
b. Click Apply Policy to associate the selected policy to the document class.
Creating redaction policies
Redaction policies can be created to redact different types of content, such as a page, a regular expression or text, a
particular region by setting redaction rules. Redaction policies are necessary to perform redaction.
1. Select Redaction in Mobius Administrator.
The list of document classes is displayed.
2. Select a document class to view the list of policies associated with that document class.
3. Click Create Policy on the Redaction Policies section to create a new redaction policy.
4. Click Add Rule.
5. Select Redact by pattern or Redact by area to specify the selection method.
6. Select a redaction template from the Select redaction template list.
Alternatively, you can click Show Advanced Options to define your custom pattern replacement.
7. Enter the pattern in the Use this pattern text box or select a format from alphabetic, digit, blank, non-blank,
and digit or blank options.
8. Select the Use RegEx checkbox to enter the regular expression you want to use in the pattern.
9. Select the Case Sensitive Match checkbox to match the case of the text.
10. Enter the text you want to replace in the To Replace With text box.
11. Select the Black Box radio button to replace the specified pattern with a black box.
Note:
◦ When you apply black box redaction for alphabets and if the document has content that is
already redacted with X, then X will be redacted with black box considering it as an
alphabet.
◦ The PDF licensing option must be enabled for image documents redaction and black box
redaction.
12. Set the selection scope to All Matches or First Match Only in the Select Scope field.
13. Perform one of the following in the Make Selection Area:
◦ Select the Use Entire Page checkbox to select the entire page as the selection area.
◦ Use the Relative to anchor points to provide values for the Top anchor and Bottom anchor.
◦ Select Fixed Position to select the selection area manually in the document.
14. Select Redact by area to perform redaction for a selected area.
15. Click Show Label/Hide Label to enable or disable field label.
16. Click Clear Rule to clear all suggested rules and create your own rules using Add Rule.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 270


## Page 271


17. Clear the specific rule checkbox in the Rules tab to view the document without the specified rules. All the rules
are selected by default.
18. Select the Use OCR to recognize text checkbox in the Settings tab to extract text from images.
Use OCR to recognize text checkbox is enabled for PDF documents and by default for images and cannot be
disabled. It is unchecked and disabled for other document types.
Note: The image quality affects the quality of the OCR. At times, OCR does not recognize
characters properly; it recognizes them as different characters or numbers or positions or aligns
them differently. To improve the quality of the OCR produced, scan the document at a higher
quality, using a better-quality scanner, or using a different font. The quality of extraction and
redaction results are directly correlated to the quality of the OCR performed.
19. Click Save or click Save As to save the policy.
Using OCR to recognize text
You can enable OCR to recognize text and extract the text from the images.
You can select the Use OCR to recognize text checkbox in the Settings tab to extract text from images.
• Use OCR to recognize text checkbox is enabled for PDF documents.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 271


## Page 272


• Use OCR to recognize text checkbox is enabled by default for images and cannot be disabled.
• Use OCR to recognize text checkbox is unchecked and disabled for other document types.
Note: The image quality affects the quality of the OCR. At times, OCR does not recognize characters
properly; it recognizes them as different characters or numbers or position or align them differently. In
order to improve the quality of the OCR produced, you can try scanning the document at a higher
quality, using a better-quality scanner, or using a different font. The quality of extraction and redaction
results are directly correlated to the quality of the OCR performed.
Add custom buttons and actions to the interface
Use Mobius Administrator to create custom buttons at the repository level to easily access custom features that you
create in Mobius View or custom actions that map to pre-defined Mobius View events.
Custom buttons act as a link to access a resource through a REST mapping for the selected document in the
repository. When the repository is deleted or removed, the associated custom buttons have to be deleted manually
to hide the display of custom buttons. The rules created by the system should not be edited.
You can create custom buttons for Mobius View, Content Automation, and for UI forms by selecting the options
available in the Action Mapping tab while creating a custom button. All the attributes configured in the Start step of
the Process under the message section must be passed for successful instantiation. When the user clicks the custom
button, the corresponding process is triggered and a toast message regarding process request submission is
displayed. You can navigate to the Inbox to view the triggered process.
Adding custom buttons for Mobius View
Create custom button in Mobius Administrator and associate it with a Mobius repository for a custom feature in
Mobius View.
In Mobius View, the custom button is displayed on all the pages, including the repository selection page, and enabled
based on relevant mapping when a user accesses the repository.
Note: When creating a custom button, a set of rules are created in the policy UI Governance screen to
control the hide button feature in Mobius View. Do not edit these rules.
1. Login to Mobius Administrator with valid credentials and click Custom Buttons from the menu.
2. Click Add to create a custom button and enter relevant details for the same.
3. Enter a name and description for the button in the Custom Button Name and Custom Button Description
fields, respectively.
4. Select an icon for the button from the Custom Button Icon dropdown.
5. Select the repository for which the button is configured from the Repository dropdown.
Note: If the repository is configured for prompt authentication and universal indexes are
disabled, you will be prompted to enter administrator credentials.
The Action Mapping tab is displayed on selecting the repository.
6. Select Invoke URL and enter the URL configuration details.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 272


## Page 273


a. Expand the On Action Response tab and select whether the response/response hyperlink must open in a
new tab or embedded pane.
7. Enter the URL and select the REST API method to use.
For POST or PUT method, the Payload section displays the below three tabs.
Sample Data Payload
Displays the sample data that defines the payload template.
Payload Template
Displays the template that is resolved when a Mobius View user clicks a custom button. If you edit the
template, the corresponding payload is generated. To define a complex payload structure, use the
payload mapping structure.
Generated Payload
Displays the sample output after the template is resolved. This output is used as the request body for
POST and PUT method calls.
8. Expand Params and Headers sections to add one or more parameters and headers for the custom button,
respectively.
a. Enter a name for the parameter/header in the text box available in the Name column.
b. Do either of the following to assign a value to the parameter/header.
i. Select a value from the dropdown in the Value column.
ii. Enable the checkbox available in the Customize column to enter a value manually in the text field
in theValue column.
The dropdown in the Value column becomes a text field on enabling the checkbox in the
Customize column.
iii. Click Add in the Action column to add more parameters.
9. Click Apply.
Adding custom buttons for Content Automation processes
Create custom buttons that map to various Content Automation processes.
1. Login to Mobius Administrator with valid credentials and click Custom Buttons from the menu.
2. Click Add to create a custom button and enter relevant details for the same.
3. Enter a name and description for the button in the Custom Button Name and Custom Button Description
fields, respectively.
4. Select an icon for the button from the Custom Button Icon dropdown.
5. Select the repository for which the button is configured from the Repository dropdown.
Note: If the repository is configured for prompt authentication and universal indexes are
disabled, you will be prompted to enter administrator credentials.
The Action Mapping tab is displayed on selecting the repository.
6. Select Start Process and enter the process configuration details.
a. Select the application name and the process name.
7. Select the application name and process name.
For Process involving index updates, the repository must be configured for the Enable Index Update option.
8. Map each field of the process in the Process Reference Mapping section.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 273


## Page 274


-prompt
Prompt the user to enter a value for the field.
_static
Map a design-time string value to the field.
_DocList
Select documents to display. After the process is triggered, the Studio user uses the document list to view
the selected documents.
9. Click Apply.
After you create a custom button, the UI Governance Decision table displays the button name with Enable and
Disable options. Use these options to hide or display the custom button.
Adding custom buttons for UI forms
Create custom buttons to open UI forms that can perform actions such as triggering a process, web service, or AI.
1. Log in to Mobius Administrator with valid credentials and click Custom Buttons from the menu.
2. Click Add to create a custom button and enter relevant details for the same.
3. Enter a name and description for the button in the Custom Button Name and Custom Button Description
fields, respectively.
4. Select an icon for the button from the Custom Button Icon dropdown.
5. Select the repository for which the button is configured from the Repository dropdown.
Note: If the repository is configured for prompt authentication and universal indexes are
disabled, you will be prompted to enter administrator credentials.
The Action Mapping tab is displayed on selecting the repository.
6. Select Invoke Form and enter the process configuration details.
Note: The Invoke Form option is displayed only when the Content Automation license is
enabled in the Authorization Code page.
7. Expand the Invoke Form Configuration section, select a project or an application from the App Name
dropdown and a UI form from the UI Form Select dropdown.
8. Expand the On Action Response tab and select whether the form must open in a new tab or embedded pane.
9. Click Apply.
When a Mobius View user clicks the button, the form is displayed.
Adding custom actions for Mobius View events
Create custom actions to map to predefined Mobius View events.
A created custom action is added to the Custom Actions pane in Mobius View and is enabled by default.
1. Log in to Mobius Administrator with valid credentials and click Custom Actions from the menu.
2. Click Add to create a custom action and enter relevant details for the same.
3. Enter a name and description for the action in the Custom Action Name and Custom Action Description
fields, respectively.
4. Select an event from the Event to initiate Custom Action dropdown.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 274


## Page 275


5. Select the required action from the Action dropdown.
6. Expand the Action Mapping tab and enter the mapping URL and select the REST API method to use.
For POST or PUT method, the Payload section displays the below three tabs.
Sample Data Payload
Displays the sample data that defines the payload template.
Payload Template
Displays the template that is resolved when a Mobius View user clicks a custom button. If you edit the
template, the corresponding payload is generated. To define a complex payload structure, use the
payload mapping structure.
Generated Payload
Displays the sample output after the template is resolved. This output is used as the request body for
POST and PUT method calls.
7. Expand Params and Headerssections to add one or more parameters and headers for the custom button,
respectively.
a. Enter a name for the parameter/header in the text box available in the Name column.
b. Do either of the following to assign a value to the parameter/header.
i. Select a value from the dropdown in the Value column.
ii. Enable the checkbox available in the Customize column to enter a value manually in the text field
in theValue column.
The dropdown in the Value column becomes a text field on enabling the checkbox in the
Customize column.
iii. Click the add icon in the Action column to add more paramaters.
8. Click Apply.

## Page 276


Searching through content
In Mobius View, users can find, view, and operate on documents. To find relevant documents in a repository, Mobius
users can browse a repository’s explicit hierarchy, or search for them in Mobius View. To use full-text search, enable
this function in Mobius (Server) Administrator.
The following diagram shows the position of this task within the workflow of common administrative tasks. The
colored box indicates the starting point, while the dashed outline box represents an optional task.

Many non-Mobius repositories, for example, CMIS and SharePoint repositories, support full-text search by default.
For Mobius repositories, an administrator must enable full-text search. For repositories that are enabled for full-text
search, users can create a search query in Mobius View by combining full text and defined fields to find all documents
that meet the specified criteria. A user can also search for documents based on an index, content class, or section
names.
Searching through content in a repository might also search through all document text. Documents typically have
associated index data and document content. A full-text search goes through archived content and metadata fields
across repositories.
When full-text search is not used, only the document metadata fields in a repository are searched for the specified
text.
Full-Text Search (FTS) for content extraction
Indexing is essential for full-text searches. Users enter text as part of a search query, which then searches for the text
in archived content and metadata fields. When full-text search (FTS) is enabled, content matching the search query is
extracted and indexed along with the metadata.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 276


## Page 277


Mobius supports full text extraction from any of these format types:
• Text, CSV, XML, PDF and PostScript file types
• PDF and PostScript reports
• Printer Command Language (PCL) and ACT! Lookup Format (ALF) (v1 & v2) files
• Xerox and Advanced Function Presentation (AFP) files
• Check Processing Formats
• Metadata extracted from images
Archiving using “image list” processing ensures that the file extensions are recognized by the full text extraction,
which means that these file types are full-text indexed. With image list archives, policy parsing is not applied yet, so
only the information outside the file is available. With full-text search, you can also search within the file content.
The following lists all associated document types and their respective file extensions.
• Microsoft Word (.doc, .docx)
• Microsoft PowerPoint (.ppt, .pptx)
• Microsoft Excel (.xls, .xlsx)
• Adobe Acrobat (.pdf)
• Extensible Markup Language (.xml)
• HyperText Markup Language (.html)
• ASCII text-formatted (UTF-8) data (.txt)
• Unicode (.utf)
Many repositories support full-text search. Mobius repositories must be indexed first to enable full-text search to
work. Use the following command line clients for full-text indexing on Mobius repositories:
• Legacy full-text indexing commands
Use legacy FTS commands on repositories with existing content that are not full-text indexed. Put existing
archives into full-text index by using legacy client command ftijobsubmit to specify a subset of an archive to
be indexed. Use the ftijobquery, ftijobdelete, and ftijobcancel commands to cancel index jobs, delete
report versions, or query index jobs.
• MobiusRemoteCLI full-text indexing commands
MobiusRemote CLI is a command line utility you use to perform operations against Mobius Server. This utility
communicates through Mobius View using public REST APIs. FTS CLI commands consists of MobiusRemoteCLI
ftijobsubmit, ftijobquery, ftijobdelete, and ftijobcancel.
In Mobius (Server) Administrator, configure new repositories for full-text index as it archives.
Once Mobius repositories are full-text indexed, enable full-text search on Mobius repositories for a full-text search
engine to locate and retrieve documents. With full-text indexing:
• Content is organized by indexing for faster searches. A search engine references the index to locate
documents.
• A search engine provides better search results and as the amount of content grows, the search performance
remains consistent.
• An indexed repository helps users find the information they are looking for.
Full-Text Search (FTS) engines provide the essential infrastructure for Full-Text Search (FTS) support. However,
Mobius View handles the indexing and search. To use full-text search functionality, first deploy Solr or Elasticsearch,
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 277


## Page 278


which are full-text search engines. Then connect Mobius to the full-text search engine through Mobius (Server)
Administrator.
For container deployment, use Elasticsearch. For non-container deployment, select either Solr or Elasticsearch.
Related information
Command line commands to manage Full Text and Simple Search
MobiusRemoteCli commands
Configuring full-text search
To use full-text search, enable a full-text search engine on a repository level, such as Solr or Elasticsearch. In Mobius
View, users build and run search queries where the underlying full-text search engine searches for documents that
meet the search criteria.
Before you begin: Mobius View and Mobius Server are installed and running.
If you have multiple content sources referring to the same database, configuring the database for one content source
will configure it for all other content sources.
1. From the left pane, click Mobius Server > Mobius Content Sources.
a. Click a content source to view its repositories.
b. To set up FTS for a repository, click a repository.
c. Hover over a repository, then click Settings.
2. Configure the Full Text and Simple Search section.
a. Expand the Full Text and Simple Search section.
b. Select Solr or Elasticsearch as the full-text search engine.
c. Select either HTTP or HTTPS from the FTS Protocol drop-down list.
d. Enter the values for FTS Server Port, FTS Host Name, and FTS Index Name. If a custom index is
configured, the FTS Index Name field displays the custom index name.
e. Select Set to the current Mobius instance to set the collection information to the Mobius server.
If the collection information does not exist in the database, Mobius creates a collection name which is a
version of the JDBC URL with the addition of a 10-digit string.
3. Select the following options in the Full Text and Simple Search Search Control and Indexing Control sections.
To enable simple text search, select Allow simple searches of document metadata.
To enable Full Text Search (FTS), select Allow full text searches of document content.
Note: To prevent specific content classes from being indexed, configure the content class
definition to disable metadata or content indexing.
To enable automatic indexing, select Automatically create index values and content during archiving for
full text and simple search.
Automatic indexing indexes only the extracted content and indexes saved to the database configured for the
content source. Also, enabling automatic indexes affects only the new archives. Index existing data using legacy
full-text indexing commands or MobiusRemoteCLI full-text indexing commands.
4. Click Apply.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 278

Distribution Services panel to distribute the packets to specific recipients.

Schedule a recurring delivery or create a one-time delivery:
Recurring delivery
The administrator specifies a list of content classes, selects the output channel to be used, a list of recipients,
and a schedule with the repeat frequency. A delivery is initiated when a required content class is archived in
Mobius and is distributed when all the required content classes are gathered and the scheduled time is met.
One-time delivery
The administrator specifies a list of content classes and versions to be used for the delivery, selects the output
channel to be used, a list of recipients and a scheduled/immediate delivery type. A delivery is initiated
immediately or when the scheduled time is met and distributed to the specified recipients.
Distribution Services contains the following modules:
Content Class
A category of documents. The time stamp on each archive when it is created acts as the unique identifier of the
content class version.
Delivery
Set up and schedule email and print distributions. Define the content classes that need to be included for
distribution, recipients, and when they are to be released for distribution.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 279


## Page 280


Create an immediate or delayed delivery of content classes using their existing content. Trigger immediate,
periodical, or manual delivery of content classes when new content arrives.
Packet
Contains details about an individual recipient of delivery, including which sections of a content class, how to
arrange and present these sections, and how and when to deliver them.
Recipients and recipient groups
Recipients are Mobius users or groups of users who receive print or email distributions. A user created or added
in the Mobius server is added to the Distribution Services as a recipient either during the repository conversion,
or automatically after a repository has been converted.
A recipient group in Distribution Services maps to one user group in Mobius. The creation and deletion of
recipient groups is synchronized with the creation and deletion of user groups in the Mobius server. When a
recipient group is included as a recipient in a delivery, a distribution is created for each member of the group.
Each recipient member of the group receives a distribution to the recipient's default email address. You can
only receive email distribution with attachment type as Link when using recipient groups.
Manage the group's preferences, including adding a new preference for email links only, deleting a preference,
using a preference to tag a delivery and untagging a preference from a delivery.
Templates
Email templates specify the dynamic and personalized content to be used in an email subject, body and
attachment name and type. Print templates specify different template files that are added to the selected
content to form a print stream.
Output Channels
Manage output channels, such as email and print, and configure print agents and email server connections.
Distribution Status
View and take actions on the email distributions in two different viewing modes – view by delivery and view by
recipient.
Release multiple delivery instances pertaining to a delivery definition, which is defined to be manually triggered,
using the release manual distributions option. To release only one instance, use the reschedule or release
action option.
Re-deliver a delivery instance and create a new one-time delivery containing the same content to deliver to
either the existing set of recipients or to newly selected recipients and either to existing output channels or to
newly selected output channels at a specified schedule – immediately or a future date and time.
Print deliveries
Configure print output channels, create print templates, configure recipients, and schedule print distributions.
Mail deliveries
Configure email output channels, create email templates, configure recipients, and schedule email
distributions.
Configuring recipients for content deliveries
A recipient in Distribution Services is created either during repository migration or after the migration is
completed.
Repository migration
To continue using existing Mobius server distribution settings in Distribution Services, run a repository
migration that converts a Mobius repository by loading Mobius information into Distribution Services. Once
migration is complete, upgrade to a new version of Mobius server and continue using Distribution Services
without having to perform any migration activity again.
Auditing events for Distribution Services
Distribution Services generates various types of audit events.
Known issues
Review the known issues and workarounds for Distribution Services.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 280


## Page 281


Print deliveries
Configure print output channels, create print templates, configure recipients, and schedule print distributions.
1. Configuring external print agents
The print agent is the gateway between Distribution Services and the printers. Add print agents to Distribution
Services manually or automatically by migrating a repository through conversion.
2. Configuring printers for content distribution
Add and configure Windows and network printers to use printers for content distribution.
3. Print templates
Print templates specify different template files that are added to the selected content to form a print stream.
4. Creating print templates for content distribution
Define a template that can be used while printing. With pre-defined variables, you can use the metadata about
delivery, recipients, and content classes in the print templates.
Configuring external print agents
The print agent is the gateway between Distribution Services and the printers. Add print agents to Distribution
Services manually or automatically by migrating a repository through conversion.
Before you begin: Deploy an external print agent.
Any fields for a print agent added as part of conversion/migration are read-only.
1. Select Distribution Services in Mobius Administrator, and then click the Output Channels tile.
2. Select All External Agents from the Agent drop-down list.
3. Click Add on the left panel and configure the following fields in the Add an Output Agent panel.
a. Enter a name for the agent.
b. Select Standalone from the Agent type drop-down list.
c. Enter the URL of the standalone print agent in the Address field in the following format:
http(s)://<hostNameorIP>:<port>/<pathname>/printrest
where,
▪ hostNameorIP is the name or the IP address of the host on which the standalone print agent is
running.
▪ port is the port number on which the standalone print agent is running on the host machine.
▪ pathname is the path of the context where the standalone print agent is running.
For example:
http://localhost:8090/printagent/printrest
d. Select the Authentication type based on the configuration on which the standalone print agent is
running.
Note: For a standalone print agent, the Authentication type field and its related fields are
editable. Any changes might take up to two minutes to take effect.
4. Click Save.
Related information
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 281


## Page 282


Deploying the stand-alone print agent
Configuring printers for content distribution
Add and configure Windows and network printers to use printers for content distribution.
Before you begin: A print agent is already configured and deployed.
If the number of copies in a job is more than one:
• When printing to a Mobius/Windows printer, the print agent sends the same job multiple times (same as copy
number) to the printer.
• When printing to a File printer, many copies are created with a <<Uniquefile>> tag in the filename field.
Without the <<Uniquefile>> tag in the filename field, the same file gets overwritten every time for as many
copies.
1. Select Distribution Services in Mobius Administrator, and then click the Output Channels tile.
2. Select Print from the Agent drop-down list.
3. Select a print agent from the list displayed on the left panel.
4. Click Add Printer.
5. Enter a name for the printer in the Add new Printer panel
6. Select a printer type from the Type drop-down list. For Windowsor Network printer type, do the following:
a. Select Passthrough and LPD for Windows and SOCKET_IO for Network from the Connection Protocol
drop-down.
b. Enter the name of the actual Windows Printer for Windows and hostname or IP address of the system on
which the Network printer is available for Network in the Address field.
7. Select a Print language from the drop-down list.
Supported languages are:
◦ Windows: Postscript_L2, PCL_5, AFP, PDF, and Text
◦ File: Postscript_L2, PCL_5, AFP, PDF, Text, and Native
◦ Network: Postscript_L2
a. Enter the Port number when the printer type is selected as Network.
8. Click Add.
Related information
Deploying the Mobius print agent (non-container deployment)
Print templates
Print templates specify different template files that are added to the selected content to form a print stream.
If a Mobius repository is migrated and the Mobius server has any print distribution groups defined with a packet
customization sub-directory, those distribution groups get converted into print templates.
Note: Applies to earlier versions of Mobius where templates are created based on unique sub-
directories as part of the print distribution group's settings defined in the Mobius server.
The following table lists each supported variable, and the field where it can be used in a print template.

Print template example
In a print distribution with a file configured as the output, you can setup the associated print template that the final
output file name also has the name of the system on which the print agent is running. Use the %COMPUTERNAME%
pre-defined variable in the OutputFileTemplate.Txt template file configured for the print distribution.
You can select File from the Use Template drop-down list under Templates > Print Template > Template Pages,
and then you can select OutputFileTemplate.Txt from the Mobius file name drop-down list.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 283


## Page 284


For a File printer, you must provide a valid path where the output file needs to be stored, and that value is defined in
the OutputFileTemplate.Txt file. In the File Content field, you must provide the path of the output file, using some
of the pre-defined variables. You can also provide the %COMPUTERNAME% pre-defined variable name in the File
Content field to get the system name as part of the final output file name.
For example, the file path for OutputFileTemplate.Txt can be: C:\Mobius_VDR\Output\
File_<<RecipientId>>_%COMPUTERNAME%_<<UniqueFile>>.pdf.
Creating print templates for content distribution
Define a template that can be used while printing. With pre-defined variables, you can use the metadata about
delivery, recipients, and content classes in the print templates.
1. Select Distribution Services in Mobius Administrator.
2. Click the Templates tile, and then select Print Template from the Define drop-down list.
3. Click Add.
4. Enter a name for the template.
Select Default to use the default print template.
5. Select Use Template > File from the Template Pages section.
◦ Click Add New to add a new file.
◦ Select a Mobius file name from the drop-down list and click Add Content.
6. Edit the File Content text box that displays the file content based on the chosen Mobius file name.
For a file printer, provide a path where the output file needs to be stored, and that value is defined in the
OutputFileTemplate.Txt file. After a successful print distribution to this printer, access the output file from
the path defined in this template file.
Note: Any changes made here are only stored inside Distribution Services. The actual file content
is not changed.
7. Click Save.
Related information
Print templates
Mail deliveries
Configure email output channels, create email templates, configure recipients, and schedule email distributions.
1. Configuring email output channels
Use the email output channel to configure SMTP server details for sending out the emails. Only one built-in
email agent is available to configure the connection.
2. Email templates
Email templates specify the dynamic and personalized content to be used in an email subject, body, and
attachment name and type.
3. Creating email templates for content distribution
Define a template that can be used while sending an email. To deliver content to recipients as an attachment
or a link in an email, creating an email template is required.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 284


## Page 285


Configuring email output channels
Use the email output channel to configure SMTP server details for sending out the emails. Only one built-in email
agent is available to configure the connection.
1. Select Distribution Services in Mobius Administrator, and then click the Output Channels tile.
2. Enter SMTP Server Address (DNS or IP address) under Configuration sub-section.
3. Select a protocol for encryption from the Protocol drop-down list.
If this field is set to None, unencrypted transportation is used in the service.
4. Enter the port number in Port
The default value is 25.
5. Select the Authentication required checkbox to turn on authentication to the SMTP server.
User credentials are required to log in to the SMTP server.
6. Click Test Email to send out a test email with or without a PDF attachment.
7. Configure the email settings:
a. Enter a valid email address in the From field.
If the Email Settings in Mobius Administrator are updated and the From address field is also set, then
the From field in the Test Email panel gets pre-filled with that address.
For details about configuring the email settings in Mobius Administrator, see Configuring email.
b. Enter a valid email address in the To field.
If the auto-generated email address is enabled and a domain address is set in the 'Email Settings' of the
Mobius Administrator, then the email address of the currently logged in user is formed and pre-filled in
the To field of the Test Email.
c. Enter the information in the Subject, Body fields and add a PDF attachment to send with the email.
d. Click Send Test Email to deliver the test email to the email addresses mentioned in the To field using the
SMTP server configured.
8. Click Save.
Related information
Configuring email
Email templates
Email templates specify the dynamic and personalized content to be used in an email subject, body, and attachment
name and type.
If a Mobius repository is migrated and that Mobius server has email distribution groups defined with a packet
customization sub-directory, those distribution groups get converted into email templates with pre-defined values for
the Subject and Body fields.
Note: Applies to earlier versions of Mobius where templates are created based on unique sub-
directories as part of the email distribution group's settings defined in the Mobius server.
You can also create a new template entirely in Distribution Services and use pre-defined variables that you can reuse
multiple times. These variables are formatted as {{_variable_name}}.
Each variable_name is replaced with the metadata that it points to. Typing {{ in a template field displays a list of
variables for selection.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 285

Field Variable
