When using variables:
• Use {{_contentType}} and {{_contentVersion}} within the {{#content}}{{/content}} list tags.
• Use {{_sectionID}} within the {{#sections}}{{/sections}} list tags.
• If the attachment type in a delivery is Link, the output preference for each recipient must be set in an email
template as a Link variable in the Email body field. If the preference is not Link, the recipient receives an email
that does not include a link.
• To generate a link that is included in the email received from Distribution Services, use {{#_link}}{{/_link}}
and {{#content}}{{/content}} variables in the email template's message body field. There are two types of
links - one for the whole content of that ingestion, and one for each section available in the ingestion. For a
section link, use {{#sections}}{{/sections}} tag combinations in the email template message body field.
The following example starts with {{#_link}} at the top, followed by {{#content}} and {{_sections}}. The
example also includes a report link URL, which is a DirectConnect URL that provides host and port information, and
report or section link information.
{{#_link}}
{{#content}}
Report Link:- Report Link:-
http://localhost:8080/mobius/directconnect?serverID={{_docServerId}}&recipientID={{_recipientI
d}}&loginPrompt=2&redirect=TRUE&reportID={{_contentId}}&versionID={{_contentVersion}}&noExplore
r=1&quickOpen=1&type=1
SECTIONS:- SECTIONS:-
{{#sections}}
* {{_sectionID}}
Section Link:- Section Link:-
http://localhost:8080/mobius/directconnect?serverID={{_docServerId}}&recipientID={{_recipientI
d}}&loginPrompt=2&redirect=TRUE&reportID={{_contentId}}&versionID={{_contentVersion}}&noExplore
r=1&quickOpen=1&type=1&rptsectID={{_sectionID}}
{{/sections}}
{{/content}}
{{/_link}}
Related information
Accessing Mobius content with DirectConnect
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 287


## Page 288


Creating email templates for content distribution
Define a template that can be used while sending an email. To deliver content to recipients as an attachment or a link
in an email, creating an email template is required.
1. Select Distribution Services in Mobius Administrator, and then click the Templates tile.
2. Click Add.
3. Enter a name for the template.
Select the Default checkbox to use the default email template.
4. Optional: Fill the fields in the Address section.
By default, the template uses the sender's email address. Enter a from address and a reply-to address to
override the default behavior.
5. Configure the fields in the Message section.
a. Write the subject of the email in the Subject field.
b. Write the body of email in the Body field.
You can insert metadata using a set of pre-defined variables.
Enter text, variables, or a combination of text and variables in the Subject and Body fields.
To enter a variable, typing {{  in the field is required. Then select the variable from the list that is displayed.
An example of text and variables entered in the Subject field:
A new {{_deliveryName}} delivery is available for {{_recipientName}}
For more information about using variables, see Email templates.
6. Enter the Attachment name and select the Packing options .
Attachment name
All the reports that are sent as an attachment in the email use this name. If more than one report is to be
sent, then the Attachment name is appended with a number for each attachment. For example, an
attachment name is set as 'Test Attachment', then in the email, each attachment will have names such as
'Test Attachment.pdf', 'Test Attachment (1).pdf', 'Test Attachment (2).pdf', and so on.
Packing options
Decides how a section from the actual content should get delivered to the recipient.
7. Click Preview to see how the variables display in the email.
The metadata is replaced in the final email that is generated from an email channel delivery.
◦ Preview to validate the content in the body field. Some styles may not be displayed.
◦ In the Body field, color and background color options are removed for security purposes. You can
manually set them by adding styles directly in the body text.
8. Click Save.
Related information
Email templates
Configuring recipients for content deliveries
A recipient in Distribution Services is created either during repository migration or after the migration is completed.
• During a Mobius repository migration, all the users that are present in the Mobius server get added as
recipients in Distribution Services after migration is completed. The recipient's name is a combination of the
repository name and the User ID of the user from Mobius server. The format for the recipient is
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 288


## Page 289


<RepositoryID>.<UserID>. If an email address was also provided for the user in Mobius server, the same
email address is added as a default email address in the Distribution Services Recipient record.
• After a Mobius repository migration, any new users added to the Mobius server will be added as recipients in
Distribution Services either by manually clicking the Sync with Mobius Server option on the Recipients panel
or automatically added at midnight.
Notes:
• If there is more than one email address assigned to a recipient, then this is displayed as a number
along with the first email address that is available for that recipient.
For example, if a recipient has two email addresses – admin@example.com and
admin2@example.com – it is displayed as "admin@example.com, +1".
• If there is more than one printer assigned to a recipient, then this is displayed as a number along
with the first printer name that is available for that recipient.
For example, if a recipient has two printers – demoRepo.PA/demoRepo.D1 and demoRepo.PA/
demoRepo.D2 – then it is displayed as "demoRepo.PA/demoRepo.D1, +1".
Printers (where required) can be assigned to a recipient manually by editing the recipient once it
is available in the Distribution Services.
1. In Mobius Administrator, select Distribution Services from the left menu.
2. On the Distribution Services landing page, click the Recipients and Groups tile and select any recipient from
the list.
3. Optional: Search for the required Mobius User ID under the Attributes section and select the ID from the list.
This Mobius UserID field links the Distribution Services recipient with the Mobius server user. This field
retrieves only IDs that have not been previously linked with an existing Distribution Services recipient.
4. Configure the Output Addresses sections.
a. Click Add New in the Emails section, and enter a valid email address for this recipient.
To make this the default, slide the toggle switch.
b. Optional: Search for the printers in the Printers section and select them from the list.
To make this the default printer for the recipient, slide the toggle switch.
5. Configure the Output Preferences section.
Note: This section is enabled only if the recipient has at least one email address or one printer
added to it.
a. Click Add in the Preferences section to add output channel- based preferences to the recipient.
b. Select or enter the information in the Create a new preference panel and click Create.
◦ Email as the preference type:
▪ The optional Use Template packing option toggle switch is set to Yes by default, and it uses the
Packing Options set in the Email Template that is assigned in this preference. If this field is set to
No, the Packing Options defined in the Delivery level is used while making the email distribution.
▪ Under the Deliveries field, search for any of the existing recurring deliveries that are available in
the Distribution Services. Search and add more than one delivery in this field. Once a delivery is
selected, view the reports and sections for each report. Once a delivery is selected, deleting the
same is also possible.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 289


## Page 290


◦ Print as the preference type:
Copies
Enter values between 1 and 1000. By default, the value is 1.
Sequence
Enter values between 1 and 1000. By default, the value is 50. The preference for having a lower
sequence number is printed first when there are multiple print jobs being created simultaneously.
Section
Enter the section name. To include all sections, enter '*'.
Sequence Number
Enter values between 1 and 5000. By default, the value is 5000. The section having a lower
sequence number is added first in the final print stream.
The Add Section field is displayed once you have tagged a delivery to this preference and selected that
delivery. You can add more than one section. If more than one content class are available in the Delivery, you
can add sections to all the content classes.
Note: Any sections added here only work as a filter to the actual access rights that have been
added to the User in the Mobius server. Removing any section details from this screen will not
affect the actual access rights that the user has in Mobius server.
6. Click Update Recipient.
Synchronizing distribution recipients with Mobius users and groups
Add a new user or user group from the Mobius server, and add them as recipients in Distribution Services using the
synchronize option.
Note: Any user or group that has been modified or deleted in the Mobius server does not get modified
or deleted in the Distribution Services. Synchronizing works only one way, from Mobius server to
Distribution Services. If you modify the recipients in Distribution Services later, the changes are not
synchronized with Mobius server.
1. In Mobius Administrator, select Distribution Services from the left menu.
2. From the Distribution Services landing page, click Recipients and Groups.
3. Click Sync with Mobius Server and view the newly added recipient.
Note: Adding a new recipient does not carry forward any report access rights into the
Distribution Services; you must manually create the output preferences and tag them to their
corresponding delivery.
Scheduling email and print deliveries
Email and print deliveries define which content classes must be included for distribution together, for which
recipients, and when they are to be released for distribution.
1. Click + Delivery to create a new delivery definition that is visible within the Deliveries tile in the Distribution
Services screen.
Alternatively, click Deliveries > Add Delivery.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 290


## Page 291


2. Enter a delivery name in the Delivery section.
3. Search for one or more content classes in the Content section.
Selected content classes are added. Reorder the content classes using the arrow icons.
4. Configure the Schedule section as follows.
a. Select a delivery type in the Schedule section after selecting at least one content class:
▪ Existing Content - Uses the already archived ingestions of the selected reports.
▪ When all selected Content arrives - Waits for new ingestions of the selected reports to arrive.
▪ Deferred - Does not trigger any new delivery distribution even when new content versions arrive.
Use this delivery type to create a new one-time delivery keeping this delivery definition as a base.
b. Select a delivery schedule.
The options vary depending on the selected delivery type.
Existing Content
▪ Immediately - The delivery is triggered immediately with all the ingestions that are selected.
This is a one-time activity.
▪ Delayed deliver - The delivery needs to be triggered at the pre-defined date and time with
all the ingestions that are selected. If this time is in the past, the delivery is immediately
triggered. This is a one-time activity.
Note: At the end of creating this delivery, a new entry is only displayed in the
Distribution Status screen.
When all selected Content arrives
▪ Immediately - The delivery needs to be triggered repeatedly and immediately after new
ingestions for all the selected reports has arrived.
▪ Periodically - The delivery needs to be triggered repeatedly at the pre-defined date and time
after new ingestions for all the selected reports have arrived. This trigger uses Quartz library-
based Cron expressions created on the basis of selected options.
▪ Manually - No new delivery is triggered. Such a delivery can only be triggered manually later.
c. Select the existing ingestions that need to be distributed using the Select Ingestion field.
The Select Ingestion field is only displayed when the delivery type is Existing Content.
Selecting the Not yet delivered checkbox displays the ingestions within the given date range that have
not yet been distributed. Otherwise, it displays all the ingestions within the given date range, that were
either distributed already or not yet distributed.
5. Select at least one of the output channels or both under the Output Channels section.
The default number of copies and the minimum required for the print is one, while the maximum is 1000
copies.
6. Search and add a sender from the list of available recipients from the Sender section.
7. Search and select the recipients to whom the content classes need to be distributed.
The Select Recipients field is required only when the Delivery Type is selected as Existing Content,
otherwise this field is optional.
a. Click a recipient to view the recipient's available preferences to configure a recipient to have one or
many output preferences.
b. Select one or more preferences from the available preferences for a recipient.
Preferences are read-only. Once a delivery is added or updated, editing or deleting the preferences or
sections is only possible from the Recipients page.
▪ If no preference is selected for a recipient, then the output channel parameters set for the delivery
in the Output Channels section is used during the distribution.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 291


## Page 292


▪ If a preference is selected for a recipient, it overrides the output channel parameters set for the
delivery.
▪ If more than one preferences are selected for a recipient, then the recipients receives that many
distributions of the delivery.
Note: If a delivery or recipient's preference has the attachment type as Link, the selected
email template has its email body field updated with a correct link. If no link is defined, the
recipient receives an email without any link to the content class or the sections.
8. Click Add Delivery.
Repository migration
To continue using existing Mobius server distribution settings in Distribution Services, run a repository migration that
converts a Mobius repository by loading Mobius information into Distribution Services. Once migration is complete,
upgrade to a new version of Mobius server and continue using Distribution Services without having to perform any
migration activity again.
If you decide not to use any existing Distribution settings, activate Distribution Services to use this feature.
Migration through conversion
Conversion is a two-step process. First, the data from the Mobius server is migrated and second, the migrated
data is loaded into Distribution Services.
Activation
Activation does not bring any existing distribution configuration from the underlying Mobius Server. It only
stores the content classes and recipients from the Mobius Server that is then used to create the delivery
definitions for distribution
Note: Before migrating a repository, make sure the built-in print agent in Mobius Server is up and
running. Otherwise, the repository migration in Distribution Services fails.
Migration process
The Distribution Services landing page displays a table with Mobius repositories with a migration status. For a first
time migration, a repository's migration status is "Action required".
Select an action:
• Convert
This action changes the status to "Converting" and displays a progress bar displaying the current loading
step.
Clicking Convert in the displayed pop-up changes the status of that repository to "Converting". If another
repository's migration is underway, then this repository’s status is in "Waiting" and once the existing
migration completes, this repository is automatically picked for migration and the status changes to
"Converting". Once the migration is completed, the repository is removed from the banner list.
Once a Mobius repository is migrated, it is added to the Content Repositories panel of the Distribution
Services landing list. This will take about three minutes. After migration completes or is bypassed, it takes
a maximum of three minutes for that repository to be removed from the banner list.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 292


## Page 293


Note:
◦ During migration, if the User ID or Email address or Device name or Template (email
or print) name contains any of these characters – " [ ] , : ; | = + * ? < > / / % – then they
are replaced with their corresponding hexadecimal code suffixed with ^. For example,
a name like mobius.!@#$%^&*().zip is converted to mobius.!@#$^25^&^2A().zip.
Any non-printable control characters in any of the above fields, are replaced with an
_(underscore).
◦ When a repository migration is underway, and any system issue occurs, wait for 30
minutes before restarting the Mobius View for the conversion to pick-up from where
it had got stuck. Starting the Mobius View before 30 minutes may cause the
conversion to stay stuck.
• Activate
The status changes to "Activating". This option enables Distribution Services for the selected repository.
Only email distribution is enabled. For print distribution, add an external print agent and configure the
required printers on it and use them in the print distribution.
• Simulate Conversion
Resources in the repository are analyzed and used during the conversion process. No conversion is done.
Once complete, the Simulation Results button is enabled.
Click Simulation Results, then download to view the tabulated results of total number of printers, email
groups, print groups, recipients, recipient groups, content classes and total content class access rights
along with the name of the content class having the maximum access rights.
The Content Repositories panel lists the converted repositories configured for distribution, repository status, and
possible actions. For each converted or activated repository, actions are:
• Rollback
Deletes all the recipients, recipient groups, delivery definitions, delivery instances, content classes and
any converted printers and templates for that repository from Distribution Services. A rollback cannot be
stopped. The repository is then removed from the Content Repositories list. Once the rollback has
completed, this repository is displayed in the Distribution Services landing page and it is available for
Conversion/Activation again.
• Stop Polling
Used when you want to create a new ingestion, but do not want to trigger any recurring deliveries for
Distribution. Once selected, it changes to Resume Polling. Once polling has been stopped, unless you
resume the polling, no new delivery distributions are triggered. The maximum time for the polling to stop
is one minute from the time it was selected.
• Resume Polling
Resumes the ingestion polling after it was stopped. Resuming the polling brings in all the unused
ingestions from the time the polling was stopped, and the option is changed back to Stop Polling. All
newly polled ingestions trigger any recurring delivery. Polling starts again within a maximum of one
minute of selecting this option.
Guidelines to convert a content class into a delivery
During the conversion, use these guidelines to convert a content class into a recurring delivery in the Distribution
Services.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 293


## Page 294


• For a recipient, one output preference is created for each Distribution Group configured in content class access
rights, including email distribution, print distribution, and email notification.
• In Mobius Server, if the recipient is using both email distribution and email notification namely, PDF/Native and
Link, then an output preference is created for each setting and added to the delivery. If they have print
distribution, that is also added as preference and tagged to the delivery.
• If the content class has the same recipients more than once with different sections and the same settings, then
the output preferences are created based on the number of unique section code access rights available and
tagged to the delivery.
• If the content class has the same recipients more than once with different sections and different settings, then
the output preferences are created with combination of unique setting and section code access rights and
tagged to the delivery.
Converting repositories
Convert repositories as part of migrating existing Mobius information into Distribution Services. All existing Mobius
templates, content classes, users, devices, and distribution groups will be loaded into Distribution Services.
Before you begin:
• Configure the print agent.
• Ensure that the repositories created in the earlier version of Mobius are available.
1. In Mobius Administrator, select Distribution Services from the left menu.
2. On the Distribution Services panel, click Convert next to each repository that you want to convert, on the
landing page.
Depending on the underlying data on the content source, the conversion time varies. If the conversion is
successful, the repository name is removed from the landing page.
3. Click the Content Repositories link on the landing page to view a list of the successfully converted
repositories.
4. Click Rollback beside the repository name.
If the conversion fails, check the Mobius log file for information about the failure.
5. Click Convert to convert the repository after correcting the issues reported in the log file.
Related information
Deploying the Mobius print agent (non-container deployment)
Deploying the Mobius print agent (container deployment)
Mobius repository post-migration
After migrating a Mobius repository into Distribution Services, review the following changes to existing print
templates, mail templates, and ingestions.
• In Distribution Services, a recipient is created for each user in the Mobius server. The recipient should contain
the email address from the Mobius user and a list of printers that are configured in the user's access rights for
receiving print distribution.
• The recipient’s name is a combination of the repository name and the user ID of the user from Mobius Server
and is in the following format - RepositoryID.UserID. An email address that was provided for the user in Mobius
Server is added as a default email address in the Distribution Services recipient record.
• Any new user added in Mobius Server will be added as a recipient in Distribution Services either by manually
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 294


## Page 295


clicking the Sync with Mobius Server option on the Recipients page or automatically added at midnight.
• The number of recipient group is equal to the number of Mobius user group.
• A printer is created in the print agent for each Mobius device.
• A print agent gets added automatically for every converted repository.
• An email and print template are created for each unique sub-directories used in email and print distribution
groups. These templates reflect the content in Mobius template files.
• A recurring delivery for email and print is created for each content class whose access rights have email or
notification distribution, or print distribution.
• A deferred delivery for email is created for each email and print distribution group that is used at least once in
an email or notification distribution, or print distribution.
• All versions of all content classes in the Mobius server are available for a one-time distribution.
Once a Mobius repository migration through conversion is complete in Distribution Services, it is possible to rollback
the conversion and delete all records created by the conversion. You can restart a conversion after a previous
conversion is rolled back.
If a Mobius repository is migrated through conversion and that Mobius Server had email and print Distribution
Groups defined with a packet customization sub-directory, then those distribution groups get converted into email
and print templates. The email templates will contain pre-defined values for the Subject and Body fields.
Email templates
The created email template name is formatted as repository_name.sub-directory_name, where
• repository_name is the name of the Mobius repository.
• sub-directory_name is the name of the sub-directory defined in the Mobius server's email distribution
group.
• If there is an email Distribution group in the Mobius server without any defined sub-directory, then an
email template is created with the name repository_name.email.Default. This template is not marked
as the default email template. The administrator can change this to the default email template by editing
it and saving.
During conversion, email templates are created based on the email template files provided in that Mobius
server version directory or sub-directories.
The following fields in the Distribution Services email template are updated from specific text files:
• The Email Body field is updated using the contents from these files and in the given order:
◦ message_body.txt
◦ report_body.txt
◦ section_body.txt
◦ message_footer.txt
Note: report_body_hyperlink.txt and section_body_hyperlink.txt are ignored. Mobius
only uses report_body.txt and section_body.txt. If the contents from
thereport_body_hyperlink.txt and section_body_hyperlink.txt files are required,
rename these files as report_body.txt and section_body.txt.
• The Subject field is updated using the contents from Subject.txt file.
• The Addresses From, CC, and Bcc fields are updated using the contents from the EmailUsers.txt file.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 295


## Page 296


• The Attachment name field is updated using the contents from attachment_name.txt file.
• The Packing Options field is updated based on the presence of the SectionSeparateAttachment.txt
and EmailSeparateAttachment.txt files:
◦ One Email with one Attachment—This is the default packing option during conversion. This packing
option is selected when the 'Each Report Separate Attachment' check-box has been disabled in the
Distribution Group in the Mobius server. If the check-box is enabled, then this packing option
depends if the SectionSeparateAttachment.txt file and EmailSeparateAttachment.txt file are
not present.
◦ One Email with separate Attachments— Selected when the 'Each Report Separate Attachment'
check-box has been enabled in the Distribution Group in the Mobius server and then depends if
the SectionSeparateAttachment.txt file exists, and the EmailSeparateAttachment.txt file is not
present
◦ One Email per version with one Attachment—This packing option will be selected when the 'Each
Report Separate Attachment' check-box has been enabled in the Distribution Group in the Mobius
server and depends if the SectionSeparateAttachment.txt file is not present and the
EmailSeparateAttachment.txt file is present.
◦ Separate Email with one Attachment—This packing option will be selected when the 'Each Report
Separate Attachment' check-box has been enabled in the Distribution Group in the Mobius Server
and depends if the SectionSeparateAttachment.txt and EmailSeparateAttachment.txt file are
present.
Note: The Use template packing options toggle switch is enabled if the Include each report as
a separate attachment to the Email checkbox was selected in the Distribution Group of the
Mobius server. "Packing Options" is used only when the Use template packing options toggle
switch is turned on. Otherwise, One Email with One Attachment is used always.
Print templates
The created print template name is formatted as repository_name.sub-directory_name[B.S.T], where
• repository_name is the name of the Mobius repository.
• sub-directory_name is the sub-directory as defined in the Mobius server's print distribution group.
• [B.S.T] are acronyms added to the print template name when the related print distribution group in
Mobius Server had a packet customization file name mentioned for Packet Cover (B), Packet Trailer (T)
and Packet Separator (S) fields.
• If there is a print distribution group in the Mobius server without any sub-directory, a print template is
created that follows the name as repository_name.sub-directory_name.Default. This template is not
marked as the default. The administrator can edit this as a default print template.
Ingestions
All versions of all content classes in a repository are loaded in the Distribution Services after the conversion of
the repository is complete.
This initial load of versions will not trigger any recurring deliveries. Although once the conversion is complete,
the previous versions can be used while creating a one-time delivery. Any new versions archived after the
conversion is complete triggers their corresponding recurring deliveries.
Auditing events for Distribution Services
Distribution Services generates various types of audit events.
To configure the audit log, configure the audit events related to the Distribution Services that are present in the
Mobius Server Events section.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 296


## Page 297


The details of each audit event type are provided below.
DistributionComplete
This audit event is triggered when Distribution of a delivery is completed.
Attribute Description
deliveryName Name of the delivery that is being distributed.
EmailDistribution
This audit event is triggered when the email distribution of a delivery is started. The attachment type of the email will
either be PDF or native.
Attribute Description
recipient Recipient in the Distribution Services.
deliveryName Name of the delivery that is used to create distribution.
contentClass Content class that is being distributed.
contentIngestion Content class ingestion that is being distributed.
section Name of the section that is being distributed.
recipientOutputAddress A recipient's email address that receives a distribution.
attachmentType
Value can either be 'pdf' or 'native' depending on the
selection done in the delivery definition or the
recipient's output preference for the content being
distributed through email.
EmailNotification
This audit event is triggered when the email distribution of a delivery is started. The attachment type of the email is
link.
Attribute Description
recipient Recipient in the Distribution Services.
deliveryName Name of the delivery that is used to create distribution.
contentClass Content class that is being distributed.
contentIngestion Content class ingestion that is being distributed.
section Name of the section that is being distributed.
recipientOutputAddress A recipient's email address that receives a distribution.
attachmentType Value is always 'link' for the content being distributed
through email.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 297


## Page 298


PrintDistribution
This audit event is triggered when the print distribution of a delivery is started.
Attribute Description
recipient Recipient in the Distribution Services.
deliveryName Name of the delivery that is used to create distribution.
contentClass Content class that is being distributed.
contentIngestion Content class ingestion that is being distributed.
section Name of the section that is being distributed.
recipientOutputAddress A recipient's printer name which receives the
distribution.
ProcessDeferredDistribution
This audit event is triggered when the distribution of a deferred delivery is started.
Attribute Description
contentClass Content class that is being distributed.
contentIngestion Content class ingestion that is being distributed.
deliveryName Name of the delivery that is used to create distribution.
ProcessImmediateDistribution
This audit event is triggered when the distribution of a recurring delivery is started.
Attribute Description
contentClass Content class that is being distributed.
contentIngestion Content class ingestion that is being distributed.
deliveryName Name of the delivery that is used to create distribution.
ProcessRerunDistribution
This audit event is triggered when the distribution of a previously completed delivery is started again.
Attribute Description
contentClass Content class that is being distributed.
contentIngestion Content class ingestion that is being distributed.
deliveryName Name of the delivery that is used to create distribution.
Related information
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 298


## Page 299


Configuring audit log and events
Known issues
Review the known issues and workarounds for Distribution Services.
• Distribution Services adapter does not support non-Mobius repository types.
• The Distribution Services will pull from the Mobius server new versions of content classes that are archived
after the Mobius repository is migrated. While any versions of content classes are pulled from the Mobius
serverrepository, if Mobius View goes down, the new versions archived in Mobius server will not be polled in
the Distribution Services during the downtime.
• Recipient level sequence in email distribution is not supported.
• Extra spaces in the pre-defined variables used in the email template files are ignored after conversion,
although no data is lost.
• While using the {{_sectionID}} tag in an email template's subject field, if the archived version gives more than
one Section ID to which the Recipient has access, then all the Section ID names are clubbed together and
displayed in the subject of the actual email that is received when the packing option selected in the email
template is 'One Email One Attachment.'
• In the Distribution Status screen, the filter that is applied works only for the currently selected page. However,
pagination displays the entire record count. When navigating to the next/previous page, the pagination arrows
may disappear if no matching records are found. Clear all the filters to get back all the records as per the
currently navigated page.
• If the email SMTP server was down for more than 24 hours, then there will be certain packets that will be
purged/deleted (based on purge/expiration time) even if the email was not yet delivered.
• The Distribution Services does not use the OutputFilePerReportTemplate.txt and
SpoolFileNamePerReportTemplate.txt in the print template as the Mobius server is not supporting these files.
• When using an SMTP server that requires authentication of pre-defined users, the 'From' address of the actual
email that is received may not be as per the Email Template / Sender's default email address as the SMTP
server's setting shall override the settings of the Distribution Services.
• While adding a user in Mobius server, the User ID is case sensitive. So, you can have two Users whose User IDs
have the same characters but in different cases, such as TestUser1 and testuser1 are two different Users in
Mobius server. But when it comes to the Distribution Services, the Recipients are mapped to Mobius User IDs,
and in the Distribution Services, the Mobius User ID is case insensitive. This means that once if the User ID
TestUser1 has been mapped to a Distribution Recipient, you cannot create another Recipient and map it to the
Mobius User ID testuser1.
• If a device that was created using the Mobius Administrator and has any special character, then that device is
not added to the Distribution Services as a printer during the migration/conversion process.
• If a Mobius User ID/Name has a special character, the repository migration/conversion fails. You could update
the Mobius User ID/Name, remove any special character in it, and then perform the conversion again.
• Rollback of a repository that was either converted or activated fails when there are recipient output
preferences that have section info defined in them. To overcome this, once the rollback has failed, manually
delete any deliveries that are still available and then retry the rollback.
Quick Start
Print agent scenarios
Deploy a stand-alone print agent or a built-in print agent with Mobius server, for email and print distribution.
Stand-alone print agent
Use a stand-alone print agent to:
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 299


## Page 300


• Print distribution but you cannot access network printers from the Mobius server host. You can deploy
the standalone print agent irrespective of an activated or migrated repository.
• Print distribution on a repository that was activated (not migrated). You can deploy the standalone print
agent and then add it to Distribution Services.
Built-in print agent with Mobius Server
Use the built-in print agent with Mobius Server to:
Enable Distribution Services and migrate any existing distribution configurations to the Distribution Services,
and use it for email distribution, print distribution, or both.
The print agent with Mobius server must be running, and then run the Convert action on the repository from
the Distribution Services landing page.
Note: Use Mobius server version 12.2 or later for a migrated repository. Otherwise, deploy the
standalone print agent and add it to Distribution Services.
You can use both types of print agents for print distribution to a network printer on a repository that was either
migrated or activated. No print agent is required when you do not have or want to carry over any existing distribution
configurations. In this case, you can enable Distribution Services to use it for email distribution alone by running the
Activate action on the repository from the Distribution Services landing page.
Synchronizing distribution recipients with Mobius users and groups
Add a new user or user group from the Mobius server, and add them as recipients in Distribution Services using the
synchronize option.
Note: Any user or group that has been modified or deleted in the Mobius server does not get modified
or deleted in the Distribution Services. Synchronizing works only one way, from Mobius server to
Distribution Services. If you modify the recipients in Distribution Services later, the changes are not
synchronized with Mobius server.
1. In Mobius Administrator, select Distribution Services from the left menu.
2. From the Distribution Services landing page, click Recipients and Groups.
3. Click Sync with Mobius Server and view the newly added recipient.
Note: Adding a new recipient does not carry forward any report access rights into the
Distribution Services; you must manually create the output preferences and tag them to their
corresponding delivery.
Email and print templates
Create email and print templates entirely from within Distribution Services or use the templates created after
migrating a Mobius repository.
For a migrated Mobius repository, that Mobius Server version contains the email and print distribution groups
defined with a package customization sub-directory. The distribution groups are then converted into email and print
templates with pre-defined values.
Once created, these templates can be mapped directly on the deliveries or can be selected as part of output
preferences for recipients.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 300


## Page 301


Converting repositories
Convert repositories as part of migrating existing Mobius information into Distribution Services. All existing Mobius
templates, content classes, users, devices, and distribution groups will be loaded into Distribution Services.
Before you begin:
• Configure the print agent.
• Ensure that the repositories created in the earlier version of Mobius are available.
1. In Mobius Administrator, select Distribution Services from the left menu.
2. On the Distribution Services panel, click Convert next to each repository that you want to convert, on the
landing page.
Depending on the underlying data on the content source, the conversion time varies. If the conversion is
successful, the repository name is removed from the landing page.
3. Click the Content Repositories link on the landing page to view a list of the successfully converted
repositories.
4. Click Rollback beside the repository name.
If the conversion fails, check the Mobius log file for information about the failure.
5. Click Convert to convert the repository after correcting the issues reported in the log file.
Related information
Deploying the Mobius print agent (non-container deployment)
Deploying the Mobius print agent (container deployment)
Scheduling email and print deliveries
Email and print deliveries define which content classes must be included for distribution together, for which
recipients, and when they are to be released for distribution.
1. Click + Delivery to create a new delivery definition that is visible within the Deliveries tile in the Distribution
Services screen.
Alternatively, click Deliveries > Add Delivery.
2. Enter a delivery name in the Delivery section.
3. Search for one or more content classes in the Content section.
Selected content classes are added. Reorder the content classes using the arrow icons.
4. Configure the Schedule section as follows.
a. Select a delivery type in the Schedule section after selecting at least one content class:
▪ Existing Content - Uses the already archived ingestions of the selected reports.
▪ When all selected Content arrives - Waits for new ingestions of the selected reports to arrive.
▪ Deferred - Does not trigger any new delivery distribution even when new content versions arrive.
Use this delivery type to create a new one-time delivery keeping this delivery definition as a base.
b. Select a delivery schedule.
The options vary depending on the selected delivery type.
Existing Content
▪ Immediately - The delivery is triggered immediately with all the ingestions that are selected.
This is a one-time activity.
▪ Delayed deliver - The delivery needs to be triggered at the pre-defined date and time with
all the ingestions that are selected. If this time is in the past, the delivery is immediately
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 301


## Page 302


triggered. This is a one-time activity.
Note: At the end of creating this delivery, a new entry is only displayed in the
Distribution Status screen.
When all selected Content arrives
▪ Immediately - The delivery needs to be triggered repeatedly and immediately after new
ingestions for all the selected reports has arrived.
▪ Periodically - The delivery needs to be triggered repeatedly at the pre-defined date and time
after new ingestions for all the selected reports have arrived. This trigger uses Quartz library-
based Cron expressions created on the basis of selected options.
▪ Manually - No new delivery is triggered. Such a delivery can only be triggered manually later.
c. Select the existing ingestions that need to be distributed using the Select Ingestion field.
The Select Ingestion field is only displayed when the delivery type is Existing Content.
Selecting the Not yet delivered checkbox displays the ingestions within the given date range that have
not yet been distributed. Otherwise, it displays all the ingestions within the given date range, that were
either distributed already or not yet distributed.
5. Select at least one of the output channels or both under the Output Channels section.
The default number of copies and the minimum required for the print is one, while the maximum is 1000
copies.
6. Search and add a sender from the list of available recipients from the Sender section.
7. Search and select the recipients to whom the content classes need to be distributed.
The Select Recipients field is required only when the Delivery Type is selected as Existing Content,
otherwise this field is optional.
a. Click a recipient to view the recipient's available preferences to configure a recipient to have one or
many output preferences.
b. Select one or more preferences from the available preferences for a recipient.
Preferences are read-only. Once a delivery is added or updated, editing or deleting the preferences or
sections is only possible from the Recipients page.
▪ If no preference is selected for a recipient, then the output channel parameters set for the delivery
in the Output Channels section is used during the distribution.
▪ If a preference is selected for a recipient, it overrides the output channel parameters set for the
delivery.
▪ If more than one preferences are selected for a recipient, then the recipients receives that many
distributions of the delivery.
Note: If a delivery or recipient's preference has the attachment type as Link, the selected
email template has its email body field updated with a correct link. If no link is defined, the
recipient receives an email without any link to the content class or the sections.
8. Click Add Delivery.
Creating a one-time email distribution
Set up and schedule new email and print distributions. You can create an immediate delivery of content classes using
their existing content.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 302


## Page 303


1. In Mobius Administrator, select Distribution Services from the left menu.
2. On the Distribution Services landing page, click the '+' Delivery button on the Deliveries tile.
3. On the Create a new Delivery panel, configure the following fields.
Delivery name (required)
Enter a meaningful name for the delivery.
Description (optional)
Enter a description for the delivery definition.
Content - Select Content Class (required)
Search for the content classes that are available in the Mobius View. From the search result, select the
content class that needs to be distributed. Search and select more than one content classes, then reorder
them using the arrow buttons. To perform a search, enter at least 3 characters.
Schedule - Delivery type (required)
Select Existing Content from the drop-down list. This delivery type uses the already archived report
versions of the selected reports.
When this delivery type is selected and the delivery definition is completed, a new delivery entry is visible
on the Distribution Status screen only.
Schedule - Deliver
Select Immediately as the delivery schedule to trigger an immediate delivery with all of the selected
report versions after the delivery definition is saved. A Delayed Delivery schedule, triggers a distribution
based on the time defined in the schedule.
Schedule - Select ingestion
Clear the Not yet delivered optional checkbox. It displays all the content class versions within the given
date range, that were either distributed already or not yet distributed. Unless a content class version for
all the selected content classes is not selected, the Add Delivery button is not enabled.
Output channel - Email
Select the checkbox and click to expand. From the Template field, select the required email template
from all the available email templates that were configured in the Email Templates section. The default
email template is selected by default.
Select the type of attachment to be sent in the email - Pdf, Link, and Native.
Output channel - Print
Select the checkbox and click to expand. From the Template field, select the required print template from
all the available print templates that were configured in the Print Templates section. The default print
template is selected by default.
Specify the number of copies to send in the email - the default is '1'.
Sender
For the Sender Name, select a sender from the list of available Distribution Services recipients. Search by
the name of the available recipients and from the result, select one of them as the sender. Once a sender
is selected, to change the selection, you must search again in the search box and select the required
recipient as the sender.
Recipient
For the Select Recipients field, select the recipients that will receive the distributed content classes.
Search for the available recipients by their name and select them.
4. Click Add Delivery.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 303


## Page 304


Creating one-time email distribution from existing deliveries
Use the existing recurring or deferred deliveries to create a one-time delivery.
1. Click Deliveries on the Distribution Services landing page.
2. Find the recurring or the deferred delivery that you need to use to create a new one-time delivery.
3. From the Action menu of the delivery, click and select Create One-time.
4. Add or edit the fields as required in the Create a new One-time Delivery from existing Delivery panel where
all the data from the base recurring or deferred delivery is prefilled.
Note: Removing any existing content classes is not possible, and at the same time, you must
select at least one ingestion for each of the content classes that are part of the base delivery.
5. Click Add Delivery.
Creating one-time email distribution from completed distributions
Use the completed distributions to trigger a new one-time delivery.
1. Click Distribution Status in the Distribution Services landing page.
2. Find the distribution you want to reuse and select Re-deliver from the Action menu available against the
delivery instance.
3. Add or edit the fields as required in the Create a new One-time Delivery from existing Delivery panel where
all the data from the base delivery instance are pre-filled.
Note: You cannot remove any existing content classes, and you cannot modify the selected
ingestion as well.
4. Click Add Delivery.
Creating email distribution for recurring deliveries
Create an email distribution from recurring deliveries simply by creating a new archive version of the content classes
related to the recurring delivery that needs to be distributed.
For the content classes that needs to be distributed based on its delivery definitions, create a new archive version
using either archive creation or Capture UI.
Once this new ingestion is (automatically) picked by the Distribution Services (which usually happens within one
minute), all the recurring deliveries that have been marked for immediate distribution are triggered, and a new
delivery instance for each of them is created.
You can track the progress and status of each delivery instance from the Distribution Status screen.
Print distribution to a file printer
Configure a file printer for print distribution.
1. Configuring a file printer in Distribution Services
Create a printer of the type 'File', manually in Output Channels or through repository migration
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 304


## Page 305


2. Configuring a print template associated with a file printer
Configure a print template related to the file printer, and use the template to specify different template files.
These files are added to the selected content to form a print stream. A default print template is applied to all
the deliveries that have a selected print output channel.
3. Creating a one-time print distribution for file/LPR/LPD/network printer
Create an immediate delivery of content classes using the existing content, as and when the new content
arrives.
Configuring a file printer in Distribution Services
Create a printer of the type 'File', manually in Output Channels or through repository migration
1. In Mobius Administrator, select Distribution Services from the left menu.
2. Click Output Channels.
3. Select Print from the Agent drop-down list.
4. Click Add Printer under the Destinations section.
5. Enter a name for the printer in the Add new Printer panel and select File from the Type drop-down list.
6. Select a value from the Print language drop-down list.
7. Click Add.
8. Navigate to Distribution Services > Templates to create a new print template.
Configuring a print template associated with a file printer
Configure a print template related to the file printer, and use the template to specify different template files. These
files are added to the selected content to form a print stream. A default print template is applied to all the deliveries
that have a selected print output channel.
A print template that contains the correctly configured OutputFileTemplate.txt file is available in Distribution
Services.
1. In Mobius Administrator, select Distribution Services from the left menu.
2. Click the Templates tile, and then select Print Template from the Define drop-down list.
3. Click Add.
4. Enter a name for the template.
Select Default to use the default print template.
5. Select Use Template > File from the Template Pages section.
6. Select OutputFileTemplate.txt from the Mobius file name drop-down list.
For a file printer, provide a valid path where the output file must be stored, and that value is defined in the
OutputFileTemplate.txt file. After a successful print distribution to this printer, access the output file from the
path defined in this template file.
7. Enter the path directly in the File Content text box.
Alternatively, load the contents from an existing file on disk by clicking + Add Content or click + Add New to
directly add an existing Mobius template file from a disk location.
The path must include the file name with its expected file extension. For example, if the expected content is
PDF format, then the file extension is .pdf. After adding content from an existing template file or adding a new
file, edit the actual content in the File Content text box. You can use the pre-defined variable <<UniqueFile>>
for a unique final output file name to avoid overwriting an existing file.
A file path for OutputFileTemplate.txt example is: C:\MobiusVDR\Output\File_<<UniqueFile>>.pdf
◦ While adding or editing the file content, the content must not end with an empty line.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 305


## Page 306


◦ Any changes made here are only stored inside Distribution Services. The actual file content is not
changed.
8. Click Save.
If there are errors related to the distribution status, go to C:\ProgramData\ASG\VDRNET\{Mobius Server
Version Number}\ErrorFile.err.
Creating a one-time print distribution for file/LPR/LPD/network printer
Create an immediate delivery of content classes using the existing content, as and when the new content arrives.
1. In Mobius Administrator, select Distribution Services from the left menu.
2. On the Distribution Services landing page, click '+' Delivery from within Deliveries.
Alternatively, click Deliveries > Add Delivery.
3. Enter a delivery name.
4. Search for the available content classes in Mobius View, and select the content class to be distributed.
You can search and select more than one content class. Enter at least three characters in the Select Content
Class field to start a search.
5. Select Existing Content from the Delivery type drop-down list in the Schedule panel to use the already
archived ingestions of the selected reports.
Note: A new entry in the delivery definitions list is added for this delivery in the Distribution
Status screen.
6. Disable the Not yet delivered checkbox in the Select Ingestion field.
All the ingestions within the given date range that were either distributed already or not yet distributed are
displayed. Unless ingestion for all the selected content classes is selected, the Add Delivery option is not
enabled.
7. Select Immediately in the Deliver field as the delivery schedule.
8. Select the print template from the drop-down in the Output Channels > Print > Template field.
The correct printer type for a selected template ensures that the distribution is directed correctly. For example,
for a file printer, select the print template that prints output to a file.
9. Select the available printers in the Distribution Services from the drop-down in the Output Channels > Print >
Printer.
If a selected printer does not have print output preferences configured for the recipient, it overrides the
recipient's default printer, and the content classes being distributed are printed onto this selected printer.
10. Enter the number of copies required for the print in the Output Channels > Print > Copies.
The default and the minimum is 1, while the maximum is 1000 copies.
11. Search and add the sender name and recipients in the subsequent fields.
Enter at least three characters to perform a search.
12. Click Add Delivery.
Print distribution to a network printer
Configure a network-type printer for print distribution.
For subsequent Mobius installation, connect a print agent in Output Channels as a result of repository migration or
by adding a standalone print agent.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 306


## Page 307


Note: When distributing PDF content to a network printer, you must deploy the MAKO library in the
Mobius View.
1. Configuring a network printer in Distribution Services
Add a new network printer through Distribution Services.
2. Configuring a print template associated with the network printer
Configure a print template associated with a network printer, and use the templates to define different
template files. These files are added into selected content to form a print stream. A standard print template is
applied to all the deliveries that have a selected print output channel.
3. Creating a one-time print distribution for file/LPR/LPD/network printer
Create an immediate delivery of content classes using the existing content, as and when the new content
arrives.
Configuring a network printer in Distribution Services
Add a new network printer through Distribution Services.
1. In Mobius Administrator, select Distribution Services from the left menu.
2. On the Distribution Services landing page, click Output Channels.
3. Select Print from the Agent drop-down list.
4. Click Add Printer under the Destinations section.
5. Enter a name for the printer and select Network from the Type drop-down list.
6. Select SOCKET_IO from the Connection protocol drop-down list.
7. Enter the host name or IP address of the system on which the network printer is available in the Address field.
8. Select Postscript_L2 from the Print language drop-down list.
9. Enter the port number the network printer is listening to the host machine.
10. Click Add.
11. Return to the Distribution Services landing page, and click Templates to create a new print template.
Configuring a print template associated with the network printer
Configure a print template associated with a network printer, and use the templates to define different template files.
These files are added into selected content to form a print stream. A standard print template is applied to all the
deliveries that have a selected print output channel.
1. In Mobius Administrator, select Distribution Services from the left menu.
2. On the Distribution Services landing page, click the Templates.
3. Select Print Template from the Define drop-down list and click Add.
4. Enter a name for the template.
Select Default to use the default print template.
5. Select Use Template > File from the Template Pages section.
6. Click Add New.
Navigate to the location that contains the StartPacketInsert.ps, EndPacketInsert.ps, PrintCopyInsert.ps
and Landscape.ps files.
7. Select one .ps file at a time and click Save.
Repeat this process for the other .ps files.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 307


## Page 308


Note: You can only edit the content of text template files here. For non-text files like .ps file, you
can only upload the content from an external file.
8. Click Save.
Creating a one-time print distribution for file/LPR/LPD/network printer
Create an immediate delivery of content classes using the existing content, as and when the new content arrives.
1. In Mobius Administrator, select Distribution Services from the left menu.
2. On the Distribution Services landing page, click '+' Delivery from within Deliveries.
Alternatively, click Deliveries > Add Delivery.
3. Enter a delivery name.
4. Search for the available content classes in Mobius View, and select the content class to be distributed.
You can search and select more than one content class. Enter at least three characters in the Select Content
Class field to start a search.
5. Select Existing Content from the Delivery type drop-down list in the Schedule panel to use the already
archived ingestions of the selected reports.
Note: A new entry in the delivery definitions list is added for this delivery in the Distribution
Status screen.
6. Disable the Not yet delivered checkbox in the Select Ingestion field.
All the ingestions within the given date range that were either distributed already or not yet distributed are
displayed. Unless ingestion for all the selected content classes is selected, the Add Delivery option is not
enabled.
7. Select Immediately in the Deliver field as the delivery schedule.
8. Select the print template from the drop-down in the Output Channels > Print > Template field.
The correct printer type for a selected template ensures that the distribution is directed correctly. For example,
for a file printer, select the print template that prints output to a file.
9. Select the available printers in the Distribution Services from the drop-down in the Output Channels > Print >
Printer.
If a selected printer does not have print output preferences configured for the recipient, it overrides the
recipient's default printer, and the content classes being distributed are printed onto this selected printer.
10. Enter the number of copies required for the print in the Output Channels > Print > Copies.
The default and the minimum is 1, while the maximum is 1000 copies.
11. Search and add the sender name and recipients in the subsequent fields.
Enter at least three characters to perform a search.
12. Click Add Delivery.
Print distribution to a Windows LPR/LPD printer
Configure a Windows LPR/LPD printer for print distribution.
A print agent must be connected in Output Channels as a result of repository migration or by adding a standalone
print agent.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 308


## Page 309


1. Configure LPR/LPD printer in Windows
An LPR or LPD printer can be configured in Windows.
2. Configuring a Windows LPD printer in Distribution Services
After adding an LPR/LPD printer in Windows, you must configure it for use.
3. Configuring a print template associated with a Windows printer
Configure a print template related to Windows printers, and use the templates to specify different template
files. These files are added to the selected content to form a print stream. A default print template is applied to
all the deliveries that have a selected print output channel.
4. Creating a one-time print distribution for file/LPR/LPD/network printer
Create an immediate delivery of content classes using the existing content, as and when the new content
arrives.
Configure LPR/LPD printer in Windows
An LPR or LPD printer can be configured in Windows.
Before you begin:
• Obtain administrator access for the system.
• Ensure that the LPR Port Monitor Windows feature is enabled.
1. Navigate to Control Panel > Programs and Features > Turn Windows Features on or off.
2. In the Windows Features window, click the + icon for the Print and Document Services feature.
3. Select the LPR Port Monitor feature and click OK.
4. Restart your system if prompted to do so.
5. From the Windows search bar, search for Printers and scanners and click + Add a Printer or scanner.
a. Click The printer that I want isn’t listed.
b. Select Add a local printer or network printer with manual settings and click Next.
c. Select Create a new port > LPR Port from the Type of port drop-down.
d. Click Next.
e. In the Add LPR compatible printer dialog, configure the following fields.
Name or address of the server providing lpd
For a Cypress Server device, the value should be the host name where Cypress Server is running.
For a printer directly on the Windows, the value should be the host name on which the printer is
available.
Name of printer or print queue on that server
For a Cypress Server, the value should be the name of the device as configured in Cypress.
For a Windows printer directly, the value should be the name of the Printer as available.
f. Click OK.
6. In the Install the printer driver dialog, click Windows Update to view and choose your printer.
7. Follow the instructions in the wizard to complete your configuration.
Configuring a Windows LPD printer in Distribution Services
After adding an LPR/LPD printer in Windows, you must configure it for use.
1. In Mobius Administrator, select Distribution Services from the left menu.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 309


## Page 310


2. Click Output Channels.
3. Select Print from the Agent drop-down list.
4. Click Add Printer under the Destinations section.
5. Enter a name for the printer in the Add new Printer panel and select Windows from the Type drop-down list.
6. Select LPD from the Connection protocol drop-down list.
7. Enter the address of the LPR printer that you added in Windows in the Address field.
8. Select a value from the Print language drop-down list.
9. Click Add.
10. Navigate to Distribution Services > Templates to create a new print template.
Configuring a print template associated with a Windows printer
Configure a print template related to Windows printers, and use the templates to specify different template files.
These files are added to the selected content to form a print stream. A default print template is applied to all the
deliveries that have a selected print output channel.
1. In Mobius Administrator, select Distribution Services from the left menu.
2. Click Templates, and then select Print Template from the Define drop-down list.
3. Click Add.
4. Enter a name for the template.
Select Default to use the default print template.
5. Select Use Template > File from the Template Pages section.
6. Select SpoolFileNameTemplate.txt from the Mobius file name drop-down list.
For a Windows printer, provide a value to the SpoolFileNameTemplate.txt file. This value is the name
displayed for the job in the Windows printer's queue.
7. Enter the path directly in the File Content text box.
Alternatively, load the contents from an existing file on disk by clicking + Add Content or click + Add New to
directly add an existing Mobius template file from a disk location.
After adding content from an existing template file or added a new file, edit the actual content in the File
Content text box. In the file content, use the pre-defined variable <<UniqueFile>> for a unique final output file
name to avoid overwriting an existing file. An example of the file content for SpoolFileNameTemplate.txt file
is:VDR-Output-<<UniqueFile>>.
◦ While adding or editing the file content, the content must not end with an empty line.
◦ Any changes made here are only stored inside Distribution Services. The actual file content is not
changed.
8. Click Save.

## Page 312


Configure auditing
Implement auditing by enabling different event categories for Mobius Server and the Mobius Server database. Log
audit events to an external database using Kafka (recommended) or to a CSV file on Windows and Linux systems.
An auditing section for Content Insights becomes available when using Kafka and Content Insights together.

Enabling auditing between Mobius View and Mobius Server
You can configure Mobius Server to send audit messages to Mobius View at the repository level.
Configuring audit log and events
Set up audit logging to track server and database events for monitoring.
Importing auditing events
When migrating from an earlier Mobius version, import an existing audit configuration by using the Bulk
Import option.
Audit event types
Mobius generates various types of audit events. After generating the audit data, also known as audit events,
review the event types to monitor and analyze activities in Mobius.
Exporting document metadata
Link the audit events in your Mobius applications to the documents that they affect by exporting Mobius
document metadata to the reporting database.
Troubleshooting auditing
Review troubleshooting and diagnostics information when audit messages cannot be viewed in the Mobius
View Audit screen for reports.

## Page 313


Enabling auditing between Mobius View and Mobius Server
You can configure Mobius Server to send audit messages to Mobius View at the repository level.
1. From Mobius Server > Mobius Content Sources, click a content source to view its repositories.
2. Hover over a repository, and click Settings.
3. To audit the content source that hosts this repository, expand the Auditing section.
4. Configure the following fields.
The information from these fields form the URL of the audit service.
Protocol
Select HTTPS or HTTP to indicate the protocol to use to connect to Mobius View.
Host name
Enter the name or IP address of the machine hosting Mobius View.
Port
Enter the port number Mobius View is running on.
Note: If an AJP connector is used, then the port number must match the port number
configured for the AJP connector. This connector would have been added as part of
configuring Mobius View and should be accessible by Mobius server.
5. Expand the Index Export tab, select the Enable Index Export checkbox, and enter the database details to
export the updates to the indexes in the content source to the audit reporting database configured for the
repository.
6. Click Apply.
Configuring audit log and events
Set up audit logging to track server and database events for monitoring.
Before you begin: Enable auditing between Mobius View and Mobius Server.
Auditing is divided into a Content Insights and Mobius Server Audit Log File sections.
Note: Content Insights is a licensed feature. The Content Insights section contains Kafka read-only
information that is only available if Kafka is configured during deployment. By default, all Mobius Server
and database events that are logged to a corresponding event to Kafka, are not selected in this section.
1. In Mobius Administrator, select Auditing from the menu.
2. Expand Mobius Server Audit Log File and configure the legacy settings used for Mobius Server audit in the
table.
Field Action
Audit log file directory Enter the directory containing the created audit log
file.
Audit log file name Enter the Audit log (.csv) file name.
Audit log file roll over size (in MB)
Enter the maximum size of the audit log file after
which the file is backed up and service starts writing
logs to a new file. The default value is 10MB.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 313


## Page 314


Field Action
Configuration update Interval (in seconds)
Enter the number of seconds after which the Kafka
client can send a message to the Kafka that was not
required to be audited. The default value is 60
seconds.
3. Expand the Mobius server Events and Database Events tabs and select the required audit events related to
server processes and database modifications, respectively.
Note: When Mobius View starts for the first time, only LogOn and LogOff Mobius Server events
are selected, by default.
Click Bulk Import to migrate old event filters from mobius.reg to Kafka in Mobius View.
Related information
Enabling auditing between Mobius View and Mobius Server
Importing auditing events
Mobius Server events
Configure the audit events to log from the Content Insights (optional) and Mobius Server Audit Log File sections on
the Auditing panel. The Content Insights section, a licensed feature, is visible once Kafka is set up during deployment.
The Content Insights and Mobius Server Audit Log File sections log messages in CSV format for easy import into any
standard spreadsheet or database application.
Each message in the file contains the following information:
• Time stamp of message
• Document server or ODBC Data Set that performed the activity
Note: To distinguish between document server names and data set names, the prefix 'DS:'
appears before data set names.
• Computer that hosts the document server
• Session identifier
• User ID
• Recipient ID
• Message text
• Message details
The following table lists the event type, logged action, and specific information types corresponding to an event in the
message.
Event type Recorded information type
AddPolicy Policy name

Mobius View audits server events and changes made to database entities that store information in the Mobius
schema about Mobius objects, that is Reports, Report Versions, and Users.
Audit can be performed for changes to information maintained by Mobius View from Mobius View Administrator or
vdrdbxml command.
Database auditing entities can be modified by different actions. Mobius View separates auditing entities by action
categories, allowing you to specify the source of the changes you want to audit.
For example: Index name Instances can be added to the database during normal archiving activity and also can be
modified using the modify action in Mobius View Administrator.
During a routine archiving activity, many changes can be made and auditing may not be required for all the changes.
To audit only modifications performed through Mobius View Administrator, enable auditing of Index name instance
changes in the Mobius View Administrator category and not enable it in the Archive Creation category.
Mobius View events
Configuring audit events from Mobius View are only available in the Content Insights section on the Auditing panel.
This section logs audit messages in CSV format for easy import into any standard spreadsheet or database
application.
Each message consists of basic information:
• time stamp
• User ID (plain text)
• Recipient ID
For some messages, this is followed by grouped 'event information', which form the message content:
• contentClass
• contentIngestion
• documentID
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 320

• documentServer
• index
• indexItem
• repositoryName (name of repository)
• Server Name (repository defined in Mobius Administrator)
• section

Database events
Configuring audit events from the database are available in the Content Insights (optional) and Mobius Server
Audit Log File sections on the Auditing panel. This section logs messages in CSV format for easy import into any
standard spreadsheet or database application.
Database audit events consists of multiple components, such as the name of the entity, the action attribute, and the
message details. The following table lists the source or entity that generates the event, the logged action, and the
message that describes the event. The message also includes specific information items corresponding to an event.
