• Distribution Comments
• Topic Item Mask
EncryptionKeyUsage • Add
• Delete
• Report ID
• Report Version
• Encryption Key Name
Importing auditing events
When migrating from an earlier Mobius version, import an existing audit configuration by using the Bulk Import
option.
Before you begin: The mobius.reg configuration file is already copied from Mobius Server to a location that is
accessible to Mobius View.
Importing moves audit event configurations from earlier versions of Mobius Server into the auditing infrastructure in
Mobius View.
1. In Mobius Administrator, select Auditing from the left menu.
2. Click Bulk Import, select the mobius.reg configuration file that has the audit event configurations, and click
Open.
Note: Current audit event configurations in Mobius View are overwritten by the imported audit
event configurations.
3. Click Apply to save the imported audit events.
Restarting Mobius View and Mobius Server is not required.
Audit event types
Mobius generates various types of audit events. After generating the audit data, also known as audit events, review
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 335


## Page 336


the event types to monitor and analyze activities in Mobius.
For additional information on audit events structure, see Audit event vocabulary structure. For information about
analyzing audit events with document metadata and exporting metadata, see Exported document metadata and
Exporting metadata, respectively.
Close Archive
Action occurs when a close archive request is performed. The archives that are left open after viewing a
document or accessing a content ingestion are closed after a specific time period resulting in this audit event.
Attribute Description
sessionDuration Duration of the session opened for archiving stored in the 000:00:00
format.
pagesPerArchive Number of pages in this archive.
message Displays the corresponding error message.
Create Archive
While capturing a report, the system creates an archive and triggers this audit event. Only a single create
archive audit event is generated per content ingestion.
Attribute Description
contentClass Content class used during archive creation.
contentIngestion Ingestion date when the archiving was done.
archiveFilename Filename of the archive as stored on the device.
policyName Policy file used during archiving.
encryptionKeyName Encryption key used during archiving.
Delete Report Section
Action occurs when a content class section is deleted. Deletion of a content class section belongs to Records
Management.
• To delete the expired content sections and trigger this audit event, run the following command with with
“-g” and “-y n” option .
adelete
• To delete expired content class sections based on the retention period, open Mobius Administrator
application, navigate to the required content class, expand the Retention tab, and select Event-based
retention from the drop-down.
Attribute Description
contentClass Content class for the report section that is deleted.
contentIngestion Ingestion date of the report section that is deleted.
section Report section that is deleted.
Delete Report Version
Action occurs when a content ingestion is deleted from a content class in a document server.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 336


## Page 337


Attribute Description
contentClass Content class for the content ingestion that is deleted.
contentIngestion Ingestion date of the content ingestion that is deleted.
archiveFilename Filename of the archive as stored on device.
sectionsDeleted Sections that are deleted.
sectionsKept Sections that are retained.
message Displays the corresponding error message.
Get Page
Action occurs when a document page in a content ingestion is accessed or viewed.
Attribute Description
auditSectionName Section name that the page belongs to.
pageNumber Page number that is accessed.
redactionAPI Indicates whether the redaction API is used while accessing the page.
documentServer Name of the document server.
redactionType Redaction type.
redactionRequested Indicates whether redaction is requested for this page.
redactionPerformed Indicates whether redaction is actually performed on the page returned.
redactionPolicyName Name of the policy used for redaction.
New Archive Instance Created
Action occurs when an archive present on a storage device such as Centera is updated for Record Management
activity like extending retention period. To create a archive instance based on the retention period, open
Mobius Administrator application, navigate to the required content class, expand the Retention tab, and select
Fixed retention from the drop-down based upon ingestion ID and define the days for retention.
After creating an archive on Centera, run the following command with the options “”.
lcupdate -M l -T <content_ingestion> -I <instance_ID> -C <extended_retention_period_in_sec
s>
Attribute Description
contentClass Content class for the created archive.
contentIngestion Ingestion date when the report section is migrated.
source Source location of the document archive file.
destination Destination location of the archived file.
OCR Processing
When an image or pdf file is captured for a content class for which OCR processing is enabled, an OCR
processing job is started, which eventually captures the processing of the report. This audit event represents
the processing step, which is performed by a specific OCR job in Mobius Server. Multiple audit events are
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 337


## Page 338


triggered during this processing for activities such as:
• Extracting image from archive
• Indexing to FTS (if FTS is enabled)
Attribute Description
OCRprocessingType Type of OCR processing performed.
JobID ID of the OCR job.
contentClass Content class for the created archive.
contentIngestion Ingestion date when the report section was being archived.
pageNumber Page number that was processed.
message Displays the corresponding error message.
OCR Processing Job Started
Action occurs when an OCR processing job is started.
Attribute Description
JobID ID of the OCR job.
JobType Type of OCR job.
archivingType Type of archiving that triggered the OCR processing.
documentServer Name of the document server.
contentClass Content class for document being processed.
contentIngestion Ingestion date of the document being processed.
archiveFilename Filename of the archive as stored on the device.
archiveFileSize Size of the file being processed.
OCRDirectory Temporary directory path where OCR processing takes place.
OCR Processing Job Ended
Action occurs when the processing of an OCR job has completed.
Attribute Description
JobID ID of the OCR job.
JobType Type of OCR job.
archivingType Type of archiving that triggered the OCR processing.
documentServer Name of the document server.
contentClass Content class for document being processed.
contentIngestion Ingestion date of the document being processed.
Open Archive
Action occurs when opening a specific content ingestion while browsing through a content class. This action
occurs again when viewing a section inside the content ingestion.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 338

Attribute Description
contentClass Content class for archive being opened.
contentIngestion Content ingestion for archive being processed.
archiveFilename Filename of the archive as stored on the device.

archive.

Privileged Deletion
Action occurs when deleting an archive with an unexpired retention period from a Centera storage system. You
must define an authorization exit, which includes a EXTValidateRecipientPrivilegedDeletionCredentials
function. For example, run the following command with “-Y U” option to trigger this audit event. That exit will
return TRUE if the recipient ID is included in the command above.
adelete

contentClass Content class for archive being processed.
contentIngestion Content ingestion for archive being processed.
archiveFilename Archive file name.
message Displays the corresponding error message.

Audit event vocabulary structure
Audit events are represented in JSON format. The JSON schema represents the vocabulary structure of the audit
events, which comprises of the mandatory event elements that are common for different client applications and
elements that are specific to the Mobius application.
{
    timestamp: "string",
    source: "string",
    action: "string",
    version: "string",
    description: "string",
    user: "string",
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 339


## Page 340


tenant: "string",
    trace-context: {
      service: "string",
      locator: "string",
      correlator: "string"
    },
    id: "string"
    type: "string"
    mobius: {
      recipient: "string",
      contentClass: "string",
      contentIngestion: "string",
      repositoryName: "string",
      repositoryId: "string",
      documentServer: "string",
      sessionDuration: "string",
      archivesPerSession:"string",
      pagesPerSession: "string",
      message: "string",
      archiveFilename: "string",
      redactionApi: "string",
      redactionType: "string",
      redactionPolicyName : "string",
      pagesPerArchive: "string",
      sectionsDeleted: "string",
      sectionsKept: "string",
      policyName: "string",
      encryptionKeyName: "string",
      section: "string",
      pageNumber: "string",
      redactionRequested: "string",
      redactionPerformed: "string",
      policyName: "string",
      contentClassFilename: "string",
      allowDistribution: "string",
      jobID: "string",
      jobType: "string",
      archivingType: "string",
      archiveFileSize: "string",
      OCRDirectory: "string",
      OCRprocessingType: "string",
      source: "string",
      destination: "string",
      detail: "string"
  }
}
The following table provides the description for each attribute included in the above schema.
Attribute Name Description
timestamp
Time at which this event occurred. The time is represented in ISO 8601 format. The
format is "date and time with zone offset". That is, yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSZ
For example, '2022-05-23T10:15:30.234+05:30'
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 340


## Page 341


Attribute Name Description
source Name of the source application or system that produced the event. Value is human
readable.
action The operation which causes triggering of the event. Value is human readable. For
example, Open Archive, Close Archive
version
The version of action and is managed by the source system. It is used by source to
map the payload to a given action. If source system changes, the schema
associated with the given action in the future release , that is to add/remove source
specific elements, this version field is used to identify the JSON schema against a
release.
description Human readable description of the event used in the UI. It is more descriptive than
the action field.
user Maps to ID or name of the user who triggered this event. It is system or service
user who is triggering event on behalf of the user.
tenant ID or name of the tenant in a multi-tenant environment. It is optional and not used
by Mobius application.
trace-context.service Name of the service that generated the event. Value is MOBIUS. This field is not
useful for business user consumption.
trace-context.locator
The location from where the event was generated. It can be host name, node
name, url, path or combination of the fields. It can be used by the source to identify
from where the event was generated. This field is not useful for business user
consumption.
trace-context.correlator
Maps to session ID or transaction ID in which the event action was performed. It
can be used by the source system to track events. For example, Web session
identifier. This field is not useful for business user consumption.
id Represents the event ID.
type Represents the event type. Value is 'audit-content'.
mobius.recipient Represents RECIPIENT in Mobius.
mobius.contentClass Report name.
mobius.contentIngestion Report version.
mobius.repositoryName Repository name.
mobius.repositoryId Repository ID.
mobius.documentServer Document server name.
mobius.sessionDuration Session duration as timestamp.
mobius.archivesPerSession Number of archives done in this session.
mobius.pagesPerSession Number of pages browsed in this session.
mobius.message Generic message. Can be reason for the event or case where detail headers cannot
be resolved.
mobius.archiveFilename Name of the file that is archived.
mobius.redactionApi Specifies whether redaction is being done.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 341


## Page 342


Attribute Name Description
mobius.redactionType Redaction type
mobius.redactionPolicyName Redaction policy name
mobius.pagesPerArchive Number of pages archived
mobius.sectionsDeleted Sections deleted
mobius.sectionsKept Sections kept
mobius.policyName Policy used during archiving.
mobius.encryptionKeyName Encryption key used in archiving.
mobius.section Name of section in GetPage.
mobius.pageNumber Page number in GetPage.
mobius.redactionRequested Specifies whether redaction is requested.
mobius.redactionPerformed Specifies whether redaction is performed.
mobius.policyName Name of the policy
mobius.contentClassFilename Name of report file
mobius.allowDistribution Specifies whether distribution is allowed.
mobius.jobID OCR job ID.
mobius.jobType OCR job type.
mobius.archivingType Type of archiving.
mobius.archiveFileSize Archive file size.
mobius.OCRDirectory Location of OCR directory.
mobius.OCRprocessingType OCR processing type.
mobius.source Source of the new archive instance in CENTERA.
mobius.destination Destination of new archive instance in CENTERA.
mobius.detail Value is comma separated detailed elements. These elements describe additional
properties of Mobius.
The attributes in mobius section appear as per the action, but do not appear in all scenarios. The following is a

  "pageNumber": "1",

Exporting document metadata
Link the audit events in your Mobius applications to the documents that they affect by exporting Mobius document
metadata to the reporting database.
Exporting document metadata works independently of the core auditing process. While auditing logs system events
and user actions, metadata export is an optional feature that enhances reporting and analysis by storing document
metadata in the Content Insights database. While not required for auditing to function, combining it with audit logs
provides additional insights.
The following diagram shows the position of this task within the workflow of common administrative tasks. The
colored box indicates the starting point, while the dashed outline box represents an optional task.

Index export data reflects the state of the Mobius indexes at the time of export. You must enable the automatic index
export option for the reporting database to reflect the current state of the indexes.
The metadata for your ingested data is stored in a JSON table inside the reporting database. This makes it convenient
to join against events that relate to your document data and aggregate or filter with respect to metadata values.
The following example displays a JSON table for a document:
"{"id": "20170520000000#LABRPT#1#0#0", "AGE": "53", "DOB": "20170520 06/17/1956", "LAB": "02",
"SEX": "F", "SSN": "100-00-3000", "rpt_id": "LABRPT", "ACCOUNT": "40050060", "CONTROL": "1002016
83", "PATIENT": "100200", "SPECIMEN": "100-250-1245-0", "PHYSICIAN": "5468903", "docserver": "vd
rnetds", "DATE_5FCOL": "20170520", "page_count": "1", "section_id": "100-250-1245-0", "page_of_d
oc": "0", "rpt_version": "20170520000000", "rpt_version_date": "1495238400000", "starting_page_n
um": "1", "document_timestamp": "1658774795316"}"
A use case might be using the metadata about physicians and patients inside the exported data to construct a report
on how many new lab reports have been filed per quarter per physician. By default, there is a document events view
in the reporting database that lets you associate documents and events.
The following example shows a SQL query on the document events table for the Postgres database:

Exporting metadata using ftijobsubmit command
You can export metadata either automatically by enabling incremental export of metadata indices using Mobius
Administrator or manually using the ftijobsubmit command.
1. Run the following command by specifying the ingestions that you want to export to the reporting database.
ftijobsubmit -t TOPIC_EXPORT command
The index export process triggers jobs.
If DocServer1 is configured as the content server link, running theftijobsubmit command will export all
metadata for the LABRPT content class.
ftijobsubmit -s DocServer1 -r LABRPT -t TOPIC_EXPORT
2. Run the following command to view the status of the triggered jobs.
ftijobquery -d -s Mobius -r report_id
A sample status report is as follows:
./MobiusRemoteCLI-1.0.4/MobiusRemoteCLI.sh ftijobquery -d -s Mobius -r LABRPT
Mobius CLI tool

rs.
Tue Aug 30 13:40:31 2022 - The following jobs were found:
Tue Aug 30 13:40:32 2022 - StatusId=1, Status=COMPLETED, User=ADMIN, docs=1, docs=1
Submitted=Mon Aug 15 13:56:48 CDT 2022
Started =Mon Aug 15 13:56:50 CDT 2022
Completed=Mon Aug 15 13:56:54 CDT 2022
Waited 2.000s
Took 4.493s

You can also use additional options for the MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobsumit command to specify a date range
or other selections.
Related information
Configuring index export for auditing
MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobsubmit
Configuring index export for auditing
Metadata (indexes) from documents in a Mobius content source can be exported to the same database that is used
to store audit information about content. Administrators use this feature only if indexes, which are a part of
document information, are required for data analysis.
Index export is enabled at the repository level in Mobius Administrator. Any updates to documents in a repository
are exported to the configured database.
1. From Mobius Server > Mobius Content Sources, select a content source to display its repositories.
2. Hover over a repository, and click the Settings icon.
3. Expand the Auditing section, followed by the Index Export section.
a. Select Enable Index Export.
b. Complete the following fields.
The information from these fields form the information for the auditing database that receives the
exported indexes. New indexes or updates to existing indexes in the content source are stored to this
database.
JDBC URL
Enter the JDBC URL name for connecting to the database used for index export. This is typically the
same database used to store audit events.
Index Export Database User ID, Password
Specify the database user credentials.
JDBC Driver for Index Export
Select a valid JDBC driver name based on the DBMS of the selected ODBC DSN.
4. Click Apply.
Troubleshooting auditing
Review troubleshooting and diagnostics information when audit messages cannot be viewed in the Mobius View
Audit screen for reports.
Audit events are not routed to Kafka Topic
Mobius View uses a special license for enabling auditing. Without enabling this license in Mobius View
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 346


## Page 347


Administrator, the generated audit events are not routed to Kafka Topic. Validate that the Content Insights
license is available on the Authorization panel in Mobius View Administrator.
Verify system configuration for auditing
Configure the content source in the Mobius Administrator for Auditing. Refer to the topic Enable auditing
between Mobius View and Mobius Server in the Mobius Administrator guide for specifying the mandatory details
about Kafka that tracks the activities of the document server.
Verify the configuration of the events reported and sent as audit events
Check the audit events that has to be captured are configured accurately to be published to the Kafka Topic.
Refer to the topic Configuring audit log and events in the Mobius Administrator guide to configure specific events.
Verify audit messages for a document
In Mobius View, check if the toolbar option is available in the document viewer for the documents.
If the View Audit toolbar option is not available in the document viewer, check if the View Audit Event
permission is enabled in the Mobius View Administrator Authorization screen.
Missing audit events in Mobius View
If the audit events are missing in Mobius View after completing the above configuration, check the Kafka topic.
Run a query against the Kafka Broker to check if the topic contains any messages to confirm the audit messages
are published to the topic. Run the console consumer client to read the events from the topic audit.
bin/kafka-console-consumer.sh --bootstrap-server <host>:<port> --topic audit --from-beginni
ng
The above command lists all the audit messages from the beginning of the topic. If there are no messages, it
indicates that the communication between Mobius and Kafka Broker is broken. Refer to the Configuring the
Kafka environment for Mobius in the Mobius Deployment guide for configuring the connection between Mobius
and Kafka.
Missing audit events in Mobius View while audit events are present in the Kafka Topic
If the audit events are present in the Kafka Topic and missing in Mobius View, check if the events are present in
the reporting database. If the Event analytics service is initialized or configured accurately, the events table can
be viewed in the database. Run a query on the events table to verify the rows contain audit messages.
Verify the Event analytics service configuration, to read the audit messages from the Kafka Topic and to write
the audit events into the reporting database in case of missing events. Refer to topic Using Helm chart to deploy
Content Insights topic in the Mobius Deployment guide for configuring the Kafka and reporting database details.
Missing rows or indexes for documents
Run the select query on the documents table to confirm the indexes for documents:
In case of missing rows or indexes, check the configuration for index export for auditing in Mobius (Server)
Administrator. If the configurations are accurate, check the export job execution status using the ftijobquery
MobiusCLI command. For example, the following command queries the document server link configured as
'Mobius' and 'LABRPT' content class:
./MobiusRemoteCLI.sh ftijobquery -d -s Mobius -r LABRPT

## Page 349


Configure index update process
The Repository Security panel provides the option to enable the Index update process for a repository.
Mobius users must have the permission to request metadata correction and correct metadata to make index
updates. The changes must be approved by the Content Automation user with the IndexUpdateApprover application
role of the default MobiusApps in the Content Automation portal to receive approval notifications.
The approval notifications are received in the portal Inbox for the index update requests made by Mobius users who
have the Request Metadata Correction permission. In addition, the user should be assigned the Correct Metadata
permission in the Mobius Administrator for approving the request.
For more information on mapping users to application roles, see the "Assign user roles" topic in the Content
Automation guide.
Configuring metadata correction permissions
With metadata correction permissions, you can designate certain users to request index value correction and
handle the request through processes. Metadata correction is also referred to as index updates, where old and
new index values are displayed to a reviewer with the appropriate permissions.
Configuring default index updating process
Configure the default index updating process for Content Automation.
Related information
Mobius assets and permissions
Configuring metadata correction permissions
With metadata correction permissions, you can designate certain users to request index value correction and handle
the request through processes. Metadata correction is also referred to as index updates, where old and new index
values are displayed to a reviewer with the appropriate permissions.
In the Authorization panel of Mobius Administrator, there are two types of permissions for metadata correction in
the Mobius Permissions group :
Request Metadata Correction
Users assigned to this permission can edit index values and request approval for the change. This permission
allows an organization to designate certain Principal users, who can request for index value correction and
handle the request through the index update process. Refer Configuring Index Update Process to enable index
update process.
Correct Metadata
Users assigned to this permission have the same abilities as users with Request Metadata Correction
permission, but any changes initiated by a Principal user with this permission will immediately take effect
without requiring approval. This permission allows an organization to designate certain Principal users, who can
immediately update an index value or approve a change made by an index update requester.
Related information
Mobius assets and permissions
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 349


## Page 350


Configuring default index updating process
Configure the default index updating process for Content Automation.
1. Download the Default Index Update Process Project MobiusAppsProject.zip from Mobius distribution.
2. Import the downloaded MobiusAppsProject.zip into Content Automation Studio.
This project contains the process model, UI model, and data sources. For importing the project, see the Import
project topic in the Content Automation documentation.
3. Make the necessary changes to this project and generate a new package.
For generating package, see Package apps topic in the Content Automation documentation.
4. Upload the generated package, configure the datasource and App roles, and enable the App in the Content
Automation portal.
Refer to the Upload apps topic in the Content Automation documentation for information on uploading apps
and configuring app roles. Refer to Configuring Data Sources for configuring the datasource topic in the
Content Automation documentation.
Notes:
◦ Process model must be named as IndexUpdateProcess.pm and the package must be
named as MobiusApps for the mapping to work by default.
◦ In case of editing and changing these names prior to configuring the default process in
application.yaml the asg.workflow.indexUpdateWorkflowProcessName name entry
must be added with the value {pacakageName-processName}.
For example: asg.workflow.indexUpdateWorkflowProcessName:MobiusApps-
IndexUpdateProcess.
◦ These names can be modified after configuring the default process, if required by
modifying the policy rule in Policy Management.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 350


## Page 351


Manage storage
Mobius requires persistent storage for different types of data. Archives, configuration files, and log files are some of
the different types of data that can be stored on local or network storage, or on the cloud.
The following diagram shows the position of this task within the workflow of common administrative tasks. The
colored box indicates the starting point, while the dashed outline box represents an optional task.
Click a bo
you to tha
Mobius S
called Vie
Access Mobius

Deploy Mobius

Deploy Mobius
Server
Configure Mobius
View
Set up Mobius
features

Server

View trace
Configure Mobius
Server repositories
Configure auditing Configure content
classes, indexes
Configure roles,
users, permissions
Configur
Server
Enable
archiving
Manage
storage
Analyz
data (me
Factors when considering storage options:
• Cost – Higher performance influences financial and operational costs.
• Performance – Active applications require quick access, while applications for long-term retention require
infrequent access.
• Backup and disaster recovery – Local storage can be a single point of failure when data saved in servers are
located on-premises, while cloud storage can save data on multiple servers at different locations.
• Data encryption - Archives in Mobius are always encrypted. Storage providers can provide additional
encryption.
• Robustness – Refers to the durability and reliability of the storage solution to protect data from loss or
corruption.
To store archives in a way that ensures they remain accessible, adjust various factors, such as cost, capacity, and data
protection measures, to match the unique needs of archival storage.
There are different storage options for consideration.
Local storage
Storage devices are physically connected to a computer or server. The types of data stored on these devices
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 351


## Page 352


usually require quick access without significant size, access, or robustness requirements, and mainly used by
small businesses or personal use. This option is typically not recommended.
Network storage
Network storage refers to devices such as NAS (Network Attached Storage) and SAN (Storage Area Network) for
centralized storage over a network. Typically, an IT department sets policies for archiving and content viewing.
One endpoint might be configured for archiving, while another endpoint is set up for users to access and view
content.
Cloud storage
Cloud storage is storage maintained by third-party cloud providers. Running cloud in-house is another option
where an organization uses cloud technology in its own infrastructure without relying on third-party cloud
providers.
Mobius supports either AWS (Amazon Web Services) or Azure platforms or other cloud storage providers. For a
list of supported cloud storage providers, see the Mobius system requirements.
In general, it is advisable to store archives and log files separately as archives and log files can become quite large
over time.
For archives, deciding a storage option might depend on scalability and retention policies to store data based on
legal, regulatory, and business requirements. You can configure persistent volume and claims, for example, on
Kubernetes, or an alternative location for archives.
For log files, you can configure rollover log files. If detailed logging is required for Support issues, configure additional
space.
For clustered environments, you can deploy Mobius on the cloud or on-premises. Each node of the cluster must have
access to shared storage.
Mobius also integrates with compliant media for external storage, for example, EMC Centera or SnapLock . For
compliant storage media, see the Mobius system requirements. Mobius also supports hierarchical storage
management systems.
Configuring archiving to external storage systems
In Mobius Administrator, configure the file name template of the content class. On some storage systems, you
must also manually configure the mobius.reg file.
Configuring document archive file names for external storage systems
To configure the path and file name to a document archive, configure the filename template for the content
class in Mobius Administrator. The template contains literals and variables that substitute directory paths and
document archive file names with system-generated information.
Configuring Centera connection settings and application profile
To use Centera as a storage system, configure the Centera sections of a content source and repository in
Mobius Administrator.
Configuring a Hierarchical Storage Management System
When using a Hierarchical Storage Manager (HSM) system, you can configure Mobius to recognize and notify
users that a file has been moved.
Related information
Mobius system requirements
Creating a persistent volume and volume claim
Configuring an alternative location for document archive files
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 352


## Page 353


Configuring archiving to external storage systems
In Mobius Administrator, configure the file name template of the content class. On some storage systems, you must
also manually configure the mobius.reg file.
Before you begin:
• Create a content class.
• For Dell EMC Centera storage, a connection to Centera is required.
• For Amazon S3 storage system, storage system access depends on the following Mobius Server scenarios:
◦ Mobius Server running in AWS – In a non-container environment, for example, standalone on EC2, access
to S3 buckets is granted by using the AWS IAM groups to allow access from the EC2 instance to the S3
bucket.
◦ Mobius Server running in AWS EKS – Use Kubernetes RBAC (role-based access control) and IAM roles to
access to the S3 bucket.
◦ Mobius Server running locally – When Mobius Server is running external to AWS, configure the AWS S3
client with the AWS key and secrets (config, credentials) on the computer Mobius Server is on. For more
information, refer to the Amazon AWS documentation.
• Refer to the Mobius system requirements for supported external storage systems.
Note: Explicit authentication on Mobius is not required when archiving storage to external storage
systems.
1. On the external storage system, create a store for the archives.
For example, on Amazon S3 create a bucket named vdrarchivebucket. On Azure Blob, create a container
named vdrarchives.
2. In Mobius Administrator, configure the file name template of the content class.
a. From the left pane, click the Mobius Server icon.
b. On the Mobius Content Sources page, click a content source, followed by a repository.
c. On the repository settings page, click Content Classes to define the content for the external storage.
d. Click Add. Then expand the Filename section.
e. In the filename template field, define the content for the external storage.
f. To add variables to the template, click the icons below the Template field.
g. To configure the archive retention period for the content, expand the Retention section.
The retention period stops deletion of the archive until the retention period has passed. The Mobius
Server database information that points to the archive must not be deleted before the retention period
has passed since the archive itself cannot be deleted.
3. Depending on the storage system, configure the mobius.reg file to access the storage system.
Non-Amazon S3 storage system
In the mobius.reg file, add the following keys in the existing [HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Mobius\
VDRNET version\VDRSRV] section:
◦ Endpoint
◦ Access Key ID
◦ Secret Access Key
For example, specify on a single line:
[HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Mobius\VDRNET 12.2\VDRSRV]
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 353


## Page 354


"S3Endpoint"=
"api.objectstorage.cloudstorage.com"
"S3AccessKeyID"="xxxxxxxx0228"
"S3SecretAccessKey"="d6M2nxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx"
Even if the storage system is not AWS, AccessKeyID and SecretAccessKey are required to verify identity
and authorize access. The Endpoint, AccessKeyID, and SecretAccessKey values are based on the
definition on the storage system.
For example, endpoints are :
◦ For IBM COS - api.objectstorage.cloudstorage.com
◦ For EMC ECS - 123456789@ecstestdrive.emc.com
◦ For Google Storage - storage.googleapis.com
Amazon S3 storage system
Review the Mobius Server scenarios in the pre-requisites before editing the mobius.reg file.
In the mobius.reg file, add the following key in the existing [HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Mobius\
VDRNET version\VDRSRV] section:
◦ Endpoint, only if using a non-default endpoint. For more information about AWS endpoints, see
Amazon AWS documentation.
For example, specify on a single line:
[HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Mobius\VDRNET 3.2\VDRSRV]
"S3Endpoint"="s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com"
Azure blob storage system
To enable support for archiving to the Azure blob storage, locate the [HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\
Mobius\VDRNet version\VDRSRV] section in the mobius.reg file, and then on a single line, add the
connection string value from the Access keys that is in the Azure storage account:
"AzBlobConnectionString"="Connection String taken from Azure"
For example, specify on a single line:
"AzBlobConnectionString"="DefaultEndpointsProtocol=https;AccountName=mobiusqablob;
AccountKey=cZBiHNYk6DGTv29vjeWPI5NSzRPbAx2hJZyj9/5Q34jhLsO22UIcyArN2oYUwboR/Ie+OdgRFox
3XdtJ8YDO1g==;
EndpointSuffix=core.windows.net"
Configuring document archive file names for external storage systems
To configure the path and file name to a document archive, configure the filename template for the content class in
Mobius Administrator. The template contains literals and variables that substitute directory paths and document
archive file names with system-generated information.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 354


## Page 355


Content class (Filename section)
Expand the Filename section of a content class definition to configure the document archive file name template.
EMC Centera storage
Document archive filename template format for content on the Centera storage:
<CENTERA>::/
Note: Enclose the <CENTERA> storage class in angle brackets followed by two colons.
Amazon S3 storage system
Document archive filename template format for content on the Amazon S3 bucket:
<AMAZONS3>:://existing-S3-bucket/rest-of-model-filename
<AMAZONS3>:: is the S3 storage identifier and must match exactly for Mobius to identify it as a S3 report.
Note: Enclose the <AMAZONS3> storage class in angle brackets followed by two colons.
Example:
<AMAZONS3>:://vdrarchivebucket/%REPORTID%/%ARCHIVEDATE%/%ARCHIVETIME%%UNIQUE.2%.DAF
Note:
• The separators in the bucket name provide the folder structure on the S3 storage system.
• Use a forward slash (/) as a separator.
• Specify the bucket name between the storage identifier and the next separator.
Azure Blob storage system
Pieces of content that are archived to the Azure blob storage system are defined in Mobius using the document
archive filename template that specifies its storage class. For each content managed by this storage system, the
document archive filename template will refer to a blob container.
When content is archived, the document archive filename template is read to make a connection to the storage
system, and archive to the defined location using migration policies. A data object name from the document
archive filename template is also generated.
Document archive filename template format for content for the Azure blob:
<AZBLOB>:://container/rest-of-model-filename
<AZBLOB>:: is the Azure blob storage identifier and must exactly match for Mobius to identify it as an Azure
blob report.
Note: Enclose the <AZBLOB> storage class in angle brackets followed by two colons.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 355


## Page 356


Example:
<AZBLOB>:://vdrachives/%REPORTID%/%ARCHIVEDATE%/%ARCHIVETIME% %UNIQUE.2%.DAF
Note:
• Use a forward slash (/) as a separator.
• Container names can be 3–63 characters and consist of only lowercase letters, numbers,
and hyphens. The name must begin with a letter or a number. Each hyphen must be
preceded and followed by a non-hyphen character.
• The separators in the container name provide the folder structure on the Azure Blob
storage.
ISILON
Document archive filename template format for content for the ISILON storage:
<ISILON>:://path/filename
Note: Enclose the <ISILON> storage class in angle brackets followed by two colons. Then set the
path (IP address, relative, or absolute) to connect to the storage system.
Examples:
<ISILON>:://216.150.141.236/isilon$/dafs/%REPORTID%/%ArchiveDate%/%UNIQUE%.DAF
<ISILON>::s:/dafs/%REPORTID%/%ArchiveDate%/%UNIQUE%.DAF
<ISILON>::/isilon/dafs/%REPORTID%/%ArchiveDate%/%UNIQUE%.DAF
SnapLock-compliant storage system
NetApp SnapLock and Permabit Permeon are examples of a Snaplock-compliant storage system. Document
archive filename template format for content for a SnapLock-compliant system:
<SNAPLOCK>::/path/filename
Note: Enclose the <SNAPLOCK> storage class in angle brackets followed by two colons. Then set the
path (IP address, relative, or absolute) to connect to the storage system.
Examples:
<SNAPLOCK>:://216.150.141.236/snaplock$/dafs/%REPORTID%/%ArchiveDate%/%UNIQUE%.DAF
<SNAPLOCK>::s:/dafs/%REPORTID%/%ArchiveDate%/%UNIQUE%.DAF
<SNAPLOCK>::/snaplock/dafs/%REPORTID%/%ArchiveDate%/%UNIQUE%.DAF
Template examples for document archive file names
Instead of naming individual document archive files, create a template to automatically generate a unique path and
document archive file name on a host system or external storage system.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 356

having access to those drives and a viewing client such as Mobius View, place them in a directory accessible to one or
more content sources but not to users. This will help secure the files.
Windows

Configuring Centera connection settings and application profile
To use Centera as a storage system, configure the Centera sections of a content source and repository in Mobius
Administrator.
For a Mobius Server instance to connect to a Centera server, configure the connection settings.
1. From Mobius Server > Mobius Content sources, select a content source.
a. On a selected content source, click the Settings icon.
b. In the details pane, expand Centera Storage System and complete these fields.
IP addresses
Enter the IP address and port number that Mobius Server uses to connect to the Centera server,
then click Add.
Repeat for each of your Centera server's available access nodes.
Buffer size
Specify the buffer size while archiving content to Centera. Mobius Server stores data in this buffer
before archiving it to Centera; when the buffer is full, the data is written to Centera. The default
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 359


## Page 360


buffer size is 1 MB.
Centera has a 10-second time limit when writing consecutive calls. Buffering restricts the amount of
data written to Centera, so that each operation does not exceed this time limit. Check the buffer
size does not exceed the time limit, but is large enough to remain efficient. You can tune the buffer
size to achieve maximum efficiency after a successful archival to Centera.
c. Click Apply.
2. To specify the application profile the content source accesses to connect to the Centera server, configure a
repository associated with the content source.
a. Select the content source from the previous step, and click to display its repositories.
b. Select a repository, then click the Settings icon.
c. In the details page, expand Centera.
d. Select the application profile to use.
Use the specified application profile
The content source accesses this profile to connect to the Centera server with settings from the
default application profile or a Pool Entry Authorization (PEA) file.
Use the default application profile specified by the environment variable
The content source accesses the profile through the CENTERA_PEA_LOCATION environment variable.
Set this variable to the Pool Entry Authorization (PEA) file to allow all repositories to access the
Centera servers that do not have an application profile configured through the user interface. If the
variable is not set, only repositories with a specific configuration can access a Centera device, or
other storage devices that use the Centera API.
Note: All content sources that are configured to connect to a Centera server will connect to
the same Centera server.
e. Click Apply.
Configuring a Hierarchical Storage Management System
When using a Hierarchical Storage Manager (HSM) system, you can configure Mobius to recognize and notify users
that a file has been moved.
To activate changes made on the HSM Sensitivity tab, restarting Mobius Server is required.
1. From Mobius Server > Mobius Content Sources, select an existing content source.
2. Click the Settings icon.
3. Expand the HSM Sensitivity pane.
a. Enable HSM sensitivity to notify users that a file has been moved by HSM.
b. Select the method of detecting HSM migration. The method that you select must match the method HSM
uses to mark migrated files.
c. If you select the option to replace the archive file with a smaller placeholder file, specify the size (bytes)
of the placeholder file.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 360

Manage content life cycle
Mobius enables organizations to manage their content throughout its entire life cycle, from creation or addition to
retention and deletion, in accordance with regulatory requirements.
