Configuring retention type for content class
Archive management
Archive management enables organizations to retain the content in accordance with the regulatory requirements. It
also helps in optimizing the information storage across enterprises.
The major operations in the archive management life cycle are as follows:
• Creation of archives
• Retention of archives
• Migration of archives
• Deletion of archives
Mobius offers multiple tools for archive management, such as unattended mode commands or records
management. You can manually perform the archive management operations or automate them with help of rules
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 361


## Page 362


and policies.
Related information
Unattended mode commands
Records Management
Creation of archives
Mobius offers multiple methods to archive content. Use these methods to set up a systematic practice of preserving
and storing digital content in an your organization.
Methods to archive content
Archive content from various repositories using several automatic or manual methods. Use the following methods to
automate the creation of archiving:
• ArchiveWrite API — This is a Mobius REST API. It supports processing methods such as List files processing and
Policy-based processing. For more information, see Mobius Developer Guide.
• acreate CLI — This is a command line utility. You can use it to perform operations on Mobius Server. This utility
communicates with Mobius View using public REST APIs. It supports processing methods, such as List files
processing, meta-file based processing, and Policy-based processing. For more information, see
MobiusRemoteCLI acreate.
• acreate command — This is an unattended mode command. It is passed onto the Mobius application to
process without using the application's user interface when working in a product environment. It supports
processing methods, such as List files processing, meta-file based processing, and Policy-based processing. For
more information, see acreate command - Creating archives.
• Meta-file based archiving — Use a single meta file to archive multiple documents in one instance, using the
ArchiveWrite REST API. This supports archiving in paginated mode, without policy processing of document
content, along with the ability to specify page-scope index values. For more information, see Meta-file based
archiving.
You can use the Capture option in Mobius View to manually archive content in Mobius View. It takes any type of
document and archives it in native format with index values that you provide while performing the archive operation.
For more information, see Mobius View User Guide.
Methods to process archives
Indexing is an important process when you create archives. Without systematic indexing, the retrieval of data that is
available in archives is impossible.
Mobius offers the following methods for indexing and processing archives:
• Policy-based archiving — A policy is a set of field-specific criteria that locates and extracts data, constants (such
as index and column headings), and metadata from content. The extracted information is used to create
archive index entries and to determine whether to archive content in a new or existing document archive file
(DAF).
• List file processing — A list file references multiple pieces of content and lets you specify the content class ID
that is used for all pieces of content in the list, along with the indexing used for each content. It is used for
content such as images. If Mobius cannot read the content, rules from a policy are not applied.
For more information, see Types of archiving.
Related information
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 362


## Page 363


MobiusRemoteCLI acreate
Types of archiving
acreate command - Creating archives
Retention of archives
Mobius is a custodian of content, retaining content for exactly the time it needs to be preserved for legal reasons,
and deleting content based on legal and privacy requirements.
You can specify rules for archives retention. These rules lets you delete groups of archives individually at different
times as they expire for different reasons. A rule typically contains a criteria to filter archives and the period for which
you want to retain the archives. Multiple methods are available in Mobius Administrator to specify the retention
period and the criteria for filtering archives.
Methods to specify retention period
• Fixed retention period—You can specify the retention period of archives in days, weeks, months, and
years.
• Event-based retention—You can retain the archives until a specific event happens or for a specific period
after a triggering event.
You can also choose to retain the archives forever or delete them immediately.
Methods to filter archives
You can use various parameters to specify the criteria for archives that must be retained. For example:
• Archive creation time
• Ingestion ID
• Document ID
• Collection ID
• Index value
For more information, see Configuring retention type for content class and Record retention policies.
When the retention period of an archive is over, it becomes eligible for deletion. However, the actual deletion
happens when the purge operations run on a server.
Related information
Configuring retention type for content class
Record retention policies
Migration of archives
Use Mobius to control the migration of archives between disk files and other storage systems.
Use Mobius to:
• Maintain multiple copies of archives on different storage media.
• Control deletion of an archive copy after a defined expiration period.
• Control where an archive resides and for how long it must be retained.
It gives you dynamic control over archive storage.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 363


## Page 364


Setting up migration
You can setup migration rules to:
• Analyze the content of the archives and determine archives that are ready for migration.
• Configure time, frequency, and the location for migrating these archives.
• Establish retention criteria for the migrated archives.
Use Mobius to add migration rules to the database by importing XML data using the vdrdbxml command. Update
migration rules in the database tables in the proper sequence to satisfy the database consistency rules. Although
modification of all the migration tables is supported, modify only the MIGRATION_RULE, MIGRATION_CLASS and
REPORT_MIGRATION tables.
For information on updating a local database, see Importing and Exporting XML Data in a Local Database.
You can use a script to run the amigrate command and automate migration schedule.
For each content class ID that is specified, amigrate verifies the existence of migration rules and creates a content
migration record for each new ingestion. This record indicates the next action date of the migration rule.
For the specified content class ID, amigrate determines if it must process any migration actions. If an ingestion has a
next action date that has expired and is not on hold, amigrate performs the migration action. Upon successfully
completing the migration action, amigrate updates the content migration table to indicate the next migration action
and date. Before processing is complete, amigrate continues to loop through the content migration table until no
actions require further processing.
For more information, see amigrate command - Migrating document archive files.
Retention of migrated archives
You can use the ingestion retention or ingestion instance retention to configure retention of migrated archives.
• Ingestion instance retention — Migration rules that are added to the database using the vdrdbxml command
set the ingestion instance retention. This is an additional retention that is applicable even after migration.
• Ingestion retention — The amount of time that an ingestion exists before adelete deletes it. If an ingestion is
due for deletion, adelete deletes it without considering the migration rules. This retention is defined when you
configure retention type for a content class. For more information, see Configuring retention type for content
class.
When migrating to a storage system that supports retention, ingestion retention is used only if the ingestion instance
retention is not set in the migration rules. By setting the retention in the migration rules to a shorter period than the
ingestion retention, an ingestion instance can be removed or migrated to another storage system before the
ingestion is deleted. If the ingestion retention is not set, setting the retention in the migration rules forces retention
of the specified instance.
Related information
amigrate command - Migrating document archive files
Configuring retention type for content class
Deletion of archives
The deletion and retention of archives depends upon the legal, regulatory, and privacy requirements. Use Mobius for
automatic and manual deletion of archives.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 364


## Page 365


You can delete archives from the primary storage, such as disk files, persistent volumes and claims or secondary
storage, such as network or cloud storage.
Deleting archives on a document sever
Use the following unattended mode command to delete archives:
adelete command — This command is passed onto the Mobius application to process without using the application's
user interface when working in a product environment. The adelete command deletes specified and expired
ingestions and indexes associated with a deleted ingestion.
You can create a script to run this command and automate the process of deleting archives.
Deleting content on a document server
Use the Mobius Administrator to delete content classes on a repository if your user role has the required
permissions.
Related information
adelete command - Deleting archives
Records Management
Records management enables organizations to produce all records relevant to an investigation, audit, or a legal case.
Records management is the professional practice of managing the records of an organization throughout their life
cycle, from the time they are created to their eventual disposal. An organization's records preserve its corporate
memory. The retention period of records depends on their capacity for re-use. Many records are retained as
evidence of a transaction.
Records Management in Mobius enables you to perform following tasks:
• Declare records and define retention
• Define folders and file plans
• Set event-based retention policies for records
• Place holds on records to prevent them from being deleted
• Remove holds on records that are no longer required
• Search the records management folder structure
• Browse, locate, and view records
The records management is an alternative method of archive management. It involves multiple Mobius components
such as Mobius Administrator, Mobius View, and Mobius z/OS.
When you implement Records Management, Mobius View archives documents as records in a records management
repository, classifies them, organizes them in a folder structure, and assigns retention periods.
The Mobius system administrator or senior records management administrator creates the classification categories
and specifies how records are classified and how long they are retained. Additionally, ordinary users can act as
records management administrators who can place holds on documents to extend their retention.
Related information
Record Types
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 365


## Page 366


Creating enterprise folders
Content viewing
Revision control
Prerequisites for records management
Mobius system administrator or senior records management administrator can perform the records management
tasks when the records management is activated in Mobius Administrator.
Ensure that the following configurations are complete in Mobius Administrator:
• Enable the application server security when you install Mobius:
Configure the application.yaml file by setting the vendor attribute to the relevant application server.
Otherwise, when vendor is set to NONE, set the primary repository recipient in a Record Management User
role on the Authorization panel.
• Configure Mobius View to support SSO repository authentication. Other authentication options are Alias or
Prompt.
• For each repository that store content for records management, you must complete the following
configurations in the repository properties:
◦ Select the Enable Records Management checkbox.
◦ Select the Disable universal index checkbox.
◦ Map the repository indexes with the universal indexes. For more information, see Mapping repository
indexes to universal indexes.
• Provide RecordsManagerUser and RecordsManagerAdministrator permissions for the selected users of
Mobius View or Mobius z/OS.
• Select the Enable hold functions checkbox in the Content Viewing section of Mobius Administrator. Users can
place and rescind holds from Mobius View.
• To set event-based retention policies for records, select Event-based retention for the Retention section of a
content class definition.
Before Mobius View users start archiving records, determine how you want to organize the records. You can either
define your folder structure ahead of time or generate automatic folders from unique index values. For more
information, see Enterprise folders.
For example, a bank may archive customers’ checks. Mobius treats each check as a document in a content class on a
repository. Each repository has a collection of indexes that it supports for its documents. If the bank wants to use
records management for the checks, it maps the repository indexes with the universal indexes. Then, each check that
clears is imaged and archived automatically as a record.
You can use the metadata in a record to define a folder structure. Here you can use the check number, account
number, transit/routing information, date, and perhaps amounts as critical metadata elements. Later, the bank/
branch information from transit/routing can be used as an automatic folder, followed by an account number
automatic folder, and finally, individual checks presented in it or within a predefined sub-folder just for checks.
Improve scalability
The maxConcurrentOpsPerServer setting in the application.yaml file controls the rate at which retention change
events are processed at the server. The default value of this parameter is set to 2 to ensure that a spike in retention
change settings does not overload the server. You can increase this number to increase throughput but it can put a
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 366


## Page 367


significant additional load on the database subsystem.
Related information
Mapping repository indexes to universal indexes
Defining content viewing options
Mobius assets and permissions
Configuring Mobius Server repositories
Managing enterprise folders
Event templates
Event template facilitate event-based records management. Each Event Template has a unique name that identifies
an event type and a list of parameters that are associated with the event.
For example, an employee leaves a company is an event and employee ID is a parameter associated with the event.
You can create a template with a unique name, such as End of Employment and create a parameter, such as
employee_num.
Mobius passes the event name and parameter to a retention policy. You can define an event-based retention policy
for a specific record type.
You can edit or delete event templates after creation but before they are associated with a record retention policy.
Event processing for records management is handled through the Mobius REST API or Records Management Client. A
typical order of event-related calls that you need to make is:
• GET /rest/rm/eventtemplates to find the appropriate event template ID.
• (Optional) GET /rest/rm/retentions to get a list of available retention reasons in use.
• POST to /rest/rm/events to trigger the event.
• (As necessary) POST to /rest/rm/events to rescind the event.
For more information, see Mobius Developer Guide.
Related information
Record retention policies
Adding event templates
Event templates are needed for creating and using policies. To add a new event template, use the Event Templates
area in the Mobius Administrator.
1. Click the
  icon and select Event Templates in the left pane.
Existing event templates appear in the left pane.
2. Click Add in the left pane.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 367


## Page 368


The New Event Template pane is displayed.
3. Enter a unique name for the event template in the Event Template Name field.
The name can contain alphanumeric characters underscores (_), and hyphens (-), and can be up to 64
characters long.
4. Optionally, provide a description in the Event Template Description field.
5. In the Params section, in the Name column, enter the name of the parameter and then click
  icon to
associate the parameter with event template.
The parameters are used by policies to qualify records and apply rules to the qualified records.
The name can contain alphanumeric characters, underscores (_), hyphens (-), and blank spaces and can be up
to 64 characters long.
You can edit the parameters list or name of the event template only when the template is not associated with
any retention policy.
Record Types
Record types are defined outside Records Management. The Records Management permits viewing available record
types that reside outside of Records Management in a Mobius View repository but does not permit modifying them.
The record types are documents that are archived by Mobius and the names of record types are generated from
Content Class IDs associated with those documents.
You can view a list of record types in Mobius Administrator. Click the
  icon and select Record Types in the left
pane. The left pane displays a list of record types. You can select a specific record type from the list of records.
The Record Types summary is displayed in the right pane. The policies list and event templates for this record type
are displayed in the Associated Record Policies and Event Templates section of the right pane.
Note: You cannot edit or delete a record type.
Related information
Creating content classes
Record retention policies
Use the record retention policies to automate the deletion of records when they are no longer required. A policy is
applied to records belonging to a record type that you select, responds to a business event, and deletes the records
meeting the criteria you have specified.
For example, a company’s Human Resources department can create record retention policies to set a number of
event-based retentions on an employee’s records. These policies can automate the deletion of records associated
with the employee when the employee leaves the company. The HR department can create an event template with
the name, End of employment. The employee number can be the parameter of the event template. The HR
department can create one policy for deletion of projects that the employee worked on.
• All projects associated with an employee are available in the record type project_name and they are stored in a
repository that has employee number as an index. The HR department can create a policy to delete all projects
associated with the employee on a specified date.
• If the same employee has a patent pending, a policy can set the retention period for all of the employee’s
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 368


## Page 369


project records to become eligible for deletion after 20 years from the date the patent is granted. The
repository can have a separate index for all patent pending projects.
Now the HR department can create a second policy for the record type pay stub. The policy can set the retention
period for all of the employee’s pay stub records to become eligible for deletion after two years from the date the
employee left.
Mobius passes the event name and parameter to a retention policy. Event processing for records management is
handled through the Mobius REST API or Records Management Client. For more information, see Mobius Developer
Guide.
Related information
Adding record deletion rules
Creating record retention policies
A record retention policy automates the deletion of records that meet the criteria you have specified. You can use the
even template and parameters to define the criteria.
1. From the left menu, click the Records Management
  icon and select Record Retention Policies in the left
pane.
Existing policies are displayed in the left pane.
2. Click Add in the left pane.
3. In the right pane, enter a unique name for the policy in the Record Retention Policy Name field.
Policy names can contain alphanumeric characters, underscores (_), hyphens (-), and blank spaces and can be
up to 64 characters long.
4. Optional: Enter a description for the policy in the Description field.
5. Choose a record type for the policy in the Record type list.
Select the All(All record types) option if the policy should apply to all record types.
The policy will apply to all repositories with the records management option enabled, which includes indexes
specified in the Record Deletion Rules of the policy.
6. Choose an event template for the policy in the Event template list.
7. In the Record Retention Rules section, click Add a rule to set the deletion date for the records that meet the
specified criteria.
You can set one or more rules for record deletion. For more information, see Adding record deletion rules.
8. Click the Event Date field to set the deletion date for the records that meet the criteria.
9. In the Policy Application section, select the Continue to apply the policy to new records added after the
initial event for a period checkbox.
10. Specify a period after the triggering event during which the policy periodically looks for newly created records
that match the policy rules and sets the appropriate retention for them.
This feature is disabled by default in the application.yaml configuration file. You must manually enable it. For
more information, see Mobius Deployment Guide.
11. Click Apply.
Related information
Record Types
Event templates
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 369


## Page 370


Adding record deletion rules
Create record retention policy and add rules to set the deletion date for the records that meet the specified criteria.
1. Click the
  icon and select Record Retention Policies in the left pane.
Existing policies are displayed in the left pane.
2. Click Add in the left pane.
3. In the right pane, enter a unique name for the policy in the Record Retention Policy Name field.
4. Choose a record type and an event template for the policy.
5. In the Record Retention Rules section, click Add a rule.
6. Click the Event Date field to set the deletion date for the records that meet the criteria.
7. In the Deletion Date dialog box, select one of the following options:
◦ Event date based- Specify the period for which you want to hold the records after the triggering event.
Use the when to delete section to specify the period.
For example, if you specify Event date + 7 years, records are retained for seven years after the event
occurs.
◦ Event parameter based - Specify the period for which you want to hold the records after the specified
parameter meets the specified criteria. The parameter is defined in the event template and you can
select the parameter in the when to delete section. Specify the retention period in the when to delete
section.
For example, the patent grant date is specified as a parameter in an event template. If you specify patent
grant date + 20 years in the when to delete section, employee’s project records are retained for 20
years from the date the patent is granted.
◦ On hold indefinitely - Mark the records to hold indefinitely or never to be deleted unless the hold is
removed.
◦ Delay deletion until the first day of the next calendar year checkbox - Retain the records till the
January 1 that follows the triggering event or the parameter based event.
8. In the Deletion Date dialog box, select the Delay deletion until the first day of the next calendar year to
retain the records till the January 1 that follows the event.
9. Click Apply in the Deletion Date dialog box.
10. Click the Click here to define criteria field to define the criteria for filtering records.
11. In the Search Criteria dialog box, select an index from the Index list and an operator from the Operators list.
12. Enter the index value in the Value field.
When the triggering event happens, records retention policy uses the specified index value to filter records
from the selected record type and sets the deletion date for those records.
If the event template has parameters, a list of parameters appears in the Value field. Select a parameter to
specify the index value for filtering the records. To enter an index value instead of selecting the parameter,
select the Customize checkbox.
13. Optional: Click Add criterion to specify more than one criterion for filtering the records.
All the criteria specified within a single record deletion rule are applied together (that is, Boolean AND).
Deletion date is set for records that meet every specified criteria.
14. Click Apply in the Search Criteria dialog box.
15. Optional: In the Record Retention Policy pane, click Add a rule to add more rules.
Each record deletion rule operates individually (that is, Boolean OR). The rule applies to a subset of the records
that meet a specific criteria and determines when that particular group is ready for deletion. You can add
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 370


## Page 371


multiple record deletion rules in a single policy if you want to delete records individually at different times as
they expire for different reasons, rather than all together.
16. Click Apply.
When the purge operations run on a document server, it deletes the records that are ready for deletion.
Related information
Record Types
Creating record retention policies
Event templates
Webhook policy
Use the webhook policy to create or update indexes for specific records in the repositories. Mobius REST APIs are
used to add and update the indexes.
The webhook policy enables you to automate the processes of creating and updating the indexes after a triggering
event. The parameters in the event can be used to filter the records for adding or updating the indexes.
For example, an event template is available in Records Management for the event, customer leaves. The event
template has parameters for customer's account number. The account number parameter can be used to filter all
records related to the customer. You can create a webhook policy to update indexes associated with the records of
the customer.
Once you create and save a policy with Webhook rules, you cannot change the name, description, event template, or
record type that is associated with the policy. However, you can add, edit, or remove Webhook rules.
Related information
Configure content classes and indexes
Event templates
Creating webhook policies
The Records Management record Webhook policy responds to a specified event, applies to a specified record type,
and causes a Webhook to be called.
Before you begin: The event template and record type which you designate when creating a policy must already
exist. Each Webhook policy must have a unique combination of event template and record type.
1. Click the Records Management
  icon.
Existing policies are displayed in the left pane.
2. On the Records Management panel, click Add.
3. In the right pane, enter a unique name for the policy in the Webhook Policy Name field.
Policy names can contain alphanumeric characters, underscores (_), hyphens (-), and blank spaces and can be
up to 64 characters long.
4. (optional) Enter a description for the policy in the Description field.
5. Select a record type for the policy in the Record type field.
6. Select an event template for the policy in the Event template field.
7. In the Webhook Rules section, add rules to create or update indexes for specific records from the selected
record type.
8. In the Policy Application section, select the Continue to apply the policy to new records added after the
initial event for a period checkbox.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 371


## Page 372


9. Specify a period after the triggering event during which the policy periodically looks for newly created records
that match the policy rules and creates or updates indexes for them.
This feature is disabled by default in the application.yaml configuration file. You must manually enable it. For
more information, see Mobius Deployment Guide.
10. Click Apply.
Related information
Adding webhook rules for creating indexes
Adding webhook rules for updating indexes
Adding webhook rules for creating indexes
Create webhook policy and add rules to create indexes for specific records as a response for a specified event.
Before you begin: The event template and record type which you designate when creating a policy must already
exist. Each Webhook policy must have a unique combination of event template and record type.
1. Click the Records Management
  icon.
Existing policies are displayed in the left pane.
2. On the Records Management panel, click Add.
3. In the right pane, enter a unique name for the policy in the Webhook Policy Name field.
Policy names can contain alphanumeric characters, underscores (_), hyphens (-), and blank spaces and can be
up to 64 characters long.
4. Optional: Enter a description for the policy in the Description field.
5. Select a record type for the policy in the Record type field.
6. Select an event template for the policy in the Event template field.
7. In the Webhook Rules section, click Add a rule.
8. Click the Click here to enter webhook information field to specify the information that is required to call the
webhook.
9. In the Webhook Info dialog box, in the Method list, select POST.
10. In the URL field, enter the Webhook url which will be called whenever event is triggered.
11. In the Accept field, enter a value which denotes the response type that the client prefers.
12. In the Content -Type field, enter a value which denotes the server format of the request.
13. In the Authorization field, enter the user name / password for the API client.
14. In the Body field, enter the payload for the POST request. For example:
{
  "indexRequests": [
    {
      "documentId": "<document ID value>",
      "indexScope": {
        "relativePageNumber": 0,
        "numberOfPages": 1
      },
      "indexEntries": [
         {
           "name": "<index>",
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 372


## Page 373


"value": "<index value>"
         }
       ]
     }
   ]
}
The document ID for a record is available in Mobius View. The Post request will add the <index> and <index
value> for the record that meets the criteria specified in the rule. Instead of document ID you can use
collection ID when you send the request.
15. Click Apply in the Webhook Info dialog box.
16. Click the Click here to define criteria field to define the criteria for selecting specific records for adding an
index and index value
17. In the Search Criteria dialog box, select an index from the Index list and then select an operator from the
Operators list.
18. Enter the index value in the Value field.
When the triggering event happens, webhook policy uses the specified index value to filter records from the
selected record type and sends the post request to add the <index> and <index value> for the records that
meet the specified criteria.
If the event template has parameters, the list of parameters appears in the Value field. Select a parameter to
specify the index value for filtering the records from the selected record type. To enter an index value instead
of selecting the parameter, select the Customize checkbox.
19. Optional: Click Add criterion to specify more than one criterion for filtering the records.
20. Click Apply in the Search Criteria dialog box.
21. Click Apply in the Webhook policy pane.
Related information
Record Types
Event templates
Content indexing examples
Adding webhook rules for updating indexes
Create webhook policy and add rules to update indexes for specific records as a response for a specified event.
Before you begin: The event template and record type which you designate when creating a policy must already
exist. Each webhook policy must have a unique combination of event template and record type.
1. Click the Records Management
  icon.
Existing policies are displayed in the left pane.
2. On the Records Management panel, click Add.
3. In the right pane, enter a unique name for the policy in the Webhook Policy Name field.
Policy names can contain alphanumeric characters, underscores (_), hyphens (-), and blank spaces and can be
up to 64 characters long.
4. Optional: Enter a description for the policy in the Description field.
5. Select a record type for the policy in the Record type field.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 373


## Page 374


6. Select an event template for the policy in the Event template field.
7. In the Webhook Rules section, click Add a rule.
8. Click the Click here to enter webhook information field to specify the information that is required to call the
webhook.
9. In the Webhook Info dialog box, in the Method list, select PUT.
10. In the URL field, enter the Webhook url which will be called whenever event is triggered.
11. In the Accept field, enter a value which denotes the response type that the client prefers.
12. In the Content -Type field, enter a value which denotes the server format of the request.
13. In the Authorization field, enter the user name / password for the API client.
14. In the Body field, enter the payload for the PUT request. For example:
{
  "indexRequests": [
    {
      "documentId": "<document ID value>",
      "indexScope": {
        "relativePageNumber": 0,
        "numberOfPages": 1
      },
      "indexEntries": [
         {
           "name": "<index>",
           "currentvalue": "<existing index value>"
           "newValue": "<new index value>"
         }
       ]
     }
   ]
}
The document ID for a record is available in Mobius View. The PUT request will replace the <existing index
value> in the <index> with the <new index value>. Instead of document ID you can use collection ID when you
send the request.
15. Click Apply in the Webhook Info dialog box.
16. Click the Click here to define criteria field to define the criteria for selecting specific records for updating the
index.
17. In the Search Criteria dialog box, select an index from the Index list and then select an operator from the
Operators list.
18. Enter the index value in the Value field.
When the triggering event happens, webhook policy uses the specified index value to filter records from the
selected record type and sends the PUT request to update the index for the filtered records.
If the event template has parameters, the list of parameters appears in the Value field. Select a parameter to
specify the index value for filtering the records. To enter an index value instead of selecting the parameter,
select the Customize checkbox.
19. Optional: Click Add criterion to specify more than one criterion for filtering the records.
20. Click Apply in the Search Criteria dialog box.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 374


## Page 375


21. Click Apply in the Webhook policy pane.

## Page 376


Configure trace and log settings
After configuring trace and log settings for the client, server, and application server, download a diagnostics file as a
compressed file. System administrators and Rocket Technical Support can use this information to resolve issues.
To configure tracing and logging, use the Diagnostics panel in Mobius Administrator.
Configuring client trace logging
Client trace logging will log errors for individual Mobius View users. Client service request information, such as
the user name, service name, and so on, are logged to the RDSWIN.MSG file.
Configuring Mobius Server trace logging
Mobius Server trace logging will log information between the Mobius Server instance and a specific viewing
client that is connected to the server. The default severity level for server trace logging is 'errors and above'.
Configuring application server logging
For application server logging, configure where to display log information. To log to a file, you must configure
the size and number of log files to retain. To log requests, resources, and specific information from Mobius,
configure the performance and diagnostics log levels.
Configuring client trace logging
Client trace logging will log errors for individual Mobius View users. Client service request information, such as the
user name, service name, and so on, are logged to the RDSWIN.MSG file.
By default, only critical errors are logged to the RDSWIN.MSG file. You can change the severity level and the location of
the log file.
The following diagram shows the position of this task within the workflow of common administrative tasks. The
colored box indicates the starting point, while the dashed outline box represents an optional task.

1. In Mobius Administrator, select Diagnostics from the left menu.
2. Expand the Client Tracing section.
3. Select the log level in the Client trace level field.
Do not change the default log level unless instructed by Rocket Software Technical Support.
4. Enter path and file name for the log file in the Client trace file field.
By default, error messages are logged to this file: ${sys:mobius.home}/logs/RDSWIN.MSG
where:
◦ $ is a variable that represents a path on the server. You can replace the variable name with a fully
qualified path. But using the variable supports deployment in a cluster where the path is different on
each node.
◦ sys:mobius.home is the path where the Mobius View application is installed.
For example, {user.home}/asg/mobius, where user.home is the user's local directory, for example,
C:/Users/johndoe.
Configuring Mobius Server trace logging
Mobius Server trace logging will log information between the Mobius Server instance and a specific viewing client that
is connected to the server. The default severity level for server trace logging is 'errors and above'.
The mobius.log file stores all messages that are generated by the Mobius View application. Do not edit this file
unless instructed by Rocket Software Technical Support.

1. In Mobius Administrator, select Diagnostics from the left menu.
2. Expand the Mobius Server Tracing section.
3. Select the level for logging messages in the Server trace level field.
Do not change the default log level unless instructed by Rocket Software Technical Support.
Configuring application server logging
For application server logging, configure where to display log information. To log to a file, you must configure the size
and number of log files to retain. To log requests, resources, and specific information from Mobius, configure the
performance and diagnostics log levels.
Do not edit any other fields unless instructed by Rocket Software Technical Support.
1. In Mobius Administrator, select Diagnostics from the left menu.
2. In the Web Application Logging section, select File, Console, or both.
3. Specify when to begin a new file if you select File, and then specify the file size and number of log files to
retain.
The same name is always used for the first log file. When a new log file is created, the date and time are
appended to the file name.
4. For Mobius Application Logging, specify the log level for requests, resources, and logging.
The default values are sufficient unless instructed by Rocket Software Technical Support.
For both Performance and Diagnostics Logging, you can set a default log level for all modules, and set a
different level for an individual module.
5. Click Apply.
Restarting the application server is not required.

## Page 379


Command line commands
Mobius provides several command line commands that you can use to interact with Mobius View or Mobius Server
directly without using the Mobius interface.
Unattended mode commands
Unattended mode takes in commands which are passed onto the Mobius application to process without using
the application's user interface when working in a product environment.
MobiusRemoteCli commands
Use MobiusRemoteCLI, a command line utility, to perform operations against Mobius Server. Instead of
connecting directly to Mobius Server, the commands you run are routed through Mobius View, which uses
public REST APIs to communicate with Mobius Server.
Mobius index utility
The Mobius index utility is a collection of stored procedures that provide maintenance for index groups.
Unattended mode commands
Unattended mode takes in commands which are passed onto the Mobius application to process without using the
application's user interface when working in a product environment.
Run a command line command at any time, or automate the command by calling it from a script on a scheduling
system. All command line commands apply to both Windows and Linux systems.
A temporary directory is required on the host system when using unattended mode commands. Create a temporary
directory before using Mobius, as if it does not exist, Mobius Server creates one.
Windows
The system function checks for the existence of environment variables in the following order and uses the first
path found:
1. The path specified by the TMP environment variable.
2. The path specified by the TEMP environment variable.
3. The path specified by the USERPROFILE environment variable.
4. The Windows directory.
Linux
• To override the system default, you can set a TMPDIR environment variable before starting the server.
• The system default is usually /var/tmp. The temporary directory is logged in Mobius Server's message log
at server startup.
For unattended mode, the command prompt displays the usage using the following conventions:
• Commands are in lowercase. On Linux systems, type commands in lowercase.
• Variables are indicated in italics.
• [ ] are optional parameters.
• For multiple parameters, a space is used as the separator for a parameter list.
dbimport command - Importing archives
The dbimport command enables a content source to import ingestion information (DAFs) into its database. You
can use this command to migrate content archives to a different storage platform by importing information
into the content source database.
vdrdbxml command - Importing and exporting XML data
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 379


## Page 380


The vdrdbxml command imports data from an XML file to a local database and exports data from a database
into an XML file.
amigrate command - Migrating document archive files
The amigrate command migrates document archive files (DAF). Mobius Server database stores migration rules
and the current status of each migrated document archive file.
acreate command - Creating archives
The acreate command creates archives. The archive content details are passed through parameters to
perform policy, list file and meta-JSON archiving.
adelete command - Deleting archives
The adelete command deletes specified ingestions, expired ingestions, and indexes associated with a deleted
ingestion.
lcupdate command - Ad hoc migration
Use the lcupdate command to change the retention period of a migrated ingestion, and specify parameters to
immediately migrate, copy, export, or delete document archive files.
replarch command - Archive replication
The replarch command replicates archived ingestions and indexes from Mobius to Mobius z/OS, or from a
Mobius instance to another instance.
replaudit command - Audit archive replication
The replaudit command deletes document archive files on Mobius that were successfully replicated to
Mobius z/OS, and updates the Report Version table for each successfully replicated ingestion on Mobius z/OS.
vdrencrypt command - Archive file (DAF) encryption
The vdrencrypt command maintains encrypted document archive files (DAFs) from encrypting DAFs that have
not been encrypted, re-encrypting existing encrypted DAFs with a new key, to repairing encryption in existing
encrypted DAFs, if required.
vdrdistclient command - Distribution with the Distribution client
The VdrDistClient command processes deferred distributions and re-runs prior distributions.
Command line commands to manage Full Text and Simple Search
Specific command line commands are provided to submit or cancel index jobs, delete report versions, or query
index jobs.
dbimport command - Importing archives
The dbimport command enables a content source to import ingestion information (DAFs) into its database. You can
use this command to migrate content archives to a different storage platform by importing information into the
content source database.
An archive is a data storage file containing information that can be easily distributed across multiple storage
platforms. Use the dbimport command to migrate the content archives, and use vdrdbxml command to move
associated indexes.
Syntax
dbimport -s srv -u user -p pswd -d ddir -e elvl [-j] [-i]
To view the full list of parameters, type dbimport -h.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 380


## Page 381


Parameters
Parameter Description
-S server
connection
(required) Name of the connection to the document server that imports the data. This document
server identifies the database that receives the imported data.
If the name contains embedded spaces, enclose the name in quotation marks.
-U
recipient
ID
(required) Recipient with permission to define content.
-P
password (required if user has password) Password for the recipient.
-D
directory
(required) Directory where the document server finds the ingestions to be imported. All valid
document archive files that reside in this directory are imported.
To specify the path of the directory, use the naming convention of the document server's operating
system. (For example, the DAFs may reside on a Windows machine, but if the document server is
running on Linux, use Linux naming conventions.) Optionally, use the UNC path for either a Windows
or Linux operating system.
If the files are in a subdirectory tree, use the -J option to import all the ingestions in the tree.
Note:
• If a directory name contains spaces, enclose the entire path in quotation marks.
• When specifying the directory, besides the server's operating system, other
considerations include its permission settings and existing mapped drives. For
example, specifying a path beginning with C: tells the document server to look on
its own C: drive rather than the C: drive on the machine where the dbimport client
is running. Using a UNC path prevents this confusion.
◦ Re-importing ingestions to a new directory location overrides the pre-
existing location.
-J (optional) Import ingestions from the source directory and any subdirectories listed in the -D option.
Examples
dbimport –S Inventory –U Jsmith –P abba -D “C:/Program Files/VDRNET/DAFS” -J
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 381


## Page 382


This example:
• Uses the Inventory document server connection to import the ingestions.
• Authenticates the user.
• Imports all ingestion information from the C:/Program Files/VDRNET/DAFS directory.
• Imports all ingestions from the sub-directories.
vdrdbxml command - Importing and exporting XML data
The vdrdbxml command imports data from an XML file to a local database and exports data from a database into an
XML file.
To use vdrdbxml, the Recipient ID must have one of these permissions: Define recipients, Define content, Define
policies, or Allow distribution migration.
Syntax
vdrdbxml -dsl dslink -u user -f reqfile [options]
To view the full list of parameters, type vdrdbxml -h.
The vdrdbxml command and its parameters are almost similar to the MobiusRemoteCLI vdrdbxml command.
Note: On Windows systems, use the (/) delimiter with parameters. On Linux systems, use the (-)
delimiter with parameters.
Parameters
Parameter Description
-s document server link, -dsl document server link
(required) Document server name. Use quotation marks
when the document server name contains embedded
spaces. For example, "Account group".
A document server with enabled auditing will record
audit operations based on defined message filters for
Kafka. Auditing is not applied if this parameter is
omitted. If a user has mandatory auditing enabled and
this parameter is omitted, the application displays an
error and exits.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 382


## Page 383


Parameter Description
-u recipient ID (required) User ID
-p password (required) Document server password for the user ID.
-f reqfile (required) Request file name that contains content
about the imported or exported information.
-x (optional) Delete request file after the command
completes.
-log file and type
(optional) Specify a different log file name or existing
directory and log type. Without this parameter, the log
file contains summary and error messages and is
created in the temporary directory specified by the
system environment variable. Values:
• All (A) - All messages
• Informational (I) - Informational, summary, and
error messages
• Summary (S) - Summary and error messages
• None (N) - No messages
-nex
(optional) When specified, non-exclusive use of the
database can result in slower processing (default). When
not specified, exclusive use of the database and
processing is faster.
-args argfile
(optional) File name containing a list of command line
arguments. If there are duplicated parameters, the
parameter that is declared last is processed.
[-out or -o] outputFile
(optional) Path and file name that receives exported
data. Without this parameter, the output is sent to a file
named DBXMLResults.XML in a temporary directory
specified by the system environment variable.
Examples
Connect to a specified content server and update the data source
vdrdbxml -dsl documentserverlink1 –f C:/XML/update1.xml –x
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 383


## Page 384


This command:
• Authenticates the user against the database associated with documentserverlink1.
• Updates the data source with information from the update1.xml file.
• Deletes the XML file when processing is complete.
Isolating index values to be exported
For both standalone index values and Index Group Member values, Mobius processes unique document
collected and exports the retention information that matches the unique document.
While exporting retention information for index values that have held sections, varied results are received
depending on whether the - sri (Skip exporting the Retention Information) parameter is specified.
Note: If an XML file is exported from a source database that has a index defined with a maximum
index length of 255 characters and then imported to a target database that has the same index
defined with a maximum index length of 30 characters, the index value is truncated to the first 30
characters.
The following table lists exported XML records when the - sri parameter is omitted.
XML export request Results when - sri- sri is omitted
Explicit Index Value Explicit Index Value AND Retention Entry for Held Document
ALL Index Values All Index Values AND All Retention Entries for Held Documents
Explicit Index Group Entry (NOT Retention
Entry)
Explicit Index Group Entry AND Retention Entry for Held
Document
Explicit Retention Entry Explicit Retention Entry
ALL Retention Entries (RET_CHG Topic Group) All Retention Entries
ALL Index Group Entries All Index Group Entries (Including Retention Entries)
The following table lists exported XML records when the - sri parameter is specified.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 384


## Page 385


XML export request Results when - sri- sri is specified
Explicit Index Value Explicit Index Value
ALL Index Values All Index Values
Explicit Index Group Entry (NOT
Retention Entry) Explicit Index Group Entry
Explicit Retention Entry Error "N542E- Invalid request when exporting retention
information" occurs.
ALL Retention Entries (RET_CHG Topic
Group)
Error "N542E- Invalid request when exporting retention
information" occurs.
ALL Index Group Entries All Index Group Entries
Related information
MobiusRemoteCLI vdrdbxml
Database update with XML file data
Mobius allows administrators to update local and remote databases by importing and exporting data.
To update a local database, use the vdrdbxml command.
To import XML data into a remote Mobius Server database, you create and run a batch file or a custom application.
XML file format
To create an XML file to add, modify, delete, or export data in the database, use the format described in this
section.
Example XML files
The XML file starts and ends with the highest level tag <VDRNET_DB_MASS_UPDATE VDRNET_VERSION="n.n">.
Within this tag are references to the database table tags. Each database table tag has an action parameter,
tags, and values for each field.
Importing XML data to a remote Mobius Server database using batch files
Batch files are an easy way to import XML data into remote Mobius Server databases.
Importing XML data to a remote Mobius Server database using a custom application
Create a custom application to import XML data using the Remote XML Importer command into a remote
Mobius Server database.
Exporting XML data from a remote Mobius Server database
To export XML data from a remote Mobius Server database, use the RemoteXMLImporter command.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 385


## Page 386


XML file format
To create an XML file to add, modify, delete, or export data in the database, use the format described in this section.
Always use the same XML file to import or export data. The action attribute determines what happens to data.
Note: TOPIC and TOPIC_GROUP are earlier terminology in the XML tags that have been preserved for
backward compatibility with earlier Mobius versions.
When Mobius exports data into an XML file, the file is created in the following format:
• The root tag of the XML file must be:
<VDRNET_DB_MASS_UPDATE VDRNET_VERSION="n.n"
where n.n is the version of Mobius, such as 5.1.
• The high-level node specifies the Mobius table to act on and the type of action. For example, to add
information to the Recipient table, specify <RECIPIENT action="add">.
This table describes the action attributes:
Action
attributes Description
add Adds a Mobius entity if it does not exist. If the entity already exists, it is not added and an
error is reported.
modify Modifies a Mobius entity if it already exists. If the entity does not exist, an error is reported.
add/modify Adds a Mobius entity if it does not already exist. If the entity already exists, it is modified.
delete Deletes an existing Mobius entity.
get Exports all Mobius entities from the specified table.
• The lower level nodes specify the field in the table to update and the value for that field. For example, to
include the Recipient ID of JSMITH with the added recipient then specify
<RECIPIENT_ID>JSMITH</RECIPIENT_ID>.
The table lists the XML tags that describe the properties of recipients:
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 386

## Page 399


XML nodes that correspond
to tables XML nodes that correspond to fields
Values:
◦ Yes or Y
◦ No or N
Note: An active key is added or modified only when there is no active
key already existing in the database. Otherwise, the new encryption
key is added, but is inactive.
• CREATEDFormat: YYYYMMDDHHMMSS
• LAST_USEDFormat: YYYYMMDDHHMMSS
Note: To delete an encryption key, delete all report versions using
that encryption key before it can be deleted.
• KEY_ENCRYPTION_KEY
Values:
◦ Default - if ‘Use internal encryption key for encryption keys stored in
the database and used for encrypting archives’ option is selected in
the Mobius Administrator to which the encryption key is imported.
◦ It should have the path and filename that contains AES 256-bit key
encoded as HEX string. It must match the exact same path that is
currently set. OpenSSL can be used (or any other key generation tool)
to generate a HEX encoded AES 256-bit key.
• KEY_ENCRYPTION_KEY_HASH
Values:
◦ Should be set to Sha-1 hash of the key pointed to by
KEY_ENCRYPTION_KEY. The Sha-1 hash can be generated through
tools online, however, when generating the Sha-1 hash, use the HEX
encoded encryption key as it appears in KEY_ENCRYPTION_KEY file
path as Shal-1 hash computation is case sensitive.
Note: KEY_ENCRYPTION_KEY_HASH has to be specified only if Use
external encryption key for encrypting keys stored in the database
and used for encrypting archives option is specified in Mobius
Administrator.
Index group tags
An index group is a set of related indexes. Each index group must have at least one MEMBER_TOPIC. For example, to
combine indexes related to a checking account, such as account number, check number, amount, and memo into a
single index group, add the index group, and add member indexes for each related index.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 399


## Page 400


Plan the index groups carefully, because the member indexes within a index group cannot be added or deleted, after
the group is added.
The table lists the XML tags that describe the properties of an index group:
XML tags Description
TOPIC_GROUP (required) Identifies the beginning of a index group entity.
TOPIC_GROUP_ID (required) Unique identifier of the index group. Value must be up to 255
characters.
TOPIC_GROUP_NAME (optional) Descriptive name for the index group. Value must be up to 255
characters.
TOPIC_GROUP_SCOPE
(optional) Indicates the scope of the instances within the index group. Only index
group instances that match the defined index group scope can exist within this
index group. Values are Any (default), Page, Pageset, or Line.
TOPIC_GROUP_IS_PROTECTED
(optional) Indicates if the index group information and the index group members in
the index group can be modified.
• (default) No or N Indicates the group is a regular index group and can be
modified.
• Yes or Y Indicates that no operations are allowed against the index group.
TOPIC_GROUP_CLASS
(optional) Indicates the index group class.
• (default) Regular indicates a regular index group.
• Retention flags a retention related index group.
• CheckInOut indicates a checkin/checkout related index group.
Member index tags
A member index is a index within a index group. For example, the index group "checking account" can consist of
related indexes “account number,” “check number,” “amount,” and “memo.”
The table lists the XML tags that describe the properties of a member index:
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 400


## Page 401


XML Tags Description
MEMBER_TOPIC (required) Identifies the beginning of the member index entity.
TOPIC_ID
(required) Identifier unique to a member index. The following special characters in
this field cannot be used: ' (apostrophe), " (quote), ? (question mark), { (left brace),
or } (right brace). Values are up to 10 characters.
TOPIC_NAME (optional) Descriptive name for the member index that is displayed in viewing
clients, such as Mobius View. Values are up to 60 characters.
DESCRIPTION (optional) Descriptive information about this member index. Values are up to 240
characters.
TOPIC_GLOBAL_ACCESS
(required) Indicates whether all recipients are allowed to access this member
index.
Values are (default) Yes, Y, No, or N.
If enabled, all recipients can access the report from this member index. If this
option is not enabled, assign access to this member index to select recipients in
the Index Access Rights section.
DATA_TYPE
(required) Specifies the type of data required in the field.
Values are Character, Date, or Number.
DATA_FORMAT
(Required when DATA_TYPE is Date or Number) Identifies the display format for
the type of data imported into or extracted from the database.
Character—If the DATA_TYPE is Character, a value is not required. Specified values
are ignored.
Date—Date in one of the date formats recognized by Mobius, for example, MM/
DD/YYYY or YYMMDD.
Number—Numeric data, including monetary amounts and binary numbers.
Number fields can contain commas and decimal points. Currency fields can
contain a currency symbol, commas, and decimals. Negative numbers, or credits,
can be indicated in one of three ways: -, (), or CR.
TOPIC_ITEM_MAX_LENGTH (required) Identifies the string length for the type of data imported into or
extracted from the database.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 401


## Page 402


XML Tags Description
Character—If the DATA_TYPE is Character, a value is 30 or 255.
Date—If the DATA_TYPE is Date, the value is 0.
Number—If the DATA_TYPE is Number, the value is 0.
CURRENT_TOPIC_VERSION (optional) Timestamp that associates the current index version with the index.
Value is YYYYMMDDHHMMSS.
TOPIC_VERSION_GRANULARITY
(optional) Defines the scope of index versions displayed. To decrease the amount
of time it takes to display indexes in a viewing client, such as Mobius View, limit
the scope of the index versions.
Values are: (default) All, A, Monthly, M, Quarterly, Q, Yearly, or Y
For example, the Monthly option displays index versions divided into 12 folders,
one for each month. When a user selects a folder for a specific month, the viewing
client retrieves only the index versions that were created during that month. This
results in faster access when compared with retrieving index versions for a longer
period of time.
CAN_SEARCH
(optional) Indicates whether this member index can be a search constraint in
viewing clients such as Mobius view, and API applications. Values are (default) Y,
Yes, N, or No.
TOPIC_IS_PROTECTED
(optional) Indicates the index member can be modified. Values:
• (default) No or N Indicates the index member can be modified.
• Yes or Y Indicates that no operations are allowed against the index group.
TOPIC_CATEGORY
(optional) Indicates the index category. Values are (default) Document, Process, or
Retention.
Note: This value should be Document, unless advised to change it
by
Index group instance tags
An index group instance is a specific group of index entries for a index group. For example, if the index group
"checking account" contains member indexes “account” and “check number,” a index group instance is a specific
occurrence of an account and check number value pair. The index group instance tags, except for the
SET_TOPIC_VALUE_PAIR tag, constrain the instances that Mobius adds, modifies, deletes, or exports. The
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 402


## Page 403


SET_TOPIC_VALUE_PAIR tag adds or modifies an instance value.
The table lists the XML tags that describe the properties of an index group instance:
XML Tags Description
TOPIC_GROUP_INSTANCE (required) Identifies the beginning of a index group instance entity.
TOPIC_GROUP_ID (required) Identifier unique to an individual index group. Value is up to
255 characters.
SET_TOPIC_VALUE_PAIR
(required with the add and modify actions - not allowed with the get and
delete actions) Applies to add and modify actions and indicates the
beginning of a new TOPIC_ID and TOPIC_VALUE pair. This pair identifies
the new value that Mobius applies to index group instances.
MATCH_TOPIC_VALUE_PAIR
(required with the delete and get actions) Indicates the beginning of an
existing TOPIC_ID and TOPIC_VALUE pair. This pair constrains the set of
instances that Mobius modifies, deletes, or exports.
TOPIC_ID
(required) Specifies the index associated with the index value in a
MATCH_TOPIC_VALUE_PAIR or SET_TOPIC_VALUE_PAIR. Value is up to 10
characters.
TOPIC_VALUE
(required) Specifies the index value associated with the index in a
MATCH_TOPIC_VALUE_PAIR or SET_TOPIC_VALUE_PAIR. Value is up to 30
characters.
REPORT_ID (required with the add action) Specifies the report ID of the index group
instance. Value is up to 10 characters.
REPORT_VERSION_ID
(required with the add action) Specifies the report version ID of the index
group instance.
The timestamp must be in the YYYYMMDDHHMMSS format.
SECTION_CODE
(optional) Identifies the row where the index group instance starts, the
column that it starts in, and the number of characters in the index group
instance. Value is up to 30 characters.
DOCUMENT_STARTING_PAGE_NUMBER (required with the add action) Indicates the first page of the report where
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 403


## Page 404


XML Tags Description
the index group instance begins.
DOCUMENT_PAGE_COUNT (required with the add action) Identifies the total number of pages
included in the report.
PAGE_OF_DOCUMENT
(optional) Identifies the page number of the report where the index group
instance occurs.
A default value of -1 indicates all pages.
LOCATION
(optional) Identifies the page and specific location of the index group
instance when multiple instances occur on the same page.
A default value of -1 indicates ignore value.
Index Access Right tags
An index access right authorizes a recipient or group to access index values and related index entries based on a
specified member index value. A user or group with permission to a index group instance has access to all member
indexes and associated content within the index group instance. For example, a index group called “checking
account” has member indexes called “account number,” “check number,” and “amount.” The instances of the group
refer to check images. If John Smith has index access rights to account 100, John also has access to the check number
and amount values and can also view the associated check image.
The table lists the XML tags that describe the properties of a index access right:
XML Tags Description
TOPIC_ITEM_ACCESS_RIGHTS (required) Identifies the beginning of a index access rights entity.
RECIPIENT_OR_GROUP_ID (required) Specifies the RECIPIENT_ID or GROUP_ID of the recipient or group given
permission to access the specified Index and values. Value is up to 128 characters.
TOPIC_ITEM_EXPRESSION (required) Indicates the beginning of a new TOPIC_ID and VALUES pair.
TOPIC_ID
(required) The member index to which permission is granted.
The Index is a second level tag to TOPIC_ITEM_EXPRESSION. Value is up to 10
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 404


## Page 405


XML Tags Description
characters.
VALUES
(required) The values to which permission is granted.
Use wildcards (* or ?) to specify multiple values.
Values are a second level tag to TOPIC_ITEM_EXPRESSION. Value is up to 30
characters.
COMMENTS (optional) Descriptive information about the index access rights. Value is up to 240
characters.
Example XML files
The XML file starts and ends with the highest level tag <VDRNET_DB_MASS_UPDATE VDRNET_VERSION="n.n">. Within
this tag are references to the database table tags. Each database table tag has an action parameter, tags, and values
for each field.
Recipient and Recipient Group updates
Example
This XML file updates the Rocket database by:
• Adding the recipient John Smith
• Deleting the recipient John Doe
• Adding the group ACC
• Adding John Smith as a member of the ACC group
• Adding report access rights for the ACC group
<?xml version = “1.0” ?>
<!—This is a small sample XML file -->
<VDRNET_DB_MASS_UPDATE VDRNET_VERSION=”5.1”>

<!-- ==================== RECIPIENT ==================== -->
 <RECIPIENT action=”add”>
  <RECIPIENT_ID>JSMITH</RECIPIENT_ID>
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 405


## Page 406


<NAME>John Smith</NAME>
 </RECIPIENT>

 <RECIPIENT action=”del”>
  <RECIPIENT_ID>JDOE</RECIPIENT_ID>
 </RECIPIENT>

<!-- ==================== GROUP ==================== -->
 <GROUP action=”add”>
  <GROUP_ID>ACC</GROUP_ID>
  <GROUP_NAME>Accounting</GROUP_NAME>
 </GROUP>

<!-- ================ GROUP_MEMBERSHIP ================ -->
 <GROUP_MEMBERSHIP action=”add”>
  <RECIPIENT_OR_GROUP_ID>JSMITH</RECIPIENT_OR_GROUP_ID>
  <GROUP_ID>ACC</GROUP_ID>
 </GROUP_MEMBERSHIP>

<!-- ================ REPORT_ACCESS_RIGHTS ================ -->
 <REPORT_ACCESS_RIGHTS action=”add/modify”>
  <RECIPIENT_OR_GROUP_ID>ACC</RECIPIENT_OR_GROUP_ID>
  <REPORT_ID>AC001</REPORT_ID>
  <SECTION_CODE>NY*</SECTION_CODE>
 </REPORT_ACCESS_RIGHTS>

</VDRNET_DB_MASS_UPDATE>
Index Group updates
For index group updates, you can add, delete, and export index groups.
Example 1 - Adding index groups
This XML file adds index group “ACCOUNT” with member indexes “ACCT NUMBER,” “DEPOSIT,” and “DATE” to the
Mobius database.
<?xml version = “1.0” ?>
<!—This is a small sample XML file -->
<VDRNET_DB_MASS_UPDATE VDRNET_VERSION=”5.1”>

<!-- ============== TOPIC GROUP =============== -->
<TOPIC_GROUP action="add">
 <TOPIC_GROUP_ID>ACCOUNT</TOPIC_GROUP_ID>
 <TOPIC_GROUP_NAME>Savings Account</TOPIC_GROUP_NAME>
 <TOPIC_GROUP_SCOPE>Any</TOPIC_GROUP_SCOPE>

<!-- ============== MEMBER TOPICS WITHIN TOPIC GROUP ================ -->
 <MEMBER_TOPIC>
  <TOPIC_ID>ACCT_NUMBER</TOPIC_ID>
  <TOPIC_NAME>Account number</TOPIC_NAME>
  <DESCRIPTION>Character Attribute Topic</DESCRIPTION>
  <TOPIC_GLOBAL_ACCESS>Y</TOPIC_GLOBAL_ACCESS>
  <DATA_TYPE>Character</DATA_TYPE>
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 406


## Page 407


<DATA_FORMAT>Character</DATA_FORMAT>
  <CURRENT_TOPIC_VERSION>20050901020202</CURRENT_TOPIC_VERSION>
  <TOPIC_VERSION_GRANULARITY>All</TOPIC_VERSION_GRANULARITY>
 </MEMBER_TOPIC>
 <MEMBER_TOPIC>
  <TOPIC_ID>DEPOSIT</TOPIC_ID>
  <TOPIC_NAME>Deposit amount</TOPIC_NAME>
  <DESCRIPTION>Number Attribute Topic</DESCRIPTION>
  <TOPIC_GLOBAL_ACCESS>Y</TOPIC_GLOBAL_ACCESS>
  <DATA_TYPE>Number</DATA_TYPE>
  <DATA_FORMAT>CR#.###</DATA_FORMAT>
  <CURRENT_TOPIC_VERSION>20050902020202</CURRENT_TOPIC_VERSION>
  <TOPIC_VERSION_GRANULARITY>All</TOPIC_VERSION_GRANULARITY>
  <CAN_SEARCH>Yes</CAN_SEARCH>
 </MEMBER_TOPIC>
 <MEMBER_TOPIC>
  <TOPIC_ID>DATE</TOPIC_ID>
  <TOPIC_NAME>Transaction date</TOPIC_NAME>
  <DESCRIPTION>Date Attribute Topic</DESCRIPTION>
  <TOPIC_GLOBAL_ACCESS>Y</TOPIC_GLOBAL_ACCESS>
  <DATA_TYPE>Date</DATA_TYPE>
  <DATA_FORMAT>YYYYMMDD</DATA_FORMAT>
  <CURRENT_TOPIC_VERSION>20050902020202</CURRENT_TOPIC_VERSION>
  <TOPIC_VERSION_GRANULARITY>All</TOPIC_VERSION_GRANULARITY>
  <CAN_SEARCH>Yes</CAN_SEARCH>
 </MEMBER_TOPIC>
</TOPIC_GROUP>

</VDRNET_DB_MASS_UPDATE>
Example 2 - Deleting Index Groups
This XML file deletes index group “FUNDS” from the Mobius database.
<?xml version = “1.0” ?>
<!—This is a small sample XML file -->
<VDRNET_DB_MASS_UPDATE VDRNET_VERSION=”5.1”>

<!-- ============== TOPIC GROUP =============== -->
<TOPIC_GROUP action="delete">
 <TOPIC_GROUP_ID>FUNDS</TOPIC_GROUP_ID>
</TOPIC_GROUP>
</VDRNET_DB_MASS_UPDATE>
Example 3 – Exporting Topic Groups
The following XML file exports topic group "BONDS" from the Mobius database.
<?xml version = “1.0” ?>
<!—This is a small sample XML file>
<VDRNET_DB_MASS_UPDATE VDRNET_VERSION=”5.1”>

<!-- ============== TOPIC GROUP =============== -->
<TOPIC_GROUP action="get">
 <TOPIC_GROUP_ID>BONDS</TOPIC_GROUP_ID>
</TOPIC_GROUP>
</VDRNET_DB_MASS_UPDATE>
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 407


## Page 408


Index group instance updates
For index group instance updates, you can add, modify, delete, and export index group instance entities.
Example 1 - Adding Index Group instances
This XML file adds an index group instance for the index group “ACCOUNT” and sets a value for each member
index.
<?xml version = “1.0” ?>
<!—This is a small sample XML file -->
<VDRNET_DB_MASS_UPDATE VDRNET_VERSION=”5.1”>

<!-- ========== TOPIC GROUP INSTANCE ========== -->
<TOPIC_GROUP_INSTANCE action="add">
 <TOPIC_GROUP_ID>ACCOUNT</TOPIC_GROUP_ID>
 <REPORT_ID>ACCT</REPORT_ID>
 <REPORT_VERSION_ID>20051014003720</REPORT_VERSION_ID>
 <SECTION_CODE>Section1</SECTION_CODE>
 <PAGE_OF_DOCUMENT>-1</PAGE_OF_DOCUMENT>
 <LOCATION>-1</LOCATION>
 <DOCUMENT_STARTING_PAGE_RBA>58</DOCUMENT_STARTING_PAGE_RBA>
 <DOCUMENT_STARTING_PAGE_NUMBER>1</DOCUMENT_STARTING_PAGE_NUMBER>
 <DOCUMENT_PAGE_COUNT>5</DOCUMENT_PAGE_COUNT>
 <SET_TOPIC_VALUE_PAIR>
  <TOPIC_ID>ACCT_NUMBER</TOPIC_ID>
  <TOPIC_VALUE>CHAR_VALUE</TOPIC_VALUE>
 </SET_TOPIC_VALUE_PAIR>
 <SET_TOPIC_VALUE_PAIR>
  <TOPIC_ID>DEPOSIT</TOPIC_ID>
  <TOPIC_VALUE type="raw">999.99</TOPIC_VALUE>
 </SET_TOPIC_VALUE_PAIR>
 <SET_TOPIC_VALUE_PAIR>
  <TOPIC_ID>DATE </TOPIC_ID>
  <TOPIC_VALUE type="raw">20051120.0</TOPIC_VALUE>
 </SET_TOPIC_VALUE_PAIR>
</TOPIC_GROUP_INSTANCE>

</VDRNET_DB_MASS_UPDATE>
Example 2 - Modifying Index Group instances
This XML file modifies all index group instances where the index group is “ACCOUNT” the Report ID is “AC001,”
the Report Version ID is “20051101125544,” and the office is “NY;” and sets the office value to “CT” and the zip
code to “06801.”
<?xml version = “1.0” ?>
<!—This is a small sample XML file -->
<VDRNET_DB_MASS_UPDATE VDRNET_VERSION=”5.1”>

<!-- ========== TOPIC GROUP INSTANCE ========== -->
<TOPIC_GROUP_INSTANCE action="modify">
 <TOPIC_GROUP_ID>ACCOUNT</TOPIC_GROUP_ID>
 <REPORT_ID>AC001</REPORT_ID>
 <REPORT_VERSION_ID>20051101125544</REPORT_VERSION_ID>

<MATCH_TOPIC_VALUE_PAIR>
  <TOPIC_ID>Office</TOPIC_ID>

 </MATCH_TOPIC_VALUE_PAIR>

  <TOPIC_ID>Office</TOPIC_ID>
  <TOPIC_VALUE>CT</TOPIC_VALUE>
 </SET_TOPIC_VALUE_PAIR>
 <SET_TOPIC_VALUE_PAIR>
  <TOPIC_ID>ZipCode</TOPIC_ID>
  <TOPIC_VALUE>06801</TOPIC_VALUE>
 </SET_TOPIC_VALUE_PAIR>
</TOPIC_GROUP_INSTANCE>

</VDRNET_DB_MASS_UPDATE>

  <TOPIC_ID>ZipCode</TOPIC_ID>

 </SET_TOPIC_VALUE_PAIR>
</TOPIC_GROUP_INSTANCE>

</VDRNET_DB_MASS_UPDATE>

<VDRNET_DB_MASS_UPDATE VDRNET_VERSION=”5.1”>

<!-- ========== TOPIC GROUP INSTANCE ========== -->
<TOPIC_GROUP_INSTANCE action="delete">
 <TOPIC_GROUP_ID>ACCOUNT</TOPIC_GROUP_ID>

<TOPIC_GROUP_INSTANCE action="get">
 <TOPIC_GROUP_ID>ACCOUNT</TOPIC_GROUP_ID>

  <TOPIC_VALUE>98118</TOPIC_VALUE>
 </MATCH_TOPIC_VALUE_PAIR>
</TOPIC_GROUP_INSTANCE>

</VDRNET_DB_MASS_UPDATE>
Index Access Rights updates
For index access rights updates, you can add, delete, and export index access right entities.
Example 1 - Adding Index Access Rights
This XML file adds index access rights for JSMITH to all accounts beginning with ACCT5 and to account
ACCT999*.
<?xml version = “1.0” ?>
<!—This is a small sample XML file -->
<VDRNET_DB_MASS_UPDATE VDRNET_VERSION=”5.1”>

<!-- ============== TOPIC ITEM ACCESS RIGHTS ======================== -->
<TOPIC_ITEM_ACCESS_RIGHTS action="add">
 <RECIPIENT_OR_GROUP_ID>JSMITH</RECIPIENT_OR_GROUP_ID>
 <TOPIC_ITEM_EXPRESSION>
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 410


## Page 411


<TOPIC_ID>ACCOUNT</TOPIC_ID>
  <VALUES>“ACCT5*”</VALUES>
 </TOPIC_ITEM_EXPRESSION>
 <COMMENTS>Allows access to accounts ACCT5*</COMMENTS>
</TOPIC_ITEM_ACCESS_RIGHTS>

<TOPIC_ITEM_ACCESS_RIGHTS action="add">
 <RECIPIENT_OR_GROUP_ID>JSMITH</RECIPIENT_OR_GROUP_ID>
 <TOPIC_ITEM_EXPRESSION>
  <TOPIC_ID>ACCOUNT</TOPIC_ID>
  <VALUES>“ACCT999/*”</VALUES>
 </TOPIC_ITEM_EXPRESSION>
 <COMMENTS>Allows access to the literal value ACCT999*</COMMENTS>
</TOPIC_ITEM_ACCESS_RIGHTS>

</VDRNET_DB_MASS_UPDATE>
Example 2 - Deleting Index Access Rights
This XML file deletes index access rights for JSMITH to account ACCT999*.
<?xml version = “1.0” ?>
<!—This is a small sample XML file -->
<VDRNET_DB_MASS_UPDATE VDRNET_VERSION=”5.1”>

<!-- ============== TOPIC ITEM ACCESS RIGHTS ======================== -->
<TOPIC_ITEM_ACCESS_RIGHTS action="delete">
 <RECIPIENT_OR_GROUP_ID>JSMITH</RECIPIENT_OR_GROUP_ID>
 <TOPIC_ITEM_EXPRESSION>
  <TOPIC_ID>ACCOUNT</TOPIC_ID>
  <VALUES>“ACCT999/*”</VALUES>
 </TOPIC_ITEM_EXPRESSION>
 <COMMENTS>Removes access to the literal value ACCT999*</COMMENTS>
</TOPIC_ITEM_ACCESS_RIGHTS>

</VDRNET_DB_MASS_UPDATE>
Example 3 - Exporting Index Access Rights
This XML file exports index access rights for JSMITH.
<?xml version = “1.0” ?>
<!—This is a small sample XML file -->
<VDRNET_DB_MASS_UPDATE VDRNET_VERSION=”5.1”>

<!-- ============== TOPIC ITEM ACCESS RIGHTS ======================== -->
<TOPIC_ITEM_ACCESS_RIGHTS action="get">
 <RECIPIENT_OR_GROUP_ID>JSMITH</RECIPIENT_OR_GROUP_ID>
</TOPIC_ITEM_ACCESS_RIGHTS>

</VDRNET_DB_MASS_UPDATE>

For encryption key updates, you can use the default encryption key or a customer provided encryption key.

<ENCRYPTION_KEY action="add/modify">

 <LAST_USED>20180426071009</LAST_USED>

 <KEY_ENCRYPTION_KEY>Default</KEY_ENCRYPTION_KEY>
 <KEY_ENCRYPTION_KEY_HASH></KEY_ENCRYPTION_KEY_HASH>
</ENCRYPTION_KEY>

This XML file uses the customer provided encryption key.

 <KEY_NAME>MySecretKey1</KEY_NAME>
 <KEY_VALUE encrypt="n">C6F001370BF70172575D0D0C500E15F11155417B44F94C45DA1D634DDE31B63B</K
EY_VALUE>
 <KEY_ACTIVE>N</KEY_ACTIVE>
