 <KEY_ENCRYPTION_KEY_HASH>BE6EA4C4E29793490828BA170076C6A7E61B36D8</KEY_ENCRYPTION_KEY_HAS
H>
</ENCRYPTION_KEY>
Importing XML data to a remote Mobius Server database using batch files
Batch files are an easy way to import XML data into remote Mobius Server databases.
1. Start Mobius server on the Mobius host.
Windows
Run vdrrmisrv.bat.
Linux
Run vdrrmisrv.sh.
These files are located in the Mobius installation directory. The default port number for the Mobius server is
1099.
2. To change the port number:
a. Open the vdrrmisrv.bat file in a text editor.
b. Change the ‑rmiRegistryPort‑  parameter.
3. Create a batch file with the following contents.
@echo off
set DOC_SERVER_ID=%1
set XML_FILE_NAME=%2
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 412


## Page 413


set USER_ID=%3
set USER_PASSWORD=%4

if "%USER_PASSWORD%"=="" set USER_PASSWORD=""

rem XMLImporter arguments  [DEFAULTS]:
rem -h RMI Server Host [LOCALHOST]
rem -r RMI Server Port [1099]
rem -s Document ServerID [%DOC_SERVER_ID%]
rem -u UserID    [%USER_ID%]
rem -p Password   [""]
rem -f XML Filename  [%XML_FILE_NAME%]
rem -? Help (this message)

java -classpath %CLASSPATH%;log4j.jar;xmlimporter.jar com..vdrnet.xmlimporter.XMLImporter
-s %DOC_SERVER_ID%-f %XML_FILE_NAME% -u %USER_ID% -p %USER_PASSWORD%
a. Name the batch file as RemoteXMLImporter.bat, then save it to the Mobius Server installation directory.
b. Open a command prompt and type this command to run the batch file:
RemoteXMLImporter <DocServer> <XML_file> <UserID> <Password
<DocServer> Document server connection name.
<XML_File> Path and filename of the XML file. The content of this file specifies what information is
imported.
<UserID> Recipient that is issuing this command. This recipient must have permission to perform
the updates specified in the XML file.
<Password> Password for the recipient (if required).
Importing XML data to a remote Mobius Server database using a custom application
Create a custom application to import XML data using the Remote XML Importer command into a remote Mobius
Server database.
The following code sample is a custom application that is modified to call the Remote XML Importer command
through Mobius Server.
String rmiHost = "LOCALHOST";

int rmiPort = 1099;
String serverId = "<DocumentServerID>";
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 413


## Page 414


String userId = "<UserID>";char[] password = "<Password>";

StringBuffer xmlDataIn = new StringBuffer("");
// Should contain actual XML request

StringBuffer xmlDataOut = new StringBuffer("");
XMLImporterStatus status = new XMLImporterStatus();
// Setup RMI request

XMLImporter xmlImporter = new XMLImporter( serverId, userId, password, rmiHost, rmiPort );

boolean success = false;
try { success = xmlImporter.processXML( xmlDataIn, xmlDataOut, status );        // RMI request}

catch(XMLImporterException e) {}
catch(Exception e) {}
Exporting XML data from a remote Mobius Server database
To export XML data from a remote Mobius Server database, use the RemoteXMLImporter command.
1. Start the RMI server on the Mobius host.
2. Open a command prompt and enter this command:
RemoteXMLImporter <DocServer> ExportAll.xml ADMIN “” <rmiHost> <rmiport> “OutAll.xml” –m b
Parameter Description
<DocServer> Document server connection name
ExportAll.xml Input XML file found in project directory that contains export requests
<rmiHost> RMI host name
<rmiport> RMI port number
OutAll.xml Output XML file
Database update with JSON file data
To update local and remote databases, you import and export JSON file data using a JSON file.
Always use the same JSON file to import data. The action attribute determines what happens to data.
In the JSON tags listed in these tables, the term TOPIC refers to an INDEX and the term TOPIC_GROUP refers to an
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 414


## Page 415


INDEX_GROUP.
Note: TOPIC and TOPIC_GROUP are earlier terminology in the JSON tags that have been preserved for
backward compatibility with earlier Mobius versions.
To create a JSON file to add, modify, or delete data in the database, use the format described in the table below.

Example JSON files
The JSON object contains list of entities like users, groups, reports, topics, and policies. You can use bulk JSON to
import data.
Recipient and Recipient Group updates
Example
This sample JSON file updates the Rocket database by:
• Adding the recipient John Smith
• Deleting the recipient John Doe
• Adding the group ACC
• Adding John Smith as a member of the ACC group
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 420


## Page 421


• Adding reports
• Adding report access rights for the ACC group
{
<!-- ==================== RECIPIENT ==================== -->
"users" : [ {
  "id" : "JSMITH",
  "name" : "John Smith",
      "action": "add"
},
{
      "id" : "JDOE",
  "name" : "John Doe"
      "action": "del"
}],
<!-- ==================== USER_GROUP ==================== -->
"userGroups" : [ {
 "id" : "ACC",
 "name" : "Accounting",
} ]
<!-- ================ GROUP_MEMBERSHIP ================ -->
"groupMembers" : [ {
 "groupId" : "@151633",
 "userIdToAdd" : "FTDEP"
}]
<!-- ================ REPORTS ================ -->
"reports" : [ {
  "id" : "AC001",
  "name" : " AC001",
  "details" : "",
}
<!-- ================ REPORT_ACCESS_RIGHTS ================ -->
"reportAccessRights" : [ {
  "reportId" : "AC001",
  "action" : "add/modify:",
  "userId" : "ACC",
  "sections" : "*"
} ]
}
Index Group updates
Example 1 - Adding index groups
This sample JSON file adds index group “ACCOUNT” with member indexes “ACCT NUMBER” to the Mobius
database.
{
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 421


## Page 422


<!-- ============== TOPIC GROUP =============== -->
"topicGroups":[
        {
          "id": "ACCOUNT",
          "name": "Savings Account",
          "action": "add",
          "scope": "Any",
          "topics": [
                 {
                   "id": "ACCT_NUMBER",
                   "name": "Account number",
                   "details": "Character Attribute Topic",
                   "topicVersionDisplay": "All",
                   "allowAccess": true,
                   "dataType": "Character",
                   "maxLength": "30",
                   "category": "Document metadata"
                  }
              ],
              "tableName": "TGRP$_CM_DOCINFO_$00006"
            } ]
}
Example 2 - Deleting index groups
This sample JSON file deletes index group “FUNDS” from the Mobius database.
{
<!-- ============== TOPIC GROUP =============== -->
"topicGroups":[
        {
          "id": "FUNDS",
          "name": "Savings Account",
          "action": "delete",
          "scope": "Any",
          "tableName": "TGRP$_CM_DOCINFO_$00006"
        } ]
}
Index access right updates
Example 1 - Adding Index Access Rights
This sample JSON file adds index access rights for all the accounts of JSMITH starting from ACCT5 and to
ACCT999*.
{
<!-- ============== TOPIC ITEM ACCESS RIGHTS ======================== -->
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 422


## Page 423


"topicAccessRights" : [ {
  "topicID" : "ACCOUNT",
  "userID" : "JSMITH",
  "action": "add",
  "topicItemValue" : "ACCT5"
},
{
  "topicID" : "ACCOUNT",
  "userID" : "JSMITH",
  "action": "add",
  "topicItemValue" : "ACCT999"
 } ]
}
Example 2 - Deleting Index Access Rights
This sample JSON file deletes index access rights for JSMITH account ACCT999*.”
{
<!-- ============== TOPIC ITEM ACCESS RIGHTS ======================== -->
"topicAccessRights" : [ {
  "topicID" : "ACCOUNT",
  "userID" : "JSMITH",
       "action": "delete",
  "topicItemValue" : "ACCT999"
}]
}
amigrate command - Migrating document archive files
The amigrate command migrates document archive files (DAF). Mobius Server database stores migration rules and
the current status of each migrated document archive file.
The amigrate command reads the migration status of each document archive file and applies the migration rules
from the database to copy, delete, or move the file.
To use amigrate, you must have permission to delete archives. Only the ingestions for which the viewing permission
is available, can be deleted.
Syntax
amigrate -s server -u user -r class ID [-t timestamp | -d days] [-options]
To view the full list of parameters, type amigrate -h.
For remote archiving, the amigrate command and its options are almost similar to the MobiusRemoteCLI amigrate.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 423


## Page 424


Parameters
Parameter Description
-S server (required) Connection to the document server that migrates the ingestions. If the name contains
embedded spaces, surround it with quotation marks, for example, “My Server”.
-U user (required) User with permission to delete archives. If a password is defined for this user, the ‑P
parameter is also required.
-P
password (required if user has a passsword) Password for the user.
-R
ReportID
(required) Content Class ID of the content class with ingestions that is to be migrated.
A trailing asterisk (*) is permitted as a wildcard in Content Class IDs; but ensure to add the -Y w option
to indicate that wildcards are used, because asterisks are valid characters in a Content Class ID.
Note: If amigrate is running on Linux, a Content Class ID ending in asterisk "*" should be
either in quotes or have the "*" escaped in order to prevent the shell from attempting to
perform wildcard expansion.
-T
timestamp
Timestamp of the most recent ingestion to be migrated. amigrate applies migration rules to all
ingestions older than the specified ingestion. The timestamp must be in the YYYYMMDDHHMMSS
format. This timestamp does not need to match an Ingestion ID in the Report Version table unless the
‑Y n option is also specified.
-D days
(required if -T is not specified) Number of days of the most recent ingestion to be migrated. amigrate
applies migration rules to all ingestions older than the specified number of days.
A day is defined as the next midnight. That is, a value of 1 becomes effective after midnight the day
the archive is created.
Example: Specifying -D 1 on July 6 would migrate an archive created any hour on July 5 or before.
-Y
processing
options
(optional) Processing options that identify items to delete with the ingestions.
n Apply next migration rule to a specific ingestion without regard to NEXT_ACTION_DATE. Requires
the ‑T parameter to match a specific ingestion.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 424


## Page 425


Parameter Description
d Delete empty directories after document archive files are deleted.
p Delete an ingestion even if there is a distribution pending for that ingestion.
h Place an ingestion on hold.
r Release an ingestion from hold.
w Support wildcard asterisk (*)
Default: No options.
-L
timestamp
(optional) Timestamp of the last (earliest) version to be pre-processed.
Note: This option shortens the processing time of repeated runs. Use this option when
everything older than a certain date has been pre-processed. For example, limit the pre-
processing phase to only look at ingestions since January 1, 2012.
The timestamp must be in the YYYYMMDDHHMMSS format. This timestamp does not need to match
an Ingestion ID in the Report Version table.
Requires the -T parameter to specify a range of ingestions to be processed.
If the -L parameter is selected, do not use the Z parameter.
-Z days
(optional) Number of days of the last (earliest) version to be migrated.
Note: This option shortens the processing time of repeated runs. Use this option when
everything older than a certain number of days has been pre-processed. For example,
limit the preprocessing phase to only look at ingestions from the last 30 days.
A day is defined as the next midnight. That is, a value of 1 becomes effective after midnight the day
the archive is created.
Example: Specifying -Z 1 on July 6 would migrate an archive created any hour on July 5.
If the -Z parameter is selected, do not use the L parameter.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 425


## Page 426


Parameter Description
-O (optional) Does not prompt for confirmation when deleting an ingestion archive.
Default: Prompt to confirm ingestion archive deletions.
Examples
Example 1 - Apply migration rules
amigrate -S Accounting -U Jsmith -P abba -R AC001 -T 20031111171212 -Y d
This example:
• Uses the Accounting document server connection to process the migration request.
• Authenticates the user.
• Applies migration rules to all ingestions of AC001 that were created on or before November 11, 2003,
5:12:12 PM.
• Deletes document archive files associated with the specified ingestions when processing delete rules, and
deletes empty document archive file directories.
Example 2 - Force migration rules
amigrate -S Accounting -U Jsmith -P abba -R AC001 -T 20031111171212 -Y dn
This example:
• Uses the Accounting document server connection to process the migration request.
• Authenticates the user.
• Forces next migration rule step for a single ingestion of AC001 that was created on November 11, 2003,
5:12:12 PM.
• Deletes document archive files associated with the specified ingestions when processing delete rules, and
deletes empty document archive file directories.
Related information
MobiusRemoteCLI amigrate
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 426


## Page 427


acreate command - Creating archives
The acreate command creates archives. The archive content details are passed through parameters to perform
policy, list file and meta-JSON archiving.
Syntax
acreate -s srv -f infile -u user -p pswd [-x file] [-r class ID] [-n] [-c name] [-a xfile] [-d
t] [-m x] [-i] [-o opts] [-v n] jm[-w w]
To view the full list of parameters, type acreate -h.
The acreate command and its parameters are almost similar to the MobiusRemoteCLI acreate command.
Parameters
Parameter Description
-s
document
server
connection
(required) Document server connection that identifies the document server that will create the
archive. Use quotation marks when the document server connection contains embedded spaces. For
example, “My Server”.
-f report
input file
(required) List the full path of each (report, meta, or list) file that will be archived. Use spaces to
separate each file name.
By default, the contents of each file is passed to the server.
• Without--send-content-url, you can specify a local drive letter (for example, C:\reports\
AC001.rpt), a mapped drive letter (for example, M:\reports\AC001.rpt), or a UNC path (for
example, \\server\sharename\reports\AC001.rpt).
• On a Windows server, the file path depends whether --send-content-url is specified. With --
send-content-url, specify the corresponding UNC path (required) rather than the mapped
drive letter only when the report input file is on a mapped drive. A mapped drive letter is not
visible by the Windows service.
Each filename can contain standard wildcard characters, * and ?. For example, -F c:\rpts\aa.rpt
c:\rpts\ab.rpt c:\rpts\ba*00??.rpt .
Note: If a filename is not found, or if a wildcard specification does not result in at least
one filename, the acreate session terminates. In this case, the remaining files on the
command line are not processed. To avoid this, specify the -i (ignore errors) option.
-u recipient
ID (required) User with permission to create archives.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 427


## Page 428


Parameter Description
-p
password (required) Document server password for the user ID.
-x
command
file
(optional) File name containing a list of command line options for unattended mode.
-r class ID
(Optional for policy-based processing. Required for section code location processing.) A Content Class
ID that is defined in the Report table.
• For section code location processing, specify the Content Class ID associated with the archive.
• For policy-based processing, the specified Content Class ID is used instead of the Content Class
ID that the policy finds on the page. This is the equivalent to the Force Report ID option on the
Archive Creation interface. If this option is used with policy-based processing, specify only one
candidate policy.
-n
(optional) If specified, policy selection rules are used to determine which policies are candidates. If not
specified, policy selection rules are not used.
Note: Use the -n option to specify that candidate policies should be selected by
applying policy selection rules rather than specifying a list of policies.
-c
candidate
name
(optional) List candidate policies, and use spaces to separate each candidate policy name.
An error will occur if there is more than one applicable policy for a Content Class ID and no candidate
policy is specified.
Note: Use the -n option to specify that candidate policies should be selected by
applying policy selection rules rather than specifying a list of policies. If the -n and the
-c options are used, only the candidate policies in the -c option are used; policy
selection rules are ignored.
-a XML
filename
(optional) Full path and name of an XML file that is used to pass metadata to a policy.
A policy can use metadata, and an XML file is the source of external metadata. If the archiving process
uses a policy that extracts metadata from an XML file, the specified XML file is used instead of the one
defined in the policy.
On Windows, enter a UNC file name because Mobius resolves the drive designation based on the
location of the server.
-d
distribution (optional) Distribution type:
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 428


## Page 429


Parameter Description
type
• D - Deferred distribution.
• I - Immediate distribution.
• X - Distribution is suppressed.
• Default - No distribution.
-b
(optional) Determines archive distribution in the following situations:
• With this option, if immediate distribution is used and an error occurs, Mobius will not
distribute the archives that failed in that acreate session.
• If this option is omitted, and a processing error occurs for one file, Mobius continues to process
the immediate distribution for all successful archives in that acreate session.
-m method
(optional) Processing method:
• p - (default) Policy-based processing.
• s - Section code location processing.
• i - List file processing for images, associated data, and transparent data.
• m - Meta-based processing (supported in MobiusRemoteCli acreate only).
-i (optional) When specified, non-critical errors that occur during policy-based or list file processing are
ignored, and continues processing the next content input file. When not specified, the default is used.
-o process
options
(optional) Content input file processing options:
• SINGLEID
Use the same Content Class ID for every page in the content input file during archive creation. A
Content Class ID that is determined during policy processing will not change on subsequent
pages.
• (default) MULTIID
If a policy extracts a different Content Class ID on a later page, the current document archive
file (DAF) will close. Archiving then starts in a new document archive file (DAF) that uses the new
Content Class ID.
• DELETE
Delete content input files after creating the archives. If the content input file is a list of images,
associated data, or transparent data, archive creation also deletes all files specified in the list
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 429


## Page 430


Parameter Description
file.
• (default) NODELETE
Retain content input files after creating the archives.
• DELETE or (default) NODELETE
Omit this option and archive creation defaults to the options specified by the processing
options.
Note: The DELETE or NODELETE option is deprecated. By default, this option is
ignored on the acreate and MobiusRemoteCli acreate command line unless the
Control content input file deletion from the client instead of the server
option is selected. For more information, see Managing the content input file after
archiving.
-v
(optional) Verbosity level of messages on the console.
• 0 - Error and warning messages.
• 1 - (default) Error and warning messages and phase changes.
• 2 - All processing information.
-e exit
filename
(optional) Full path of a file containing the EXTProcessInputFile function with options for processing
the input file.
-t
(optional) Use with list file processing. Scans the list file for syntax errors before creating an archive.
Detected errors are written to the archive creation processing log file.
When an error is detected,acreate does not create the archive unless -i is specified. If multiple list
files are specified, acreate validates all files before starting the archive creation process.
-w
(optional) The minimum severity level that Mobius Server records into the error log during archive
creation. Values can be upper or lower case characters that correspond with the severity levels
written to the logs.
• 0 - (A)ll severity levels
• 4 - (W)arnings and above
• 8 - E(x)ceptions and above
• 12 - (E)rrors and above (default)
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 430


## Page 431


Parameter Description
• 16 - (C)ritical
Example 1
-f option with embedded spaces in filenames
If the filename or wildcard specification contains embedded spaces, use quotation marks. For example:
"c:/VDRNET/AC/Report Input Files/AC*.txt"
-f option with quoted file names on Windows
On Windows, if a filename or wildcard specification is enclosed in double quotes ("), any spaces in the text are
preserved and acreate processes the text as same as other filename or wildcard specifications.
For example:
 -f c:/rpts/aa.rpt c:/rpts/ab.rpt "c:/rpts/account report.rpt" "c:/rpts/qa res* 00??.rpt"
Archives the following files:
• c:/rpts/aa.rpt
• c:/rpts/ab.rpt
• c:/rpts/account report.rpt
• c:/rpts/qa results 0001.rpt
• c:/rpts/qa response 0091.rpt
If the filename or wildcard specification is enclosed in single quotes ('), the exact text between the enclosing
single quotes, is sent to the Mobius server. Single quoted wildcard specifications are expanded on the server.
Using single quotes allows processing files that can be found on the server but not on the local machine where
acreate is running.
If the filename or wildcard specification contains embedded spaces and files can be found on the server but not
on the local machine where acreate is running, surround the filename with quotation marks followed by single
quotes.
For example:
-f '//myhost/rpts/aa.rpt' '//myhost/rpts/Q0??.rp*' '"//myhost/rpts/acc res* 00??.rpt"'
or
-F '//myhost/rpts/aa.rpt' '//myhost/rpts/Q0??.rp*' '//myhost/rpts/qa res* 00??.rpt'
The document server looks for these files:
• //myhost/rpts/aa.rpt
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 431


## Page 432


• //myhost/rpts/Q001.rpt
• //myhost/rpts/Q099.rpa ...
• //myhost/rpts/acc result 0022.rpt
• //myhost/rpts/acc response 0023.rpt ...
-f option with quoted file names on Linux
On Linux, wildcard specifications are expanded by the Linux shell. For example:
 -f /user/home/vdrnet/qa*00??.rpt
On Linux, this is expanded to the file names that match the wildcard specification:
 -f /user/home/vdrnet/qazz0001.rpt /user/home/vdrnet/qazz0021.rpt …
Note: If a filename or wildcard specification is enclosed in double (") or single (') quotes, any
spaces in the text are preserved.
Wildcard specifications are not expanded by the Linux shell and acreate receives the exact text including any
spaces. acreate processes the text the same as other filenames.
For example:
-f "/user/home/vdrnet/rpts/accounting results.rpt" "/user/home/vdrnet/acc test*00??.rpt"
Archives the following files:
• /user/home/vdrnet/rpts/accounting results.rpt
• /user/home/vdrnet/acc testzz0001.rpt
• /user/home/vdrnet/acc testzz0002.rpt ...
Note: If the filename or wildcard specification is enclosed in escaped double (/") or single (/')
quotes, the exact text between the enclosing quotes is sent to the Mobius server.
The Linux shell does not expand wildcard specifications, wildcard specifications are expanded on the server.
Using escaped quotes allows processing files that can be found on the server, but not on the local machine
where acreate is running.
For example:
-f <file>
The //myhost server looks for one of the following files:
Report file Meta file List file
• //myhost/rpts/aa.rpt
• //myhost/rpts/Q001.rpt
file:/C:/work/mvs-13208/
2024-01-22/out4/meta.1/
INV1.20231101.111024.1.txt
• //myhost/lst/aa.lst
• //myhost/lst/Q001.lst
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 432


## Page 433


Report file Meta file List file
• //myhost/rpts/Q099.rpa
...
• //myhost/rpts/acc result
0022.rpt
• //myhost/rpts/acc
response 0023.rpt ...
Here, it looks for the .txt file to
archive.
Example 2
acreate -S Inventory -U Jsmith -P abba -F ship.rpt receive*.rpt -M S -R Rpt1 -D X
This example creates an archive using the document server called 'Inventory', authenticates the user, then creates
the archive from the ship.rpt content input file and all content input files with a filename beginning with “receive.”
Determine sections from the section code locations specified in the Rpt1 report record, and suppress distribution.
Alternatively, you can use a file to run the command line options defined in the file. For example, create a file called
MYFILE containing the same parameters. Running the command with the -X option creates an archive using the
parameters in the file.
acreate -X MYFILE
Example 3
acreate -S Inventory -U Jsmith -P abba -F C:/ReportInputFiles/listfile.rpt C:/ReportInputFiles/l
istfile2.rpt -M P -C PolicyA PolicyB PolicyZ -O SINGLEID DELETE –D I
This example creates an archive using the document server called 'Inventory', authenticates the user, then creates
the archive from the listfile.rpt and listfile2.rpt input files, located in the C:/ReportInputFiles directory.
Determine content sections and indexes from the candidate policies PolicyA, PolicyB, and PolicyZ, then use the same
Content Class ID for every page in the content input file. Delete the content input file after the creating the archive,
then distribute the content immediately.
Example 4
acreate -S Inventory –U Jsmith -P abba -F ship.rpt -M P -C PolicyA -R Rpt1
This example creates an archive using the document server called 'Inventory', authenticates the user, then creates
the archive from the Ship.rpt content input file using PolicyA to determine the content section and indexing. A
Report ID of Rpt1 is forced.
Example 5
acreate -S Inventory -U Jsmith -P abba -F ship.rpt -N –D I
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 433


## Page 434


This example creates an archive using the document server called 'Inventory', authenticates the user, then creates
the archive from the Ship.rpt content input file using policy selection rules to determine candidate policies, then
immediately distributes the contents immediately after creating the archive.
Example 6
acreate -S Inventory -U Jsmith -P abba -F ship.rpt -M I -V 2 -T
This example creates an archive using the document server called 'Inventory', authenticates the user, then creates
the archive from the Ship.rpt content input file using list file processing. The console displays the processing
information and before archiving, the list file is scanned for errors.
Related information
MobiusRemoteCLI acreate
adelete command - Deleting archives
The adelete command deletes specified ingestions, expired ingestions, and indexes associated with a deleted
ingestion.
To decrease the deleting time, Mobius deletes an archive's indices from the Mobius Server database before deleting
the document archive file. If the document archive file has moved or cannot be accessed due to network failure, or
the permissions on the file have changed, it is possible for the deletion of the document archive file to fail. If this
occurs, use a system-specific file management tool to manually delete the document archive file. Run adelete again
to ensure that the archive version record is removed from the Mobius Server database after manually deleting the
document archive file.
To use adelete, you must have permission to delete archives.
Syntax
The syntax for this command is:
adelete -s srv -u user -p pswd -r class ID [[-t ver] | [-d days]] [-l] [-c] [-n] [-o] [-v vl] [-
e elvl] [-g] [-y Options]
To view the full list of options, type adelete -h.
The adelete command and its options are almost similar to the MobiusRemoteCLI adelete.
Parameters
Parameter Description
-S server
connection
(required) Connection to the document server that deletes the ingestions. If the name contains
embedded spaces, surround it with quotation marks, e.g., “My Server”.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 434


## Page 435


Parameter Description
-U
recipient
ID
(required) Recipient with permission to delete archives. If a password is defined for this recipient, the
-p parameter is also required.
-P
password (required only if user has password) Password for the Recipient ID.
-R report
ID
(required) Content Class ID of the content with ingestions that needs to be deleted.
A trailing asterisk (*) is permitted as a wildcard in Content Class IDs; also add the -Y w option to
indicate that wildcards are used, because asterisks are valid characters in a Content Class ID.
Note: If running adelete on Linux, a Content Class ID ending in an asterisk should be
either in quotes or have the asterisk escaped in order to prevent the shell from
attempting to perform wildcard expansion.
-T
timestamp
(required only if-D is not specified) Timestamp of the most recent ingestion to be deleted. By default,
adelete deletes all ingestions equal to or older than the specified ingestion. (Also see the -L
parameter.) The timestamp must be in the format YYYYMMDDHHMMSS. This timestamp does not
need to match a Version ID in the Report Version table.
Note: Do not use this option if the -N or -C option is specified.
-TRO
timestamp
Timestamp of the last report version to be deleted. The timestamp must be in the format
YYYYMMDDHHMMSS.
-TRN
timestamp
Timestamp of the first report version to be deleted. The timestamp must be in the format
YYYYMMDDHHMMSS.
-C
(required only if-N is not specified) Deletes ingestions exceeding the retention period specified on the
selected content class under Content Class Access Rights > Retention page in Mobius
Administrator.
To delete all versions that exceed the number of generations or the retention period specified in
Mobius Administrator, enter both the -C and -N parameters in the command.
-D days
(required only if-T is not specified) Deletes ingestions older than the number of days specified from
the most recent ingestion. adeletedeletes all ingestions older than the specified number of days.
Note:
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 435


## Page 436


Parameter Description
• A day is defined as starting at the next midnight. A value of 1 becomes effective
after midnight the day the archive is created.
• Do not use this option if the -N or -C option is specified.
For example:
• Specifying -D 1 on July 6 deletes an archive created any hour on July 5 or before.
• Specifying -D 365 deletes archives at least 366 days old.
-N
(required only if-C is not specified) Deletes ingestions exceeding the number of generations specified
on the selected content class under Content Class Access Rights > Retention page in Mobius
Administrator.
To delete all ingestions that exceed the number of generations or the retention period specified in
Mobius Administrator, enter both the -C and -N parameters in the command.
-Y
processing
options
(required only if-G is not specified, unless specifying -Yc, -Yn, or -Ycn) Processing options that identify
items to delete with the ingestions.
Note: Do not use this option if the -G option is specified.
i Delete indexes associated with specified ingestions.
a Delete annotations associated with specified ingestions.
v Delete ingestion records and their associated indexes from the Mobius Server database.
f Delete document archive files associated with specified ingestions.
d Delete empty directories after document archive files are deleted.
p Delete the ingestion even if there is distribution pending for that ingestion.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 436


## Page 437


Parameter Description
e Enforce a complete transaction. If adelete cannot find one of the items for a specified ingestion,
it does not delete any of the items for that ingestion.
c
Delete expired content sections without extending the retention period.
Note: Specify the -G option.
n
Delete expired content sections without considering the intermediate archive retention.
Note: Specify the -G option.
u
Delete archives with unexpired retention periods.
Note: -Y u requires –Y f.
For Centera archives:
• Assign the privileged-delete capability to the Centera access profile.
• Add the EXTValidateRecipientPrivilegedDeletionCredentials function to the custom
authorization library.
w Support wildcard asterisk (*)
x Excludes archives during processing that are being held or contain unexpired content sections.
ALL Use all options above except u, w, c, n, and x. Equivalent to specifying: iavfdpe.
T Indicates that a "partition aware topic delete" should be attempted.
G Indicates that if the '-Y T' processing returns a "report version range " error, then proceed with
version deletion.
Default: No options
-G (optional - Required only if -Yc, -Yn, or -Ycn is specified) Deletes expired content sections from
contents configured for Event-Based retention (EBR) or contents whose normal retention period has
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 437


## Page 438


Parameter Description
not expired.
Note:
• Do not use this option if the -Y option is specified. But, if the -Yc, -Yn, or -Ycn
option is specified, then the -G option is required.
• For SnapLock, Isilon, Centera, and S3-with-lock storage systems, deletion of
content sections in an archive by Records Manager can only take place if the
whole archive has expired. Records Manager can then delete individual content
sections.
-E error
level
(optional) Indicates the minimum error level that Mobius records during archive deletion processing.
The errors are recorded in the error log.
i Information
w Warning
x Exception
e Error
-O
(optional) Deletes all specified items without prompting for confirmation.
Default: Prompt to confirm ingestion deletions.
-L
(optional) Deletes only the ingestion indicated by the –T parameter.
It is not valid to include –L in the same command as –C or –N.
Default: Delete all ingestions equal to or older than the specified ingestion.
-V verbose
level
(optional) Verbose levels that indicate which information is reported by Mobius during the archive
deletion process.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 438


## Page 439


Parameter Description
0 Only content errors and the final status of the archive deletion process.
1
Content all information, including which ingestions have been successfully processed. Please
note that some successful operations result in either a partial DAF deletion or an operation is
scheduled for a future time.
Default: 1
Examples
adelete –s Accounting –U Jsmith –P abba –r AC001 –t 20001111171212 –c –n –y vfd
This example connects to the Accounting document server to process the deletion request, authenticates the user,
then deletes all ingestions of AC001 that were created on or before November 11, 2000, have an expiration date past
the date specified in the report table, or have a generation number higher than the one specified in the report table.
The command then deletes ingestion records and document archive files associated with the specified ingestions,
followed by deleting empty document archive file directories.
Related information
MobiusRemoteCLI adelete
lcupdate command - Ad hoc migration
Use the lcupdate command to change the retention period of a migrated ingestion, and specify parameters to
immediately migrate, copy, export, or delete document archive files.
Permission to delete archives is required for this command. Only the ingestions with available viewing permission can
be deleted.
Syntax
lcupdate -S server -U user -R class ID -T timestamp [-options]
To view the full list of options, type lcupdate -h.
The lcupdate command and its options are almost similar to the MobiusRemoteCLI lcupdate.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 439


## Page 440


Parameters
Parameter Description
-s content repository
(required) Content repository name. Use quotation marks when the
repository name contains embedded spaces. For example, "Account
group".
-u recipient ID (required) User with permission to delete archives.
-p password (required) Document server password for the user ID.
-r class ID (required) Class ID with ingestion IDs to migrate.
-t timestamp (required if -d is not specified.) Specify the ingestion IDs equal to or
older than YYYYMMDDHHMMSS.
-l
Specify the filename template with the location to move (-m m), copy
(-m c) or export (-m x) archive. for filename template information, see
filename template.
-i Ingestion ID instance to move (-m m), remove (-m r), export (-m x),
extend (-m e), or lengthen (-m l).
-n
(optional) Sets current instance after processing. Values:
• integer
• n (next instance)
Required for these parameters: -m c and -m r if you are removing the
current instance
-c retention period
Sets compliant storage system:
• retention period (seconds), or
• (-c EventBased) when event based retention is used, or
• (-c Forever) for unlimited retention on Centera systems, or
• (-c YearEnd) for end of the year retention. -x is required only if
-c YearEnd is specified.
-j (optional) Sets compliant storage system intermediate retention
period (days) when event based retention is used (-c EventBased).
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 440


## Page 441


Parameter Description
-k
(optional) Last ingestion ID (YYYYMMDDHHMMSS) to process. This
option shortens the processing time of repeated runs. Use this option
when everything older than a certain date has been pre-processed.
This timestamp does not need to match an ingestion ID in the Report
Version table. Use this option to specify a range of ingestions to be
processed.
-x
(optional) Sets compliant storage system year end retention period
(years) when year end retention is used (-c YearEnd). The value must
be greater or equal to zero (0). For the end of the current year, use
zero (0).
-y processing options
(optional) Processing options:
• d (delete) - Delete empty directories
• p (pending) - Delete ingestion IDs with distribution pending
• h (hold) - Place ingestion ID on hold
• r (release) - Release ingestion ID from hold
• 1 (one) - Process only one ingestion ID
• u (unexpired) - Remove (-m r) instance with unexpired
retention periods. To use this option, -yf is required.
For Centera archives:
◦ Assign the privileged-delete capability to the Centera
access profile.
◦ Add the
EXTValidateRecipientPrivilegedDeletionCredentials
function to the custom authorization library.
-o (optional) No prompting when migrating ingestions IDs. Default is
false.
-m migration option
Migration action (-t must match an existing ingestion ID for d,e,m,r):
• c (copy) - Copy ingestion ID to new location (-l).
• r (remove) - Remove ingestion ID instance (-i)
• m (move) - Move instance (-i) to location (-l)
• d (delete) - Delete entire ingestion and all instances including all
indexes
• t (index) - Delete ingestion ID index names
• e (extend) - Extend ingestion ID instance (-i) retention. -c is
required when -m e is specified.
• l (lengthen) - Lengthen the retention period of the current
ingestion instance. Similar to -m e but without making a new
copy.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 441


## Page 442


Parameter Description
If -m l is specified -c is required.
• x (export) - Exports archive file to specified location (-l)
Example1 - Migrate ingestions
To migrate ingestions to a Centera storage, enter this command:
lcupdate -S Accounting -U Jsmith -P abba -R AC001 -T 20031111171212 -M c -L "<CENTERA>::\\" -C 9
4608000
to:
• Connect to the Accounting document server to process the migration request.
• Authenticate Jsmith with a password abba.
• Migrate (copy only) a single ingestion of AC001 that was created on November 11, 2003, 5:12:12 PM to a
Centera cluster.
• Set retention period to three years (94,608,000 seconds).
To migrate ingestions to Amazon S3, use "<AMAZONS3>:://" instead of "<CENTERA>::\\". Enter this command:
lcupdate -S Accounting -U ADMIN -R AC001 -T 20250718133038 -M c -L "<AMAZONS3>:://vdrnet/%REPORT
ID%/%ARCHIVEDATE%/%ARCHIVETIME%%UNIQUE.2%.DAF" -N 1 -Y 1
to:
• Connect to the Accounting document server to process the migration request.
• The user is an administrator.
• Make the migrate copy the new primary instance of a single ingestion of AC001 that was created on July 18,
2025, 13:30:38 PM to an Amazon S3 bucket. In the example, vdrnet is the name of the S3 bucket, followed by
placeholders for a content class ID, ingestion date and time, and the archive file identifier.
Example 2 - Modify current ingestion instance
To modify the current instance, first remove the current instance and then specify a new current instance. The
current instance is 0 for this example. Enter this command:
lcupdate -S Accounting -U Jsmith -P abba -R AC001 -T 20031111171212 -M r –I 0 –N 1 –Y d
to:
• Connect to the Accounting document server to process the migration request.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 442


## Page 443


• Authenticate Jsmith with a password abba.
• Delete the instance 0 and the associated document archive file of an ingestion created on November 11, 2003,
5:12:12 PM of content class AC001.
• Set the current instance for this ingestion to 1.
• Delete empty document archive file directories.
Example 3 - Export ingestion instances to disk
Enter this command:
lcupdate -S Accounting -U Jsmith -P abba -R AC001 -T 20031111171212 -M x –I 5 –Y 1 –O -L "c:/Exp
orted/%REPORTID.10%_ %UNIQUE.4%.daf"
to:
• Connect to the Accounting document server to process the migration request.
• Authenticate Jsmith with a password abba.
• Export the sixth instance from the most recent ingestion of AC001 that was created on or before November 11,
2003, 5:12:12 PM.
• Stop the confirmation prompt when exporting an ingestion.
Related information
MobiusRemoteCLI lcupdate
replarch command - Archive replication
The replarch command replicates archived ingestions and indexes from Mobius to Mobius z/OS, or from a Mobius
instance to another instance.
The archives are replicated to:
• Provide Mobius z/OS recipients with archives that cannot be created on Mobius z/OS, such as image, PCL, PDF,
PS, and SAP pieces of content.
• Retain copies of archives and indexes for long-term storage. Instead of retaining infrequently viewed archives
on Mobius, replicate those archives to Mobius z/OS where storage space might be less expensive and then
delete them from Mobius.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 443


## Page 444


• Share archives with remote offices.
While retrieving the data source credentials, parameters are divided into two categories:
• Parameters that identify and authorize access to the content and index information.
• The VDRNET user ID and password identify a VDRNET user within a Mobius document server.
• Parameters that describe the information to be replicated, the target, and options that apply.
• If the user has adequate permissions, the data source information is recorded at the Mobius Server and is
retrieved from there.
Syntax
replarch -u recipientID -r class ID -m mode  [options]
To view the full list of parameters, type replarch -h.
Parameters
Parameter Description
-S doc
server link (required) Name of the document server used to validate the Mobius user.
-U
recipient
ID
(required) Recipient with permission to archive. If a password is defined for this recipient, enter the -p
parameter.
-P
password (required if user has password) Password for the Recipient ID.
-R Report
ID
(required) Content Class ID of the ingestions to be replicated.
The Content Class ID may contain the asterisk wildcard character.
-M mode (required) Defines the replication mode.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 444


## Page 445


Parameter Description
A Asynchronous. Replicates archives from Mobius to Mobius z/OS.
A2 Asynchronous. Replicates archives from Mobius to Mobius z/OS with the extended topics output
data format.
S Synchronous. Replicates archives from one Mobius instance to another Mobius instance.
-RV
timestamp
(required if neither -PD or -FD are specified) Timestamp of the most recent ingestion to be replicated.
Archive Replication replicates all ingestions equal to the specified ingestion.
The timestamp must be in the YYYYMMDDHHMMSS format.
-PD days
(required if neither -RV or -FD are specified)The number of days prior to the current date that replarch
replicates all ingestions.
For example, if 60 days are specified, ingestions between the current day and 60 days prior to the
current day are selected for replication.
To replicate ingestions for the current date only, enter a value of zero.
-FD
timestamp
(required if neither -RV or -PD are specified) The start date of a range of days on which replarch
replicates ingestions. Enter the -TD parameter to specify the end date of the range. If -TD parameter is
omitted, then end date is the current date.
For example, to replicate pieces of content from 1/31/03 to 4/15/03. Enter these values:-FD 20030131
-TD 20030415.
-TD
timestamp
(optional) The end date of a range of dates on which replarch replicates ingestions. Enter the -FD
parameter to specify the start date of the range.
If -FD is specified and this parameter is omitted, then the end date is the current date.
For example, to replicate pieces of content from 1/31/03 to 4/15/03. Enter these values:-FD 20030131
-TD 20030415.
-H host
name
(required if -J is not specified) The host name or IP address that receives the replicated ingestions.
• For Mobius to Mobius z/OS replication, this is the host name of the File Transfer Protocol (FTP)
server.
• For Mobius to Mobius replication, this is the host name of the RMI server.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 445


## Page 446


Parameter Description
-HU
recipient
ID
(required if -J is not specified) Recipient who has permission to perform the type of processing
required on the host specified by the -H parameter.
• For Mobius to Mobius z/OS replication, the recipient must have permission to create and delete
files.
• For Mobius to Mobius replication, the recipient must have permission to archive.
If a password is defined for this recipient,
enter the -HP parameter.
-HP
password
(required if the Recipient ID in -HU has a password or the -J is not specified) The password associated
with the host recipient ID.
-DF
filename
The destination path and filename for ingestions replicated to Mobius z/OS.
This file is created on Mobius and sent to Mobius z/OS through an FTP program.
-DSI server
(required with the -M and -S parameters) Name of the document server that receives the replicated
ingestions. If the name contains embedded spaces, enclose the name in quotation marks.
Note: This parameter is only for Mobius to Mobius replication.
-PORT port
(optional) The port number of the host machine receiving the replicated archives.
For Mobius to Mobius replication, this is the port number of the RMI server on the host machine.
-TID topic
ID
(optional) List of indexes to replicate.
If there is more than one index, use spaces to separate each Index.
The index may contain the asterisk wildcard character.
For example, to replicate Indexes Dept and Region and all indexes beginning with the word “sales”,
enter this value:-TID Dept Region sales*.
-TEMP
directory
(optional) The directory to store temporary files. Use the naming convention of the server’s operating
system. Optionally, use the UNC path for Windows or Linux operating system.
If a directory name contains spaces, enclose the entire path in quotation marks.
If this parameter is omitted, replarch uses the operating system’s temporary directory.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 446


## Page 447


Parameter Description
-AF
template
(optional) The archive model filename used to create Content Class IDs on the Mobius destination
server.
If the Content Class ID already exists on the Mobius destination server, replarch does not replicate
the Content Class ID.
-NOMOVE
(optional) Indicates that the control files are not moved to the destination directory.
Note: To omit the host name, UserID, and password, do not move the files.
-LOG
filename
type
(optional) Name of the path, filename, and the type of information recorded in the log file.
Specifying the directory path where the application logs are created. Automatically generates unique
ReplArch application logs.
If the path is omitted, replarch stores the log file in the operating system’s temporary directory.
If the filename is omitted, replarch generates a filename from this template:
REPLARCH%ARCHIVEDATE%%ARCHIVETIME%.log.
After archive replication is complete, view the log file on the destination machine.
These message types are recorded in the replication log:
A All messages
I Informational, summary, and error messages
S Summary and error messages
N No messages
Default: I
-X filename (optional) Name of a path and filename that specifies the parameters to be used in unattended mode.
List the parameters in a file instead of providing them on the command line.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 447


## Page 448


Examples
Example 1
replarch –S documentserverlink1 –u jsmith –P abba –M A –LOG c:/replication/log REPLARCH%ARC
HIVEDATE%%ARCHIVETIME%.log
a –R AC001 –RV “20001111171212” –H 99.99.99.99 –HU ADMIN –HP bccb –DF “c:/VDR/DBMAINT.INRP
T.txt” –TEMP c:/replication/temp
–TID Expense TaxID Acct11223344
This example:
• Authenticates databases and users associated with documentserverlink1.
• Authenticates the user credentials.
• Processes in asynchronous mode to replicate archives from Mobius to Mobius z/OS.
• Logs all messages to the c:/replication/log/REPLARCH20001111130946.log file.
• Replicates the ingestion of AC001 that was created on November 11, 2000.
• Replicates the ingestions to the host, 99.99.99.99.
• Creates the replicated information in the c:/VDR/DBMAINT.INRPT.txt file.
• Stores the replicated file in the c:/replication/temp directory.
• Replicates Indexes, Expense TaxID, and Acct11223344.
Example 2
replarch –S documentserverlink1 –u jsmith –P abba –M S –LOG c:/replication/log REPLARCH%ARC
HIVEDATE%%ARCHIVETIME%.log a
–R AC001 –FD “20041111171212” –H 99.99.99.99 –PORT 2451 –DSI mydocserver –HU ADMIN –HP bccb
–AF “c:/VDR/AC001_%UNIQUE%”
–TID TID Expense TaxID Acct112233*
This example:
• Authenticates databases and users associated with documentserverlink1.
• Authenticates the user credentials.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 448


## Page 449


• Processes in synchronous mode to replicate archives from one Mobius instance to another instance.
• Logs all messages to the c:/replication/log/REPLARCH20001111130946.log file.
• Replicates the ingestion of AC001 that was created on November 11, 2000.
• Replicates the ingestions to the host, 99.99.99.99, with port number 2451, on the mydocserver document
server.
• Stores the replicated information in DAF, c:/VDR/AC001_%UNIQUE%.
• Replicates Indexes, Expense, TaxID, and any Index beginning with Acct112233.
replaudit command - Audit archive replication
The replaudit command deletes document archive files on Mobius that were successfully replicated to Mobius z/OS,
and updates the Report Version table for each successfully replicated ingestion on Mobius z/OS.
Syntax
replaudit -u recipientID -f filename [options]
To view the full list of options, type replaudit -h.
To specify a file name on Linux systems, enclose the directory path within quotes or add a second forward slash (/).
For example, specify c:/VDR/paramsFile.txt as “c:/VDR/paramsFile.txt” or c://VDR//paramsFile.txt .
Parameters
Parameter Description
-S doc
server link Name of the document server used to validate the Mobius user.
-U recipient
ID
(required) Recipient with permission to delete archives. If a password is defined for this recipient,
enter the -p parameter.
-P
password (required only if User ID has password) Password for the recipient ID.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 449


## Page 450


Parameter Description
-F filename
(required) Enter the path and filename of the replication parameter file generated during the
replarch replication process.
This file is created on Mobius z/OS and is sent to Mobius through an FTP program. The MVS
administrator determines the path and filename.
-D (optional) Deletes document archive files on Mobius that were successfully replicated to Mobius z/OS.
-A option (optional) Indicates if the ingestion record should be deleted or updated.
D Deletes the ingestion record from the Mobius Server database.
U Updates the Report Filename field of the ingestion record. The updated information indicates
that the archive has been replicated to Mobius z/OS.
-LOG
filename
type
(optional) Name of the path, filename, and the type of information recorded in the log file.
If the path is omitted, replaudit stores the log file in the operating system’s temporary directory.
If the filename is omitted, replaudit generates a filename from this template:
REPLARCH%ARCHIVEDATE%%ARCHIVETIME%.log.
These message types are recorded in the replication audit log:
A All messages
I Informational, summary, and error messages
S Summary and error messages
N No messages
Default: I
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 450


## Page 451


Examples
Example 1
replaudit –S documentserverlink1 –u jsmith –P abba –f DBMAINT.INRPT.txt –d N –a U

This example:
• Authenticates databases and users associated with documentserverlink1.
• Authenticate the user.
• Audits the archive replication file, DBMAINT.INRPT.txt.
• Updates the target file name in the Report Version table record to Mobius z/OS.
Example 2
replaudit –S MyServer –u jsmith –P abba –f DBMAINT.INRPT.txt –d Y –a D
This example:
• Authenticates databases and users associated with MyServer.
• Authenticates the user.
• Audits the archive replication file called DBMAINT.INRPT.txt.
• Deletes the replication instance table record.
• Deletes the document archive file (DAF).
• Deletes the ingestion record.
vdrencrypt command - Archive file (DAF) encryption
The vdrencrypt command maintains encrypted document archive files (DAFs) from encrypting DAFs that have not
been encrypted, re-encrypting existing encrypted DAFs with a new key, to repairing encryption in existing encrypted
DAFs, if required.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 451


## Page 452


Syntax
vdrencrypt parameter list
To view the full list of options, type vdrencrypt -h.
Parameters
Parameter Description
-S s erver (required) Name of a document server link. The same value that would be used for other Mobius
Administrator programs like acreate or adelete.
-U user (required) The Mobius user accessing the document server.
-P
password (optional) Mobius user’s password.
The next group of parameters select the source of DAFs to be processed. There are three possible sources:
• a Content Class ID and an ingestion or range of ingestions, or
• the switch -ALL_ENCRYPTED, which requests that all currently encrypted DAFs be re-encrypted using the
current active encryption key
• a list file containing DAFs to process
These sources are mutually exclusive, so only one of the following groups of parameters would appear on the
command line.
To process one or more ingestions for a particular Content Class ID:
Parameter Description
-r report_id (required) The Content Class ID to process.
-v report_ver (optional) A single ingestion to process. Will process one DAF whose ingestion exactly matches
the value on the command line.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 452


## Page 453


Parameter Description
-vs
report_ver_start
(optional) The first ingestion to process. Processes all ingestions equal to or greater than the
value on the command line. May be used in combination with -ve to define a range of
ingestions. If both -vs and -ve are specified, the -vs ingestion must be less than or equal to the
-ve ingestion.
-ve
report_ver_end
(optional) The last ingestion to process. Processes all ingestions equal to or less than the value
on the command line. May be used in combination with -vs to define a range of ingestions. If
both -vs and -ve are specified, the -vs ingestion must be less than or equal to the -ve ingestion.
-ALL_ENCRYPTED (optional) Processes all currently encrypted DAFs, re-encrypting them using the current active
encryption key.
To process a list of DAF files:
-l list_file (optional) Path to the file containing the list of DAFs to process.
-o out_file
(required) The output status content file. Each run of vdrencrypt produces a content file that
details what the program did in this run. This argument must be an absolute path to the file
including the file name. The path must exist, and if the file already exists, vdrencrypt must be
able to write over it. A status report file of each archive file (DAF) is generated after processing
the archive files.
-b backup_dir
(required) The DAF backup directory. This directory must exist and be on local storage, and be
writable. It must also be on a volume with sufficient space available to hold all the backup files
produced during the current run.
Before vdrencrypt modifies a DAF file, it creates a backup of the file under this directory. This
directory is prepended to the first directory in the original DAF file name and a copy of the
original DAF is made at the new path using the original DAF file name and extension. In other
words, the directory tree where the original DAF was written is reproduced under the backup
directory. In the case where the original DAF is on compliant media, the path below the backup
directory starts with the first directory in the compliant media path.
For example:
If the original DAF is located at C:/dafs/AC001/20180303010101.daf and the backup directory
on the command line is -b C:/data/backups then the backup file will be written as C:/data/
backups/dafs/AC001/20180303010101.daf.
A compliant media example:
If the original DAF is located at <SNAPLOCK>:://sl.mydomain.com/Snaplock/dafs/AC001/
20180303010101.daf, the backup directory on the command line is -b C:/data/backups then
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 453


## Page 454


Parameter Description
the backup file will be written as C:/data/backups/Snaplock/dafs/AC001/
20180303010101.daf.
-t thread_count
(optional) Sets the number of threads to use for processing, valid values are 1 - 16. Running with
multiple threads may improve performance on a multiprocessor machine. If omitted, the
default value is 1 thread.
-NO_BACKUP (optional) Overrides mandatory backup. No backup will be taken. Use only if another reliable
source of backups for the DAFs to be processed is available.
-h (optional) Displays a usage message to the console and exits with no further action.
Examples
Example 1
vdrencrypt -s myDS -u ADMIN -p adm123 -o
C:/temp/run1.out -NO_BACKUP -r AC001 -v 20180408111409
This example processes a single ingestion for content class AC001 with no backup.
Example 2
vdrencrypt -s myDS -u ADMIN -p adm123 -o C:/temp/run1.out -b C:/temp/backup
-r AC001 -vs 20180408000000 -t 4
This example processes all ingestions from April 8, 2018 or later for content class AC001, 4 threads.
Example 3
vdrencrypt -s myDS -u ADMIN -p adm123 -o C:/temp/run1.out -b C:/temp/backup -ALL_ENCRYPTED
This example processes all encrypted ingestions.
Example 4
vdrencrypt -s myDS -u ADMIN -p adm123 -o C:/temp/run1.out -b C:/temp/backup
-r AC001 -ve 20180330235959
This example processes all ingestions earlier than and including March 30, 2018 for content class AC001.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 454


## Page 455


Example 5
vdrencrypt -s myDS -u ADMIN -p adm123 -o
C:/temp/run1.out -b C:/temp/backup -l /Users/vdruser/AppData/Local/Temp/encr_rv_flagged.myD
S.20180409100302.csv
This example processes a list file generated by vdrchkencr.
Example 6
vdrencrypt -s myDS -u ADMIN -p adm123 -o C:/temp/run1.out -b C:/temp/backup
-r AC001 -vs 20180331000000 -ve 20180408235959
This example processes all ingestions between March 31, 2018 through April 8, 2018 for content class AC001.
Related information
vdrencrypt files
vdrencrypt files
To process archive files, the vdrencrypt command can optionally use an input file containing a list of DAFs , and an
output file with the status of each encrypted archive file.
Input DAF list file
To use an input list file, specify each line of the input file with the following values, separated by a tab character:
• Content Class ID
• Ingestion
• DAF file name
• Size of the DAF file in bytes
• Number of pages in DAF file
Status report file
After processing the archive files, the vdrencrypt command writes to a file. Each line in the file lists status
information per processed DAF. Each line consists of the following information separated by a tab space:
• Content Class ID
• Ingestion
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 455


## Page 456


• Original DAF file name
• Size of the DAF file in bytes
• Number of pages in DAF
• File name of processed DAF
• Process status code (N=not processed, Y=processed successfully, P=currently being processed, E=error
occurred in processing)
• Error description (empty field unless process status code is E)
• Old encryption key hash (empty field, if processed DAF was not originally encrypted)
• New encryption key hash
This file does not contain any header line to facilitate its use as input to other programs.
For example:
ENCR01 20180406235722 C:/dafs/2794/ENCR01/20180406/23572200.DAF 40928 14 C:/temp/backup/dafs/279
4/ENCR01/20180406/23572200.DAF Y 50103 50103
ENCR01 20180406235744 C:/dafs/2794/ENCR01/20180406/23574400.DAF 40928 14 C:/temp/backup/dafs/279
4/ENCR01/20180406/23574400.DAF
E S043E- The report version could not be found 0 0
In this example, all tabs were replaced with spaces.
vdrdistclient command - Distribution with the Distribution client
The VdrDistClient command processes deferred distributions and re-runs prior distributions.
Issue this command for each content with deferred distributions, unless the -X parameter is used to input an XML
file.
Syntax
VdrDistClient -s document server -u recipient ID -p password [-M R|D] [-R class ID] [-T timestam
p]
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 456


## Page 457


Parameters
Parameter Description
-S document server link, -s document server link (required) Name of the document server link that
processes this distribution.
-U recipient ID, -u recipient ID, -user recipient ID
(required) User id with Allow distribution migration
permission to view the distributed content sections.
If a password is defined for this recipient, the -P
parameter is also required.
-P password, -p password, -password password
(required only if the Recipient ID has a password)
Password for the recipient specified in the -U
parameter.
-R report ID, -r report ID, -report-id report ID
Content Class ID of a content with deferred
distributions. If a Content Class ID is specified without
deferred ingestions, no processing occurs.
Default: All pieces of content with deferred distribution.
Do not include -M in the same command as -X.
This parameter is optional for deferred distributions,
and is not used when rerunning prior distributions or
using XML input files with the -X option.
-M distribution type, -m distribution type, -distribution-
type distribution type
Indicates type of distribution processing.
• D or d - Processing deferred distributions.
• R or r - Rerunning prior distributions.
Do not include -M in the same command as -X.
Default: Deferred distribution
Optional - Not used with the -X option.
-T timestamp, -t timestamp, -timestamp timestamp
(Required when rerunning prior distributions)
Timestamp of the distribution to be rerun. All packets
for this distribution are re-created. The distribution
timestamp is displayed in the Report Version table.
If the parameter does not match a timestamp of a
previous run, no processing occurs.
Do not include -M in the same command as -X.
Not used when processing deferred distributions or XML
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 457


## Page 458


Parameter Description
input files with the -X option.
-I, -i, -ignore-warnings
Allows distribution when the XML input file process
generates warnings. By default, distribution does not
run if warnings are present.
(optional) Can use with -X and is compatible with all
parameters.
-X filename
Path and filename of the Distribution XML file. The
content of this file specifies which information will be
distributed.
Do not include -X in the same command as -M, -R or -T.
(optional) Required only with the -I option.
Examples
Example 1 - Create packets for all undistributed versions of the SALES content
VdrDistClient -S MyDocServer -U Jsmith -P abba -M D -R SALES
This example:
• Invokes the Distribution client for document server link MyDocServer.
• Authenticates Jsmith with a password of 'abba'.
• Creates packets for all undistributed versions of the SALES content.
Example 2 - Redistribute all packets with a specific distribution timestamp
VdrDistClient -S MyDocServer -U Jsmith -P abba -M R -T 19990801151013
This example:
• Invokes the Distribution client for document server link MyDocServer.
• Authenticates Jsmith with a password of 'abba'.
• Redistributes all packets with the distribution timestamp 08/01/1999 3:10:13 PM.
• Uses the following timestamp format:
◦ 1999 - year
◦ 08 - month number
◦ 01 - day of the month
◦ 15 - hour of the day
◦ 10 - minutes
◦ 13 - seconds
Example 3 - Create packets for deferred distributions to recipients and groups based on the rules specified in
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 458


## Page 459


an XML file while ignoring warnings
Windows
VdrDistClient -S MyDocServer -U Jsmith -P abba -I -X deferred_distribution.xml
Linux
VdrDistClient.sh -S MyDocServer -U Jsmith -P abba -I -X deferred_distribution.xml
This example:
• Invokes the Distribution client for document server link MyDocServer.
• Authenticates John Smith with a password of 'abba'.
• Creates packets for deferred distributions to recipients and groups based on the rules specified in the
deferred_distribution.xml file while ignoring warnings.
Example 4 - Create packets for re-run distributions to recipients and groups based on the rules specified in an
XML file
Windows
VdrDistClient -S MyDocServer -U Jsmith -P abba -X rerun_distribution.xml
Linux
VdrDistClient.sh -S MyDocServer -U Jsmith -P abba -X rerun_distribution.xml
This example:
• Invokes the Distribution client for document server link MyDocServer.
• Authenticates JSmith with a password of 'abba'.
• Creates packets for re-run distributions to recipients and groups based on the rules specified in the
rerun_distribution.xml file.
Distributing packets with XML file data
The vdrdistclient command specifies the details for the following items for distribution by Mobius in an XML file.
• Distribution groups
• Recipients
• Content classes and content class versions
• Deferred and rerun distributions
The vdrdistclient -X command line option allows full distribution automation in the production environment.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 459


## Page 460


Distribution XML file format
Use the following format for creating an XML file to distribute packets. The root tag of the XML file must be one of the
following:
• <VDRNET_DISTRIBUTION _Version="n.n"> where n.n is the Mobius version.
• The inner element must be DISTRIBUTION_INSTANCE ,which indicates a single distribution packet performed by
the VdrDistClient command.
• The DISTRIBUTION_INSTANCE element has an attribute Type of either DEFERRED or RERUN and must be defined.
When Type=DEFERRED:
<DISTRIBUTION_INSTANCE Type="DEFERRED">
 <DISTRIBUTION_GROUPS>...</DISTRIBUTION_GROUPS>
 <REPORTS>...</REPORTS>
</DISTRIBUTION_INSTANCE>
When Type=RERUN:
<DISTRIBUTION_INSTANCE Type="RERUN">
 <DISTRIBUTION_GROUPS>...</DISTRIBUTION_GROUPS>
 <RECIPIENTS>...</RECIPIENTS>
 <RERUN_DISTRIBUTIONS>...</RERUN_DISTRIBUTIONS>
</DISTRIBUTION_INSTANCE>
• When the DISTRIBUTION_INSTANCE element has an attribute Type set to DEFERRED, two attributes specifying a
distribution time range is required.
XML file format for distributing deferred packets
The following table lists the elements that correspond to distributing content whose distribution was deferred during
archiving.
Note:
• Required attributes are marked with an asterisk (*).
• n represents a particular ID of that element or attribute.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 460

<DISTRIBUTION_INSTANCE Type="DEFERRED"> <DISTRIBUTION_INSTANCE Type="DEFERRED">
Element Attributes Immediate sub-elements or text content
DISTRIBUTION_INSTANCE
Type="DEFERRED" *
DistStartTime *

DISTRIBUTION_GROUPSREPORTS

DIST_GROUP_PRINT
Dist_Group_ID_n

Sample XML file format for XML file format for distributing deferred packets:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!-- Root tag -->
<!-- This tag is mandatory -->
<VDRNET_DISTRIBUTION Version="5.0">
    <!-- Defines a set of criteria for a single distribution call of DEFERRED type -->
    <!-- This tag is mandatory -->
    <!-- process report versions between DistStartTime & DistEndTime -->
    <!-- DistStartTime & DistEndTime attributes are mandatory -->
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 461


## Page 462


<!-- Type attribute is mandatory. Its value can be one of the following:
                 DEFERRED - Deferred distribution type
                 RERUN    - Rerun distribution type
     -->
    <DISTRIBUTION_INSTANCE Type="DEFERRED" DistStartTime="YYYYMMDDHHMMSS" DistEndTime="YYYYMMDDH
HMMSS">

        <!-- Defines a set of distribution groups for the current distribution -->
        <!-- This tag is mandatory -->
        <!-- Option attribute is not mandatory. Its value can be one of the following:
                ALL_GROUPS         - if specified, all nested <DISTRIBUTION_GROUP> tags will be
ignored
                ALL_EMAIL_GROUPS   - if specified, all nested <DISTRIBUTION_GROUP Type="DIST_GRO
UP_EMAIL"> tags will be ignored
                ALL_PRINT_GROUPS   - if specified, all nested <DISTRIBUTION_GROUP Type="DIST_GRO
UP_PRINT"> tags will be ignored
          -->
        <!-- This form of the tag defines a set of all print distribution groups for the current
distribution -->
        <DISTRIBUTION_GROUPS Option="ALL_PRINT_GROUPS">

            <!-- Defines a distribution group for the current distribution -->
            <!-- Repeatable tag -->
            <!-- Tag value is mandatory: tag can't be empty -->
            <!-- Type attribute is mandatory. Its value can be one of the following:
                          DIST_GROUP_EMAIL
                          DIST_GROUP_PRINT
             -->
            <DISTRIBUTION_GROUP Type="DIST_GROUP_EMAIL">Dist_Group_Id_1</DISTRIBUTION_GROUP>
            <DISTRIBUTION_GROUP Type="DIST_GROUP_PRINT">Dist_Group_Id_N</DISTRIBUTION_GROUP>
        </DISTRIBUTION_GROUPS>
        <!-- Defines a set of reports and report versions for a deferred distribution -->
        <!-- This tag is mandatory -->
        <!-- Option attribute is not mandatory. Its only value is ALL_REPORTS -->
        <!-- This form of the tag defines a set of reports for the current distribution -->
        <REPORTS Option="ALL_REPORTS">
            <!-- Defines a report for the current distribution -->
            <!-- Repeatable tag -->
            <!-- ID attribute is mandatory. Its value specifies an id of report for distribution
-->
            <!-- Option attribute is not mandatory. Its only value is ALL_REPORT_VERSIONS -->
            <!-- This form of the tag defines a set of all report versions of the report -->
            <REPORT Id="Report_Id_1" Option="ALL_REPORT_VERSIONS"/>
            <!-- You can define specific report versions with the help of this tag -->
            <REPORT Id="Report_Id_2">
                <!-- Defines a report version of the specified report  -->
                <!-- Repeatable tag -->
                <!-- Tag value is mandatory: tag cannot be empty -->
                <REPORT_VERSION>Report_Version_1</REPORT_VERSION>
                <REPORT_VERSION>Report_Version_N</REPORT_VERSION>
            </REPORT>
        </REPORTS>
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 462


## Page 463


</DISTRIBUTION_INSTANCE>
</VDRNET_DISTRIBUTION>
