Sample XML file format for rerunning distributions:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!-- Root tag -->
<!-- This tag is mandatory -->
<VDRNET_DISTRIBUTION Version="5.0">
    <!-- Defines a set of criteria for a single distribution call of RERUN type -->
    <!-- This tag is mandatory -->
    <!-- process previous distributions between DistStartTime & DistEndTime. These attributes ar
e optional and may be set only together -->
    <!-- Type attribute is mandatory. Its value can be one of the following:
                   DEFERRED - Deferred distribution type
                   RERUN    - Rerun distribution type
     -->
    <DISTRIBUTION_INSTANCE Type="RERUN" DistStartTime="YYYYMMDDHHMMSS" DistEndTime="YYYYMMDDHHMM
SS">
        <!-- Defines a set of distribution groups for the current distribution -->
        <!-- This tag is mandatory -->
        <!-- Option attribute is not mandatory. Its value can be one of the following:
                   ALL_GROUPS         - if specified, all nested <DISTRIBUTION_GROUP> tags will
be ignored
                   ALL_EMAIL_GROUPS   - if specified, all nested <DISTRIBUTION_GROUP Type="DIS
T_GROUP_EMAIL"> tags will be ignored
                   ALL_PRINT_GROUPS   - if specified, all nested <DISTRIBUTION_GROUP Type="DIS
T_GROUP_PRINT"> tags will be ignored
          -->
        <!-- This form of the tag defines a set of all print distribution groups for the current
distribution -->
        <DISTRIBUTION_GROUPS Option="ALL_PRINT_GROUPS">
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 464


## Page 465


<!-- Defines a distribution group for the current distribution -->
            <!-- Repeatable tag -->
            <!-- Tag value is mandatory: tag cannot be empty -->
            <!-- Type attribute is mandatory. Its value can be one of the following:
                                                            DIST_GROUP_EMAIL
                                                            DIST_GROUP_PRINT
             -->
            <DISTRIBUTION_GROUP Type="DIST_GROUP_EMAIL">Dist_Group_Id_1</DISTRIBUTION_GROUP>
            <DISTRIBUTION_GROUP Type="DIST_GROUP_PRINT">Dist_Group_Id_N</DISTRIBUTION_GROUP>
        </DISTRIBUTION_GROUPS>
        <!-- Defines a set of recipients for the current distribution -->
        <!-- This tag is mandatory for rerun distribution type -->
        <!-- Option attribute is not mandatory. Its only value is ALL_RECIPIENTS. If specified,
all nested <RECIPIENT> tags will be ignored -->
        <RECIPIENTS Option="ALL_RECIPIENTS">
            <!-- Defines a recipient for the current distribution -->
            <!-- Repeatable tag -->
            <!-- Tag value is mandatory: tag cannot be empty -->
            <RECIPIENT>Recipient_1</RECIPIENT>
            <RECIPIENT>Recipient_N</RECIPIENT>
        </RECIPIENTS>

        <!-- Defines a set of previous distributions for rerunning. The Option is optional and s
pecifies whether all previous distributions should be rerun. -->
        <!-- Its only value is ALL_DISTRIBUTIONS: all previous distributions should be rerun -->
        <RERUN_DISTRIBUTIONS Option="ALL_DISTRIBUTIONS">
            <!-- Denotes a specific previous distribution that should be rerun. -->
            <!-- Repeatable tag -->
            <!-- Option attribute is not mandatory. Its only value is ALL_REPORTS -->
            <!-- DistTimeStamp attribute is mandatory. It defines specific previous distribution
for rerunning -->
            <RERUN_DISTRIBUTION DistTimeStamp="YYYYMMDDHHMMSS" Option="ALL_REPORTS"/>

            <!-- This form of the tag defines a set of reports of the previous distribution for
rerunning -->
            <RERUN_DISTRIBUTION DistTimeStamp="YYYYMMDDHHMMSS">
                <!-- Defines a report for the current distribution -->
                <!-- Repeatable tag -->
                <!-- ID attribute is mandatory. Its value should contain the id of the report sp
ecified -->
                <!-- Option attribute is not mandatory. Its only value is ALL_REPORT_VERSIONS --
>
                <!-- This form of the tag defines a set of report versions that should participa
te in distribution -->
                <REPORT Id="Report_Id_N" Option="ALL_REPORT_VERSIONS"/>

                <!-- You can define specific report versions with the help of this form of the t
ag -->
                <REPORT Id="Report_Id_1">
                    <!-- Defines a report version of the parent report  -->
                    <!-- Repeatable tag -->
                    <!-- Tag value is mandatory: the tag cannot be empty -->
                    <REPORT_VERSION>Report_Version_1</REPORT_VERSION>
                    <REPORT_VERSION>Report_Version_N</REPORT_VERSION>
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 465


## Page 466


</REPORT>
            </RERUN_DISTRIBUTION>

        </RERUN_DISTRIBUTIONS>
    </DISTRIBUTION_INSTANCE>
</VDRNET_DISTRIBUTION>
Distribution XML file examples
Here are example XML files for content class version distribution and rerunning distributions.
Distribute content class versions
This example XML file distributes ingestions of the ACDIST_T1 report which were archived between April 20,
2014 and April 30, 2014 inclusive, to all print distribution groups, and to EmailDG1 and EmailDG2. Additionally,
three specified ingestions of the AC001 content class are distributed.
<?xml version = "1.0" ?>
<!-This is a small sample XML file -->
<VDRNET_DISTRIBUTION Version="<version>">
        <DISTRIBUTION_INSTANCE Type="DEFERRED"
                DistStartTime="20140420000000" DistEndTime="20140430235959">

                <DISTRIBUTION_GROUPS Option="ALL_PRINT_GROUPS">
                        <DISTRIBUTION_GROUP Type="DIST_GROUP_EMAIL">EmailDG1</DISTRIBUTIO
N_GROUP>
                        <DISTRIBUTION_GROUP Type="DIST_GROUP_EMAIL">EmailDG2</DISTRIBUTIO
N_GROUP>
                </DISTRIBUTION_GROUPS>
                <REPORTS>
                        <REPORT Id="AC001">
                                <REPORT_VERSION>20150810170640</REPORT_VERSION>
                                <REPORT_VERSION>20150820114426</REPORT_VERSION>
                                <REPORT_VERSION>20140401104047</REPORT_VERSION>
                        </REPORT>
                        <REPORT Id="ACDIST_T1" Option="ALL_REPORT_VERSIONS"/>
                </REPORTS>
        </DISTRIBUTION_INSTANCE>
</VDRNET_DISTRIBUTION>
Review the following XML file examples that are used to rerun distributions.
Example 1 - Rerunning all distributions
This XML file example reruns all distributions performed between 2014-04-20 and 2014-05-01 for all recipients and
distribution print groups.
<?xml version = "1.0" ?>
<!-This is a small sample XML file -->
<VDRNET_DISTRIBUTION Version="<version>">
        <DISTRIBUTION_INSTANCE Type="RERUN"
                DistStartTime="20140420000000" DistEndTime="20140501235959">
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 466


## Page 467


<DISTRIBUTION_GROUPS Option="ALL_PRINT_GROUPS"/>
                <RECIPIENTS Option="ALL_RECIPIENTS"/>
                <RERUN_DISTRIBUTIONS Option="ALL_DISTRIBUTIONS"/>
        </DISTRIBUTION_INSTANCE>
</VDRNET_DISTRIBUTION>
Example 2 - Rerunning specific distributions
This XML file example file reruns two specific distributions:
• Two specific ingestions of the AC001 content class.
• All ingestions of content class AC002 in the first distribution
• All content in the second distribution
• Rerunning distributions for all recipients and distribution print groups
<?xml version = "1.0" ?>
<!-This is a small sample XML file -->
<VDRNET_DISTRIBUTION Version="<version>">
        <DISTRIBUTION_INSTANCE Type="RERUN">

                <DISTRIBUTION_GROUPS Option="ALL_PRINT_GROUPS"/>
                <RECIPIENTS Option="ALL_RECIPIENTS"/>
                <RERUN_DISTRIBUTIONS>
                        <RERUN_DISTRIBUTION DistTimeStamp="20150102215500">
                                <REPORT Id="AC001">
                                        <REPORT_VERSION>20150102215300</REPORT_VERSION>
                                        <REPORT_VERSION>20150102215200</REPORT_VERSION>
                                </REPORT>
                                <REPORT Id="AC002" Option="ALL_REPORT_VERSIONS"/>
                        </RERUN_DISTRIBUTION>
                        <RERUN_DISTRIBUTION DistTimeStamp="20150102215700" Option="ALL_REPORT
S"/>
                </RERUN_DISTRIBUTIONS>

        </DISTRIBUTION_INSTANCE>
</VDRNET_DISTRIBUTION>
Command line commands to manage Full Text and Simple Search
Specific command line commands are provided to submit or cancel index jobs, delete report versions, or query index
jobs.
Setting up indexes is required when configuring simple or full text search for a content source.
ftijobsubmit command - Submit index jobs
The ftijobsubmit command instructs the content source to index content versions. The index job action
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 467


## Page 468


includes adding, merging, or modifying indexes.
ftijobdelete command - Delete report versions from FTS index
The ftijobdelete command deletes report version FTS indexes.
ftijobcancel command - Cancel index jobs
The ftijobcancel command cancels submitted and incomplete index jobs.
ftijobquery command - Query index jobs
The ftijobquery command provides statuses and lists of full text search (FTS) index jobs.
Related information
Configuring full-text search
ftijobsubmit command - Submit index jobs
The ftijobsubmit command instructs the content source to index content versions. The index job action includes
adding, merging, or modifying indexes.
Syntax
ftijobsubmit.bat parameter list
To view the full list of parameters, type ftijobsubmit -h.
The ftijobsubmit command and its parameters are almost similar to the MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobsubmit
command.
Parameters
Parameter Description
-s docserver (required) Name of the document server connection. If the name contains spaces, enclose
the name in quotation marks, for example, "My Server".
-uuser (required) User with permission to index. If a password is defined for this user, enter the
-p parameter.
-p password (required only user has password) Password for the user.
-r report ID
(required) Content class ID of the report with versions to index.
Asterisks (*) and question marks (?) are permitted as multi-character wildcard expressions
and single-character wildcard expressions respectively.
-v report version
(optional) Content versions to index.
Asterisks (*) and question marks (?) are permitted as multi-character wildcard expressions
and single-character wildcard expressions respectively, if only one report version is
specified.
If multiple content versions are provided, enter valid and fully specified 14-digit version
IDs.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 468


## Page 469


Parameter Description
-vr version-range-
start version-
range-end
(optional) Specifies the range of content versions to index with a start and end report
version.
-e error level
(optional) Indicates the minimum error level to record in the error log during indexing.
• i (information)
• w (warning)
• x (exception)
• e (error)
• d (disaster)
-o filename (optional) Specifies the absolute path and file name of the file that contains the output of
the command.
-t jobtype
(optional) Specifies the type of action performed on the database.
MERGE
(Default) Merges indexes from the specified databases.
UPDATE
Modifies an index if it already exists. If the index does not exist, an error is reported.
ADD
Adds an index if it does not exist. If the index already exists, it is not added and an
error is reported.
-i [true|false]
(optional) Determines if the job process is halted or processed when errors occur while
indexing individual report versions.
true
Allows the job process to continue when errors occur.
false
(Default) Halts the job process when errors occur.
Examples
Example 1 - Submit an index job for all existing reports
c:/Program Files/ASG/ASG-ViewDirect for Networks>ftijobsubmit.bat -u ADMIN -s MyDocServer
-r "*"
This example authenticates the 'ADMIN' user, specifies the MyDocServer content source connection to process
the index request, then indexes all existing contents.
Example 2 - Submit an index job for a range of content versions
c:/Program Files/ASG/ASG-ViewDirect for Networks>ftijobsubmit.bat -u ADMIN -s MyDocServer
-r AC001 -t add -VR 20150101000000 20150601000000
This example authenticates the 'ADMIN' user, specifies the MyDocServer content source connection to process
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 469


## Page 470


the index request, then add indexes for content versions between 1/1/2015 and 6/1/2015 for the content class.
Related information
MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobsubmit
ftijobdelete command - Delete report versions from FTS index
The ftijobdelete command deletes report version FTS indexes.
Syntax
ftijobdelete.bat parameter list
To view the full list of parameters, type ftijobdelete -h.
The ftijobdelete command and its parameters are almost similar to the MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobdelete
command.
Parameters
Parameter Description
-s docserver (required) Name of the document server connection. If the name contains embedded
spaces, surround it with quotation marks, for example, "My Server".
-u user (required) User with permission to delete indexes. If a password is defined for this user,
enter the p parameter.
-p password (required only if the user has a password) Password for the user.
-r report ID
(required) Content Class ID of the report with versions indexes to be deleted.
Asterisks (*) and question marks (?) are permitted as multi-character wildcard and single-
character wildcard expressions respectively.
-v report version
(optional) Content versions indexes to be deleted.
Asterisks (*) and question marks (?) are permitted as multi-character wildcard and single-
character wildcard expressions respectively, if only one content version is specified.
If multiple content versions are provided, enter valid and fully specified 14 digit version IDs.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 470


## Page 471


Parameter Description
-vr version-range-
start version-range-
end
(optional) Specifies the range of content versions indexes to be deleted with a start and end
content version.
-e error level
(optional) Indicates the minimum error level that Mobius records during indexing. The
errors are recorded in the error log.
i Information
w Warning
x Exception
e Error
d Disaster
-o filename (optional) Specifies the absolute path and filename of the file that contains the output of the
command.
Examples
Example 1 - Delete indexes for all content versions
c:/Program Files/ASG/ASG-ViewDirect for Networks>ftijobdelete.bat -r AC001 -v "*" -u ADMIN
-s MyDocServer
This example authenticates the 'ADMIN' user, specifies the MyDocServer content source (document server)
connection to process the index request, then deletes all content class versions for the content class.
A successful job displays the following:
Job 25 successfully queued.
Run ftijobquery with job id 25 to check on job processing status.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 471


## Page 472


Example 2 - Deleting a range of indexes
c:/Program Files/ASG/ASG-ViewDirect for Networks>ftijobdelete.bat -u ADMIN -s MyDocServer1,
MyDocServer2 -r AC001
-VR 20150101000000 20150601000000
This example authenticates the 'ADMIN' user, specifies the two content source connections (MyDocServer1 and
2) to process the index request, then deletes indexes for content versions between 1/1/2015 and 6/1/2015 for
the content class.
Related information
MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobdelete
ftijobcancel command - Cancel index jobs
The ftijobcancel command cancels submitted and incomplete index jobs.
Syntax
ftijobcancel.bat parameter list
To view the full list of parameters, type ftijobcancel -h.
The ftijobcancel command and its parameters are almost similar to the MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobcancel
command.
Parameters
Parameter Description
-s docserver (required) Name of the document server connection. If the name contains embedded
spaces, surround it with quotation marks, for example, “My Server”.
-u user (required) User with permission to index. If a password is defined for this user, enter
the p parameter.
-p password (required only if user has a password) Password for the user.
-r report ID
(optional as long as other criteria are provided) Content Class ID of the content with
versions that you want to index.
Asterisks (*) and question marks (?) are permitted as multi-character wildcard and
single-character wildcard expressions respectively.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 472


## Page 473


Parameter Description
-v report version
(optional) Content versions to be indexed.
Asterisks (*) and question marks (?) are permitted as multi-character wildcard and
single-character wildcard expressions respectively, if only one report version is
specified.
If multiple content versions are provided, enter valid and fully specified 14 digit
version IDs.
-vr version-range-start
version-range-end
(optional) Specifies the range of content versions to be indexed with a start and end
content version.
-e error level
(optional) Indicates the minimum error level that Mobius records during indexing.
The errors are recorded in the error log.
i Information
w Warning
x Exception
e Error
d Disaster
-o filename (optional) Specifies the absolute path and filename of the file that contains the
output of the command.
-st
Or
-status
(optional) Specifies the status of the index job to be canceled.
• PENDING
• RUNNING
• COMPLETED
• ERROR
• CANCELLED
• CANCELING
• COMMITTING
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 473


## Page 474


Parameter Description
• UNKNOWN
-j joblist (optional) List of jobs to cancel.
-subrange Submission-
range-start Submission-
range-end
(optional) Specifies the range of index jobs to be canceled with a start and end
submission-range.
Dates are in the format of the current locale. Specify date and time in quotation
marks (").
-b submitter (optional) Specifies to only cancel jobs by the specified submitter.
Examples
Example 1 - Cancel an indexing job for jobs trying to index specific content
This example authenticates the 'ADMIN' user, specifies the MyDocServer content source connection to process
the index request, then cancels jobs for the specific content class.
c:/Program Files/ASG/ASG-ViewDirect for Networks>ftijobcancel.bat -u ADMIN -s MyDocServer
-r AC001
Example 2 - Cancel an indexing job for jobs trying to index trying index content from a specific time period
c:/Program Files/ASG/ASG-ViewDirect for Networks>ftijobcancel.bat -u ADMIN -s MyDocServer
-r AC001 -t add -VR 20150101000000 20150601000000
This example authenticates the 'ADMIN' user, specifies the MyDocServer content source connection to process
the index request, then cancels jobs that are attempting to index any report between 1/1/2015 and 6/1/2015.
Related information
MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobcancel
ftijobquery command - Query index jobs
The ftijobquery command provides statuses and lists of full text search (FTS) index jobs.
Syntax
ftijobquery.bat parameter list
To view the full list of parameters, type ftijobquery -h.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 474


## Page 475


The ftijobquery command and its parameters are almost similar to the MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobquery command.
Parameters
Parameter Description
-s docserver (required) Name of the document server connection. If the name contains embedded
spaces, surround it with quotation marks, for example, "My Server".
-u user (required) User with permission to query the database. If a password is defined for
this user, enter the ‑p parameter.
-p password (required only if the user has a password) Password for the user.
-r report ID
(optional as long as other criteria are provided) Content Class ID of the report with
versions to be queried.
Asterisks (*) and question marks (?) are permitted as multi-character wildcard and
single-character wildcard expressions respectively.
-v report version
(optional) Content versions to be queried.
Asterisks (*) and question marks (?) are permitted as multi-character wildcard and
single-character wildcard expressions respectively, if only one content version is
specified.
If multiple content versions are provided, enter valid and fully specified 14 digit
version IDs.
-vr version-range-start
version-range-end
(optional) Specifies the range of content versions to be indexed with a start and end
report version.
-e error level
(optional) Indicates the minimum error level that Mobius records during indexing.
The errors are recorded in the error log.
i Information
w Warning
x Exception
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 475


## Page 476


Parameter Description
e Error
d Disaster
-o filename (optional) Specifies the absolute path and filename of the file that contains the output
of the command.
-st
Or
-status
(optional) Specifies the status of the index job queried.
• PENDING
• RUNNING
• COMPLETED
• ERROR
• CANCELLED
• CANCELING
• COMMITTING
-j joblist (optional) List of jobs to query.
-subrange Submission-
range-start Submission-
range-end
(optional) Specifies the range of index jobs to be canceled with a start and end
submission-range.
Dates are in the format of the current locale. Specify date and time in quotation
marks (“).
-b submitter (optional) Query for jobs by the specified submitter.
-d (optional) Provides a detailed summary.
-dd (optional) Provides unabridged details.
Examples
Example 1 - Query an index by a job ID
c:/Program Files/ASG/ASG-ViewDirect for Networks>ftijobquery.bat -j 25 -u ADMIN -s MyDocSer
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 476


## Page 477


ver
This example authenticates the 'ADMIN' user, specifies the MyDocServer content source connection to process
the index request, then queries job 25.
A successful job displays the following:
Id=25, Status=COMPLETED,  User=ADMIN,  docs=14
Submitted=Mon Aug 27 11:32:03 EDT 2018
Started =Mon Aug 27 11:32:03 EDT 2018
Completed=Mon Aug 27 11:32:06 EDT 2018
Waited 0.562s
Took 2.170s
Example 2 - Retrieving lists of content, content versions, and the state of the job
c:/Program Files/ASG/ASG-ViewDirect for Networks>ftijobquery.bat -j 25 -u ADMIN -s MyDocSer
ver -d
This example authenticates the 'ADMIN' user, specifies the MyDocServer content source connection to process
the index request, then queries job 25 with a detailed summary.

ignoreErrors: N

Related information
MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobquery
MobiusRemoteCli commands
Use MobiusRemoteCLI, a command line utility, to perform operations against Mobius Server. Instead of connecting
directly to Mobius Server, the commands you run are routed through Mobius View, which uses public REST APIs to
communicate with Mobius Server.
MobiusRemoteCLI is a collection of commands which are the equivalent of stand-alone applications. Mobius View
routes requests from MobiusRemoteCLI to run commands from any remote computer to a deployed Mobius Server in
the cloud or in an environment with restricted access. For example, you can run commands on a containerized
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 477


## Page 478


Mobius Server by sending MobiusRemoteCLI requests through Mobius View.
All command line commands apply to both Windows and Linux systems.
Prerequisites to using MobiusRemoteCLI
MobiusRemoteCLI is a Java program that communicates with Mobius View, which routes REST requests to
Mobius Server. To use MobiusRemoteCLI, review the following conditions:
Configuring a secure connection from MobiusRemoteCLI to Mobius View
To use the MobiusRemoteCLI command line tool through Mobius View, a secure connection is required. You will
need the certificate from Mobius View and import it into the Java JRE trust store.
MobiusRemoteCLI configure
MobiusRemoteCLI configure walks you through the configuration for adding a repository, authentication
(basic and OAuth), headers, and cookies.
MobiusRemoteCLI acreate
MobiusRemoteCLI acreate creates content archives. The archive content details are passed through
parameters to perform policy, list file and meta-JSON archiving.
MobiusRemoteCLI adelete
Deleting archives can be performed using the MobiusRemoteCLI adelete command.
MobiusRemoteCLI amigrate
MobiusRemoteCLI amigrate migrates document archive files (DAF).
MobiusRemoteCLI lcupdate
MobiusRemoteCLI lcupdate can be used to change the retention period of a migrated ingestion, with
additional options to immediately migrate, copy, export, or delete archive files.
MobiusRemoteCLI vdrdbxml
MobiusRemoteCLI vdrdbxml helps import data from an XML file to a local database and export data from a
database into an XML file.
MobiusRemoteCLI entitiesimporter
MobiusRemoteCLI entitiesimporter helps import values to a Mobius Server database through a JSON
document.
MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobsubmit
MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobsubmit indexes content versions through the content source. The index job action
includes adding, merging, or modifying indexes.
MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobcancel
MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobcancel cancels submitted and incomplete index jobs.
MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobdelete
MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobdelete deletes report version FTS indexes.
MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobquery
MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobquery provides statuses and lists of full text search (FTS) index jobs.
Related information
Prerequisites to using MobiusRemoteCLI
Configuring a secure connection from MobiusRemoteCLI to Mobius View
Prerequisites to using MobiusRemoteCLI
MobiusRemoteCLI is a Java program that communicates with Mobius View, which routes REST requests to Mobius
Server. To use MobiusRemoteCLI, review the following conditions:
• Mobius View and Mobius Server are running.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 478


## Page 479


◦ A repository is already configured, which provides a connection to the Mobius Server.
◦ Mobius View is connected to the Mobius Server instance that hosts the repository.
• Ensure that Java is installed and added to your PATHenvironment variable.
• To enable MobiusRemoteCLI to communicate with Mobius Server through Mobius View, ensure that the Mobius
View certificate is available in the Java JRE trust store for MobiusRemoteCLI to use.
• Ensure that authentication is configured to authenticate the connection to the Mobius Server.
• Download the MobiusRemoteCLI.zip file from the Rocket Community Portal. From the Mobius View > Mobius
View Linux Docker product category, download, then extract the contents into a single directory.
The following files are provided:
◦ MobiusRemoteCLI.sh (Linux shell script)
◦ MobiusRemoteCLI.bat (Windows Batch file)
◦ MobiusRemoteCLI.jar
If the directory where the files are installed is on the run time path, then it is not necessary to be in that
directory to run the scripts. Once extracted, you can run MobiusRemoteCLI sub-commands.
Related information
MobiusRemoteCli commands
Configuring a secure connection from MobiusRemoteCLI to Mobius View
To use the MobiusRemoteCLI command line tool through Mobius View, a secure connection is required. You will need
the certificate from Mobius View and import it into the Java JRE trust store.
Before you begin: Mobius View is configured to use TLS (SSL) for client connections and is provided with a
certificate.
1. Export the certificate from Mobius View.
The certificate should be either in .crt or .PEM file format.
2. Import the certificate into the Java JRE trust store using the keytool utility.
MobiusRemoteCLI will use the certificate from the trust store. Keytool command line to import the certificate
example:
{JAVA_HOME}/keytool -importcert -file mobiueviewservercertificate.crt -alias mvcert4cli -ke
ystore {JAVA_HOME}/jre/lib/security/cacerts -storepass actualstorepass
3. Configure the repository to use the following URL:
https://hostnameformobiusview:TLSportnumber/mobius
where port 8443 is the secure port number. For example:
https://myhost:8443/mobius
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 479


## Page 480


Related information
MobiusRemoteCli commands
MobiusRemoteCLI configure
MobiusRemoteCLI configure walks you through the configuration for adding a repository, authentication (basic and
OAuth), headers, and cookies.
Before you begin:
• A connection to a Mobius View repository is required, which provides a connection to a Mobius Server.
• To use adelete, admin crendentials are required to access the deletion API.
• A Java executable is required in the PATH.
1. At the command prompt, type MobiusRemoteCLI.bat configure (use .sh if you are on Linux).
2. On the displayed menu, type '1' to add a repository.
a. To create a new connection to a Mobius View repository, type A.
To return to the previous menu, type #, or type * to quit.
b. Follow the instructions on the Repository Configuration Details section.
c. Enter a repository name. The repository name must be identical to the Repository name you created in
Mobius View.
d. Enter a repository URL. The URL should be to Mobius View, for example, http//localhost:19080/mobius
(This assumes you have Mobius View running locally on port 19080).
e. (optional) Enter a user name and password. The user is a recipient ID on Mobius View server. These
fields are not required, so you can press Enter to skip the fields.
f. Press Enter to save the configuration, then type * to exit.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 480


## Page 481


3. You now can run all the commands except adelete.
To runadelete, select one of the following methods to configure authentication:
◦ OAuth Authentication
◦ OAuth Client Credentials
◦ Basic Authentication
When multiple authentication mechanism is configured, the following precedence is used in Mobius CLI - client
credentials flow > OAuth token authentication > Basic authentication.
Note: Before running any other CLI commands, ensure to configure authentication.
OAuth Authentication
The OAuth authentication is used to connect to Mobius View using a valid Oath token.
Note: Before using OAuth authentication, ensure to have a valid OAuth token.
The OAuth token can be generated from authentication server like Keycloak using the token URL. Tokens have
expiry and must be valid when it is used.
To use OAuth authentication, follow the steps given below:
a. Select OAuth Authentication option from configuration options. In the example given below, it is
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 481


## Page 482


b. To set the token, type S.
c. To view the token, type V.
d. To delete the existing basic authentication configuration, type U.
OAuth Client Credentials
The OAuth 2.0 client credentials grant flow permits a service (confidential client) to use its own service user
credentials, instead of impersonating a user, to authenticate when calling another service. This type of grant is
commonly used for server-to-server interactions that must run in the background, without immediate
interaction with a user.
Before proceeding with this configuration, ensure to have the following:
◦ A valid client ID and secret.
◦ The token URL.
◦ Generated token for the client must have administrative privileges in Mobius. To achieve this, perform
one of the following:
▪ Provide administrative privileges in Mobius for your client as a user.
▪ Configure your OAuth client with hardcoded claim that includes group information.
If configuring hardcoded claim, token claim name should match with what is configured in Mobius for
mapping groups, which by default is "groups". The claim value should be name of the group with
administrative privileges in Mobius, which by default is "mobiusadmin".
Below is an example of hardcoded claim in
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 482


## Page 483


To use Oauth client credentials, follow the steps given below:
a. Select Oauth Client Credentials option from configuration options. In the example, it is
b. To set the token URL, client id and client secret., type S.
c. To view the client credentials, type V.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 483


## Page 484


d. To delete the existing basic authentication configuration, type U.
Basic Authentication (Not Recommended for Production Deployment)
The Basic authentication is used to connect to Mobius View using username and password. This method is not
recommended in production environment.
To use basic authentication, perform the following:
a. Select Basic Authentication option from configuration options. In the example given below, it is
b. To set the username and password, type S.
c. To view the username and password, type V.
d. To delete the existing basic authentication configuration, type U.
After repository and authentication are configured, any other remote CLI commands can be executed. A
successful configuration displays a The configuration has been saved successfully message.
4. Type 'V' to review or exit the command.
Related information
MobiusRemoteCli commands
Prerequisites to using MobiusRemoteCLI
MobiusRemoteCLI acreate
MobiusRemoteCLI acreate creates content archives. The archive content details are passed through parameters to
perform policy, list file and meta-JSON archiving.
Review the prerequisites before using MobiusRemoteCLI commands.
Note: Depending on the configured security for Mobius View, the archive creation command call from
MobiusRemoteCLI might need authentication to be configured in the utility. To make MobiusRemoteCLI
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 484


## Page 485


use authentication and make the acreate command secure, configure Mobius View to secure archiving
request calls. Contact the Mobius View Administrator to configure security for archive creation request
calls.
Syntax
Windows
MobiusRemoteCLI.bat acreate [options]
Linux
MobiusRemoteCLI.sh acreate [options]
To view the full list of options, type MobiusRemoteCLI acreate -h.
MobiusRemoteCLI acreate command and its options are almost the same as the heritage acreate command.
Options
Options Description
-s content repository
(required) Content repository name. Use quotation
marks when the repository name contains embedded
spaces. For example, "Account group".
-f report input file
(required) List the full path of each (report, meta, or list)
file that will be archived. Use spaces to separate each
file name.
By default, the contents of each file is passed to the
server.
• Without--send-content-url, you can specify a
local drive letter (for example, C:\reports\
AC001.rpt), a mapped drive letter (for example,
M:\reports\AC001.rpt), or a UNC path (for
example, \\server\sharename\reports\
AC001.rpt).
• On a Windows server, the file path depends
whether --send-content-url is specified. With
--send-content-url, specify the corresponding
UNC path (required) rather than the mapped
drive letter only when the report input file is on a
mapped drive. A mapped drive letter is not visible
by the Windows service.
Each filename can contain standard wildcard characters,
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 485


## Page 486


Options Description
* and ?. For example, -F c:\rpts\aa.rpt c:\rpts\
ab.rpt c:\rpts\ba*00??.rpt .
Note: If a filename is not found, or if a
wildcard specification does not result in at
least one filename, the acreate session
terminates. In this case, the remaining files
on the command line are not processed.
To avoid this, specify the -i (ignore errors)
option.
-u recipient ID (required) User with permission to create archives.
-p password (optional) Repository password for the user id.
-x command file (optional) File name containing a list of command line
options for unattended mode.
-r content class ID, --report content class ID
(Optional for policy-based processing. Required for
section code location processing.) A Content Class ID
that is defined in the Report table.
• For section code location processing, specify the
Content Class ID associated with the archive.
• For policy-based processing, the specified Content
Class ID is used instead of the Content Class ID
that the policy finds on the page. This is the
equivalent to the Force Report ID option on the
Archive Creation interface. If this option is used
with policy-based processing, specify only one
candidate policy.
-n
(optional) If specified, policy selection rules are used to
determine which policies are candidates. If not specified,
policy selection rules are not used.
Note: Use the -n option to specify that
candidate policies should be selected by
applying policy selection rules rather than
specifying a list of policies.
-c candidate name
(optional) List candidate policies, and use spaces to
separate each candidate policy name.
An error will occur if there is more than one applicable
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 486


## Page 487


Options Description
policy for a Content Class ID and no candidate policy is
specified.
Note: Use the -n option to specify that
candidate policies should be selected by
applying policy selection rules rather than
specifying a list of policies. If the -n and the
-c options are used, only the candidate
policies in the -c option are used; policy
selection rules are ignored.
-a XML filename
(optional) Full path and name of an XML file that is used
to pass metadata to a policy.
A policy can use metadata, and an XML file is the source
of external metadata. If the archiving process uses a
policy that extracts metadata from an XML file, the
specified XML file is used instead of the one defined in
the policy.
On Windows, enter a UNC file name because Mobius
resolves the drive designation based on the location of
the server.
-m method
(optional) Processing method:
• p - (default) Policy-based processing.
• s - Section code location processing.
• i - List file processing for images, associated data,
and transparent data.
• m - Meta-based processing (supported in
MobiusRemoteCli acreate only).
-i
(optional) When specified, non-critical errors that occur
during policy-based or list file processing are ignored,
and continues processing the next content input file.
When not specified, the default is used.
-o process options
(optional) Content input file processing options:
• SINGLEID
Use the same Content Class ID for every page in
the content input file during archive creation. A
Content Class ID that is determined during policy
processing will not change on subsequent pages.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 487


## Page 488


Options Description
• (default) MULTIID
If a policy extracts a different Content Class ID on
a later page, the current document archive file
(DAF) will close. Archiving then starts in a new
document archive file (DAF) that uses the new
Content Class ID.
• DELETE
Delete content input files after creating the
archives. If the content input file is a list of images,
associated data, or transparent data, archive
creation also deletes all files specified in the list
file.
• (default) NODELETE
Retain content input files after creating the
archives.
• DELETE or (default) NODELETE
Omit this option and archive creation defaults to
the options specified by the processing options.
Note: The DELETE or NODELETE
option is deprecated. By default, this
option is ignored on the acreate
and MobiusRemoteCli acreate
command line unless the Control
content input file deletion from
the client instead of the server
option is selected. For more
information, see Managing the
content input file after archiving.
-v (optional) Verbosity level of messages on the console.
When not specified, no messages are displayed.
-t, --jobtype
(optional) Specifies the type of action performed on the
database. Values :
• MERGE - (default) Merges indexes from the
specified databases.
• UPDATE - Modifies an index if it already exists. If
the index does not exist, an error is reported.
• ADD - Adds an index if it does not exist. If the
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 488


## Page 489


Options Description
index already exists, it is not added and an error is
reported.
• TOPIC_EXPORT - Submits an index export job for
the specified content class. The exported indexes
are used by Content Insights for analytics
purposes.
-i, --ignoreErrors
(optional) When specified, continues the job process
when errors occur while indexing individual report
versions.
-w
(optional) The minimum severity level that Mobius
Server records into the error log during archive creation.
Values can be upper or lower case characters that
correspond with the severity levels written to the logs.
• 0 - (A)ll severity levels
• 4 - (W)arnings and above
• 8 - E(x)ceptions and above
• 12 - (E)rrors and above (default)
• 16 - (C)ritical
--send-content-url
Send the content URL, which passes the original file
name to Mobius Server. If running MobiusRemoteCLI on
a Linux system while Mobius Server is running on
Windows, enclose the filename within single quotes.
Required for the following scenarios:
• the policy for archive creation uses file-related
metadata fields,
• archiving a content input file from an Amazon S3
storage
Optionally, it can be used at any time Mobius Server
sees the content input file.
Note: This option does not apply to the
original acreate command.
Always pass the original file name if the policy uses file-
related metadata fields. In other cases, the policy
processing will not see the original file name and if there
are policy fields that rely on file-related metadata field, a
user might not get the information they need.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 489


## Page 490


Options Description
Note: If used together with --omit-file-
metadata, the MobiusRemoteCLI acreate
command will not send archive input file
related metadata information to Mobius
Server. Do this only for policies that do not
require file metadata.
--defer-status-check (optional) When specified, request for status is not sent
immediately after submitting request.
--omit-file-metadata (optional) When specified, file metadata is not sent in
the request.
--set-status-time (optional) Number of seconds to wait before checking
for status of request. Default: 0.25
Examples: Archiving from Amazon S3 storage
To archive content input files that are on Amazon S3 storage, check that Mobius Server is configured to access the S3
device. For example, the path to the device is s3://MyBucket/Reports/AC001E.rpt
Filename syntax
s3://bucket_name/path-to-file
Specify the --send-content-url so MobiusRemoteCLI acreate will pass the original filename to Mobius Server.
Example
MobiusRemoteCLI.bat acreate -s MyDocServer -u ADMIN -f s3://MyBucket/Reports/AC001E.rpt -c
SAMPLE_POLICY --send-content-url
The following archiving types are supported for Amazon S3 storage:
• Policy-based archiving with the --send-content-url option.
• List file archiving with the --send-content-url option provided:
◦ The input file is not a directory of list files that references S3 input files.
◦ The input file does not contain a wild card path.
Example: Meta-based archiving
MobiusRemoteCLI acreate supports meta-based archiving where a single meta file archives multiple documents. It
requires the following information in JSON files through the -f parameter.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 490


## Page 491


• (optional) Archive ingestion.
• (required) documentClassId - content class in which the document must be archived.
• (required) index - value where the document must be indexed.
Through meta-based archiving, MobiusRemoteCLI acreate allows EOS content to be stored into Mobius. To migrate
EOS content into Mobius, you will need the EOS import toolkit configuration file.
Syntax
The syntax to use for archiving the content using meta-based archiving:
MobiusRemoteCLI.bat acreate -s <RepositoryName> -u <UserName> -p <Password> -f <MetaJsonFil
eLocation> -m <ArchivingType>
Example
MobiusRemoteCLI.bat acreate -s MyDocServer -u ADMIN -p ADMIN -f C:\Reports\AC001E.json -m m
Additional examples
Example 1
MobiusRemoteCLI.bat acreate -s Inventory -u Jsmith -p abba -f ship.rpt receive*.rpt -m S -r
Rpt1 -d X
This example creates an archive using the document server called 'Inventory', authenticates the user, then
creates the archive from the ship.rpt content input file and all content input files with a file name beginning
with “receive.” Determine sections from the section code locations specified in the Rpt1 report record, and
suppress distribution.
Alternatively, you can use a file to run the command line options defined in the file. For example, create a file
called MYFILE containing the same parameters. Running the command with the -X option creates an archive
using the parameters in the file.
MobiusRemoteCLI.bat acreate -x MYFILE
Example 2
MobiusRemoteCLI.bat acreate -s Inventory -u Jsmith -p abba -f C:/ReportInputFiles/listfil
e.rpt C:/ReportInputFiles/listfile2.rpt -m P -c PolicyA PolicyB PolicyZ -o SINGLEID DELETE
–d I
This example creates an archive using the document server called 'Inventory', authenticates the user, then
creates the archive from the listfile.rpt and listfile2.rpt input files, located in the
C:/ReportInputFiles directory.
Determine content sections and indexes from the candidate policies PolicyA, PolicyB, and PolicyZ, then use the
same Content Class ID for every page in the content input file. Delete the content input file after the creating the
archive, then distribute the content immediately.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 491


## Page 492


Example 3
MobiusRemoteCLI.bat acreate -s Inventory –u Jsmith -p abba -f ship.rpt -m P -c PolicyA -r R
pt1
This example creates an archive using the document server called 'Inventory', authenticates the user, then
creates the archive from the Ship.rpt content input file using PolicyA to determine the content section and
indexing. A Report ID of Rpt1 is forced.
Example 4
MobiusRemoteCLI.bat acreate -s Inventory -u Jsmith -p abba -f ship.rpt -n –d I
This example creates an archive using the document server called 'Inventory', authenticates the user, then
creates the archive from the Ship.rpt content input file using policy selection rules to determine candidate
policies, then immediately distributes the contents immediately after creating the archive.
Example 5
MobiusRemoteCLI.bat acreate -s Inventory -u Jsmith -p abba -f ship.rpt -m I -v 2 -t
This example creates an archive using the document server called 'Inventory', authenticates the user, then
creates the archive from the Ship.rpt content input file using list file processing. The console displays the
processing information and before archiving, the list file is scanned for errors.

MobiusRemoteCLI adelete
Deleting archives can be performed using the MobiusRemoteCLI adelete command.
Review the prerequisites before using MobiusRemoteCLI commands.
Syntax
Windows
MobiusRemoteCLI.bat adelete [options]
Linux
MobiusRemoteCLI.sh adelete [options]
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 492


## Page 493


To view the full list of options, type MobiusRemoteCLI adelete -h.
MobiusRemoteCLI adelete and its options are almost the same as the heritage adelete command.
Options
Option Description
-s content repository
(required) Content repository name. Use quotation marks when the
repository name contains embedded spaces. For example, "Account
group".
-u recipient ID (required) User with permission to delete archives.
-p password (optional) Repository password for the user id.
-e error level
(optional) The minimum error level that Mobius Server records into
the error log during archive deletion processing. Values can be upper
or lower case characters:
• a - all
• w - warning
• x - exception
• e - (default) error
• c - critical
-r class ID (required) Class ID of files for deletion.
-t timestamp
(required only if -d is not specified.) Timestamp of the most recent
ingestion to be deleted. By default, adelete deletes all ingestions
equal to or older than the specified ingestion. See also the -l option.
The timestamp format is YYYYMMDDHHMMSS. The timestamp does
not need to match a Version ID in the Report Version table, unless -l
is specified.
Note: Do not use this option if the -n or -c option is
specified.
-d days
(required only if -t is not specified.) Deletes ingestions older than the
specified number of days. For example:
• -d 0 deletes ingestions created today or earlier
• -d 366 deletes all ingestions that are more than a year old
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 493


## Page 494


Option Description
Note: Do not use this option if the -n or -c option is
specified.
-l
(optional) Deletes only the ingestion indicated by the –t parameter. By
default, all ingestions equal or older than the specified ingestion are
deleted.
Do not include -l in the same command as –c or –n.
-tro timestamp, -trn timestamp
(optional) -tro and -trn combination are an alternative to the -t and
-d combination. -tro and -trn together specify a time range, with
-tro specifying an earlier or older time stamp and -trn specifying a
newer time stamp. Any ingestions that fall inclusively within that time
range will be deleted. The time stamp format is YYYYMMDDHHMMSS.
Note: Use -tro together with -trn.
-c
(optional) Delete expired ingestion IDs based on retention period of a
selected content class in Mobius Administrator (Content Class Access
Rights > Retention). If not specified, the retention period is ignored.
-n (optional) Delete expired content sections regardless of the
intermediate archive retention (only if -g is specified)
-g
(optional) Deletes expired content sections based on retention period.
Retention period applies to content configured for event-based
retention (EBR), or contents with non-expired retention period.
Required only if -yc, -yn, or -ycn is specified.
For EBR reports, it is possible to delete individual sections before the
archive file has expired on a compliant device, for example, SnapLock.
-y processing options
(optional) Processing options that identify items to delete with the
ingestions. The default is to use no options.
Note: Do not use this option if the -g option is specified.
Required only if -g is not specified, unless specifying -yc,
-yn, or -ycn.
Values:
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 494


## Page 495


Option Description
• i=Delete indexes associated with specified ingestions.
• a=Delete annotations associated with specified ingestions.
• v=Delete ingestion records and their associated indexes from
the Mobius Server database.
• f=Delete document archive files (DAF) associated with specified
ingestions.
• d=Delete empty directories after document archive files are
deleted.
• p=Delete the ingestion even if there is distribution pending for
that ingestion.
• e=Enforce a complete transaction. If adelete cannot find one of
the items for a specified ingestion, it does not delete any of the
items for that ingestion.
• ALL=Use all options above. Equivalent to specifying: iavfdpe.
• c=Delete expired content sections without extending the
retention period. To use this option, -g is required.
• n=Delete expired content sections without considering the
intermediate archive retention. To use this option, -g is
required.
• u=Delete archives with unexpired retention periods. To use this
option, -yf is required.
For Centera archives:
◦ Assign the privileged-delete capability to the Centera
access profile.
◦ Add the
EXTValidateRecipientPrivilegedDeletionCredentials
function to the custom authorization library.
• w=Support wildcard asterisk (*)
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 495


## Page 496


Option Description
• x=Excludes archives during processing that are being held or
contain unexpired content sections.
• t=Indicates that a "partition aware topic delete" should be
attempted. For Oracle database, specify only -tro and -trn
options.
• g=Indicates that if the '-y t' processing returns a "report
version range " error, then proceed with version deletion.
-o (optional) Do not prompt when deleting archives. Default is false.
-v
(optional) Verbosity level of messages on the console.
• 0 - Error and warning messages.
• 1 - (default) Error and warning messages.
• 2 - All processing information.
--set-status-time (optional) Number of seconds to wait before checking for status of
request. Default: 0.25
Examples
Example 1 - Delete archives based on date and retention option
MobiusRemoteCLI.bat adelete –s Accounting –u Jsmith –p abba –r AC001 –t 20001111171212 –c
–n –y vfd
This example connects to the Accounting document server to process the deletion request, authenticates the
user, then deletes all ingestions of AC001 that were created on or before November 11, 2000, have an
expiration date past the date specified in the report table, or have a generation number higher than the one
specified in the report table. The command then deletes ingestion records and document archive files
associated with the specified ingestions, followed by deleting empty document archive file directories.
Example 2 - Time limit for archive deletion
-d days example:
• Specifying -d 1 on July 6 deletes an archive created any hour on July 5 or before.
• Specifying -d 365 deletes archives at least 366 days old.
Related information
MobiusRemoteCli commands

adelete command - Deleting archives
MobiusRemoteCLI amigrate
MobiusRemoteCLI amigrate migrates document archive files (DAF).
Review the prerequisites before using MobiusRemoteCLI commands.
Syntax
Windows
MobiusRemoteCLI.bat amigrate [options]
Linux
MobiusRemoteCLI.sh amigrate [options]
To view the full list of options, type MobiusRemoteCLI amigrate -h.
MobiusRemoteCLI amigrate and its options are almost the same as amigrate heritage command.
Options
Option Description
-s content repository
(required) Content repository name. Use quotation
marks when the repository name contains embedded
spaces. For example, "Account group".
-u recipient ID (optional) Recipient ID. Optional only if
MobiusRemoteCLI authentication is configured.
-p password (optional) Repository password for the user id.
-r class ID (required) Class ID with ingestion IDs to migrate.
-t (required if -d is not specified.) Specify the ingestion IDs
equal to or older than YYYYMMDDHHMMSS.
-d days (optional) The age (days) of the ingestion ID. Use when
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 497


## Page 498


Option Description
-t is not specified. Apply migration rules to all ingestions
older than the specified number of days. For example,
specifying -d 1 on July 6 would migrate an archive
created any hour on July 5 or before.
-o (optional) No prompting when migrating ingestions IDs.
Default is false.
-y processing options
(optional) Processing options:
• n (next) - Force next migration rule
• d (delete) - Delete empty directories
• p (pending) - Delete content with distribution
pending
• h (hold) - Place ingestion ID on hold
• r (release) - Release ingestion ID from hold
• 1 (one) - Process only one ingestion ID
• w (wildcard) - Class ID ends w/ '*' for wildcards
Default - no options are specified.
-l
(optional) Last ingestion ID (YYYYMMDDHHMMSS) to
preprocess. This timestamp does not need to match an
Ingestion ID in the Report Version table. Do not use
together with -z.
-z days
(optional) Number of days back from today to pre-
process for migration. Do not use -l when -z is
specified.
-v
(optional) Verbosity level of messages on the console.
• 0 - Error and warning messages.
• 1 - (default) Error and warning messages and
phase changes.
• 2 - All processing information.
--set-status-time (optional) Number of seconds to wait before checking
for status of request. Default: 0.25
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 498


## Page 499


Example
This example migrates archive file with class ID 'AC001' based on a specified ingestion ID from the datamin01v
repository.
MobiusRemoteCLI.bat amigrate -s datamin01v -t 20201124084151 -y n -r AC001 -u ADMIN
The following sample output is displayed:

MobiusRemoteCli commands

amigrate command - Migrating document archive files
MobiusRemoteCLI lcupdate
MobiusRemoteCLI lcupdate can be used to change the retention period of a migrated ingestion, with additional
options to immediately migrate, copy, export, or delete archive files.
Review the prerequisites before using MobiusRemoteCLI commands.
Permission to delete archives is required for this command. Only the ingestions with available viewing permissions
are candidates for deletion.
Syntax
Windows
MobiusRemoteCLI.bat lcupdate [options]
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 499


## Page 500


Linux
MobiusRemoteCLI.sh lcupdate [options]
To view the full list of options, type MobiusRemoteCLI lcupdate -h.
MobiusRemoteCLI lcupdate command and its options are almost the same as the heritage lcupdate command.
Options
Option Description
-s content
repository
(required) Content repository name. Use quotation marks when the repository name contains
embedded spaces. For example, "Account group".
-u
recipient
ID
(optional) Recipient ID. Optional only if MobiusRemoteCLI authentication is configured.
-p
password (optional) Repository password for the user id.
-r class ID (required) Class ID with ingestion IDs to migrate.
-t
timestamp (required if -d is not specified.) Specify the ingestion IDs equal to or older than YYYYMMDDHHMMSS.
-l Specify the filename template with the location to move (-m m), copy (-m c) or export (-m x) archive. for
filename template information, see filename template.
-i Ingestion ID instance to move (-m m), remove (-m r), export (-m x), extend (-m e), or lengthen (-m l).
-n
(optional) Sets current instance after processing. Values:
• integer
• n (next instance)
Required for these parameters: -m c and -m r if you are removing the current instance
-c
retention
period
Sets compliant storage system:
• retention period (seconds), or
• (-c EventBased) when event based retention is used, or
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 500


## Page 501


Option Description
• (-c Forever) for unlimited retention on Centera systems, or
• (-c YearEnd) for end of the year retention. -x is required only if -c YearEnd is specified.
-j (optional) Sets compliant storage system intermediate retention period (days) when event based
retention is used (-c EventBased).
-k
(optional) Last ingestion ID (YYYYMMDDHHMMSS) to process. This option shortens the processing time
of repeated runs. Use this option when everything older than a certain date has been pre-processed.
This timestamp does not need to match an ingestion ID in the Report Version table. Use this option to
specify a range of ingestions to be processed.
-x
(optional) Sets compliant storage system year end retention period (years) when year end retention is
used (-c YearEnd). The value must be greater or equal to zero (0). For the end of the current year, use
zero (0).
-y
processing
options
(optional) Processing options:
• d (delete) - Delete empty directories
• p (pending) - Delete ingestion IDs with distribution pending
• h (hold) - Place ingestion ID on hold
• r (release) - Release ingestion ID from hold
• 1 (one) - Process only one ingestion ID
• u (unexpired) - Remove (-m r) instance with unexpired retention periods. To use this option, -yf
is required.
For Centera archives:
◦ Assign the privileged-delete capability to the Centera access profile.
◦ Add the EXTValidateRecipientPrivilegedDeletionCredentials function to the custom
authorization library.
-o (optional) No prompting when migrating ingestions IDs. Default is false.
-m
migration
option
Migration action (-t must match an existing ingestion ID for d,e,m,r):
• c (copy) - Copy ingestion ID to new location (-l).
• r (remove) - Remove ingestion ID instance (-i)
• m (move) - Move instance (-i) to location (-l)
• d (delete) - Delete entire ingestion and all instances including all indexes
• t (index) - Delete ingestion ID index names
• e (extend) - Extend ingestion ID instance (-i) retention. -c is required when -m e is specified.
• l (lengthen) - Lengthen the retention period of the current ingestion instance. Similar to -m e but
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 501


## Page 502


Option Description
without making a new copy.
If -m l is specified -c is required.
• x (export) - Exports archive file to specified location (-l)
-v
(optional) Verbosity level of messages on the console.
• 0 - Error and warning messages.
• 1 - (default) Error and warning messages and phase changes.
• 2 - All processing information.
--set-
status-
time
(optional) Number of seconds to wait before checking for status of request. Default: 0.25
Example
MobiusRemoteCLI.bat lcupdate -s Accounting -r AC001 -o -m c -l "c:/DAF/%ARCHIVEDATE.6%/%REPORTI
D.10%/%UNIQUE%_%ARCHIVEDATE%_%ARCHIVETIME%.DAF" -n 1 -y 1 -t 20201204151423 -u ADMIN
The following sample output is displayed:

MobiusRemoteCLI vdrdbxml
MobiusRemoteCLI vdrdbxml helps import data from an XML file to a local database and export data from a database
into an XML file.
Review the prerequisites before using MobiusRemoteCLI commands.
Syntax
vdrdbxml [options]
To view the full list of parameters, type MobiusRemoteCLI vdrdbxml -h.
MobiusRemoteCLI vdrdbxml and its options are almost the same as the heritage vdrdbxml command.
Options
Option Parameter
-s repository_name, -dsl repository_name
(required) Content repository name. Use quotation
marks when the repository name contains embedded
spaces. For example, "Account group".
-u recipient ID (optional) Recipient ID. Optional only if
MobiusRemoteCLI authentication is configured.
-p password (optional) Repository password for the user id.
-f file , --input-file file (required) File name containing XML input.
-x or --delete-input (optional) When specified, deletes input XML file after
the command completes.
-l file and type, -log file and type, --log-file file and type
(optional) Specify a different log file name or existing
directory and log type. Without this parameter, the log
file contains summary and error messages and is
created in the temporary directory specified by the
system environment variable. Values:
• All (A) - All messages
• Informational (I) - Informational, summary, and
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 503


## Page 504


Option Parameter
error messages
• Summary (S) - Summary and error messages
• None (N) - No messages
-n, -nex, --non-exclusive-db
(optional) When specified, non-exclusive use of the
database can result in slower processing (default). When
not specified, exclusive use of the database and
processing is faster.
-a argfile , -args argfile
(optional) File name containing a list of command line
arguments. If there are duplicated parameters, the
parameter that is declared last is processed.
-o outputFile, -out outputFile, --output-file outputFile
(optional) Path and file name that receives exported
data. Without this parameter, the output is sent to a file
named DBXMLResults.XML in a temporary directory
specified by the system environment variable.
-v
(optional) Verbosity level of messages on the console.
• 0 - Error and warning messages.
• 1 - (default) Error and warning messages and
phase changes.
• 2 - All processing information.
--set-status-time (optional) Number of seconds to wait before checking
for status of request. Default: 120
--send-content-url
(optional) Send the content URL, which passes the
original XML file name to Mobius Server. If running
MobiusRemoteCLI on a Linux system while Mobius
Server is running on Windows, enclose the filename
within single quotes. Values are: true/(default) false.
Related information
MobiusRemoteCli commands
Prerequisites to using MobiusRemoteCLI
vdrdbxml command - Importing and exporting XML data

MobiusRemoteCLI entitiesimporter
MobiusRemoteCLI entitiesimporter helps import values to a Mobius Server database through a JSON document.
An error.json file listing the error entities is created if the imported JSON file contains error entries. Fix the error
entries, then re-import the returned JSON file to continue processing the remaining entities.
Review the prerequisites before using MobiusRemoteCLI commands.
Syntax
Windows
MobiusRemoteCLI.bat entitiesimporter [options]
Linux
MobiusRemoteCLI.sh entitiesimporter [options]
To view the full list of options, type MobiusRemoteCLI entitiesimporter -h.
Options
Option Description
-s document server link, -dsl document server link
(required) Document server name. Use quotation marks
when the document server name contains embedded
spaces. For example, "Account group".
-u recipient ID (optional) Recipient ID. Optional only if
MobiusRemoteCLI authentication is configured.
-p password (optional) Repository password for the user id.
-f file , --input-file file (required) File name containing JSON input.
-x , --delete-input (optional) When specified, deletes input JSON file after
command completes.
-l file and type, -log file and type, --log-file file and type
(optional) Specify a different log file name or existing
directory and log type. Without this parameter, the log
file contains summary and error messages and is
created in the temporary directory specified by the
system environment variable. Values:
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 505


## Page 506


Option Description
• All (A) - All messages
• Informational (I) - Informational, summary, and
error messages
• Summary (S) - Summary and error messages
• None (N) - No messages
-n, -nex, --non-exclusive-db
(optional) When specified, non-exclusive use of the
database can result in slower processing (default). When
not specified, exclusive use of the database and
processing is faster.
-a argfile , -args argfile
(optional) File name containing a list of command line
arguments. If there are duplicated parameters, the
parameter that is declared last is processed.
-e exception_file, --exception-file exception_file
(optional) File name that receives JSON output. When
omitted, the output is sent to the DBJSONResults.json
in the temporary directory specified by the system
environment variable.
-v
(optional) Verbosity level of messages on the console.
• 0 - Error and warning messages.
• 1 - (default) Error and warning messages and
phase changes.
• 2 - All processing information.
--set-status-time (optional) Number of seconds to wait before checking
for status of request. Default: 120

MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobsubmit
MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobsubmit indexes content versions through the content source. The index job action includes
adding, merging, or modifying indexes.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 506


## Page 507


Review the prerequisites before using MobiusRemoteCLI commands.
Syntax
Windows
MobiusRemoteCLI.bat ftijobsubmit [options]
Linux
MobiusRemoteCLI.sh ftijobsubmit [options]
To view the full list of options, type MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobsubmit -h.
MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobsubmit and its options are almost the same as the heritage ftijobsubmit command.
Options
Option Description
-s document server link, --doc-server document server link
(required) Content repository name. Use quotation
marks when the repository name contains embedded
spaces. For example, "Account group".
-u recipient ID, --user recipient ID (optional) Recipient ID. Optional only if
MobiusRemoteCLI authentication is configured.
-p password (optional) Repository password for the user id.
-e error level, --error-level error level
(optional) Minimum error level to record during
indexing. Values can be upper or lower case characters:
• i - informational
• w - warning
• x - (default) exception
• e - error
• d - disaster
-o outputFile, --output-file outputFile (optional) Absolute path and file name of the file that
contains the output of the command.
-r content class ID, --report content class ID
(required) Ingestion IDs support (* and ?) wildcards
when a single ingestion ID is specified. Use double
quotes to enclose ingestion IDs with wildcards.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 507


## Page 508


Option Description
-v, --version , --versions
(optional) Content versions to index. Asterisks (*) and
question marks (?) are permitted as multi-character
wildcard expressions and single-character wildcard
expressions respectively, if only one content version is
specified. If multiple content versions are provided,
enter valid and fully specified 14-digit version IDs.
-vr , --version-range (optional) Specifies the ingestion ID range that includes
the start and end of the ingestion.
--verbose (optional) Verbosity level of messages on the console.
When not specified, no messages are displayed.
-t, --jobtype
(optional) Specifies the type of action performed on the
database. Values :
• MERGE - (default) Merges indexes from the
specified databases.
• UPDATE - Modifies an index if it already exists. If
the index does not exist, an error is reported.
• ADD - Adds an index if it does not exist. If the
index already exists, it is not added and an error is
reported.
• TOPIC_EXPORT - Submits an index export job for
the specified content class. The exported indexes
are used by Content Insights for analytics
purposes.
-i, --ignoreErrors
(optional) When specified, continues the job process
when errors occur while indexing individual report
versions.
Examples
Example 1 - Submit an index job for all existing reports
MobiusRemoteCLI.bat ftijobsubmit -u ADMIN -s MyDocServer -r "*"
This example authenticates the 'ADMIN' user, specifies the MyDocServer content source connection to process
the index request, then indexes all existing contents.
Example 2 - Submit an index job for a range of content versions
MobiusRemoteCLI.bat ftijobsubmit -u ADMIN -s MyDocServer -r AC001 -t add -VR 20150101000000
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 508


## Page 509


20150601000000
This example authenticates the 'ADMIN' user, specifies the MyDocServer content source connection to process
the index request, then add indexes for content versions between 1/1/2015 and 6/1/2015 for the content class.
Related information
MobiusRemoteCli commands
Prerequisites to using MobiusRemoteCLI
ftijobsubmit command - Submit index jobs
MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobcancel
MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobcancel cancels submitted and incomplete index jobs.
Review the prerequisites before using MobiusRemoteCLI commands.
Syntax
Windows
MobiusRemoteCLI.bat ftijobcancel [options]
Linux
MobiusRemoteCLI.sh ftijobcancel [options]
To view the full list of options, type MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobcancel -h.
MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobcancel and its options are almost the same as the heritage ftijobcancel command.
Options
Option Description
-s content repository
(required) Content repository name. Use quotation
marks when the repository name contains embedded
spaces. For example, "Account group".
-u recipient ID, --user recipient ID (optional) Recipient ID. Optional only if
MobiusRemoteCLI authentication is configured.
-p password (optional) Repository password for the user id.
-e error level, --error-level error level (optional) Minimum error level to record during
indexing. Values can be upper or lower case characters:
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 509


## Page 510


Option Description
• i - informational
• w - warning
• x - (default) exception
• e - error
• d - disaster
-o outputFile, --output-file outputFile (optional) Absolute path and file name of the file that
contains the output of the command.
-r content class ID, --report content class ID
(required) Ingestion IDs support (* and ?) wildcards
when a single ingestion ID is specified. Use double
quotes to enclose ingestion IDs with wildcards.
-v, --version , --versions
(optional) Content versions to index. Asterisks (*) and
question marks (?) are permitted as multi-character
wildcard expressions and single-character wildcard
expressions respectively, if only one content version is
specified. If multiple content versions are provided,
enter valid and fully specified 14-digit version IDs.
-vr , --version-range (optional) Specifies the ingestion ID range that includes
the start and end of the ingestion.
--verbose (optional) Verbosity level of messages on the console.
When not specified, no messages are displayed.
-st, --status
(optional) Status of a job to cancel. It is possible to
include one or more statuses. List of available statuses:
• UNKNOWN,
• PENDING,
• RESOLVING,
• RESOLVED,
• EXTRACTING,
• EXTRACTED,
• RUNNING,
• COMMITTING,
• CANCEL_REQ,
• CLEANUP_REQ,
• CLEANING,
• CANCELLED,
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 510


## Page 511


Option Description
• ERROR,
• COMPLETED,
• COMPLETED_WITH_ERRORS
-j, --joblist (optional) List of jobs to cancel.
-subrange, --submission-range
(optional) Specify the range of index jobs for
cancellation with a start and end submission-range.
Dates are in the format of the current locale. Specify
date and time in quotation marks (").
-b, --submitter (optional) Cancel jobs by specified job submitter.
-jr, –jobrange (optional) Specify the job range that includes the start
and end of the job range.
Examples
Example 1 - Cancel an indexing job for jobs trying to index specific content
This example authenticates the 'ADMIN' user, specifies the MyDocServer content source connection to process
the index request, then cancels jobs for the specific content class.
MobiusRemoteCLI.bat ftijobcancel -u ADMIN -s MyDocServer -r AC001
Example 2 - Cancel an indexing job for jobs trying to index trying index content from a specific time period
MobiusRemoteCLI.bat ftijobcancel -u ADMIN -s MyDocServer -r AC001 -t add -VR 20150101000000
20150601000000
This example authenticates the 'ADMIN' user, specifies the MyDocServer content source connection to process
the index request, then cancels jobs that are attempting to index any report between 1/1/2015 and 6/1/2015.
Related information
MobiusRemoteCli commands
Prerequisites to using MobiusRemoteCLI
ftijobcancel command - Cancel index jobs
MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobdelete
MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobdelete deletes report version FTS indexes.
Review the prerequisites before using MobiusRemoteCLI commands.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 511


## Page 512


Syntax
Windows
MobiusRemoteCLI.bat ftijobdelete [options]
Linux
MobiusRemoteCLI.sh ftijobdelete [options]
To view the full list of options, type MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobdelete -h.
MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobdelete and its options are almost the same as the heritage ftijobdelete command.
Options
Option Description
-s content repository
(required) Content repository name. Use quotation
marks when the repository name contains embedded
spaces. For example, "Account group".
-u recipient ID, --user recipient ID (optional) Recipient ID. Optional only if
MobiusRemoteCLI authentication is configured.
-p password (optional) Repository password for the user id.
-e error level, --error-level error level
(optional) Minimum error level to record during
indexing. Values can be upper or lower case characters:
• i - informational
• w - warning
• x - (default) exception
• e - error
• d - disaster
-o outputFile, --output-file outputFile (optional) Absolute path and file name of the file that
contains the output of the command.
-r content class ID, --report content class ID
(required) Ingestion IDs support (* and ?) wildcards
when a single ingestion ID is specified. Use double
quotes to enclose ingestion IDs with wildcards.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 512


## Page 513


Option Description
-v, --version , --versions
(optional) Content versions to index. Asterisks (*) and
question marks (?) are permitted as multi-character
wildcard expressions and single-character wildcard
expressions respectively, if only one content version is
specified. If multiple content versions are provided,
enter valid and fully specified 14-digit version IDs.
-vr , --version-range (optional) Specifies the ingestion ID range that includes
the start and end of the ingestion.
--verbose (optional) Verbosity level of messages on the console.
When not specified, no messages are displayed.
Examples
Example 1 - Delete indexes for all content versions
MobiusRemoteCLI.bat ftijobdelete -r AC001 -v "*" -u ADMIN -s MyDocServer
On a Windows system, this example authenticates the 'ADMIN' user, specifies the MyDocServer content source
(document server) connection to process the index request, then deletes all content class versions for the
content class.
A successful job displays the following:
Mobius CLI tool

ors.

us.
Example 2 - Deleting a range of indexes
MobiusRemoteCLI.bat ftijobdelete -u ADMIN -s MyDocServer1,MyDocServer2 -r AC001 -vr 2015010
1000000 20150601000000
On a Windows system, this example for authenticates the 'ADMIN' user, specifies the two content source
connections (MyDocServer1 and 2) to process the index request, then deletes indexes for content versions
between 1/1/2015 and 6/1/2015 for the content class.
A successful job displays the following:

MobiusRemoteCli commands
