ftijobdelete command - Delete report versions from FTS index
MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobquery
MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobquery provides statuses and lists of full text search (FTS) index jobs.
Review the prerequisites before using MobiusRemoteCLI commands.
Syntax
Windows
MobiusRemoteCLI.bat ftijobquery [options]
Linux
MobiusRemoteCLI.sh ftijobquery [options]
To view the full list of options, type MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobquery -h.
MobiusRemoteCLI ftijobquery and its options are almost the same as the heritage ftijobquery command.
Options
Option Description
-s document server link, --doc-server document server link
(required) Content repository name. Use quotation
marks when the repository name contains embedded
spaces. For example, "Account group".
-u recipient ID, --user recipient ID (optional) Recipient ID. Optional only if
MobiusRemoteCLI authentication is configured.
-p password (optional) Repository password for the user id.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 514


## Page 515


Option Description
-e error level, --error-level error level
(optional) Minimum error level to record during
indexing. Values can be upper or lower case characters:
• i - informational
• w - warning
• x - (default) exception
• e - error
• d - disaster
-o outputFile, --output-file outputFile (optional) Absolute path and file name of the file that
contains the output of the command.
-r content class ID, --report content class ID
(required) Ingestion IDs support (* and ?) wildcards
when a single ingestion ID is specified. Use double
quotes to enclose ingestion IDs with wildcards.
-v, --version , --versions
(optional) Content versions to index. Asterisks (*) and
question marks (?) are permitted as multi-character
wildcard expressions and single-character wildcard
expressions respectively, if only one content version is
specified. If multiple content versions are provided,
enter valid and fully specified 14-digit version IDs.
-vr , --version-range (optional) Specifies the ingestion ID range that includes
the start and end of the ingestion.
--verbose (optional) Verbosity level of messages on the console.
When not specified, no messages are displayed.
-st, --status
(optional) Status of a job to cancel. It is possible to
include one or more statuses. List of available statuses:
• UNKNOWN,
• PENDING,
• RESOLVING,
• RESOLVED,
• EXTRACTING,
• EXTRACTED,
• RUNNING,
• COMMITTING,
• CANCEL_REQ,
• CLEANUP_REQ,
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 515


## Page 516


Option Description
• CLEANING,
• CANCELLED,
• ERROR,
• COMPLETED,
• COMPLETED_WITH_ERRORS
-j, --joblist (optional) List of jobs to cancel.
-subrange, --submission-range
(optional) Specify the range of index jobs for
cancellation with a start and end submission-range.
Dates are in the format of the current locale. Specify
date and time in quotation marks (").
-b, --submitter (optional) Cancel jobs by specified job submitter.
-jr, –jobrange (optional) Specify the job range that includes the start
and end of the job range.
-d, --detail (optional) Details will include a list of content class IDs,
and ingestion IDs affected by this job. Default is false.
-dd, --superdetail
(optional) Lists all ingestion IDs and the document count
for each. Document counts reflect the index's current
state rather than the document count at the time of the
job, in case they are different. Default is false.
Examples
Example 1 - Query an index by a job ID
MobiusRemoteCLI.bat ftijobquery -j 25 -u ADMIN -s MyDocServer
This example authenticates the 'ADMIN' user, specifies the MyDocServer content source connection to process
the index request, then queries job 25.
A successful job displays the following:

ftijobquery command - Query index jobs
Mobius index utility
The Mobius index utility is a collection of stored procedures that provide maintenance for index groups.
You can call the stored procedures using scripts, or a database tool such as Oracle SQL Developer or SQL Server
Management Studio. Call stored procedures in the correct sequence, providing the necessary information as
arguments to the procedure calls.
Note: Applies to Mobius installations using Oracle Version 11g revision 2 or later, or Microsoft SQL
Server 2017 or later.
Error reporting in Oracle is done by writing to a virtual console; these messages can be echoed to the user console
for review. In SQL Server, the equivalent PRINT functionality is used.
Use the Mobius index utility for the following maintenance functions:
• Merging existing standalone indexes into a new index group.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 517


## Page 518


(optional) Renaming the indexes in the destination group.
(optional) Constrained by one or more content class IDs.
• Adding a new, empty index to an existing index group.
• Combining two existing index groups into a new index group.
• Extracting a index from a index group to a new independent index.
• Dropping a member index from an existing index group.
The general organization of any maintenance task that you perform with the Mobius index utility is to collect a
sequence of procedure calls into a job. A job consists of an initial setup procedure call, one or more procedure calls
to complete the steps of the maintenance task, and finally a finish procedure call that performs all housekeeping
cleanup for the task. For the duration of the job, the job status and any necessary work information is stored in
special tables and is persistent. For this reason, a job in progress may be run with any amount of time elapsed
between the steps.
Combining independent indexes into index groups
Combine independent indexes to create a new index group, or combine and add the independent indexes to
an existing index group. You can combine entire index tables in a single step or perform the process
incrementally, one Content Class ID at a time.
Modifying index groups
Index groups can be modified to add or drop an index from an index group, extract a member index from an
index group to a new independent index, and suppress table and index analysis while adding member indexes.
Combining existing index groups into a new index group
Combine two existing index groups, creating a new index group. All data is transferred to the new index group,
and the source index groups are removed.
Combining two existing index groups into one index group
Combine two existing index groups into a single index group by replacing the second source index group with
the combined index group. All data is transferred to the second source and then the first source index group is
removed.
Data formats for numeric and date indexes
Review the following tables for supported date and numeric formats.
Combining independent indexes into index groups
Combine independent indexes to create a new index group, or combine and add the independent indexes to an
existing index group. You can combine entire index tables in a single step or perform the process incrementally, one
Content Class ID at a time.
Every index, also known as a 'topic', combining job requires several steps to be run in the correct order. The exact
sequence of steps needed varies depending on the particular job requirements.
Every index combination job consists of the following broad phases:
Initialize the job
In this phase the utility makes backups of the index catalog tables and clears the job status tables.
Input job specifications
You supply information about which indexs are to be combined, the scope of the rows to be selected, and the
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 518


## Page 519


destination index group.
Copy the index (topic) data
This phase performs the actual data copy. It is the most resource-intensive step, sometimes requiring several
hours for large copy jobs.
Finish the job
This includes creating or recreating indexes on the output group table, and cleaning up the job status.
Sample index merge scripts for several typical index merge jobs are provided. You can these scripts by extracting the
samples and pasting it into a text editor. Adjust the parameters and stored procedure calls to suit your index merge
job.
Note: When using the content merge script on SQL Server 2016 or later, make sure that the user which
creates and runs the index merge script is part of the db_owner role and also that this users default
schema is the same as the Mobius table schema. Otherwise, one of the following will happen:
• If the index merge scripts are being created, the script will detect the differences in schema and
will not create them, indicating that there is a difference in schema and that the user should log
in as the user who created the Mobius table.
• If a user with a different default schema than that of the Mobius tables tries to run one of the
index merge script, the procedure will detect the differences in schema and stop execution
before doing its intended task. The user is showed a message indicating the need to log in as the
user who created the Mobius tables. Note that every procedure mentioned in this document has
the schema check logic.
Moving independent indexes to a new group table example
Independent indexes, also known as 'topics', can be moved to a new group table using the index merge script.
Moving entire independent indexes to a new group table example
Independent indexes, also known as 'topics', can be moved to a new group table using the index merge script.
Copying portions of independent indexes to a new group table example
Portions of independent indexes, also known as 'topics', can be copied to a new group table using the index
merge script.
Copying portions of independent indexes to an existing group table example
Portions of independent indexes, also known as 'topics', can be copied to an existing group table using the
index merge script.
Moving independent indexes to a new group table example
Independent indexes, also known as 'topics', can be moved to a new group table using the index merge script.
Oracle
This is a sample index merge script for moving entire independent indexes to a new group table, using Oracle:
variable rc number
exec VdrTopicUtil.StartTopicMergeJob(:rc);
exec SetTablespaceForTables(:rc,'TS_FSA_TABLE_DATA');
exec SetTablespaceForIndex(:rc,'TS_FSA_INDEX_DATA');
exec VdrTopicUtil.IncludeTopic(:rc,'ACCOUNT');
exec VdrTopicUtil.IncludeTopic(:rc,'CHECK_NUM');
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 519


## Page 520


exec VdrTopicUtil.IncludeTopic(:rc,'AMOUNT');
exec VdrTopicUtil.IncludeTopic(:rc,'DATE');
exec VdrTopicUtil.IncludeTopic(:rc,'PAYEE');
exec VdrTopicUtil.CreateNewGroup(:rc,5,'CHECKS','Check Image Application')
exec VdrTopicUtil.CopyTopicData(:rc);
exec VdrTopicUtil.FinishTopicMergeJob(:rc);
PostgreSQL
Equivalent PostgreSQL example:
select * from VDRTopicUtil.StartTopicMergeJob();
select * from VDRTopicUtil.IncludeTopic('ACCOUNT');
select * from VDRTopicUtil.IncludeTopic('CHECK_NUM');
select * from VDRTopicUtil.IncludeTopic('AMOUNT');
select * from VDRTopicUtil.IncludeTopic('DATE');
select * from VDRTopicUtil.IncludeTopic('PAYEE');
select * from VDRTopicUtil.CreateNewGroup(5, 'CHECKS','Check Image Application');
select * from VDRTopicUtil.CopyTopicData();
select * from VDRTopicUtil.FinishTopicMergeJob();

SQL Server
Equivalent SQL Server example:
declare @rc numeric
exec VDRTopicUtil_StarttopicMergeJob
exec VDRTopicUtil_IncludeTopic 'ACCOUNT' ,null,1,1,@rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_IncludeTopic 'CHECK_NUM' ,null,1,1,@rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_IncludeTopic 'AMOUNT' ,null,1,1,@rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_IncludeTopic 'DATE'  ,null,1,1,@rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_IncludeTopic 'PAYEE'  ,null,1,1,@rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_CreateNewGroup 5, 'CHECKS','Check Image Application',@rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_CopyTopicData @rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_FinishTopicMergeJob @rc out
Moving entire independent indexes to a new group table example
Independent indexes, also known as 'topics', can be moved to a new group table using the index merge script.
Oracle
This is a sample index merge script for moving entire independent indexes to a new group table, renaming the
indexes in the index group using Oracle:
exec VdrTopicUtil.StartTopicMergeJob(:rc);
exec SetTablespaceForTables(:rc,'TS_FSA_TABLE_DATA');
exec SetTablespaceForIndexes(:rc,'TS_FSA_INDEX_DATA');
exec VdrTopicUtil.IncludeTopic(:rc,'ACCOUNT', 'CHK_ACCT');
exec VdrTopicUtil.IncludeTopic(:rc,'CHECK_NUM', 'CHK_NUM');
exec VdrTopicUtil.IncludeTopic(:rc,'AMOUNT', 'CHK_AMT');
exec VdrTopicUtil.IncludeTopic(:rc,'DATE',  'CHK_DATE');
exec VdrTopicUtil.IncludeTopic(:rc,'PAYEE',  'CHK_PAYEE');
exec VdrTopicUtil.CreateNewGroup(:rc,5,'CHECKS','Check Image Application')
exec VdrTopicUtil.CopyTopicData(:rc);
exec VdrTopicUtil.FinishTopicMergeJob(:rc);
PostgreSQL
Equivalent PostgreSQL example:
select * from VDRTopicUtil.StartTopicMergeJob();
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 522


## Page 523


select * from VDRTopicUtil.IncludeTopic('ACCOUNT','CHK_ACCT');
      select * from VDRTopicUtil.IncludeTopic('CHECK_NUM','CHK_NUM');
      select * from VDRTopicUtil.IncludeTopic('AMOUNT','CHK_AMT');
      select * from VDRTopicUtil.IncludeTopic('DATE','CHK_DATE');
      select * from VDRTopicUtil.IncludeTopic('PAYEE','CHK_PAYEE');
      select * from VDRTopicUtil.CreateNewGroup(5, 'CHECKS','Check Image Application');
      select * from VDRTopicUtil.CopyTopicData();
      select * from VDRTopicUtil.FinishTopicMergeJob();

declare @rc numeric
exec VDRTopicUtil_StarttopicMergeJob
exec VDRTopicUtil_IncludeTopic 'ACCOUNT','CHK_ACCT',1,1,@rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_IncludeTopic 'CHECK_NUM','CHK_NUM',1,1,@rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_IncludeTopic 'AMOUNT','CHK_AMT',1,1,@rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_IncludeTopic 'DATE','CHK_DATE',1,1,@rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_IncludeTopic 'PAYEE','CHK_PAYEE',1,1,@rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_CreateNewGroup 5, 'CHECKS','Check Image Application',@rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_CopyTopicData @rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_FinishTopicMergeJob @rc out
Copying portions of independent indexes to a new group table example
Portions of independent indexes, also known as 'topics', can be copied to a new group table using the index merge
script.
Oracle
This is a sample index merge script to copy a portion of independent indexes to a new group table, selected by
Content Class ID and using Oracle:
exec VdrTopicUtil.StartTopicMergeJob(:rc);
exec SetTablespaceForTables(:rc,'TS_FSA_TABLE_DATA');
exec SetTablespaceForIndexes(:rc,'TS_FSA_INDEX_DATA');
exec VdrTopicUtil.RestrictByReport(:rc,'CHKIMAGES');
exec VdrTopicUtil.IncludeTopic(:rc,'ACCOUNT', 'CHK_ACCT');
exec VdrTopicUtil.IncludeTopic(:rc,'CHECK_NUM', 'CHK_NUM');
exec VdrTopicUtil.IncludeTopic(:rc,'AMOUNT', 'CHK_AMT');
exec VdrTopicUtil.IncludeTopic(:rc,'DATE',  'CHK_DATE');
exec VdrTopicUtil.IncludeTopic(:rc,'PAYEE',  'CHK_PAYEE');
exec VdrTopicUtil.CreateNewGroup(:rc,5,'CHECKS','Check Image Application')
exec VdrTopicUtil.CopyTopicData(:rc);
exec VdrTopicUtil.FinishTopicMergeJob(:rc);
PostgreSQL
Equivalent PostgreSQL example:
select * from VDRTopicUtil.StartTopicMergeJob();
select * from VDRTopicUtil.RestrictByReport(:rc,'CHKIMAGES');
select * from VDRTopicUtil.IncludeTopic('ACCOUNT', 'CHK_ACCT');
select * from VDRTopicUtil.IncludeTopic('CHECK_NUM', 'CHK_NUM');
select * from VDRTopicUtil.IncludeTopic('AMOUNT', 'CHK_AMT');
select * from VDRTopicUtil.IncludeTopic('DATE',  'CHK_DATE');
select * from VDRTopicUtil.IncludeTopic('PAYEE',  'CHK_PAYEE');
select * from VDRTopicUtil.CreateNewGroup(5, 'CHECKS','Check Image Application');
select * from VDRTopicUtil.CopyTopicData();
select * from VDRTopicUtil.FinishTopicMergeJob();

exec VDRTopicUtil_StarttopicMergeJob
exec VDRTopicUtil_RestrictByReport 'CHKIMAGES', @rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_IncludeTopic 'ACCOUNT', 'CHK_ACCT',1,1,@rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_IncludeTopic 'CHECK_NUM', 'CHK_NUM',1,1,@rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_IncludeTopic 'AMOUNT', 'CHK_AMT',1,1,@rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_IncludeTopic 'DATE',  'CHK_DATE',1,1,@rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_IncludeTopic 'PAYEE',  'CHK_PAYEE',1,1,@rc out

exec VDRTopicUtil_CopyTopicData @rc out

Copying portions of independent indexes to an existing group table example
Portions of independent indexes, also known as 'topics', can be copied to an existing group table using the index
merge script.
Oracle
This is a sample index merge script for copying a portion of independent indexes to an existing group table, selected
by Content Class ID and using Oracle:
set linesize 300
set trimspool on
set pagesize 1000
set serveroutput on size 1000000
set autoprint on
variable rc number
variable rtncurs refcursor
exec VdrTopicUtil.StartTopicMergeJob(:rc);
exec SetTablespaceForTables(:rc,'TS_FSA_TABLE_DATA');
exec SetTablespaceForIndexes(:rc,'TS_FSA_INDEX_DATA');
exec VdrTopicUtil.RestrictByReport(:rc,'CHKSTMTS');
exec VdrTopicUtil.RestrictByReport(:rc,'CHKAUDIT');
exec VdrTopicUtil.IncludeTopic(:rc,'ACCOUNT', 'CHK_ACCT');
exec VdrTopicUtil.IncludeTopic(:rc,'CHECK_NUM', 'CHK_NUM');
exec VdrTopicUtil.IncludeTopic(:rc,'AMOUNT', 'CHK_AMT');
exec VdrTopicUtil.IncludeTopic(:rc,'DATE',  'CHK_DATE');
exec VdrTopicUtil.IncludeTopic(:rc,'PAYEE',  'CHK_PAYEE');
exec VdrTopicUtil.ConfirmGroup(:rc,5,'CHECKS')
exec VdrTopicUtil.CopyTopicData(:rc);
exec VdrTopicUtil.FinishTopicMergeJob(:rc);
set feedback on
set verify on
set termout on
set echo on
exit
SQL Server
Equivalent SQL Server example:
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 528


## Page 529


declare @rc numeric
exec VDRTopicUtil_StarttopicMergeJob
exec VDRTopicUtil_RestrictByReport 'CHKSTMTS', @rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_RestrictByReport 'CHKAUDIT', @rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_IncludeTopic 'ACCOUNT', 'CHK_ACCT',1,1,@rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_IncludeTopic 'CHECK_NUM', 'CHK_NUM',1,1,@rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_IncludeTopic 'AMOUNT', 'CHK_AMT',1,1,@rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_IncludeTopic 'DATE',  'CHK_DATE',1,1,@rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_IncludeTopic 'PAYEE',  'CHK_PAYEE',1,1,@rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_ConfirmGroup 5 , 'CHECKS', @rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_CopyTopicData @rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_FinishTopicMergeJob @rc out
Modifying index groups
Index groups can be modified to add or drop an index from an index group, extract a member index from an index
group to a new independent index, and suppress table and index analysis while adding member indexes.
Adding a new index to an existing index group
A new empty index can be added to an existing index group. The index is added as the last index in the group,
and the column is added to the rightmost position in the group table.
Suppressing table and index analysis while adding member indexes
To ensure that execution plans created for the modified objects are using up-to-date information, Mobius
analyzes modified tables and indexes, also known as 'topics', while adding member indexes to a group.
Dropping an index from an index group
An empty index that is recently added to an index group can be deleted if its parameters are incorrect.
Extracting a member index from an index group to a new independent index
You can extract a member index from an existing index group into a new independent index. All data is moved,
and the new independent index has the same characteristics as it did before.
Adding a new index to an existing index group
A new empty index can be added to an existing index group. The index is added as the last index in the group, and
the column is added to the rightmost position in the group table.
Every index add job consists of the following broad phases:
1. Initialize the job. In this phase, VDRTopicUtil performs the setup housekeeping for the job.
2. Select the target index group. In this phase, you specify the index group to which you want to add a column.
The existence of the index group is verified, and the current indexes in the group are listed to the console.
3. Define a new index and add it. These two steps may be repeated as many times as needed to add all new
indexes in one job:
◦ Define. This phase allows you to specify all the parameters necessary to set up the new index.
◦ Add the index. This phase modifies the index table, and updates the index catalog information to reflect
the existence of the new index.
4. Finish the job. This phase includes analyzing the modified group table, and cleaning up the job status.
When adding a new TOPIC, follow these rules when specifying parameters for the
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 529


## Page 530


VdrTopicUtil.DefineAdditionalTopic procedure.
• If you are adding a CHARACTER-type index, the TOPIC_ITEM_MAX_LENGTH parameter must be set to 30 or
255. No other value is permitted.
• If you are adding a DATE or NUMERIC-type index, the TOPIC_ITEM_MAX_LENGTH parameter must be set to 0.
No other value is permitted.
• The fifth parameter from the left is used to specify the data format of the TOPIC:
◦ If the data type of the TOPIC is CHARACTER, this parameter must be set to 0.
◦ If the data type of the TOPIC is NUMERIC, this parameter must be a value between 0 and 146. Refer to
the "Date formats" tpoic for format specifications.
◦ If the data type of the TOPIC is DATE, this parameter must be value between 0-59. See topic "Data format
for numeric and date indexes" for format specifications.
Oracle
This is a sample script for adding a new independent index to an existing group table, using Oracle:
exec VdrTopicUtil.StartAddTopicToGroupJob;
      exec VdrTopicUtil.SelectTopicAddGroup(:rc, 'GRPRNM');
      exec VdrTopicUtil.DefineAdditionalTopic(:rc, 'NEWTOPIC2', 'Y','CHAR', 0, 30);
      exec VdrTopicUtil.AddTopicToGroup(:rc);
      exec VdrTopicUtil.DefineAdditionalTopic(:rc, 'NEWTOPIC3', 'Y','NUM', 0, 0);
      exec VdrTopicUtil.AddTopicToGroup(:rc);
      exec VdrTopicUtil.FinishAddTopicToGroupJob(:rc);
PostgreSQL
Equivalent PosgreSQL example:
select * from VDRTopicUtil.StartAddTopicToGroupJob();
      select * from VDRTopicUtil.SelectTopicAddGroup('GRPRNM');
      select * from VDRTopicUtil.DefineAdditionalTopic('NEWTOPIC2', 'Y','CHAR', 0, 30);
      select * from VDRTopicUtil.AddTopicToGroup();
      select * from VDRTopicUtil.DefineAdditionalTopic('NEWTOPIC3', 'Y','NUM', 0, 0);
      select * from VDRTopicUtil.AddTopicToGroup();
      select * from VDRTopicUtil.FinishAddTopicToGroupJob();

SQL Server
Example SQL Server equivalent (the output should be similar to that in Oracle):
set nocount on
exec VdrTopicUtil_StartAddTopicToGroupJob
exec VdrTopicUtil_SelectTopicAddGroup 'GRPRNM'
exec VdrTopicUtil_DefineAdditionalTopic 'NEWTOPIC2', 'Y','CHAR', 0 ,30
exec VdrTopicUtil_AddTopicToGroup
exec VdrTopicUtil_DefineAdditionalTopic 'NEWTOPIC3', 'Y','NUM', 0 ,0
exec VdrTopicUtil_AddTopicToGroup
exec VdrTopicUtil_FinishAddTopicToGroupJob
set nocount off

Related information
Data formats for numeric and date indexes
Suppressing table and index analysis while adding member indexes
To ensure that execution plans created for the modified objects are using up-to-date information, Mobius analyzes
modified tables and indexes, also known as 'topics', while adding member indexes to a group.
This process can be time consuming if there are a large number of group instance records. To avoid this, you can
suppress the table and index analysis and use standard maintenance procedures to keep statistics up-to-date. To do
this, add the input parameter of TRUE or 1 to the final job step.
Oracle
This is a sample script for adding a new independent index to an existing group table with table and index analysis
suppressed, using Oracle:
Note: The second parameter for FinishAddTopicToGroupJob has a TRUE value to suppress analysis.
 exec VdrTopicUtil.StartAddTopicToGroupJob;
      exec VdrTopicUtil.SelectTopicAddGroup(:rc, 'GRPRNM');
      exec VdrTopicUtil.DefineAdditionalTopic(:rc, 'NEWTOPIC2', 'Y','CHAR', 0, 30);
      exec VdrTopicUtil.AddTopicToGroup(:rc);
      exec VdrTopicUtil.DefineAdditionalTopic(:rc, 'NEWTOPIC3', 'Y','NUM', 0, 0);
      exec VdrTopicUtil.AddTopicToGroup(:rc);
      exec VdrTopicUtil.FinishAddTopicToGroupJob(:rc, TRUE);
PostgreSQL
Equivalent PostgreSQL example:
select * from VDRTopicUtil.StartAddTopicToGroupJob();
      select * from VDRTopicUtil.SelectTopicAddGroup('GRPRNM');
      select * from VDRTopicUtil.DefineAdditionalTopic('NEWTOPIC2', 'Y','CHAR', 0, 30);
      select * from VDRTopicUtil.AddTopicToGroup();
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 535


## Page 536


select * from VDRTopicUtil.DefineAdditionalTopic('NEWTOPIC3', 'Y','CHAR', 0, 30);
      select * from VDRTopicUtil.AddTopicToGroup();
      select * from VDRTopicUtil.FinishAddTopicToGroupJob(TRUE);

Dropping an index from an index group
An empty index that is recently added to an index group can be deleted if its parameters are incorrect.
Note: Once you delete the index from the group, all index data is lost and cannot be recovered. You
should have a complete, reliable backup of your database before attempting this operation.
In general, deleting indexes should be accomplished using the vdrdbxml command, and not this function.
Since this operation requires several steps, and prints diagnostic information to the console at each step, it is better
to run each job step in a separate script and examine the console output before proceeding.
Every index delete job consists of the following broad phases:
Initialize the job
In this phase, VDRTopicUtil performs the setup housekeeping for the job.
Select the target index group
In this phase you specify the index group from which you want to drop a index. The existence of the index
group is verified and the current indexes in the group are listed to the console.
Confirm the index
In this phase you must confirm the selection of the index that you want to drop.
Drop the index
This phase modifies the index table and updates the index catalog information to reflect the deletion of the
index.
Finish the job
This phase includes analyzing the modified group table, and cleaning up the job status.
Selecting an existing group table
You can specify the index group from which you wish to drop a index. The existence of the index group is
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 540


## Page 541


verified and the current indexes in the group are listed to the console.
Confirming the index to drop
Confirm the selection of the index, also known as a 'topic' that you want to drop.
Dropping an index
Dropping an index, also known as a 'topic', modifies the index table and updates the index catalog information
to reflect the deletion of the index.
Selecting an existing group table
You can specify the index group from which you wish to drop a index. The existence of the index group is verified and
the current indexes in the group are listed to the console.
Oracle
This is a sample script for selecting an existing group table, using Oracle:
exec VdrTopicUtil.StartDropTopicFromGroupJob;
exec VdrTopicUtil.SelectTopicDropGroup(:rc, 'GRPRNM');
PostgreSQL
Equivalent PostgreSQL example:
select * from VDRTopicUtil.StartDropTopicFromGroupJob();
select * from VDRTopicUtil.SelectTopicDropGroup('GRPRNM');

## Page 546


Extracting a member index from an index group to a new independent index
You can extract a member index from an existing index group into a new independent index. All data is moved, and
the new independent index has the same characteristics as it did before.
Every index extract job consists of the following broad phases:
1. Initialize the job. In this phase, VDRTopicUtil performs the setup housekeeping for the job.
2. Select the target index group. In this phase you specify the index group from which you want to extract a index.
The existence of the index group is verified and the current indexes in the group are listed to the console.
3. Confirm the index. In this phase you must confirm the selection of the index that you want to extract.
4. Extract the index. This phase modifies the index table and updates the index catalog information to reflect the
extraction of the index.
5. Finish the job. This phase includes analyzing the modified group table, the new independent index table, and
cleaning up the job status.
Oracle
This is a sample script for extracting a member index from a index group to a new independent index, using Oracle:
set linesize 300
set trimspool on
set pagesize 1000
set serveroutput on size 1000000
set autoprint on
set time on timi on
variable rc number
variable rtncurs refcursor
exec VdrTopicUtil.StartExtractTopicFromGroup;
exec VdrTopicUtil.SelectExtractTopicGroup(:rc, 'GRPRNM');
exec VdrTopicUtil.ConfirmExtractTopic(:rc, 'NEWTOPIC5');
exec VdrTopicUtil.ExtractTopicFromGroup(:rc);
exec VdrTopicUtil.FinishExtractTopicFromGroup(:rc);
exit
PostgreSQL
This is the same script using PostgreSQL syntax:
  select * from VDRTopicUtil.StartExtractTopicFromGroup();
      select * from VDRTopicUtil.SelectExtractTopicGroup('GRPRNM');
      select * from VdrTopicUtil.ConfirmExtractTopic('NEWTOPIC5');
      select * from VdrTopicUtil.ExtractTopicFromGroup();
      select * from VdrTopicUtil.FinishExtractTopicFromGroup();

Combining existing index groups into a new index group
Combine two existing index groups, creating a new index group. All data is transferred to the new index group, and
the source index groups are removed.
Every index group combine job consists of the following phases:
1. Initialize the job. VDRTopicUtil performs the setup housekeeping for the job.
2. Select the target index groups:
3. Merge the two source index groups into the new index group.
◦ Specify the two existing index groups you want to combine.
◦ Specify a name for the new index group that will be created.
4. Finish the job. The new group table is analyzed and a clean up of the job status is done.
Oracle
This sample script combines two existing index groups into a new index group, using Oracle:
set linesize 300
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 549


## Page 550


set trimspool on
set pagesize 1000
set serveroutput on size 1000000
set autoprint on
set time on timi on
variable rc number
variable rtncurs refcursor
exec VdrTopicUtil.StartTopicGroupMerge;
exec VdrTopicUtil.SelectGroupsToMerge (:rc, 'SRC_GRP1', 'SRC_GRP2', 'DEST_GRP3');
exec VdrTopicUtil.MergeGroups (:rc);
exec VdrTopicUtil.FinishTopicGroupMerge (:rc);
exit
SQL Server
Equivalent SQL Server example (the output should be similar to that in Oracle):
set nocount on
declare @rc numeric
exec VDRTopicUtil_StartTopicGroupMerge
exec VDRTopicUtil_SelectGroupsToMerge @rc out, 'SRC_GRP1', 'SRC_GRP2', 'DEST_GRP3'
exec VDRTopicUtil_MergeGroups @rc out
exec VDRTopicUtil_FinishTopicGroupMerge @rc out
set nocount off

Combining two existing index groups into one index group
Combine two existing index groups into a single index group by replacing the second source index group with the
combined index group. All data is transferred to the second source and then the first source index group is removed.
Every index group combine job consists of the following phases:
1. Initialize the job. VDRTopicUtil performs the setup housekeeping for the job.
2. Select the target index groups
3. Merge the two source index groups into one.
◦ Specify the first of the existing index group you to combine.
◦ Specify the second of the existing index group you to combine. This index group will contain all the
combined data.
4. Finish the job. The modified group table is analyzed and a clean up of the job status is done.
Oracle
This sample script combines two existing index groups into a single index group, using Oracle:
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 551


## Page 552


exec VdrTopicUtil.StartTopicGroupMerge;
exec VdrTopicUtil.SelectGroupsToMerge (:rc, 'SRC_GRP1', 'SRC_GRP2', 'DEST_GRP3');
exec VdrTopicUtil.MergeGroups (:rc);
exec VdrTopicUtil.FinishTopicGroupMerge (:rc);
PostgreSQL
Equivalent PostgreSQL example:
select * from VDRTopicUtil.StartTopicGroupMerge();
select * from VDRTopicUtil.SelectGroupsToMerge('SRC_GRP1', 'SRC_GRP2', 'DEST_GRP3');
select * from VdrTopicUtil.MergeGroups();
select * from VdrTopicUtil.FinishTopicGroupMerge();

SQL Server
Equivalent SQL Server example (the output should be similar to that in Oracle):
set nocount on
  declare @rc numeric
  exec VDRTopicUtil_StartTopicGroupMerge
  exec VDRTopicUtil_SelectGroupsToMerge @rc out, 'SRC_GRP_A', 'SRC_GRP_B'
  exec VDRTopicUtil_MergeGroups @rc out
  exec VDRTopicUtil_FinishTopicGroupMerge @rc out

  set nocount off

## Page 569


Accessing Mobius content with DirectConnect
Use DirectConnect to create encrypted URLs that provide users with direct access to content.
First, the underlying REST service creates the unencrypted URL, which the user submits to launch Mobius View and
display the content.
• The URL can have a built-in expiration time and can be disabled by deleting the associated AES encryption key.
• If you regenerate the AES key, pre-existing URLs fail. You must submit the URL source version to the REST
service again to generate encrypted URLs that use the new AES key.
• Direct Connect supports GET requests and POST requests.
• Use HTTPS so that passwords can be passed as query parameters.
Syntax
Syntax to create the URL:
scheme://host:port/path[?parameters]
where:
scheme
Usually HTTPS, but could also be FTP, DATA, and so on.
host, port
Specify the server machine and port number.
path
Specifies the path to the REST service for creating the URL.
parameters
An ampersand-delimited sequence of key=value pairs following a question mark. Provided parameters include
download, search, open, display, and navigation.
Examples
Unencrypted URL example
https://appserver.mobius:8443/mobius/directconnect?type=1&redirect=false&serverID=MobiusDir
ect&reportID=AC001&versionID=20180625063405&rptsectID=SALEEACHA&pageNum=2&pageFrom=1&pageLi
mit=2&viewFormat=PDF&noExplorer=1
Encrypted URL example
https://appserver.mobius:8443/mobius/view?dc=AL0w-NUgc_royZQKYaZ127PxW7fm2PIQpsG0L90lgPt3Hs
QnEghEIWcqK-etzntbGn6RDQquFyW6RYAeJhA6L_MdsSvSZeXW6iXtuu9MO03Lmg5zsukkbk5o00S4ScFViZTOVmhSD
D34DHNiQUJnUzHWi77FG8aM2IeZRkY7xFGmqHv34mteNTr_yDxAbWCp84v9OLVy7qI5n34EYdtWOvlArxa5Z-w-LCC4
VvATdSTaOVsj43gyGcNzw_nbC7VMsesgC-wqO7hO33FadhNp619u2JN_F8ubtA
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 569


## Page 570


DirectConnect URL - Download parameters
Specify the entire path to the content class or index that contains the content to download.
Examples
This example downloads only page 1 of content class MANUEA.
https://appserver.mobius:8443/mobius/directconnect?type=1&dldownloadType=PAGE,1&serverID=testser
ver&reportID=AC001&versionID=20050116000000&rptsectID=MANUEA&dlconvertType=PDF&dldoCompress=tru
e&recipientID=myrecipient&password=mypassword&dldirect=true
This example is the same as the previous example, except that the download occurs immediately, without loading the
Mobius View.
https://usryecustsup04v.tcs.asgsupport.loc:8443/mobius/directconnect?serverID=VDRNET_8.0_FTS_1&r
ecipientID=ADMIN&password=&type=1&reportID=AC001&versionID=20180917164204&rptsectID=MANUEANWK&dl
downloadType=PAGE,1&dlconvertType=PDF&dldoCompress=true&redirect=true&dldirect=true
Parameters
dldownloadType (required)
Downloads the report to the user’s computer. If you omit this parameter, the URL opens the document in
Mobius View, rather than downloading it.
Valid values:
• ALL - Downloads all pages.
• ALLINSECTION - Downloads all the pages of the current section.
• PAGE,# - Downloads the specified page.
• RANGE,#,# - Downloads a specified range of pages. By default, you can specify a range of pages that
includes pages in subsequent sections. However, the page range is ignored if you specified that user
operations are restricted to the current section.
dlcombineSingleFile
For documents that allow it, combines multiple pages into one file. Valid values:
• False (default); do not combine
• True, combine
dldoCompress
Controls compression of the downloaded content. Values are:
• 0, F, False - Compresses data only from multiple-page files and files in an external format. Do not
compress single pages in CSV, XML, TXT, and PDF formats.
• 1, T, True - Compress data. This is the default value.
• 2 - Do not compress data. This option is valid only when the dldownloadType parameter is set to PAGE,#.
dlflatten
When compressing the downloaded content, specifies whether documents are organized into a hierarchy of
folders or only one folder. Valid values:
• False (default) - Classify documents into a folder hierarchy that defines the repository, content class,
ingestion, and index.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 570


## Page 571


• True - Classify documents into one folder that has a name that identifies the content class, ingestion, and
index.
dlconvertType (required)
Specifies how to convert the document into a specific document type. The conversion options for a particular
document depends on its original format. If you specify a format that is not suitable, an error message is
displayed.
Valid values: DEFAULT, PNG, BMP, JPEG, PDF, TEXT, CSV, HTML, XML
dlfileExt
Specifies the file extension. If this optional parameter is not set, the value of the dlconvertType parameter is
used.
Valid value: File extension.
dldirect
Downloads the page immediately without loading the Mobius View and specifies that embedded credentials are
used. If prompting is required, an error message displayed. This parameter is similar to the redirect parameter,
when redirect=true, where it doesn't generate an encoded URL.
Valid value: True
dlpolicyFile/dlpolicyName
This parameter is applicable only for extraction requests when dlconvertType=CSV. If you set dlconvertType to
CSV and you omit this parameter, the policy that has the same name as the report is used.
Valid value: policy name
dlpolicyGroup
Downloads the required group of fields in a group during extraction using Direct Connect process when
dlpolicyGroup=<policy field group name>. This parameter is applicable only for extraction requests when
dlconvertType=CSV. During extraction,
• If a group name is not provided on the REST call, the group defined as the default group in the policy is
used.
• If the default group is not defined, the first available group is used.
• If no groups are defined in the policy, then all fields are extracted.
password
Specifies the password for the specified serverID. If the specified server requires a password and you omit or
enter an incorrect password parameter, a login error message is displayed.
primaryRepoUser
Specifies the user for the primary repository. If the specified server requires a primary repository user and you
omit or enter an incorrect primaryRepoUser, a login error message is displayed.
redirect
After a user enters the unencrypted source URL in a browser, the redirect parameter returns the encrypted
URL to the browser, which automatically submits it for display.
Valid values:
• True - Redirects the encrypted URL to the browser.
• False - Sends the encrypted URL back to the user.
recipientID
Specifies the recipient ID for the specified serverID. If the specified server requires a recipient ID and you omit
or enter an invalid value, the login dialog box is displayed.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 571


## Page 572


reportID
Specifies the content class.
rptsectID
Specifies the section ID.
serverID
Specifies the name of the document server defined in Mobius Administrator. This field is required.
type
Specifies the request type.
Valid values:
• 1 - Specifies a restricted report request. The user can navigate only to versions and sections that are
specified in the reportID, rptsectID, and versionID parameters.
• 3 - Specifies a restricted topic request. The user can navigate only to topics specified in the topicID,
topicItem, topicVer, reportID, rptsectID, and versionID parameters.
• 4 - Specifies an extended report request. The user can navigate only to content classes that are on the
document server specified in theserverID parameter.
• 5 - Specifies an extended topic request. The user can navigate to indexes that are displayed in the
navigation pane and are on the document server specified in theserverID parameter.
• 6 - Specifies a request that disables access control. The user can navigate to indexes and content classes
that are on the document server specified in the serverID parameter.
• 7 - Specifies a request that disables access control. The user can navigate only to indexes that are on the
document server specified in the serverID parameter.
• 8 - Specifies a request for a workflow. The user can navigate to the workflow specified in the
workflowInstanceId, workflowNotificationId, and the optional expirationTimeMinutes parameters.
• 9 - Specifies a Microsoft Office request. The user can share a Microsoft Office document for editing.
• 10 - Specifies a request for a non-Mobius DocumentID. The user can view the document.
Note: DirectConnect is not supported for EOS and Folders.
versionID
Specifies the version ID in the format yyyymmddhhmmss.
DirectConnect URL - Search parameters
Create URLs that search Mobius repositories. Search parameters are not valid in URLs that retrieve documents,
navigate folders, and download content.
Example
This example locates all documents that include the metadata MARKETING and that the specified recipient has access
to.
https://appserver.mobius:8443/mobius/rest/directconnect?EQDCSearchType=VDR&server1=testserver&re
cip1=jwsmith&pw1=abcxyz&fld1=ACCT_NUM&dtype1=__AllMetaData&op1=EQ&val1=MARKETING&EQDCnoExplore
r=0
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 572


## Page 573


Required parameters
EQDCSearchType
Runs a search against a Mobius repository.
Valid value: VDR
server1
Specifies the server name defined in Mobius Administrator. For example, server1=myFirstServer.
recip1
Specifies the recipient ID defined on the document server. For example, recip1=jwsmith.
pw1
Password defined on the document server. For example, pw1=abcxyz.
Search criteria parameters
fld#
The name of the field to search. Each fld parameter must be followed by the numeric identifier of the search
condition that it belongs to. For example, fld1=ACCT_NUM and fld2=CHCK_NO.
op#
Comparison operator that defines the relationship between the fld, val, and endval parameter values,
followed by the numeric identifier of the search condition to which it belongs.
If thedtyype parameter is DATE or VERSION, these parameters apply.
Operator Description
EQ is on
LE is on or before
GE is on or after
NE is not on
YR is within the last 12 months
6M is within the last 6 months
3M is within the last 3 months
MO is within last month
WK is within last week
TD is today
YD is within year to date
BT is between
NB is not between
YD is within year to date
endval#
Specifies an additional field value that further restricts the result set. The end value is applicable only with the
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 573


## Page 574


between (BT) or not between (NB) operators. Each endval parameter must be followed by the numeric identifier
of the search criterion to which it belongs.
For example, to search for items with account numbers between 21000 and 25000, specify this:
fld1=ACCT_NUM&op1=BT&val1=21000&endval1=25000.
dtype#
Specifies the type of information that you are searching for. Each dtype parameter must be followed by the
numeric identifier of the search condition to which it belongs. For example, to find all items containing the
account number 21000, you might specify this: fld1=ACCT_NUM&op1=EQ&val1=21000&dtype1=STRING.
Valid values are REPORTID, VERSION, SECTIONID, DATE, STRING (default), NUMBER, __AllMetadata,
__FullContent, __ContentWithAllMetaData.
You can use the REPORTID, VERSION, and SECTIONID values together or individually, but only in combination
with a Topic search.
If you define a dtype as one of the following values, you must add a fld# with the same name as the dtype#.
__AllMetaData
Performs search against all topics. The Mobius server must be configured for simple search.
__FullContent
Performs a full-text search. The Mobius server must be configured for full-text search.
__ContentWithAllMetaData
Performs a full-text search and a simple search, and combines the results. The Mobius server must be
configured for full-text search.
showme#
Specifies whether the field is displayed as a column in search results. For example, showme1=false. The default
value is true, display the field.
To use a field in a query but prevent the field from being displayed in search results, specify showme1=false.
sort#
Specifies the sort order for a field. For example, fld2=RecordType&sort2=ascending&dtype2=STRING. The
default value is none, do not sort. Sorting is not supported for searches against VSAM topic indexes.
Valid values: none (default), ascending, and descending.
Interface parameters
EQDCnoExplore
Specifies whether users can navigate the folder hierarchy after they run the query.
Valid values are 0 (allow navigation) and 1 (default) do not allow navigation.
EQDCShowDialog
Specifies whether users can edit the query in the search editor before running it.
Valid values:
• 0 (default) - Run the query immediately without displaying the search editor
• 1 - Display the search editor with its fields populated, so that users can edit the query before running it.
annotationsMode
Controls whether users are allowed to create, edit, and delete annotations in documents. This might be
restricted based on repository capabilities and user access.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 574


## Page 575


Valid values:
• 0 (default) - Do not allow annotations
• 1 - Allow annotations
DirectConnect URL - Open, display, and navigation parameters
These parameters are used to locate and display documents, and navigate through the document hierarchy.
Example
This example shows the URL for a restricted report with multiple pages and no navigation pane. It specifies page 2 in
the section that has 12 pages, page from=1, and page limit=12.
https://appserver.mobius:8443/mobius/rest/directconnect?type=1&serverID=testserver&reportID=MARK
ETING+&versionID=20131022144943&rptsectID=TCI+and+SharePoint+White+Paper&pageNum=2&pageFrom=1&pa
geLimit=12&viewFormat=PDF&noExplorer=1
The result displays page 2 in the content class MARKETING and allows the viewing of all 12 pages. The navigation
pane is not accessible, so there is no way to browse the content class and indexes.
Parameters
exactMatch
Specifies how to match the values in a request.
Note: The exactMatch parameter is not compatible with the quickOpen parameter and
downloading operations.
Valid values:
0
If there is no exact match, the next sequential value is retrieved. If specified value is sequentially higher
than the value of items, the last value is retrieved.
For example, the repository has three content classes: ABC, ACCT, and ADS.
• You request reportID=ABB and exactMatch=0. Because there is no exact match to content classABB,
the next sequential content class, ABC, is retrieved.
• You request reportID=ADS and exactMatch=0. Because there is an exact match to content classADS,
it is retrieved.
• You request reportID=AE and exactMatch=0. Because there is no exact match to content classAE
and the value AE is higher than all content class values, the last content class, ADS, is retrieved.
1
Retrieves only exact matches.
For example, the repository has three content classes: ABC, ACCT, and ADS.
• You request reportID=ABE and exactMatch=1. Because there is no exact match to ABE, nothing is
retrieved.
• You request reportID=ABC and exactMatch=1. Because there is an exact match to content class ABC,
that content class is retrieved.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 575


## Page 576


2 (default)
Treats the value as a prefix, and retrieves the first exact match for the prefix. This setting provides a
shortcut to parameter entries because you can omit some characters of the value. If the request includes
the partialMatch-1 parameter, all items that match the prefix are retrieved.
For example, the repository has three content classes: ABC, ACCT, and ADS.
• You request reportID=ABEand exactMatch=2. Because there is no content class with the initial value
of ABE, nothing is retrieved.
• You request reportID=AC and exactMatch=2. Because the ACCT content class begins with the
characters AC, that content class is retrieved.
expirationTimeMinutes
Specifies the number of minutes that the URL is valid. If you omit this parameter, the URL never expires.
A valid value is a number greater than or equal to 0.
hostname
Specifies a hostname to replace the hostname in the generated URL.
noExplorer
Controls whether Mobius View displays the navigation pane.
Valid values:
• 0 (default) - Displays the navigation pane.
• 1 - Hide the navigation pane.
• 2 - Displays the navigation pane with breadcrumb navigation disabled.
pageFrom
Page number that defines the start page of a group of pages within each section that a recipient may view. The
pageLimit parameter works in with the pageFrom parameter to determine the actual page range.
If the pageLimit parameter is included in the URL, viewing is restricted to the number of pages specified by the
pageLimit. If the pageLimit parameter is omitted from the URL, recipients can view pages from the start page
to the end of the section.
Valid values:
• 0 (default) - Recipients can view pages from the beginning of the section.
• A whole number.
pageLimit
Specifies the number of pages that the recipient may view in each section, beginning with the page number
specified in the pageFrom parameter.
For example, if pageFrom=4 and the pageLimit=3, the recipient may view only the fourth, fifth and sixth pages of
the sections accessed by the URL.
Valid values:
• 0 (default) - Recipients may view pages from the start page, which is defined in the pageFrom parameter,
to the end of the section.
• A whole number.
pageNum
Specifies the page to open. If a page range is specified in the request, pageNum refers to the sequence within the
range. For example, if pageFrom=3, 1 refers to the third page, 2 refers to the fourth page, and so on.
Valid values:
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 576


## Page 577


• 1 (default)
• A whole number.
pageRBA
Specifies the read buffer address of the requested page. This parameter is valid only in conjunction with an
index request (type=3 or type =5).
partialMatch
Specifies whether to retrieve the first matching index item for all specified levels.
The partialMatch parameter is valid only when used with type=3 or type=5, where the lowest specified level is
topicItem=.The partialMatch parameter cannot be used with the quickOpen parameter.
Valid values:
• 0 (default) - Partial match is not in effect. Retrieves the first matching index item for all specified levels
and expands the index item at the lowest specified level.
• 1 - Partial match is in effect. Retrieves the first matching index item for all specified levels except for the
lowest. At the lowest level, retrieves the first n index items.
password
Specifies the password for the specified serverID. If the specified server requires a password and you omit or
enter an incorrect password parameter, a login error message is displayed.
primaryRepoUser
Specifies the user for the primary repository. If the specified server requires a primary repository user and you
omit or enter an incorrect primaryRepoUser, a login error message is displayed.
quickOpen
Automatically opens a page in Mobius View and determines which nodes to display and expand in the
navigation pane.
Valid values:
• 0 (default) - If the lowest node specifies a section (rptsectID= or pageRBA=), Mobius View displays a page.
Enables the automatic navigation of the document hierarchy to the lowest node specified in the URL.
When the navigation pane is displayed, the lowest node specified is expanded unless the lowest node is
Section.
For example, if the lowest node specified in the URL is Report ID, when quickOpen=0, the Report ID is
displayed in the Mobius View navigation pane and expanded to show its list of versions. No page is
displayed.
• 1 - Opens the first page that the recipient is authorized to view. Enables automatic navigation through the
document hierarchy to the lowest node specified in the URL and expands its parent node from that point.
The lowest specified node is expanded down to the section level.
For example, if the lowest node specified in the URL is Report ID, when quickOpen=1, Mobius View
displays reports beginning with the specified Report ID. The specified Report ID is expanded to show a list
of versions. The first version of the Report ID is expanded to show a list of sections. The first page of the
first section in the list is opened.
recipientID
Specifies the recipient ID for the specified serverID. If the specified server requires a recipient ID and you omit
or enter an invalid value, the login dialog box is displayed.
redirect
After a user enters the unencrypted source URL in a browser, the redirect parameter returns the encrypted
URL to the browser, which automatically submits it for display.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 577


## Page 578


Valid values:
• True - Redirects the encrypted URL to the browser.
• False - Sends the encrypted URL back to the user.
reportID
Specifies the content class.
rptsectID
Specifies the section ID.
serverID
Specifies the name of the document server defined in Mobius Administrator. This field is required.
subtype
Controls whether the navigation pane in Mobius View displays the topic and report branches. The value of 2
displays both.
topicID
Specifies the topic ID. Used only with the type=3 or type=5 parameter.
topicItem
Specifies the topic item name. Used only with the type=3 or type=5 parameter.
topicVer
Specifies the index version ID in the format yyyymmddhhmmss. Used only with the type=3 or type=5 parameter.
type
Specifies the request type.
Valid values:
• 1 - Specifies a restricted report request. The user can navigate only to versions and sections that are
specified in the reportID, rptsectID, and versionID parameters.
• 3 - Specifies a restricted topic request. The user can navigate only to topics specified in the topicID,
topicItem, topicVer, reportID, rptsectID, and versionID parameters.
• 4 - Specifies an extended report request. The user can navigate only to content classes that are on the
document server specified in theserverID parameter.
• 5 - Specifies an extended topic request. The user can navigate to indexes that are displayed in the
navigation pane and are on the document server specified in theserverID parameter.
• 6 - Specifies a request that disables access control. The user can navigate to indexes and content classes
that are on the document server specified in the serverID parameter.
• 7 - Specifies a request that disables access control. The user can navigate only to indexes that are on the
document server specified in the serverID parameter.
• 8 - Specifies a request for a workflow. The user can navigate to the workflow specified in the
workflowInstanceId, workflowNotificationId, and the optional expirationTimeMinutes parameters.
• 9 - Specifies a Microsoft Office request. The user can share a Microsoft Office document for editing.
• 10 - Specifies a request for a non-Mobius DocumentID. The user can view the document.
versionID
Specifies the version ID in the format yyyymmddhhmmss.
annotationsMode
Specifies whether users can create, edit, and delete annotations in documents. These actions might be
restricted based on repository configuration and user access.
Valid values:
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 578


## Page 579


• 0 (default) - Users cannot add, edit, and delete annotations.
• 1 - Users can add, edit, and delete annotations.
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 579


## Page 580


Migrating content from EOS to Mobius
Configuring the migration of content and archives from EOS to Mobius.
Before you begin: To migrate the content structure and archives from EOS to Mobius, ensure the following
components are added:
• EOS Archive Creator and FTP - Used to gather and transfer the content structure and the content archives from
EOS. The contents are transferred to a location accessible by the EOS Import Toolkit.
• EOS Import Toolkit - Download EOS Import Toolkit from Rocket Customer Community Portal and configure to
locate the Remote CLI and EOS files. Unzip the content to a folder. The JAR file is used to generate files for the
Remote CLI. Different sets of files and commands are generated depending on the migration of the content
structure and archives.
• MobiusRemote CLI - MobiusRemoteCLI entitiesimporter migrates the EOS content structure and
MobiusRemoteCLI acreate migrates the EOS archives.
• Mobius View - API and view server for the content.
• Mobius Server - Stores the migrated content.
1. Export and FTP the EOS structural files.
The distribution directory contains the following examples of JCL scripts to be executed from the EOS
environment.
◦ ReportDefinitions.jcl
◦ reportList.jcl
◦ UserGroups.jcl
Generated BIN files:
◦ EDU.EOS18M.PROFLSTM
◦ EDU.EOS18M.RINDX.RILIST
◦ EDU.EOS18M.FILIST
FTP the BIN files to the Open Systems for the next step.
2. Run the EOS Import Toolkit to generate the bulk JSON File.
Create an exporter.yaml file and place it in the directory where the jar file is executed.
Example of the yaml file.
pathToProfileList: "./EOSExports/EDU.EOS18M.PROFLSTM.bin"
pathToFormList: "./EOSExports/EDU.EOS18M.FILIST.bin"
pathToReportList: "./EOSExports/EDU.EOS18M.RINDX.RILIST.bin"
3. Run java -jar exporters-cli.jar -p from the command line.
On successful execution, a folder named out is created with EOSStructure.json file imported by the Remote
CLI.
4. Import the EOSStructure.json file into Mobius using the MobiusRemoteCLI entitiesimporter command.
MobiusRemoteCLI EntitiesImporter
-s repository
-u ADMIN
-f "./out/EOStructure.json"
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 580


## Page 581


--set-status-time 10
--log-file "log" --exception-file "./error.json"
Validate the Mobius repository to confirm if all the entities are created successfully.
5. Export and FTP the EOS archives.
The distribution directory contains the ArchiveExtraction.jcl JCL script example to be run from the EOS
environment.
Modify the script to ensure the proper archive is extracted. FTP the generated BIN file to the Open Systems for
the next step.
6. Run the EOS Import Toolkit to convert the archives.
Identify the EOS archives to migrate to Mobius. Use the exporter.yaml file created previously.
7. Run java -jar exporters-cli.jar -m EOSArchiveFilename from the command line to process an EOS
Archive.
On successful execution, a folder named out is created with meta.#.json files pointing the files to be archived
to Mobius using Remote CLI.
8. Archive the files into Mobius using the MobiusRemoteCLI acreate.
Use processing method m  for Meta based archiving.
MobiusRemoteCLI acreate
-s
  RepositoryName
-u
  UserName
-p
  Password
-f
  MetaJsonFileLocation
-m
  processingmethod
Validate the migration by ensuring that the report versions are archived, versions are indexed, and content is
rendered and accessible.
Note: Before running MobiusRemoteCLI acreate, specify the Index value in the Index Access
Rights for the Admin user as * (asterisk) in the Mobius Content Sources page.
9. Add EOS users to Mobius.
The EOS user is not ported as a Mobius user. The EOS Import Toolkit creates Mobius groups and not Mobius
users.
◦ If Mobius View for SSO is configured, the user can log in to Mobius with the authentication belonging to
the user group created by EOS.
◦ If the user is defined in Mobius Server, define the Mobius “recipientid” and add the user to the
appropriate user group created by the EOS Import Toolkit.
Related information
MobiusRemoteCLI acreate
MobiusRemoteCLI entitiesimporter
© 2026 Rocket Software, Inc. All Rights Reserved | Mobius Administrator Guide V12.4 581


## Page 582


EOS Import Toolkit configuration file
Define the fields in the EOS Import Toolkit configuration file to locate the Remote CLI and EOS files.
The following table describes the fields for the EOS Import Toolkit configuration file.
Table: Fields for the EOS Import Toolkit configuration file
Field Description Default Value
eosVersion
The version
of EOS
associated
with the
exported
files.
18
pathToProfileList
The local
path of the
exported
PROFILE
LIST file.
none
pathToFormList
The local
path of the
exported
FORM LIST
file.
none
pathToReportList
The local
